title: Dialog records stronger 3Q26 as it keeps focus on long-term plans
text: Dialog records stronger 3Q26 as it keeps focus on long-term plans

title: Dialog records stronger 3Q26 as it keeps focus on long-term plans
text: PETALING JAYA: Dialog Group Bhd is staying focused in the pursuit of its key long-term strategies, while remaining confident that its business model is well structured to weather headwinds arising from economic uncertainty, oil price volatility and currency movements.

title: Dialog records stronger 3Q26 as it keeps focus on long-term plans
text:
The group said it will continue to build and strengthen its competencies by

investing in as well as upskilling its workforce and digital transformation to ensure efficiency and competitiveness.

title: Dialog records stronger 3Q26 as it keeps focus on long-term plans
text: Releasing its results for the third quarter (3Q26) for the financial year ending June (FY26) yesterday, Dialog saw net profit improve by 9.9% year-on-year (y-o-y) to RM148.3mil, as revenue climbed 20.4% to RM696.9mil.

title: Dialog records stronger 3Q26 as it keeps focus on long-term plans
text: In a bourse filing, the group attributed the improved 1Q26 y-o-y showing to strong performance that was driven by positive contribution across its businesses, particularly its Malaysia operations.

title: Dialog records stronger 3Q26 as it keeps focus on long-term plans
text: Cumulatively for the nine months ended March, bottom line almost tripled y-o-y to RM426mil, as turnover increased by 11.5% to RM2.11bil.

title: Dialog records stronger 3Q26 as it keeps focus on long-term plans
text: “The group’s Malaysia operations remained very active across the downstream, midstream and upstream businesses, translating into improved profit contribution overall in the current financial quarter.

title: Dialog records stronger 3Q26 as it keeps focus on long-term plans
text: “The downstream business recorded increased contributions from various engineering, procurement, construction and commissioning projects, supported by ongoing new projects,” said Dialog.

title: Dialog records stronger 3Q26 as it keeps focus on long-term plans
text: It added that its midstream business continued to deliver stable revenue and earnings, underpinned by healthy tank storage occupancy, while its upstream operations benefitted from the higher realised oil prices.

title: Dialog records stronger 3Q26 as it keeps focus on long-term plans
text: Compared to the preceding quarter ended December 2025, Dialog charted a 7.7% growth in net profit from RM137.7mil, despite top line slipping by 7.9% from RM756.3mil.

title: Dialog records stronger 3Q26 as it keeps focus on long-term plans
text: For 3Q26, Dialog proposed a dividend of 1.7 sen per share, its only dividend declaration so far for FY26.

title: Dialog records stronger 3Q26 as it keeps focus on long-term plans
text: The group is confident that it is positioned to weather any short-term challenges that may arise due to the current volatile external environment.

title: Dialog records stronger 3Q26 as it keeps focus on long-term plans
text: “At the same time, our strategies place us in a favourable position to capitalise on any emerging medium and long term opportunities,” it said.

title: Mohamad Hasan joins BRICS Foreign Ministries' meeting in New Delhi on May 14-15
text: Mohamad Hasan joins BRICS Foreign Ministries' meeting in New Delhi on May 14-15

title: Mohamad Hasan joins BRICS Foreign Ministries' meeting in New Delhi on May 14-15
text: NEW DELHI: Malaysia’s participation in the upcoming BRICS Foreign Ministers’ Meeting in India reflects the country’s push to strengthen economic resilience, accelerate digital transformation and expand cooperation with emerging global economies.

title: Mohamad Hasan joins BRICS Foreign Ministries' meeting in New Delhi on May 14-15
text: Foreign Minister Datuk Seri Mohamad Hasan will attend the two-day meeting here beginning Thursday (May 14), with Malaysia expected to reinforce its commitment to multilateralism, sustainable development, global governance reform, inclusive economic cooperation and strengthening the voice of the Global South.

title: Mohamad Hasan joins BRICS Foreign Ministries' meeting in New Delhi on May 14-15
text: Malaysian High Commissioner to India Datuk Muzafar Shah Mustafa said the participation was also in line with Malaysia’s aspiration to become a full BRICS member.

title: Mohamad Hasan joins BRICS Foreign Ministries' meeting in New Delhi on May 14-15
text: "In line with the country’s aspiration to become a BRICS member state, Malaysia will continue to engage constructively with BRICS to strengthen cooperation in sectors of strategic importance,” he told reporters during a briefing on Wednesday (May 13).

title: Mohamad Hasan joins BRICS Foreign Ministries' meeting in New Delhi on May 14-15
text: He also described the meeting as a key preparatory platform ahead of the BRICS Leaders’ Summit scheduled for September.

title: Mohamad Hasan joins BRICS Foreign Ministries' meeting in New Delhi on May 14-15
text: According to Muzafar Shah, during the meeting, Mohamad is expected to deliver Malaysia’s statement in two main sessions themed "BRICS at 20: Building for Resilience, Innovation, Cooperation and Sustainability” and "Reforms of Global Governance and Multilateral System”.

title: Mohamad Hasan joins BRICS Foreign Ministries' meeting in New Delhi on May 14-15
text: "The minister is also expected to share Malaysia’s views on major global issues, including trade, global supply chains, energy security, digitalisation and climate action,” he said.

title: Mohamad Hasan joins BRICS Foreign Ministries' meeting in New Delhi on May 14-15
text: On the sidelines of the meeting, Mohamad is scheduled to pay a courtesy call on Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi and hold bilateral meetings with counterparts from countries including Cuba, Russia and India.

title: Mohamad Hasan joins BRICS Foreign Ministries' meeting in New Delhi on May 14-15
text: "Participation in this meeting reflects Malaysia’s commitment to a principled, balanced and pragmatic foreign policy, while strengthening its role as a proactive middle power in shaping a more inclusive, stable and sustainable global system,” Muzafar Shah said.

title: Mohamad Hasan joins BRICS Foreign Ministries' meeting in New Delhi on May 14-15
text: He added that Malaysia’s engagement with BRICS could open opportunities for cooperation in technology, energy, food security and climate change initiatives.

title: Mohamad Hasan joins BRICS Foreign Ministries' meeting in New Delhi on May 14-15
text: BRICS is a major intergovernmental and geopolitical bloc representing key emerging economies, originally formed by Brazil, Russia, India, China and South Africa.

title: Mohamad Hasan joins BRICS Foreign Ministries' meeting in New Delhi on May 14-15
text: Since 2024, the bloc has expanded to include the United Arab Emirates, Ethiopia, Indonesia, Iran and Egypt.

title: Mohamad Hasan joins BRICS Foreign Ministries' meeting in New Delhi on May 14-15
text: Malaysia, alongside Belarus, Bolivia, Cuba, Kazakhstan, Thailand, Uganda and Uzbekistan, became a BRICS partner country on Jan 1, 2025.

title: Mohamad Hasan joins BRICS Foreign Ministries' meeting in New Delhi on May 14-15
text: — Bernama

title: About 700,000 traders to benefit from Agro Penjaja-i initiative, says Agrobank
text: About 700,000 traders to benefit from Agro Penjaja-i initiative, says Agrobank

title: About 700,000 traders to benefit from Agro Penjaja-i initiative, says Agrobank
text: PUTRAJAYA: About 700,000 licensed traders under various hawker associations stand to benefit from Agrobank’s Agro Penjaja-i initiative, which aims to empower traders and micro-entrepreneurs, particularly in navigating global economic uncertainties.

title: About 700,000 traders to benefit from Agro Penjaja-i initiative, says Agrobank
text: Agrobank Group president and chief executive officer Datuk Tengku Ahmad Badli Shah Raja Hussin said the initiative, launched on Wednesday (May 13), targets small traders, particularly hawkers, by offering financing, advisory services and digital financial management and savings solutions.

title: About 700,000 traders to benefit from Agro Penjaja-i initiative, says Agrobank
text: “Through the initiative, traders and micro-entrepreneurs will be able to enjoy various benefits and special facilities, including micro financing schemes of up to RM100,000 and a Cashline-i credit facility of up to RM5,000.

title: About 700,000 traders to benefit from Agro Penjaja-i initiative, says Agrobank
text: “Agrobank also offers hibah, or returns on savings, under the Agro Penjaja-i account, which is additionally protected by takaful, with free takaful for the first 3,000 new customers for a year.

title: About 700,000 traders to benefit from Agro Penjaja-i initiative, says Agrobank
text: “We also provide a green lane for Ar-Rahnu processing of up to RM300,000, with special rates and returns on traders’ savings.

title: About 700,000 traders to benefit from Agro Penjaja-i initiative, says Agrobank
text: “Among the terms and conditions, applicants must be registered with a hawkers’ association and a local authority (PBT) to carry out business activities as traders,” he said at a press conference after the launch of the programme.

title: About 700,000 traders to benefit from Agro Penjaja-i initiative, says Agrobank
text: He said approvals would be based on repayment capacity and the needs of individual traders, including those who already receive financing from government agencies.

title: About 700,000 traders to benefit from Agro Penjaja-i initiative, says Agrobank
text: Meanwhile, Agriculture and Food Security Minister Datuk Seri Mohamad Sabu said the programme aligns with Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim’s call for banks, government-linked companies (GLCs) and government-linked investment companies (GLICs) to help ease the burden of small traders while sustaining economic activity.

title: About 700,000 traders to benefit from Agro Penjaja-i initiative, says Agrobank
text: “The initiative is a holistic approach by Agrobank to empower the trader and micro-entrepreneur community, which forms one of the backbones of the national economy.

title: About 700,000 traders to benefit from Agro Penjaja-i initiative, says Agrobank
text: “Through this initiative, we are not only providing easy and inclusive access to financing but also helping them improve their entrepreneurial knowledge, financial management and digital business capabilities,” he said.

title: About 700,000 traders to benefit from Agro Penjaja-i initiative, says Agrobank
text: Mohamad said the comprehensive approach could help traders increase their income, expand their businesses and strengthen their economic sustainability.

title: About 700,000 traders to benefit from Agro Penjaja-i initiative, says Agrobank
text: “We recognise that traders play a very important role in the national economic chain.

title: About 700,000 traders to benefit from Agro Penjaja-i initiative, says Agrobank
text: “They not only provide job opportunities and income for families but also form the backbone of the local community economy,” he said.

title: ECB urges banks to brace quickly for AI-assisted cyberattacks
text: ECB urges banks to brace quickly for AI-assisted cyberattacks

title: ECB urges banks to brace quickly for AI-assisted cyberattacks
text: FRANKFURT, May 13 (Reuters) - ⁠European Central Bank board member Frank Elderson ⁠on Wednesday urged banks in the euro area ‌to quickly prepare for potential cyberattacks launched with the help of Anthropic's Mythos AI model or similar tools.

title: ECB urges banks to brace quickly for AI-assisted cyberattacks
text: Elderson, who is ​Vice Chair of the ECB's bank ⁠supervision arm, said ⁠in an interview in ECB publication Supervision Newsletter that ⁠euro ‌area banks currently do not have access to Mythos.

title: ECB urges banks to brace quickly for AI-assisted cyberattacks
text: "Lack of access is not an ⁠excuse for inaction.

title: ECB urges banks to brace quickly for AI-assisted cyberattacks
text: On the contrary, it ​makes it ‌even more critical that banks step up and ⁠act now," ​he said.

title: ECB urges banks to brace quickly for AI-assisted cyberattacks
text: Reuters reported this week that large U.S. banks, which have been granted early access to Mythos, ⁠are rushing to fix scores of ​data system weaknesses flagged by the tool.

title: ECB urges banks to brace quickly for AI-assisted cyberattacks
text: Mythos is viewed by cybersecurity experts as a significant challengeto the banking ⁠industry and its data technology systems, prompting a series of warnings from regulators and policymakers.

title: ECB urges banks to brace quickly for AI-assisted cyberattacks
text: Elderson warned that banks need to brace for future AI models ​that enable even more aggressive ⁠cyberattacks.

title: ECB urges banks to brace quickly for AI-assisted cyberattacks
text: "We need to be able to deal with ​ever more capable future models that ‌could be released in relatively ​quick succession," he said.

title: ECB urges banks to brace quickly for AI-assisted cyberattacks
text: (Reporting by Ludwig Burger and Reinhard Becker, Editing by Linda Pasquini)

title: Germany eyes deeper economic footprint in Johor, 70 business reps visit JS-SEZ
text: Germany eyes deeper economic footprint in Johor, 70 business reps visit JS-SEZ

title: Germany eyes deeper economic footprint in Johor, 70 business reps visit JS-SEZ
text: ISKANDAR PUTERI: Germany is looking to deepen its economic footprint in Johor, with about 70 German business representatives set to explore opportunities in the Johor-Singapore Special Economic Zone (JS-SEZ).

title: Germany eyes deeper economic footprint in Johor, 70 business reps visit JS-SEZ
text: Germany’s ambassador to Malaysia Silke Riecken-Daer said the high-level “Team Germany” delegation was in Johor to strengthen cross-border cooperation and explore new investment opportunities in the rapidly developing economic zone.

title: Germany eyes deeper economic footprint in Johor, 70 business reps visit JS-SEZ
text: “We came here together in the true spirit of a cross-border trip to Johor,” she said after a meeting with Johor investment, trade, consumer affairs and human resources committee chairman Lee Ting Han at Bangunan Dato Jaafar Muhammad in Kota Iskandar here on Wednesday (May 13).

title: Germany eyes deeper economic footprint in Johor, 70 business reps visit JS-SEZ
text: The delegation included representatives from the German embassy in Singapore, the Malaysian-German Chamber of Commerce and Industry (MGCC), the Singaporean-German Chamber of Commerce (GCS), Germany Trade and Invest (GTAI) and 55 German companies based in Malaysia and Singapore.

title: Germany eyes deeper economic footprint in Johor, 70 business reps visit JS-SEZ
text: Riecken-Daer said the delegation also appreciated the presence of Johor Mentri Besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi, who joined part of the discussion.

title: Germany eyes deeper economic footprint in Johor, 70 business reps visit JS-SEZ
text: “We just had a very interesting initial discussion with Lee and his team, and also the Mentri Besar found the time to join us for part of the meeting, which is very much appreciated,” she said.

title: Germany eyes deeper economic footprint in Johor, 70 business reps visit JS-SEZ
text: Riecken-Daer said Germany already has a strong investment presence in Malaysia, with around 750 German companies operating nationwide.

title: Germany eyes deeper economic footprint in Johor, 70 business reps visit JS-SEZ
text: She said about 90 German companies are currently based in Johor, mainly in advanced manufacturing and industrial sectors.

title: Germany eyes deeper economic footprint in Johor, 70 business reps visit JS-SEZ
text: “We do feel that there is potential for further growth in that respect,” she said.

title: Germany eyes deeper economic footprint in Johor, 70 business reps visit JS-SEZ
text: Riecken-Daer noted that German firms, especially those already operating in Singapore, were showing increasing interest in expanding into Johor through the JS-SEZ initiative.

title: Germany eyes deeper economic footprint in Johor, 70 business reps visit JS-SEZ
text: “We do observe a sense of dynamics here in the special economic zone, which you have designed.

title: Germany eyes deeper economic footprint in Johor, 70 business reps visit JS-SEZ
text: “We see a lot of cross-border interest from our many companies who are based in Singapore and a lot of strategic interest in expanding operations to Johor,” she said.

title: Germany eyes deeper economic footprint in Johor, 70 business reps visit JS-SEZ
text: She described Johor as an attractive investment destination due to its strategic location, maritime access, availability of land and a skilled multilingual workforce.

title: Germany eyes deeper economic footprint in Johor, 70 business reps visit JS-SEZ
text: “I remember standing in the boardroom overlooking the Straits, seeing the vastness of Johor, while also viewing across Singapore and even Indonesia on the horizon.

title: Germany eyes deeper economic footprint in Johor, 70 business reps visit JS-SEZ
text: “So that is very impressive,” she said.

title: Germany eyes deeper economic footprint in Johor, 70 business reps visit JS-SEZ
text: Riecken-Daer said current global uncertainties and supply chain disruptions were prompting German companies to diversify investments and strengthen partnerships in Southeast Asia.

title: Germany eyes deeper economic footprint in Johor, 70 business reps visit JS-SEZ
text: “As a trading nation and a middle-class power, we really have an interest in diversifying our supply chains, forging new partnerships and investment opportunities.

title: Germany eyes deeper economic footprint in Johor, 70 business reps visit JS-SEZ
text: “I think Johor is a very good candidate for that,” she said.

title: Germany eyes deeper economic footprint in Johor, 70 business reps visit JS-SEZ
text: Meanwhile, Lee said most German companies currently operating in Johor are involved in advanced manufacturing, chemical industries and logistics.

title: Germany eyes deeper economic footprint in Johor, 70 business reps visit JS-SEZ
text: He added that Johor was keen to strengthen cooperation with Germany in talent development and technical education.

title: Germany eyes deeper economic footprint in Johor, 70 business reps visit JS-SEZ
text: “We are aware that Germany is very strong in technical and vocational education,” he said.

title: Germany eyes deeper economic footprint in Johor, 70 business reps visit JS-SEZ
text: Lee said Johor already has existing collaboration with German partners through the Johor Skills Development Centre, particularly in the oil and gas sector.

title: Germany eyes deeper economic footprint in Johor, 70 business reps visit JS-SEZ
text: However, he said the state hoped to expand cooperation into other high-value industries.

title: Germany eyes deeper economic footprint in Johor, 70 business reps visit JS-SEZ
text: “Advanced manufacturing, automation, electric vehicles and the automotive industry are areas where Germany is very strong.

title: Germany eyes deeper economic footprint in Johor, 70 business reps visit JS-SEZ
text: “We can continue discussions to establish further cooperation,” he added.

title: Asia stocks turn green as AI cheer trumps Iran, inflation gloom
text: SINGAPORE: Asian stocks found their footing after an initial selloff on Wednesday, helped by a turnaround in Korean shares as revived AI optimism dwarfed concerns about stalled talks between Washington and Tehran and hotter-than-expected U.S. inflation.

title: Asia stocks turn green as AI cheer trumps Iran, inflation gloom
text: MSCI's broadest index of Asia-Pacific shares outside Japan was up 0.3% after rebounding from an earlier decline of as much as 1%.

title: Asia stocks turn green as AI cheer trumps Iran, inflation gloom
text: Japan's Nikkei 225 rose 0.8%, while S&P 500 e-mini futures tacked on 0.2%.

title: Asia stocks turn green as AI cheer trumps Iran, inflation gloom
text: Korean shares fell as much as 3.2% on news Samsung Electronics had failed to reach a pay deal with its union before rebounding 2.6% to a record close on reports the government was seeking to manage the situation.

title: Asia stocks turn green as AI cheer trumps Iran, inflation gloom
text: The Korean market has been on a tear in recent weeks, breaching records regularly on an AI-led rally that some traders say was ripe for a pullback.

title: Asia stocks turn green as AI cheer trumps Iran, inflation gloom
text: "Robust AI-led exports by South Korea - and to a lesser extent, Japan, Singapore and Malaysia - are buffering the energy price shock," analysts from Nomura wrote in a research report.

title: Asia stocks turn green as AI cheer trumps Iran, inflation gloom
text: The conflict in the Middle East remained in a stalemate, as U.S. President Donald Trump said on Tuesday he does not think he will need China's help to end the war with Iran, ahead of his meeting with President Xi Jinping later this week.

text: It noted improved earnings, growing supply chain dominance and a stronger yuan.

title: Asia stocks turn green as AI cheer trumps Iran, inflation gloom

title: Asia stocks turn green as AI cheer trumps Iran, inflation gloom
text: "AI and capex related to AI will remain pertinent across asset classes and regions."

text: Other investors expressed scepticism that Trump and Xi's meeting would lead to significantly improved relations.

title: Asia stocks turn green as AI cheer trumps Iran, inflation gloom
text: "That creates a pretty low bar for success: As long as Trump and Xi can get along and the trade detente continues, that should be enough to count this meeting as a win for both sides."

text: In Seoul, Samsung Electronics shares plunged as much as 6.1% after the electronics behemoth failed to reach a pay deal with its South Korean labour union on Wednesday, setting the stage for more than 50,000 workers to go ahead with a full strike.

title: Looming Samsung Electronics walkout raises economic, political stakes in South Korea
text: Looming Samsung Electronics walkout raises economic, political stakes in South Korea

title: Looming Samsung Electronics walkout raises economic, political stakes in South Korea
text: SEOUL: Samsung Electronics is edging closer to a possible full-scale strike, as its largest union presses ahead with an 18-day walkout plan that could disrupt production at the heart of Korea’s chip industry.

title: Looming Samsung Electronics walkout raises economic, political stakes in South Korea
text: The risk became more immediate after last-ditch talks mediated by the National Labour Relations Commission ended without a breakthrough early Wednesday (May 13).

title: Looming Samsung Electronics walkout raises economic, political stakes in South Korea
text: The union has fixed both the timing and duration of the strike, leaving little room for a quick compromise unless either side makes a major concession.

title: Looming Samsung Electronics walkout raises economic, political stakes in South Korea
text: At issue is the union’s demand to institutionalize a performance bonus equivalent to 15 per cent of operating profit and remove the current bonus cap.

title: Looming Samsung Electronics walkout raises economic, political stakes in South Korea
text: The dispute has drawn attention at home and abroad because of Samsung’s weight as the world’s largest memory chipmaker.

title: Looming Samsung Electronics walkout raises economic, political stakes in South Korea
text: The company sits at the centre of Korea’s export engine and the global AI chip supply chain.

title: Looming Samsung Electronics walkout raises economic, political stakes in South Korea
text: Any prolonged walkout could have consequences for production, exports, investor sentiment and the company’s supplier network.

title: Looming Samsung Electronics walkout raises economic, political stakes in South Korea
text: JPMorgan also warned in a recent report that a prolonged strike could weigh on Samsung’s annual operating profit.

title: Looming Samsung Electronics walkout raises economic, political stakes in South Korea
text: Semiconductor fabs run around the clock, and even limited disruptions can affect production schedules.

title: Looming Samsung Electronics walkout raises economic, political stakes in South Korea
text: Uncertainty over high-bandwidth memory and other AI-related chips could also weigh on delivery timelines and customer confidence.

title: Looming Samsung Electronics walkout raises economic, political stakes in South Korea
text: Song Heon-jae, an economics professor at the University of Seoul, said a shutdown of production lines could cost “tens of billions of won, or tens of millions of dollars, per minute,” and as much as one trillion won (US$670 million) per day.

title: Looming Samsung Electronics walkout raises economic, political stakes in South Korea
text: Industry watchers have estimated direct damage from a full-scale strike at 20 trillion won to 30 trillion won.

title: Looming Samsung Electronics walkout raises economic, political stakes in South Korea
text: That has brought emergency arbitration, one of the government’s strongest labour dispute tools, into focus.

title: Looming Samsung Electronics walkout raises economic, political stakes in South Korea
text: Under Korea’s labour law, the Labour Minister can invoke emergency arbitration when a strike is deemed likely to endanger public life or seriously harm the national economy.

title: Looming Samsung Electronics walkout raises economic, political stakes in South Korea
text: Once invoked, the union must stop industrial action and is barred from striking for up to 30 days while labour authorities proceed with mediation or arbitration.

title: Looming Samsung Electronics walkout raises economic, political stakes in South Korea
text: The measure has been used only four times in Korea: during a 1969 strike at The Korea Shipbuilding Corp., now HJ Shipbuilding and Construction; a 1993 strike at Hyundai Motor; and strikes by the pilots’ unions of Asiana Airlines and Korean Air in 2005.

title: Looming Samsung Electronics walkout raises economic, political stakes in South Korea
text: For the government, the choice is politically and legally delicate.

title: Looming Samsung Electronics walkout raises economic, political stakes in South Korea
text: A Samsung strike could carry national economic risks, but emergency arbitration is an exceptional tool that limits workers’ right to collective action.

title: Looming Samsung Electronics walkout raises economic, political stakes in South Korea
text: Deputy Prime Minister and Finance Minister Koo Yun-cheol wrote on X that “a strike must not happen under any circumstances,” while Labour Minister Kim Young-hoon said the government would do its utmost to arrange further talks before the dispute leads to a strike.

title: Looming Samsung Electronics walkout raises economic, political stakes in South Korea
text: Legal and labour experts offered sharply different views on whether the government should invoke emergency arbitration.

title: Looming Samsung Electronics walkout raises economic, political stakes in South Korea
text: Park Ji-soon, a professor at Korea University School of Law, said a fixed bonus tied to operating profit is “highly unusual,” adding, “A general strike at Samsung Electronics would have an enormous impact on the national economy.

title: Looming Samsung Electronics walkout raises economic, political stakes in South Korea
text:
The government needs to consider invoking emergency arbitration as a last line of defence.”

Jung Heung-jun, a professor at Seoul National University of Science and Technology, disagreed, saying emergency arbitration should only be used in exceptional cases.

title: Looming Samsung Electronics walkout raises economic, political stakes in South Korea
text: “It is difficult to regard the Samsung union’s strike as illegal at this point, so applying emergency arbitration would not be appropriate,” Jung said.

title: Looming Samsung Electronics walkout raises economic, political stakes in South Korea
text: A court ruling due before the planned walkout could also shape the union’s room for action.

title: Looming Samsung Electronics walkout raises economic, political stakes in South Korea
text: The Suwon District Court is reviewing Samsung’s request for an injunction against what the company calls unlawful industrial action.

title: Looming Samsung Electronics walkout raises economic, political stakes in South Korea
text: If the court accepts part of the request, the union could face limits on how it conducts the strike or holds rallies at worksites.

title: Looming Samsung Electronics walkout raises economic, political stakes in South Korea
text: If the request is rejected, the union could gain further momentum before May 21.

title: Looming Samsung Electronics walkout raises economic, political stakes in South Korea
text: The court could issue its decision as early as Thursday, and no later than May 20, a day before the planned walkout.

title: Looming Samsung Electronics walkout raises economic, political stakes in South Korea
text:
Samsung union chief Choi Seung-ho said, “Even if part of the injunction is accepted, there will be no problem with the strike itself.”

He also said emergency arbitration would “only delay the strike a little” and give the union “more time to prepare.” - The Korea Herald/ANN

title: Thai economy faces heightened risks, needs coordinated policy mix, central bank minutes show
text: Thai economy faces heightened risks, needs coordinated policy mix, central bank minutes show

title: Thai economy faces heightened risks, needs coordinated policy mix, central bank minutes show
text: BANGKOK: Thailand's economic growth outlook is facing heightening risks from the impacts of the Middle East war, requiring a coordinated policy mix, the minutes of the central bank's April 29 policy meeting showed on Wednesday (May 13).

title: Thai economy faces heightened risks, needs coordinated policy mix, central bank minutes show
text: At the meeting, the monetary policy committee unanimously voted to keep the one-day repurchase rate unchanged at one per cent as it assessed the impact of higher oil prices driven by the conflict in the Middle East.

title: Thai economy faces heightened risks, needs coordinated policy mix, central bank minutes show

title: Slow rise in staple food prices
text: PETALING JAYA: While food prices in Malaysia remain largely under control, consumers are beginning to feel the pinch as prices of some common items such as fish and vegetables are slowly rising, according to the Economy Ministry.

title: Slow rise in staple food prices
text: During a briefing on Malaysia’s economic supply challenges yesterday, Minister Akmal Nasrullah Mohd Nasir (pic) said between May 4 and 6, selected food prices either saw a 2% drop, or a 6.7% rise compared with the previous week.

title: Slow rise in staple food prices
text: “Beef prices fell by 1.4%, from RM36.40 (per kg) to RM35.88, while standard chicken prices went from RM9.50 to RM9.51,” he said during a live session on social media.

title: Slow rise in staple food prices
text: However, a few commonly consumed food items have recorded increases.

title: Slow rise in staple food prices
text: Kembong prices rose by 3.9% from RM16.76 to RM17.42, while white prawns prices increased by 1.2% to RM32.11 per kg.

title: Slow rise in staple food prices
text: Sawi prices also went up by 4% to RM7.23 per kg, while fresh coconut milk prices increased by 6.7% to RM16.88 per kg.

title: Slow rise in staple food prices
text: “Although statistics show food price movements remain under control, the Prime Minister and the ministry have expressed concern that some groups, especially those in rural areas, may feel some pressure.

title: How thoughtful taxation can support the economy
text: How thoughtful taxation can support the economy

title: How thoughtful taxation can support the economy
text: THE evolving geopolitical landscape and supply chain disruptions are causing economic turbulence, posing significant challenges for countries worldwide, and Malaysia is no exception.

title: How thoughtful taxation can support the economy
text: As an emerging economy, Malaysia has faced a range of economic shocks over the years, including the 1997 Asian Financial Crisis, the 2008 Global Financial Crisis and more recently, the Covid-19 pandemic.

title: How thoughtful taxation can support the economy
text: Such turbulent periods require carefully drawn up fiscal plans, with taxation policies playing a crucial role in stabilising the economy, protecting vulnerable groups and promoting sustainable recovery.

title: How thoughtful taxation can support the economy
text: Malaysia’s economy is diverse, with significant contributions from the manufacturing, services and commodities sectors.

title: How thoughtful taxation can support the economy
text: However, it remains vulnerable to external shocks, such as fluctuations in global commodity prices, trade disruptions and a global economic slowdown.

title: How thoughtful taxation can support the economy
text: The Covid-19 pandemic, for instance, severely impacted Malaysia’s economic growth, employment and government revenue, necessitating swift fiscal responses.

title: How thoughtful taxation can support the economy
text:
In the local context, taxation policy during economic turbulence must aim to:

> Stabilise economic activity: Support consumption and investment to mitigate the risk of recession.

title: How thoughtful taxation can support the economy
text: > Protect low-income and vulnerable groups: Provide relief to households and small businesses most affected by economic shocks.

title: How thoughtful taxation can support the economy
text: > Encourage business continuity and growth: Incentivise investments, new technologies and innovation, to drive recovery.

title: How thoughtful taxation can support the economy
text: > Maintain fiscal sustainability: Government revenues must be sufficient to fund essential services and stimulus measures without excessive borrowing.

title: How thoughtful taxation can support the economy
text:
Principles guiding Malaysia’s taxation design

A good taxation system should be guided by principles of equity, efficiency and simplicity.

title: How thoughtful taxation can support the economy
text: During turbulent times, these principles are adapted to enhance flexibility and responsiveness.

title: How thoughtful taxation can support the economy
text: The government may emphasise progressive taxation, targeted support and minimising economic distortions, to balance short-term relief with long-term growth.

title: How thoughtful taxation can support the economy
text:
Key taxation strategies in Malaysia during economic uncertainties include:

Temporary tax relief and deferrals

Malaysia has implemented various temporary tax relief measures during economic downturns.

title: How thoughtful taxation can support the economy
text:
For example, during the Covid-19 pandemic, the government introduced:

> Targeted Import Duty Sales Tax and Service Tax (SST) exemptions.

title: How thoughtful taxation can support the economy
text: > Tax payment deferrals for companies and individuals to ease cash-flow constraints.

title: How thoughtful taxation can support the economy
text: > Special tax deductions for businesses investing in digitalisation and automation to adapt to new economic realities.

title: How thoughtful taxation can support the economy
text: These measures helped sustain consumption and business operations during periods of restricted movement and economic activity.

title: How thoughtful taxation can support the economy
text:
Progressive taxation and targeted support

The government has increasingly enhanced support for lower-income groups through tax reliefs and rebates.

title: How thoughtful taxation can support the economy
text: For instance, additional tax reliefs for medical expenses and education were introduced to reduce the financial burden on households.

title: How thoughtful taxation can support the economy
text: Moreover, targeted cash transfers and subsidies complement tax policies to protect vulnerable groups for a more inclusive economic recovery.

title: How thoughtful taxation can support the economy
text:
Investment incentives and tax credits

To stimulate economic recovery, the country may offer various tax incentives, particularly for sectors critical to growth and innovation.

title: How thoughtful taxation can support the economy
text:
These include:

> Reduced tax rates or Investment Tax Allowance for manufacturing and high-tech industries.

title: How thoughtful taxation can support the economy
text: > Tax incentives for green technology and renewable energy projects, aligning economic recovery with sustainability goals.

title: How thoughtful taxation can support the economy
text: > Enhanced tax deduction for research and development activities to encourage innovation.

title: How thoughtful taxation can support the economy
text: Such incentives encourage businesses to invest despite economic uncertainties, supporting job creation and productivity.

title: How thoughtful taxation can support the economy
text:
Support for SMEs

Small and medium enterprises (SMEs) are vital to Malaysia’s economy but are often the hardest hit during crises.

title: How thoughtful taxation can support the economy
text:
The government has implemented tax measures such as:

> Reduced corporate tax rates for SMEs.

title: How thoughtful taxation can support the economy
text: > Simplified tax filing procedures.

title: How thoughtful taxation can support the economy
text: > Special grants and tax incentives for digital adoption and business transformation.

title: How thoughtful taxation can support the economy
text: These measures help SMEs maintain operations and adapt to changing market conditions.

title: How thoughtful taxation can support the economy
text:
Environmental and socially responsible taxation

Malaysia is increasingly integrating environmental considerations into its tax policy.

title: How thoughtful taxation can support the economy
text: The introduction of green taxes and sales tax on plastic resins (introduced from July 1 2025), balanced with incentives for environmentally friendly activities support sustainable development.

title: How thoughtful taxation can support the economy
text: Revenue from these taxes should be used to fund environmental initiatives.

title: How thoughtful taxation can support the economy
text:
Challenges in taxation design during uncertain times

Despite these strategies, Malaysia faces several challenges:

> Revenue shortfalls: Economic slowdowns reduce tax collections, limiting fiscal space for stimulus.

title: How thoughtful taxation can support the economy
text: > Administrative capacity: Efficient implementation of tax relief and incentives requires robust tax administration, which can be stretched during crises.

title: How thoughtful taxation can support the economy
text: > Tax base narrowness: Malaysia’s reliance on indirect taxes like SST means revenue is sensitive to consumption fluctuations.

title: How thoughtful taxation can support the economy
text: > Balancing act: The government must balance providing relief with maintaining fiscal discipline to avoid excessive debt.

title: How thoughtful taxation can support the economy
text: The recent economic crisis faced by Malaysia and globally highlights the importance of flexibility and speed in tax policy responses.

title: How thoughtful taxation can support the economy
text: Malaysia’s swift introduction of tax deferrals, exemptions and incentives has helped cushion the economic blow in the past.

title: How thoughtful taxation can support the economy
text:
However, difficult times also underscore the need for:

> Strengthening tax administration and digital infrastructure.

title: How thoughtful taxation can support the economy
text: > Broadening the tax base to enhance revenue resilience.

title: How thoughtful taxation can support the economy
text: > Enhancing coordination between tax policy and social programmes.

title: How thoughtful taxation can support the economy
text: In conclusion, taxation design is a critical lever for Malaysia to support its economy during periods of uncertainty and into recovery.

title: How thoughtful taxation can support the economy
text: By implementing flexible, progressive and targeted tax policies, such as temporary relief and investment incentives, Malaysia can stabilise economic activity, protect vulnerable groups and foster sustainable recovery.

title: How thoughtful taxation can support the economy
text: While challenges remain, thoughtful and adaptive tax policies are essential tools for building resilient economies capable of withstanding future uncertainties.

title: How thoughtful taxation can support the economy
text: Farah Rosley is Malaysia tax managing partner, Ernst & Young Tax Consultants Sdn Bhd.

title: How thoughtful taxation can support the economy
text: The views expressed here are the writer’s own.

title: Malaysia’s unemployment rate holds steady at 2.9% in March 2026
text: PUTRAJAYA: Malaysia's labour market remained stable in March 2026, with the unemployment rate unchanged at 2.9%, supported by continued expansion in employment and labour force participation, according to the Statistics Malaysia Department.

title: Malaysia’s unemployment rate holds steady at 2.9% in March 2026
text: Chief Statistician Datuk Seri Dr Mohd Uzir Mahidin said the positive economic outlook during the month enabled the country’s labour market to sustain its steady growth momentum.

title: Malaysia’s unemployment rate holds steady at 2.9% in March 2026
text: "The labour force increased by 0.1% to 17.31 million persons in March 2026 from 17.30 million in February, while the labour force participation rate (LFPR) remained at 70.9%,” he said in a statement on Tuesday (May 12).

title: Malaysia’s unemployment rate holds steady at 2.9% in March 2026
text: Mohd Uzir said the number of employed persons also edged up by 0.1 per cent to 16.80 million persons compared with 16.79 million recorded in the previous month.

title: Malaysia’s unemployment rate holds steady at 2.9% in March 2026
text: The department's Statistics of the Labour Force, Malaysia, March and First Quarter 2026 also show that the number of unemployed persons rose slightly by 0.4% to 509,000 persons from 506,800 in February, leaving the unemployment rate unchanged at 2.9%.

title: Malaysia’s unemployment rate holds steady at 2.9% in March 2026
text: The chief statistician said employees continued to make up the largest share of total employment at 75%, increasing marginally to 12.60 million persons in March from 12.59 million previously.

title: Malaysia’s unemployment rate holds steady at 2.9% in March 2026
text: "On the same note, the number of own-account workers recorded a 0.3% rise to 3.14 million persons compared with 3.13 million in February," he added.

title: Malaysia’s unemployment rate holds steady at 2.9% in March 2026
text: Mohd Uzir said the services sector remained the main contributor to employment growth, particularly in accommodation and food and beverage services, information and communication activities, as well as transportation and storage activities.

title: Malaysia’s unemployment rate holds steady at 2.9% in March 2026
text: "The manufacturing, construction and agriculture sectors also recorded increases in employment, while the mining and quarrying sector saw a slight decline," he added.

title: Malaysia’s unemployment rate holds steady at 2.9% in March 2026
text: On unemployment, Mohd Uzir said actively unemployed persons, defined as those available for work and actively seeking jobs, accounted for 79.7% of total unemployed persons.

title: Malaysia’s unemployment rate holds steady at 2.9% in March 2026
text: This category increased by 0.3% to 405,800 persons in March from 404,700 in February.

title: Malaysia’s unemployment rate holds steady at 2.9% in March 2026
text: At the state level, Mohd Uzir said the Federal Territory of Putrajaya recorded the lowest unemployment rate at 1.3%, followed by Pahang at 1.9%, while Selangor and Melaka each posted 2%.

title: Malaysia’s unemployment rate holds steady at 2.9% in March 2026
text: He said Putrajaya also registered the highest LFPR at 79.2%, followed by Selangor at 78% and the Federal Territory of Kuala Lumpur at 76%.

title: Malaysia’s unemployment rate holds steady at 2.9% in March 2026

title: Gold seen bullish, to hit US$5,600 by year-end - UBS
text: Gold seen bullish, to hit US$5,600 by year-end - UBS

title: Gold seen bullish, to hit US$5,600 by year-end - UBS
text: KUALA LUMPUR: Gold prices are expected to trend higher, with a target of US$5,600 per troy ounce by the end of 2026 (US$1=RM3.92), amid prolonged geopolitical conflict in West Asia, persistent macroeconomic uncertainty and continued strong central bank demand.

title: Gold seen bullish, to hit US$5,600 by year-end - UBS
text: UBS Investment Bank precious metals strategist Joni Teves said the underlying drivers supporting gold prices remained intact despite recent market consolidation, with diversification demand from institutional, retail and official sector investors continuing to underpin the precious metal’s momentum.

title: Gold seen bullish, to hit US$5,600 by year-end - UBS
text: "We have maintained our bullish outlook on gold, as we still think that prices can recover from current levels and continue to make new highs this year,” Teves said during a virtual media call today.

title: Gold seen bullish, to hit US$5,600 by year-end - UBS
text: She said the higher oil prices due to the West Asia conflict were creating growing upside risks to medium- and long-term gold price forecasts.

title: Gold seen bullish, to hit US$5,600 by year-end - UBS
text: Besides, a weaker global growth environment combined with a more dovish United States Federal Reserve could prolong gold’s bull cycle over the next few years.

title: Gold seen bullish, to hit US$5,600 by year-end - UBS
text: Teves said the market would be watching for key factors on how the inflation-growth mix evolves, particularly in the second half of the year, as investors assess the economic impact of elevated energy prices.

title: Gold seen bullish, to hit US$5,600 by year-end - UBS
text: She further said that if the outlook increasingly compressed real interest rates, driven by rising inflation, while nominal yields remained flat, this would provide a supportive backdrop for gold prices.

title: Gold seen bullish, to hit US$5,600 by year-end - UBS
text: Therefore, she said the current period of consolidation in gold prices could present investors with an opportunity to build positions as a hedge against weaker real rates.

title: Gold seen bullish, to hit US$5,600 by year-end - UBS
text: On central bank demand, Teves opined that official institutions might not reduce gold holdings following recent market volatility, noting the broader trend of reserve diversification into gold was likely to continue.

title: Gold seen bullish, to hit US$5,600 by year-end - UBS
text: Meanwhile, she said physical demand for gold also remained supportive, particularly in Asia, where investment demand had offset weaker jewellery purchases amid record-high prices.

title: Gold seen bullish, to hit US$5,600 by year-end - UBS
text: Teves said sentiment in China remained positive, with strong demand recorded especially in the first quarter of the year.

title: Gold seen bullish, to hit US$5,600 by year-end - UBS
text: On silver, she said UBS also expected a positive outlook, with a target reaching above US$100 per troy ounce by year-end, tracking the trends in the gold market.

title: Gold seen bullish, to hit US$5,600 by year-end - UBS
text: "We expect interest in silver to be more tactical, in contrast to more strategic positions in gold.

title: Gold seen bullish, to hit US$5,600 by year-end - UBS
text: "Robust physical demand from China, where restocking and physical investment flows have room to continue, should also help support global investor sentiment,” she said.

title: Gold seen bullish, to hit US$5,600 by year-end - UBS
text: However, she added that silver could underperform gold if slowing economic growth weakened industrial demand.

title: Gold seen bullish, to hit US$5,600 by year-end - UBS
text: - Bernama

title: Penang open to hosting international music festivals, says exco chairman
text: Penang open to hosting international music festivals, says exco chairman

title: Penang open to hosting international music festivals, says exco chairman
text: GEORGE TOWN: Penang is open to hosting international-level music festivals if the budget, location and sponsors permit, says state Tourism and Creative Economy committee chairman Wong Hon Wai.

title: Penang open to hosting international music festivals, says exco chairman
text: Wong said the state government viewed music festivals as a tourism product that could attract domestic and international visitors.

title: Penang open to hosting international music festivals, says exco chairman
text: “We have experience organising music festival-style events.

title: Penang open to hosting international music festivals, says exco chairman
text: “In 2024, when we moved the Penang Bridge International Marathon to Batu Kawan, we held a music festival before the run took place.

title: Penang open to hosting international music festivals, says exco chairman
text: “The festival was held before the marathon and was mostly attended by the participants,” he said during the state assembly sitting in Lebuh Light on Tuesday (May 12).

title: Penang open to hosting international music festivals, says exco chairman
text: Wong was responding to an additional question by Joseph Ng (PH-Air Itam) on whether Penang was ready to host festivals like the recent Rain Rave 2026 Water Music Festival event in Bukit Bintang, Kuala Lumpur.

title: Penang open to hosting international music festivals, says exco chairman
text: Wong said many might not be aware a rave-style music festival had been held in conjunction with the marathon in 2024.

title: Penang open to hosting international music festivals, says exco chairman
text: “It was held at night before the run so the runners could enjoy the music festival experience before the running event,” he said.

title: Penang open to hosting international music festivals, says exco chairman
text: Wong said the state government would consider organising more festivals in the future based on the suitability of the location, financial capacity and the organisers’ commitment.

title: Penang open to hosting international music festivals, says exco chairman
text: Earlier, responding to Ng’s written question on new tourism products and attractions and tourism promotion initiatives introduced by the state government and private sector in conjunction with Visit Malaysia Year 2026, Wong said the state government, through Penang Global Tourism, had formulated various strategic initiatives to strengthen the state’s tourism sector.

title: Penang open to hosting international music festivals, says exco chairman
text: Wong said there were 14 different attractions, including museums, sights and locations, to attract tourists.

title: Penang open to hosting international music festivals, says exco chairman
text: “We not only want to introduce new products, but we want to give our existing ones more dimension as well.

title: Penang open to hosting international music festivals, says exco chairman
text: “Taking Penang Hill as an example, people can not only take the funicular train but also opt to hike up.

title: Penang open to hosting international music festivals, says exco chairman
text: “They can camp overnight and there is even a night walk.

title: Penang open to hosting international music festivals, says exco chairman
text: “Many dimensions can be added to existing experiences,” he said.

title: Penang open to hosting international music festivals, says exco chairman
text: Wong said tourists could also experience Penang by sea, including taking a boat ride to see both bridges up close.

title: Penang open to hosting international music festivals, says exco chairman
text: “It may be the same sea and the same hill, but there are different ways of seeing it, which will encourage people to return multiple times,” he said.

title: Governor: Stability drives S’wak growth
text: Governor: Stability drives S’wak growth

title: Governor: Stability drives S’wak growth
text: KUCHING: Sarawak’s harmony and political stability are strengths which must be upheld for economic development and prosperity, says Tun Dr Wan Junaidi Tuanku Jaafar.

title: Governor: Stability drives S’wak growth
text: The Yang di-Pertua Negeri emphasised that it is the collective responsibility of all to uphold a peaceful and secure environment, as such actions will instil confidence in investors.

title: Governor: Stability drives S’wak growth
text: “This is important because investors and global economic players prefer to invest in countries with political stability, good administration and a society that lives in peace and harmony,” he said when opening the first meeting of the fifth term of the 19th Sarawak Legislative Assembly here yesterday.

title: Governor: Stability drives S’wak growth
text: Wan Junaidi also urged Sarawakians to preserve their unity, mutual respect and harmony amid racial, religious and cultural diversity.

title: Governor: Stability drives S’wak growth
text: “With forward-looking leadership, Sarawak can continue to develop into a modern, pros­perous and high-income state,” he added.

title: Governor: Stability drives S’wak growth
text: He also called on the assemblymen to uphold the sanctity and dignity of the Sarawak Legislative Assembly through mature and respectful debates and courteous speech.

title: Governor: Stability drives S’wak growth
text: “As leaders, we must set a good example for society, especially the younger generation,” he said.

title: Governor: Stability drives S’wak growth
text: Wan Junaidi added that elected representatives also served as a link between the government and the people.

title: Governor: Stability drives S’wak growth
text: “Assemblymen must remain close to the people and understand their needs and aspirations to provide accurate feedback to the government so that policies and development plans will benefit the rakyat,” he said.

title: Governor: Stability drives S’wak growth
text: Four Bills will be tabled during the current meeting, including the Sarawak Economic Develop­ment Corporation (Amendment) Bill, the Sarawak Padi and Rice Board Bill and two supplementary supply Bills.

title: Governor: Stability drives S’wak growth
text: The meeting will conclude on May 20.

title: A voice for women, family and youth
text: A voice for women, family and youth

title: A voice for women, family and youth
text:
Wong pledges support for everyday Malaysians

KUALA LUMPUR: Newly appointed senator Datuk Wong You Fong has pledged to champion issues affecting everyday Malaysians, acting as a voice for women, families and young people facing social and economic pressures.

title: A voice for women, family and youth
text: Wong, who was sworn in at Dewan Negara yesterday, said she also hoped to contribute constructively to policies that strengthen national unity, social stability and sustainable economic growth.

title: A voice for women, family and youth
text: The MCA Wanita chief and se­nior lawyer described her appointment as a great honour and an even greater responsibility.

title: A voice for women, family and youth
text: Women’s participation in decision-­making, she said, was vital for Malaysia’s future.

title: A voice for women, family and youth
text: “Women bring different perspectives, experiences and priorities into policymaking – especially in areas such as education, family well-being, healthcare, women’s safety, youth development, mental health and community welfare.

title: A voice for women, family and youth
text: “A balanced and progressive nation requires both male and female perspectives in decision-­making.

title: A voice for women, family and youth
text: Giving women meaningful opportunities at the highest levels of leadership is not about symbolism, but about strengthening the quality, inclusiveness and compassion of national policies,” she said.

title: A voice for women, family and youth
text: While acknowledging that progress had been made, Wong noted that women were still under-­represented in leadership compared with many developed countries and called for more opportunities, mentoring and exposure to nurture the next generation of women leaders.

title: A voice for women, family and youth
text: “I would like to encourage all women in Malaysia to believe in their abilities and never underestimate their potential.

title: A voice for women, family and youth
text: Do not be afraid to participate, to voice your views and to take on leadership roles,” she said.

title: A voice for women, family and youth
text: “I humbly express my deepest gratitude to His Majesty Sultan Ibrahim, King of Malaysia, for consenting to my appointment as a member of the Dewan Negara.

title: A voice for women, family and youth
text:
“My thanks also go to the Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim, Deputy Prime Minister and Barisan Nasional chairman Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi and MCA president Datuk Seri Dr Wee Ka Siong.”

Wong is the only woman among the 13 senators sworn in yesterday.

title: A voice for women, family and youth
text: She has held key posts at the central, state, division and branch levels in MCA and has led Wanita MCA nationally since December 2022, while also heading Johor Wanita MCA and the Pulai division.

title: A voice for women, family and youth
text: She is also a non-independent non-executive director of Star Media Group Bhd.

title: A voice for women, family and youth
text: Congratulating Wong, Dr Wee said he was confident she would discharge her duties effectively and serve the country and champion women’s empowerment better at a higher level.

title: A voice for women, family and youth
text: “With her distinguished academic background, as well as extensive experience in the legal profession and community service, she will continue to contri­bute meaningfully to the nation and champion the advancement and empowerment of women,” he said on Facebook yesterday.

title: A voice for women, family and youth
text: Wanita MCA secretary-general Chan Quin Er said Wong’s elevation to the Dewan Negara was both a recognition and a call to service.

title: A voice for women, family and youth
text: “At a time when women remain under-represented in Malaysia’s political leadership, this moment carries deeper meaning for all women striving for a stronger voice at the nation’s decision-­making table,” she said, adding that Wanita MCA believed capable and principled women must continue to be entrusted with leadership roles for the country’s benefit.

title: A voice for women, family and youth
text: Apart from Wong, the other new members of the Senate are acting Bentong Umno chief Datuk Seri Mohamad Zaini Salleh, Sabah DAP chairman Datuk Phoong Jin Zhe, Johor DAP vice-chairman Sheikh Umar Bagharib Ali, Sarawak DAP assistant secretary Larry Asap and R. Thiagarajah.

title: A voice for women, family and youth
text: Six senators were reappointed – Tan Sri Low Kian Chuan, Prof Datuk Seri Dr Awang Sariyan, Datuk Dr Mohd Hatta Md Ramli, Isaiah D. Jacob, Mohd Hasbie Muda and Abun Sui Anyit – while Abdul Nasir Idris was re-elected by the Kedah State Legislative Assembly.

title: Cutting fuel subsidies will cripple Sabah, Sarawak economic activities, says SAPP
text: Cutting fuel subsidies will cripple Sabah, Sarawak economic activities, says SAPP

title: Cutting fuel subsidies will cripple Sabah, Sarawak economic activities, says SAPP
text: KOTA KINABALU: Sabah Progressive Party (SAPP) urged Putrajaya to stop any further fuel subsidy rationalisation in east Malaysia, cautioning that any reduction would directly cripple economic activities across the region.

title: Cutting fuel subsidies will cripple Sabah, Sarawak economic activities, says SAPP
text: SAPP vice-president Gee Tien Siong said that subsidised RON95 at RM1.99 per litre and diesel at RM2.15 per litre remain lifelines for families who must travel vast distances without alternatives such as railways.

title: Cutting fuel subsidies will cripple Sabah, Sarawak economic activities, says SAPP
text: He said the upcoming T20 targeted subsidy rationalisation under the Budi95 programme was unacceptable, given that Sabah and Sarawak are the nation’s primary oil producers.

title: Cutting fuel subsidies will cripple Sabah, Sarawak economic activities, says SAPP
text: Gee warned that any fuel subsidy cut, whether through quota reductions or income‑based targeting, will trigger a direct drop in economic activity, as already evident in Sabah today.

title: Cutting fuel subsidies will cripple Sabah, Sarawak economic activities, says SAPP
text: He pointed out that the existing monthly 200‑litre cap already burdens families, as just three round-trip trips between Kota Kinabalu and Tawau — each 450km one way — exhaust the quota before the fourth week.

title: Cutting fuel subsidies will cripple Sabah, Sarawak economic activities, says SAPP
text: Similar depletion occurs on the over 300km routes between Kota Kinabalu–Sandakan or Sandakan–Tawau, he said in a statement on Monday (May 11).

title: Cutting fuel subsidies will cripple Sabah, Sarawak economic activities, says SAPP
text: “High fuel consumption in Sabah is not about preference for large vehicles, but the immense distances residents are forced to cover for daily needs,” Gee said.

title: Cutting fuel subsidies will cripple Sabah, Sarawak economic activities, says SAPP
text: He added that once the quota is exhausted, families are reluctant to travel further as unsubsidised fuel costs nearly double, leaving essential economic activities stranded for about 25 per cent of every month.

title: Cutting fuel subsidies will cripple Sabah, Sarawak economic activities, says SAPP
text: He urged Putrajaya to immediately scrap the 200‑litre limit, abandon the T20 cut plan, and guarantee that RM1.99 RON95 fuel remains permanent and unrestricted for all residents in Sabah and Sarawak.

title: Cutting fuel subsidies will cripple Sabah, Sarawak economic activities, says SAPP
text: While acknowledging the federal government’s fiscal squeeze, Gee said that Sabah’s constitutionally guaranteed 40% revenue entitlement remains unpaid, even during periods of national windfalls.

title: Gold falls on oil-driven inflation worries as US-Iran peace talks falter
text: Gold falls on oil-driven inflation worries as US-Iran peace talks falter

title: Gold falls on oil-driven inflation worries as US-Iran peace talks falter
text: Gold prices fell on Monday, as a lack of progress in U.S.-Iran peace negotiations pushed oil prices higher, fuelling concerns that elevated inflation could keep interest rates higher for longer.

title: Gold falls on oil-driven inflation worries as US-Iran peace talks falter
text: Spot gold fell 1.2% at $4,657.89 per ounce, as of 0607 GMT.

title: Gold falls on oil-driven inflation worries as US-Iran peace talks falter
text: U.S. gold futures for June delivery lost 1.4% at $4,665.70.

title: Gold falls on oil-driven inflation worries as US-Iran peace talks falter
text: The dollar rose, making greenback-priced bullion more expensive for holders of other currencies.

title: Gold falls on oil-driven inflation worries as US-Iran peace talks falter
text: U.S. President Donald Trump on Sunday rejected Iran's response to a U.S. proposal for peace talks, dashing hopes for an imminent end to the 10-week-old conflict that has caused widespread damage in Iran and Lebanon, paralyzed maritime traffic in the Strait of Hormuz, and driven up global energy prices.

title: Gold falls on oil-driven inflation worries as US-Iran peace talks falter
text: "We're essentially seeing an unwinding of hopes for an imminent (peace) deal, and gold is feeling the pinch from the renewed rise in crude prices," said Tim Waterer, chief market analyst at KCM Trade.

title: Gold falls on oil-driven inflation worries as US-Iran peace talks falter
text: Oil prices jumped as the Strait of Hormuz remained largely closed, keeping global energy supplies tight.

title: Gold falls on oil-driven inflation worries as US-Iran peace talks falter
text: Rising crude oil prices risk pushing inflation higher, increasing the prospects of elevated interest rates.

title: Gold falls on oil-driven inflation worries as US-Iran peace talks falter
text: While gold is traditionally seen as a hedge against inflation, high interest rates tend to weigh on the non-yielding asset.

title: Gold falls on oil-driven inflation worries as US-Iran peace talks falter
text: The ongoing war with Iran and its shock to oil prices and supplies have rocketed to the top of the list of concerns for financial stability, according to a semi-annual Federal Reserve report released on Friday.

title: Gold falls on oil-driven inflation worries as US-Iran peace talks falter
text: Goldman Sachs pushed back its forecast of Fed rate cuts to December 2026 and March 2027 from its previous outlook of cuts in September and December this year, saying high energy prices would likely keep inflation elevated.

title: Gold falls on oil-driven inflation worries as US-Iran peace talks falter
text: Investors are now looking out for April's U.S. Consumer Price Index data, due later this week, for further clues on the Fed's monetary policy direction.

title: Gold falls on oil-driven inflation worries as US-Iran peace talks falter
text: Meanwhile, China's gold production fell in the first quarter of 2026 from a year earlier, the China Gold Association said on Saturday, as safety inspections led some smelters to suspend production for maintenance.

title: Gold falls on oil-driven inflation worries as US-Iran peace talks falter
text: "In the near-to-medium term, the $4,400 to $4,800 range still looks firmly in play while we remain in this ceasefire-without-a-peace-deal stalemate," Waterer added.

title: Gold falls on oil-driven inflation worries as US-Iran peace talks falter
text: Spot silver fell 0.2% at $80.13 per ounce, platinum slid 1.2% to $2,029.95, and palladium was down 0.7% at $1,481.09.

title: Gold falls on oil-driven inflation worries as US-Iran peace talks falter
text: - Reuters

text: KUCHING: Sarawak's emergence as a regional pioneer of renewable energy will open up new economic and investment opportunities for the state, says Tun Dr Wan Junaidi Tuanku Jaafar.

text: The Sarawak Governor said the state was expanding the use of renewable energy, including hydro, solar and biomass, to ensure energy security and sustainability.

title: Philippine Vice President Duterte facing second impeachment vote
text: Philippine Vice President Duterte facing second impeachment vote

title: Philippine Vice President Duterte facing second impeachment vote
text: MANILA: Philippines’ Vice President Sara Duterte (pic) is facing a second impeachment as soon as Monday (May 11), a possible victory for her ally-turned-rival President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. that will likely deepen the country’s political and economic turmoil.

title: Philippine Vice President Duterte facing second impeachment vote
text: One-third of the 318-member House of Representatives is needed to impeach, which would then kick the process up to the Senate, where two-thirds of its 24 members will be needed to convict her.

title: Philippine Vice President Duterte facing second impeachment vote
text: A conviction in the Senate, which is far from certain, would ban her from politics for life and remove her from office.

title: Philippine Vice President Duterte facing second impeachment vote
text: House Deputy Speaker Janette Garin said the House will vote on Duterte’s impeachment Monday, without providing further details.

title: Philippine Vice President Duterte facing second impeachment vote
text: There are at least 215 votes to impeach the vice president, Congressman Terry Ridon said in a post on X.

title: Philippine Vice President Duterte facing second impeachment vote
text: "At this point, the evidence has clearly established probable cause for betrayal of public trust and culpable violation of the Constitution,” Ridon said.

title: Philippine Vice President Duterte facing second impeachment vote
text: Duterte is facing four charges, including misusing public funds and threatening to assassinate Marcos.

title: Philippine Vice President Duterte facing second impeachment vote
text: She’s denied any wrongdoing, claiming the charges are politically motivated.

title: Philippine Vice President Duterte facing second impeachment vote
text: The vice president, daughter of former President Rodrigo Duterte, leads opinion polls as the most popular candidate to succeed Marcos when his six-year term expires in 2028.

title: Philippine Vice President Duterte facing second impeachment vote
text: She was impeached for the first time in February 2025, but the Supreme Court threw out the complaint on procedural grounds.

title: Philippine Vice President Duterte facing second impeachment vote
text: Her impeachment heightens the political stakes in a nation already rattled by a massive government corruption crisis and an oil-price shock stemming from the Middle East conflict.

title: Philippine Vice President Duterte facing second impeachment vote
text: With inflation surging, the South-East Asian country last week reported its slowest economic expansion outside of the pandemic since 2009.

title: Philippine Vice President Duterte facing second impeachment vote
text: Marcos and Duterte teamed up to win the 2022 election but political differences caused their alliance to collapse.

title: Philippine Vice President Duterte facing second impeachment vote
text: Their rivalry has since dominated Philippine politics.

title: Philippine Vice President Duterte facing second impeachment vote
text:
In February, an impeachment effort against Marcos failed after the justice committee found the underlying complaint was "insufficient in substance.”

The Philippines has a history of impeaching government officials, most notably former President Joseph Estrada and the late Chief Justice Renato Corona.

title: Philippine Vice President Duterte facing second impeachment vote

title: BMI sees Bank Negara holdings OPR at 2.75% in July amid contained inflation
text: BMI sees Bank Negara holdings OPR at 2.75% in July amid contained inflation

title: BMI sees Bank Negara holdings OPR at 2.75% in July amid contained inflation
text: KUALA LUMPUR: BMI, a unit of Fitch Solutions, expects Bank Negara Malaysia (BNM) to leave the overnight policy rate (OPR) on hold at 2.75 per cent at its next meeting on July 9, following the central bank’s May 7 rate decision.

title: BMI sees Bank Negara holdings OPR at 2.75% in July amid contained inflation
text: BMI said there was little concern on the inflation front, with headline inflation edging higher to 1.7 per cent in March from 1.4 per cent year-on-year (y-o-y) in February 2026 as unsubsidised fuel prices spiked.

title: BMI sees Bank Negara holdings OPR at 2.75% in July amid contained inflation
text: "However, the inflation rate remains comfortably between the government’s target for inflation to average 1.5 per cent to 2.5 per cent and the long-term average of 2.0 per cent,” it said in a note.

title: BMI sees Bank Negara holdings OPR at 2.75% in July amid contained inflation
text: BMI was also confident that BNM would leave the OPR on hold at 2.75 per cent for the rest of the year.

title: BMI sees Bank Negara holdings OPR at 2.75% in July amid contained inflation
text: "While BNM acknowledged that elevated global commodity prices will probably nudge inflation higher, it reiterated its expectation for the impact to ‘remain contained’ as ongoing subsidy measures will keep a lid on price pressures.

title: BMI sees Bank Negara holdings OPR at 2.75% in July amid contained inflation
text: "We are aligned on this and have retained our forecast for inflation to average 1.9 per cent in 2026,” it said.

title: BMI sees Bank Negara holdings OPR at 2.75% in July amid contained inflation
text: On export performance, BMi said that the external sector remained resilient, with timely data showing exports rising 12.7 per cent y-o-y in the first quarter of 2026, supported in large part by artificial intelligence-related tailwinds.

title: BMI sees Bank Negara holdings OPR at 2.75% in July amid contained inflation
text: "Looking ahead, we expect the trade surplus to remain robust as the ongoing semiconductor upcycle shows little sign of moderation.

title: BMI sees Bank Negara holdings OPR at 2.75% in July amid contained inflation
text: "This suggests that exports of electronics and electrical goods, which remain closely tied to that of global semiconductor sales, will help strengthen Malaysia’s trade position,” it said.

title: BMI sees Bank Negara holdings OPR at 2.75% in July amid contained inflation

title: Govt finalising income threshold for targeted fuel subsidies, says Economy Minister
text: Govt finalising income threshold for targeted fuel subsidies, says Economy Minister

title: Govt finalising income threshold for targeted fuel subsidies, says Economy Minister
text: PUTRAJAYA: The government is working out the income threshold for fuel subsidies to ensure that vulnerable groups do not experience sudden financial shocks, said Economy Minister Akmal Nasrullah Mohd Nasir.

title: Govt finalising income threshold for targeted fuel subsidies, says Economy Minister
text: According to Akmal Nasrullah, determining the exact dividing line between those eligible for subsidies and those who will be excluded remains the government’s biggest challenge.

title: Govt finalising income threshold for targeted fuel subsidies, says Economy Minister
text: "We do not want to cause a significant shock to groups that still require support, or those who are not fully ready to transition.

title: Govt finalising income threshold for targeted fuel subsidies, says Economy Minister
text: "That is the core of our discussions to determine the exact dividing lines between those who rightfully deserve the subsidy and those who should be removed from the system," said Akmal to reporters during a press conference on Monday (May 11).

title: Govt finalising income threshold for targeted fuel subsidies, says Economy Minister
text: Akmal Nasrullah said the matter was brought up at the recent National Economic Action Council meeting, with Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim expressing the government’s readiness to balance the needs of the people through a targeted approach.

title: Govt finalising income threshold for targeted fuel subsidies, says Economy Minister
text: Akmal Nasrullah also said the government is moving away from blanket subsidies and instead focusing on needs and usage to ensure the subsidy truly benefits targeted groups.

title: Govt finalising income threshold for targeted fuel subsidies, says Economy Minister
text: "The details are being scrutinised by a crisis management committee," he added.

title: Govt finalising income threshold for targeted fuel subsidies, says Economy Minister
text: On when it would be implemented, Akmal Nasrullah said the new targeted subsidy mechanism will likely be rolled out at the beginning of a month.

title: Govt finalising income threshold for targeted fuel subsidies, says Economy Minister
text: "In my view, it is best that we finalise this before the start of a new month.

title: Govt finalising income threshold for targeted fuel subsidies, says Economy Minister
text: Bringing about this change involves system readiness to ensure the screening process runs smoothly.

title: Govt finalising income threshold for targeted fuel subsidies, says Economy Minister

title: Govt finalising income threshold for targeted fuel subsidies, says Economy Minister
text: Asked if the Central Database Hub (Padu) will be used to classify income groups, Akmal Nasrullah said the evaluation will be multidimensional.

title: Govt finalising income threshold for targeted fuel subsidies, says Economy Minister
text: "Evaluation will be based on income, but we are also considering other aspects.

title: Govt finalising income threshold for targeted fuel subsidies, says Economy Minister
text: The Economy Ministry is ready to provide the necessary data points and dimensions as a reference.

title: Govt finalising income threshold for targeted fuel subsidies, says Economy Minister
text: "Once the policy is decided, we will share the details.

title: Govt finalising income threshold for targeted fuel subsidies, says Economy Minister
text: Having a clear dividing line will make it easier for the target groups to accept, and we must also provide room for appeals," he added.

title: Govt finalising income threshold for targeted fuel subsidies, says Economy Minister
text: Addressing concerns from the T20 group, who are the nation’s largest taxpayers, Akmal Nasrullah acknowledged their contributions but stressed that blanket subsidies are unsustainable.

title: Govt finalising income threshold for targeted fuel subsidies, says Economy Minister
text: "The Economy Ministry’s stance is consistent: the provision of this assistance must be targeted.

title: Govt finalising income threshold for targeted fuel subsidies, says Economy Minister
text: We definitely take their contributions to the country into account.

title: Govt finalising income threshold for targeted fuel subsidies, says Economy Minister
text: "However, if the current massive amount of subsidies is not managed carefully, it will have negative implications.

title: Govt finalising income threshold for targeted fuel subsidies, says Economy Minister
text: We are trying to strike a balance between protecting the people, managing inflation, and ensuring national development.

title: Govt finalising income threshold for targeted fuel subsidies, says Economy Minister
text: "By maintaining our economic resilience and ensuring economic activities continue to thrive, the community as a whole will ultimately reap the benefits," he said.

title: Govt finalising income threshold for targeted fuel subsidies, says Economy Minister
text: On Sunday (May 10), Anwar said Putrajaya will finalise the rationalisation of fuel subsidies, particularly targeting higher-income earners soon.

title: Govt finalising income threshold for targeted fuel subsidies, says Economy Minister

title: Sarawak plans to take over more federal entities, says Abang Johari
text: Sarawak plans to take over more federal entities, says Abang Johari

title: Sarawak plans to take over more federal entities, says Abang Johari
text: KUCHING: Sarawak plans to take over more strategic entities and assets of the federal government to strengthen the state's economy in line with the Post-COVID-19 Development Strategy 2030 (PCDS 2030).
