title: Sarawak plans to take over more federal entities, says Abang Johari
text: Sarawak Premier Tan Sri Abang Johari Tun Openg said the move was important in expanding the state's economic dominance and creating more high-skilled job opportunities for Sarawakians, especially in the technology and new industry sectors.

title: Sarawak plans to take over more federal entities, says Abang Johari
text: According to him, in the past six years, Sarawak has taken over several strategic assets and entities from the federal government, including the Bakun Dam, Bintulu Port and MASwings Sdn Bhd.

title: Sarawak plans to take over more federal entities, says Abang Johari
text: "We are also establishing Petroleum Sarawak Berhad (Petros), we are taking over equity in Affin Bank (becoming the largest shareholder of Affin Bank Bhd) and we want to take over many more.

title: Sarawak plans to take over more federal entities, says Abang Johari
text: "We are expanding our economy based on PCDS… this means new job opportunities will be created," he said when speaking at the Sarawak-level Workers' Day celebration at Borneo Convention Centre Kuching (BCCK) here on Sunday (May 10).

title: Sarawak plans to take over more federal entities, says Abang Johari
text: Abang Johari said Sarawak's economic transformation is currently moving towards a high-tech economy that focuses on renewable energy, automation, artificial intelligence (AI), digitalisation and the semiconductor industry.

title: Sarawak plans to take over more federal entities, says Abang Johari
text: According to him, Sarawak needs more skilled and professional workforce in various fields including aerospace, digital technology and the semiconductor chip industry.

title: Sarawak plans to take over more federal entities, says Abang Johari
text: - Bernama

title: SMEs under pressure from rising diesel costs
text: SMEs under pressure from rising diesel costs

title: SMEs under pressure from rising diesel costs
text: THE continued rise in diesel prices, coupled with increasing raw material, transportation, and operating costs, is placing local small and medium enterprises (SMEs) under unprecedented pressure.

title: SMEs under pressure from rising diesel costs
text: At this stage, what businesses need most is not one-off financial assistance, as temporary subsidies only provide short-term relief and do not fundamentally improve the business environment.

title: SMEs under pressure from rising diesel costs
text: Instead, the government should consider reducing tax burdens, simplifying licensing procedures, and waiving unnecessary fees, measures that would have a more direct and meaningful impact on restoring business confidence.

title: SMEs under pressure from rising diesel costs
text: Higher diesel prices have significantly increased logistics and transportation costs, while fluctuations in raw material prices continue to squeeze profit margins.

title: SMEs under pressure from rising diesel costs
text: At the same time, the growing burden of local licences, renewal procedures, administrative charges, and compliance requirements has made it increasingly difficult for SMEs to operate.

title: SMEs under pressure from rising diesel costs
text: Traditional retail businesses, small food and beverage operators, logistics companies, and service industries are among the hardest hit.

title: SMEs under pressure from rising diesel costs
text:
To address these challenges, MCA proposes several measures:

- Simplify and digitalise application procedures to reduce unnecessary bureaucracy and duplicate documentation.

title: SMEs under pressure from rising diesel costs
text: - Review and reduce local licensing and administrative fees, including introducing nominal charges where appropriate.

title: SMEs under pressure from rising diesel costs
text: - Expedite approval processes to prevent delays that disrupt business operations.

title: SMEs under pressure from rising diesel costs
text: - Work closely with local business associations and civil society organisations to improve communication and ensure policies better reflect grassroots concerns.

title: SMEs under pressure from rising diesel costs
text: - Ensure enforcement is carried out fairly and transparently, without unnecessarily burdening law-abiding businesses.

title: SMEs under pressure from rising diesel costs
text: - Consider temporary income tax exemptions or targeted tax reductions for affected sectors to ease financial pressure and restore market confidence.

title: SMEs under pressure from rising diesel costs
text: The government should not rely solely on short-term subsidies or one-off assistance measures, as these do not provide sustainable solutions.

title: SMEs under pressure from rising diesel costs
text: What is needed are reforms to taxation, licensing, and administrative systems that genuinely reduce operating burdens and eliminate unnecessary red tape.

title: SMEs under pressure from rising diesel costs
text: In many cases, businesses are not asking the government for financial handouts.

title: SMEs under pressure from rising diesel costs
text: What they want is for policies not to further increase their burden.

title: SMEs under pressure from rising diesel costs
text: Reducing tax pressure, lowering licensing fees, simplifying procedures, or waiving unnecessary charges would already provide significant relief and help strengthen confidence among business operators.

title: SMEs under pressure from rising diesel costs
text: Furthermore, with the global economic outlook remaining uncertain, any large-scale collapse of local SMEs would not only affect employment but also weaken the broader national economic ecosystem.

title: SMEs under pressure from rising diesel costs
text: SMEs have long been a key pillar of the economy, and widespread business closures would disrupt supply chains, consumer activity, and local economic development.

title: SMEs under pressure from rising diesel costs
text: It is time for the government to seriously address the real challenges faced by local businesses.

title: SMEs under pressure from rising diesel costs
text: Only by effectively reducing operating costs and easing regulatory burdens can local enterprises weather current difficulties, protect livelihoods, and support the long-term stability of the national economy.

title: SMEs under pressure from rising diesel costs
text:
DATUK LAWRENCE LOW

MCA Vice President, MCA Economic & SMEs Affairs Committee Chairman

title: PM to announce plan to ensure continuity of M'sia's oil supply in mid-May, says Economy Minister
text: PM to announce plan to ensure continuity of M'sia's oil supply in mid-May, says Economy Minister

title: PM to announce plan to ensure continuity of M'sia's oil supply in mid-May, says Economy Minister
text: KUALA LUMPUR: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim is expected to announce a plan to ensure the continuity of the country’s oil supply in mid-May, following global supply uncertainties caused by the prolonged conflict in the Middle East and the closure of the Strait of Hormuz, says Akmal Nasrullah Mohd Nasir.

title: PM to announce plan to ensure continuity of M'sia's oil supply in mid-May, says Economy Minister
text: The Economy Minister said the plan is expected to focus on efforts to ensure domestic oil supply remains sufficient to support the continuity of the country’s economic activities, after the government previously assured that current supplies could last until June.

title: PM to announce plan to ensure continuity of M'sia's oil supply in mid-May, says Economy Minister
text: He said Petroliam Nasional Bhd (PETRONAS) is now at the final stage of finalising new supply sources to ensure Malaysia’s oil supply continuity for the next period.

title: PM to announce plan to ensure continuity of M'sia's oil supply in mid-May, says Economy Minister
text: “The Prime Minister previously stated that several new sources are being finalised and he is expected to present the next schedule related to Malaysia’s oil supply,” he told reporters after being a panellist at the forum “Energy Crisis 2026: Are We Ready to Rethink Refuel?”, held in conjunction with the University of Malaya Economic Summit (UMES) here on Saturday (May 9).

title: PM to announce plan to ensure continuity of M'sia's oil supply in mid-May, says Economy Minister
text: Meanwhile, Akmal Nasrullah said the country’s oil reserves are currently still at an operational level, and Malaysia is still able to withstand supply disruptions due to the intervention and management measures that have been implemented.

title: PM to announce plan to ensure continuity of M'sia's oil supply in mid-May, says Economy Minister
text: Beyond domestic efforts, he said the government is also looking at regional co-operation as an important approach to strengthen energy supply resilience.

title: PM to announce plan to ensure continuity of M'sia's oil supply in mid-May, says Economy Minister
text: “At the Asean level, I understand there are discussions in the context of direction for regional-level reserves, but of course in terms of the framework, mechanisms and so on, we need further details because it involves diplomacy and relationships among member countries,” he said.

title: PM to announce plan to ensure continuity of M'sia's oil supply in mid-May, says Economy Minister
text: On the use of B15 biodiesel, he said the government is optimistic that implementation of the higher biodiesel blend can begin on June 1 to help extend the lifespan of the country’s diesel supply.

title: PM to announce plan to ensure continuity of M'sia's oil supply in mid-May, says Economy Minister
text: He added that the implementation structure involves the entire biodiesel supply chain, including distribution to petrol stations, and has received good commitment from the relevant ministries and agencies.

title: PM to announce plan to ensure continuity of M'sia's oil supply in mid-May, says Economy Minister
text: — Bernama

title: Singapore PM Wong urges Asean to prepare for new normal of disruptions by strengthening integration
text: Singapore PM Wong urges Asean to prepare for new normal of disruptions by strengthening integration

title: Singapore PM Wong urges Asean to prepare for new normal of disruptions by strengthening integration
text: CEBU (The Straits Times/ANN): ASEAN has to prepare for a new normal of economic disruptions and shocks by pursuing broader and deeper regional cooperation, said Prime Minister Lawrence Wong at the ASEAN Summit on May 8.

title: Singapore PM Wong urges Asean to prepare for new normal of disruptions by strengthening integration
text: He called on the grouping to double down on integration, strengthen energy security in the region and bolster intra-ASEAN supply chains, especially for critical goods such as foods.

title: Singapore PM Wong urges Asean to prepare for new normal of disruptions by strengthening integration
text: This will bolster the region’s resilience against events such as the Covid-19 pandemic, the US Liberation Day tariffs and the current Middle East conflict, he added.

title: Singapore PM Wong urges Asean to prepare for new normal of disruptions by strengthening integration
text: “The realities of the global economy going forward are that there will be more disruptions, more shocks, and more volatility,” he said.

title: Singapore PM Wong urges Asean to prepare for new normal of disruptions by strengthening integration
text:
“In this environment, ASEAN must do more to strengthen our collective resilience.”

The implications of the Middle East situation have topped the agenda at the biannual meeting held in Cebu amid a national energy emergency in the Philippines.

title: Singapore PM Wong urges Asean to prepare for new normal of disruptions by strengthening integration
text: South-east Asian countries, which import much of their oil from the Gulf, have been among the hardest hit by the energy supply crisis caused by the war in Iran and the effective closure of the Strait of Hormuz.

title: Singapore PM Wong urges Asean to prepare for new normal of disruptions by strengthening integration
text: Opening the summit at the Mactan Expo, Philippine President Ferdinand Marcos Jr said the increasingly volatile situation in the Middle East has threatened the lifestyles, livelihoods and lives of people in the region.

title: Singapore PM Wong urges Asean to prepare for new normal of disruptions by strengthening integration
text: “Over the past months, each of our countries has had to make adjustments, to modify our approaches.

title: Singapore PM Wong urges Asean to prepare for new normal of disruptions by strengthening integration
text: So we come together now to study those adjustments, to find the best approaches, to face the future together,” he said.

title: Singapore PM Wong urges Asean to prepare for new normal of disruptions by strengthening integration
text: Speaking later at the same plenary session, PM Wong extended his condolences to member states that have lost citizens to the conflict.

title: Singapore PM Wong urges Asean to prepare for new normal of disruptions by strengthening integration
text: “We all hope the crisis will end soon, but we have to be mentally prepared that the impact will be with us for some time,” he said.

title: Singapore PM Wong urges Asean to prepare for new normal of disruptions by strengthening integration
text: In the meantime, ASEAN can double down on integration, and one concrete measure is to swiftly ratify the upgraded ASEAN Trade in Goods Agreement (ATIGA) so that it can enter into force within the year, he said.

title: Singapore PM Wong urges Asean to prepare for new normal of disruptions by strengthening integration
text: At a time when it is tempting for countries to put up more barriers and turn inwards, doing so will give investors confidence that ASEAN is integrated and determined to work together in times of crisis, he said.

title: Singapore PM Wong urges Asean to prepare for new normal of disruptions by strengthening integration
text: ATIGA includes crisis-related provisions and commitments on trade in essential goods, such as food and medical supplies.

title: Singapore PM Wong urges Asean to prepare for new normal of disruptions by strengthening integration
text: PM Wong said ASEAN member states can leverage these mechanisms to work closely together during times of shortages or emergencies.

title: Singapore PM Wong urges Asean to prepare for new normal of disruptions by strengthening integration
text: He added that the regional trade pact should be treated as a base, calling on ASEAN countries to go even further.

title: Singapore PM Wong urges Asean to prepare for new normal of disruptions by strengthening integration
text: Like-minded countries can come together on other agreements that will provide mutual support as well as give assurances of open markets and no export restrictions even in times of shortages, he suggested, saying that the ASEAN economic ministers can expedite these measures.

title: Singapore PM Wong urges Asean to prepare for new normal of disruptions by strengthening integration
text: The grouping should also strengthen energy security through the swift ratification of the ASEAN Petroleum Security Agreement (APSA), which was recently renewed in 2025, he said.

title: Singapore PM Wong urges Asean to prepare for new normal of disruptions by strengthening integration
text: The agreement sets out a framework for the sharing of petroleum during emergencies, and PM Wong said it would give member states more tools to respond during energy crises.

title: Singapore PM Wong urges Asean to prepare for new normal of disruptions by strengthening integration
text: Similarly, APSA should be treated as a base and not be the be-all and end-all, he added.

title: Singapore PM Wong urges Asean to prepare for new normal of disruptions by strengthening integration
text: He noted that ASEAN ministers and officials have been discussing various ideas, including the possibility of a regional fuel stockpile, and said this should be encouraged.

title: Singapore PM Wong urges Asean to prepare for new normal of disruptions by strengthening integration
text: Additionally, the grouping should speed up the realisation of the ASEAN Power Grid (APG), which will allow member states to trade surplus electricity and share reserve capacities.

title: Singapore PM Wong urges Asean to prepare for new normal of disruptions by strengthening integration
text: PM Wong said this will build resilience against future supply disruptions and external shocks, while powering the region’s growth in a more sustainable manner.

title: Singapore PM Wong urges Asean to prepare for new normal of disruptions by strengthening integration
text: ASEAN is aiming to achieve a fully integrated regional electricity grid by 2045.

title: Singapore PM Wong urges Asean to prepare for new normal of disruptions by strengthening integration
text: Another thing it can do is to strengthen intra-ASEAN supply chains, especially for critical goods like food, said PM Wong.

title: Singapore PM Wong urges Asean to prepare for new normal of disruptions by strengthening integration
text: As a major production centre, the region accounts for almost 10 per cent of global agricultural exports and should tap this inherent strength to meet the needs of its people, he added.

title: Singapore PM Wong urges Asean to prepare for new normal of disruptions by strengthening integration
text: The upgraded ATIGA reduces non-tariff barriers to food trade and encourages the exchange of food safety information, and can complement existing efforts such as the ASEAN Plus Three Emergency Rice Reserve and the ASEAN Food Security Information System, he said.

title: Singapore PM Wong urges Asean to prepare for new normal of disruptions by strengthening integration
text: PM Wong added that even as member states deal with the crisis, it is important to keep alive the spirit of ASEAN solidarity.

title: Singapore PM Wong urges Asean to prepare for new normal of disruptions by strengthening integration
text: That is why Singapore helped to bring back citizens from other ASEAN countries when it repatriated its own citizens from the Middle East in the early days of the conflict, he said.

title: Singapore PM Wong urges Asean to prepare for new normal of disruptions by strengthening integration
text: “We wanted to assist where we could, just as how our ASEAN family had lent a hand to Singaporeans in previous crises,” he added.

title: Singapore PM Wong urges Asean to prepare for new normal of disruptions by strengthening integration
text: In the years ahead, ASEAN must also work closely to achieve its community-building agenda, he said.

title: Singapore PM Wong urges Asean to prepare for new normal of disruptions by strengthening integration
text: A significant milestone in this effort is the adoption of the Cebu Protocol, the first ever amendment to the ASEAN Charter to reflect the membership of Timor-Leste.

title: Singapore PM Wong urges Asean to prepare for new normal of disruptions by strengthening integration
text: One of the world’s youngest countries, Timor-Leste officially became the 11th member of the grouping in 2025.

title: Singapore PM Wong urges Asean to prepare for new normal of disruptions by strengthening integration
text: PM Wong said Singapore supports Timor-Leste’s ASEAN chairmanship in 2029.

title: Singapore PM Wong urges Asean to prepare for new normal of disruptions by strengthening integration
text:
Thanking the Philippines for its steady leadership of the grouping during the challenging times, he said: “I look forward to working with fellow colleagues across ASEAN member states to chart a course that will provide ASEAN with lasting security, stability and prosperity.”

Singapore is scheduled to assume the ASEAN chairmanship in 2027.

title: Singapore PM Wong urges Asean to prepare for new normal of disruptions by strengthening integration
text: -- The Straits Times/ANN

title: Malaysia's economy remains resilient despite West Asia risks, says Bank Negara governor
text: Malaysia's economy remains resilient despite West Asia risks, says Bank Negara governor

title: Malaysia's economy remains resilient despite West Asia risks, says Bank Negara governor
text: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia's economy remains resilient despite mounting risks from the ongoing West Asia conflict, supported by strong domestic demand, sustained investment activity and robust exports linked to the global technology and artificial intelligence (AI) upcycle.

title: Malaysia's economy remains resilient despite West Asia risks, says Bank Negara governor
text: Bank Negara Malaysia (BNM) Governor Datuk Seri Abdul Rasheed Ghaffour said Malaysia's growth momentum remains intact, underpinned by private consumption, ongoing multi-year investment projects and continued implementation of government development initiatives.

title: Malaysia's economy remains resilient despite West Asia risks, says Bank Negara governor
text: "Growth momentum is continuing, driven by domestic demand and strong exports.

title: Malaysia's economy remains resilient despite West Asia risks, says Bank Negara governor
text: Investment and consumption are still holding up.

title: Malaysia's economy remains resilient despite West Asia risks, says Bank Negara governor
text: This will help drive Malaysia’s growth for the rest of the year.

title: Malaysia's economy remains resilient despite West Asia risks, says Bank Negara governor
text: "Employment conditions remain good.

title: Malaysia's economy remains resilient despite West Asia risks, says Bank Negara governor
text: Unemployment stood at 2.9 per cent in February.

title: Malaysia's economy remains resilient despite West Asia risks, says Bank Negara governor
text: Wages are still growing and government policy support remains in place to sustain consumption," he told Bernama.

title: Malaysia's economy remains resilient despite West Asia risks, says Bank Negara governor
text: Abdul Rasheed said targeted support and subsidies would help cushion household spending amid rising global uncertainties.

title: Malaysia's economy remains resilient despite West Asia risks, says Bank Negara governor
text: He also noted that strong global demand for AI- and digitalisation-related investments has supported Malaysia's growth in the first quarter of 2026 (1Q 2026), with the momentum likely to continue into the second half of the year.

title: Malaysia's economy remains resilient despite West Asia risks, says Bank Negara governor
text: Additionally, projects approved by Malaysian Investment Development Authority (MIDA) continue to record high realisation rates, while government spending under Budget 2026 and the 13th Malaysia Plan is expected to support economic activity.

title: Malaysia's economy remains resilient despite West Asia risks, says Bank Negara governor
text: Beyond this, Malaysia's financial sector remains strong, ensuring continued financing for the economy despite current stress.

title: Malaysia's economy remains resilient despite West Asia risks, says Bank Negara governor
text: On the gross domestic product (GDP) forecast of 4.0 to 5.0 per cent for 2026, he said BNM has already factored in the impact of the West Asia conflict, including higher energy prices, supply chain disruptions and uncertain global growth momentum.

title: Malaysia's economy remains resilient despite West Asia risks, says Bank Negara governor
text: "Our range is wide enough to capture the current risks.

title: Malaysia's economy remains resilient despite West Asia risks, says Bank Negara governor
text: We had already taken this into account when setting the 4.0 to 5.0 per cent forecast," he said.

title: Malaysia's economy remains resilient despite West Asia risks, says Bank Negara governor
text: Nonetheless, he said conditions remain uncertain, with the impact on Malaysia depending on the intensity and duration of the conflict.

title: Malaysia's economy remains resilient despite West Asia risks, says Bank Negara governor
text: Abdul Rasheed also noted that international projections remain within BNM’s range, with the World Bank forecasting 4.4 per cent, the ASEAN+3 Macroeconomic Research Office (AMRO) 4.6 per cent, and the International Monetary Fund (IMF) 4.7 per cent.

title: Malaysia's economy remains resilient despite West Asia risks, says Bank Negara governor
text: He said the current Overnight Policy Rate (OPR) at 2.75 per cent is appropriate and consistent with the Monetary Policy Committee’s latest growth and inflation outlook.

title: Malaysia's economy remains resilient despite West Asia risks, says Bank Negara governor
text: At the same time, he acknowledged that the impact of the West Asia conflict had started to filter into higher price pressures, with Malaysia’s inflation rate rising to 1.6 per cent in 1Q 2026 from 1.4 per cent a year earlier.

title: Malaysia's economy remains resilient despite West Asia risks, says Bank Negara governor
text: However, the pass-through of higher global costs into domestic prices remains contained due to subsidies, price controls, and enforcement measures against profiteering.

title: Malaysia's economy remains resilient despite West Asia risks, says Bank Negara governor
text: "Price pressures remain contained for now, but we must stay vigilant.

title: Malaysia's economy remains resilient despite West Asia risks, says Bank Negara governor
text: We are alert and monitoring developments very closely," he said.

title: Malaysia's economy remains resilient despite West Asia risks, says Bank Negara governor
text: He assured that BNM would continue to closely monitor developments in West Asia and conduct ongoing assessments of their implications for Malaysia’s growth and inflation outlook.

title: Malaysia's economy remains resilient despite West Asia risks, says Bank Negara governor
text: He added the country had undertaken reforms to strengthen its resilience and ability to absorb external shocks, while policymakers would prudently utilise available fiscal and monetary policy buffers going forward.

title: Malaysia's economy remains resilient despite West Asia risks, says Bank Negara governor
text: Abdul Rasheed, who is also a member of the National Economic Action Council, said the government’s initial priority amid the West Asia conflict was to ensure sufficient domestic fuel supply and maintain adequate stockpiles.

title: Malaysia's economy remains resilient despite West Asia risks, says Bank Negara governor
text: He said authorities have been closely monitoring supply levels, including assessing existing reserves and securing alternative sources should disruptions occur in current supplier countries.

title: Malaysia's economy remains resilient despite West Asia risks, says Bank Negara governor
text: He also noted that Malaysia benefits from the global network and capabilities of Petroliam Nasional Bhd (Petronas), which enabled the national oil company to source crude oil and feedstock from various markets if necessary.

title: Malaysia's economy remains resilient despite West Asia risks, says Bank Negara governor
text: - Bernama

title: Anwar says the bloc must remain united in uncertain times
text: Anwar says the bloc must remain united in uncertain times

title: Anwar says the bloc must remain united in uncertain times
text: PETALING JAYA: As the world faces an energy supply crisis, climate change and economic uncertainty, Asean must have a bold and an integrated approach to deal with these challenges, says Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim.

title: Anwar says the bloc must remain united in uncertain times
text: As such, the Prime Minister said Malaysia supports strengthening cross-border connectivity investments in renewable energy and sustainable solutions such as solar and mini-grids.

title: Anwar says the bloc must remain united in uncertain times
text: “This is so that the benefits of development can be enjoyed fairly and inclusively by all citizens, including communities in the rural areas.

title: Anwar says the bloc must remain united in uncertain times
text: “Asean will continue to advocate development agendas that honour people, strengthen communities and spur a prosperous future for generations to come,” he said in a social media post.

title: Anwar says the bloc must remain united in uncertain times
text: The Prime Minister is in Cebu, the Philippines, to attend the 48th Asean Summit.

title: Anwar says the bloc must remain united in uncertain times
text: Anwar also said Malaysia is committed to strengthening regional cooperation through the Brunei-Indonesia-Malaysia-Philippines East Asean Growth Area (BIMP-EAGA).

title: Anwar says the bloc must remain united in uncertain times
text: The platform, he said, is seen as a strategic vehicle to bridge the development gap, as well as widen the network of trade, investments, digital connectivity and food security across the region.

title: Anwar says the bloc must remain united in uncertain times
text: “At today’s BIMP-EAGA Special Summit in Cebu, I reiterated that BIMP-EAGA Vision 2035 must be driven by the spirit of togetherness, sustainable governance and a commitment to ensure remote and underdeveloped regions do not continue to slip off the flow of development,” he said.

title: Anwar says the bloc must remain united in uncertain times
text: On the sidelines of the Asean Summit, the Prime Minister held a bilateral meeting with his Singapore counterpart Lawrence Wong, where the focus of discussion, among others, included expediting the implementation of the Johor-Singapore Special Economic Zone (JS-SEZ) and ensuring the RTS Link project can be operational by the end of the year.

title: Anwar says the bloc must remain united in uncertain times
text: “The meeting also allowed Malaysia and Singapore to continue strengthening strategic cooperation that upholds mutual interest, regional stability and the prosperity of the people of both countries,” Anwar said.

title: Anwar says the bloc must remain united in uncertain times
text: He added that Malaysia also spoke on the importance of continuing maritime border negotiations constructively and in accordance with international law, in addition to ensuring issues of water supply, airspace and management of the Kuala Lumpur Flight Information Region are addressed in a respectful manner and through bilateral understanding.

title: Anwar says the bloc must remain united in uncertain times
text: “Malaysia also emphasises the importance of a lasting ceasefire in the Middle East, freedom of navigation in the Strait of Hormuz and the need for Asean to continue to play a principled role in maintaining stability, security and sustainability of the global supply chain,” he said.

title: Anwar says the bloc must remain united in uncertain times
text: Anwar also met with his Laos counterpart Dr Sonexay Siphandone, stating that Malaysia and Laos share a strong determination to continue strengthening relations and strategic cooperation.

title: Anwar says the bloc must remain united in uncertain times
text: “We also reaffirmed commitment to expand cooperation in trade, investment, renewable energy, higher education, defence, telecommunications and regional networks.

title: Anwar says the bloc must remain united in uncertain times
text: “Malaysia also expressed support for efforts to strengthen Asean’s economic and logistics integration, including through the Asean Express rail network that connects Malaysia, Thailand, Laos and China.

title: Anwar says the bloc must remain united in uncertain times
text: “In the spirit of Asean togetherness, we believe that sustainable development, mutual respect and prosperity sharing are important vehicles to guarantee stability and the future of the region,” he added.

title: Anwar says the bloc must remain united in uncertain times
text: Earlier, Anwar and his wife Datuk Seri Dr Wan Azizah Wan Ismail landed at the Brigadier General Benito N. Ebuen Air Base.

title: Call for prudence as interest rates stay pat
text: Call for prudence as interest rates stay pat

title: Call for prudence as interest rates stay pat
text: PETALING JAYA: Economists are advocating a cautious outlook on the country’s growth and inflation scene, as Bank Negara Malaysia (BNM) has decided to keep the overnight policy rate (OPR) at 2.75% after the latest Monetary Policy Committee meeting yesterday.

title: Call for prudence as interest rates stay pat
text: The central bank, while acknowledging that global growth had remained resilient in the first quarter of 2026 (1Q26), said sharp increases in energy and commodity prices as well as supply chain disruptions from the Middle East conflict are beginning to weigh on worldwide growth momentum.

title: Call for prudence as interest rates stay pat
text: “Downside risks to global growth remain elevated stemming from the uncertainties surrounding the length and severity of the conflict, tighter global financial conditions and concerns over valuations in financial markets,” said BNM in a statement.

title: Call for prudence as interest rates stay pat
text: Nevertheless, it pointed out that upside potential includes de-escalation of the US-Iran conflict, leading to improving supply chain conditions, stronger tech spending and pro-growth policy measures in key economies.

title: Call for prudence as interest rates stay pat
text: For Malaysia, the central bank reported that latest indicators are pointing towards continued growth momentum in 1Q26, driven by sustained domestic demand and strong export performance.

title: Call for prudence as interest rates stay pat
text: Bank Muamalat Malaysia Bhd chief economist Mohd Afzanizam Abdul Rashid believes that BNM has turned more dovish as the impact from the oil shock is likely to drag the global growth lower.

title: Call for prudence as interest rates stay pat
text: “Hence, being an open economy, Malaysia will continue to be susceptible to downside risk to global growth.

title: Call for prudence as interest rates stay pat
text: “Higher prices will mean consumer budgets will be negatively affected, and therefore, they will become selective in their spending pattern,” he told StarBiz.

title: Call for prudence as interest rates stay pat
text: Similarly, Mohd Afzanizam is expecting businesses to be more prudent with their capital and operating expenditure, as they look for ways to lower costs.

title: Call for prudence as interest rates stay pat
text: Crucially, he said that the OPR will either stay at 2.75% for the whole year or there could be a scope to cut to support growth.

title: Call for prudence as interest rates stay pat
text: “For now, we see keeping the OPR steady is the best course of action by BNM,” he said.

title: Call for prudence as interest rates stay pat
text: On the flip side, executive director at the Socio-Economic Research Centre Lee Heng Guie felt that BNM, being constrained by rising inflation risks amid continued economic growth momentum, is not about to embark on monetary easing soon.

title: Call for prudence as interest rates stay pat
text: Given the supply-driven oil shock, he believes the central bank is leaning more towards anchoring inflation expectations, especially second-round inflation, which he defined as the indirect, subsequent impact of an initial supply-side shock on wage demand and price setting behaviour.

title: Call for prudence as interest rates stay pat
text: At present, Lee said while the first-round shocks such as direct oil price increases and rising costs due to supply chain disruptions will inevitably cause higher inflation, the demand pressure is not prevalent.

title: Call for prudence as interest rates stay pat
text: “Should the conflict in the Middle East escalate, however, accompanied by the deepening supply chain disruptions and persistent soaring energy costs, this will likely trigger a global recession with high inflation, affecting global trade and investment.

title: Call for prudence as interest rates stay pat
text: “Malaysia will be impacted via slowing exports, higher imported inflation and sustained domestic cost pressures, and hence, reduced consumer discretionary spending and lower business spending,” Lee cautioned, before adding that he is holding a baseline 2026 gross domestic product (GDP) estimate of 4.3% for Malaysia.

title: Call for prudence as interest rates stay pat
text: At the same time, Mohd Afzanizam highlighted that logistics costs have also gone up, following the sharp rise in transportation by shipping and air cargo, observing that the producers’ price index (PPI) had jumped 4.1% month-on-month in March.

title: Call for prudence as interest rates stay pat
text: The PPI measures the average change over time in the selling prices received by domestic producers for their output.

title: Call for prudence as interest rates stay pat
text: Eventually, Mohd Afzanizam said these higher business costs will be passed on to the end-consumer, who, in turn, would review their budgets.

title: Call for prudence as interest rates stay pat
text: “Consumers could down-trade into lower-quality products and focus on needs rather than wants.

title: Call for prudence as interest rates stay pat
text: As such, airlines and tourism would see an immediate impact.

title: Call for prudence as interest rates stay pat
text: “Big-ticket items such as automobiles and properties will also be impacted, as consumers would want to save more,” he noted.

title: Call for prudence as interest rates stay pat
text: More importantly, the economist now anticipates that subsidies would remain as a prominent policy tool in dealing with the higher cost of living, despite there having been efforts for subsidy rationalisation from the government.

title: Call for prudence as interest rates stay pat
text: “Nonetheless, how high could oil prices go?

title: Call for prudence as interest rates stay pat
text: How sustainable are the fiscal resources to support the subsidies bill?

title: Call for prudence as interest rates stay pat
text: These are the questions that would shape the impact on the Malaysian economy,” he said.

title: Call for prudence as interest rates stay pat
text: Fellow economist Doris Liew echoed Mohd Afzanizam’s sentiments, as she stressed that Malaysia’s ability to cushion the shock through subsidies is increasingly limited, with the government already stretching its fiscal position, cutting development spending and entering a consolidation phase.

title: Call for prudence as interest rates stay pat
text: “Continuing to rely on broad-based subsidies while tightening fiscally elsewhere creates trade-offs that may not be sustainable if the shock persists.

title: Call for prudence as interest rates stay pat
text: The policy challenge ahead lies in managing these constraints as the government seeks to balance cost-of-living support, fiscal space, and longer-term growth,” she said.

title: Call for prudence as interest rates stay pat
text: Of interest, senior Asean economist at HSBC Yun Liu commented that before the Middle East conflict, the economy was in a “Goldilocks” stage, with strong growth and stable inflation prospects.

title: Call for prudence as interest rates stay pat
text: While the conflict has increased the possibility of downside risks to growth and upside risks to inflation, she said Malaysia is on a more resilient footing than its peers, as it is a net energy exporter.

title: Call for prudence as interest rates stay pat
text: “This is not to say Malaysia will not be insulated from the energy shock, but the impact should be smaller than other Asian economies, which are heavily dependent on oil and gas imports from the Gulf region,” said Liu.

title: Call for prudence as interest rates stay pat
text: Professor of economics at Sunway University Dr Yeah Kim Leng feels that Bank Negara’s continued hold on the OPR is appropriate given Malaysia’s healthy first quarter advance GDP growth estimate, coupled with slowing but still healthy manufacturing exports.

title: Call for prudence as interest rates stay pat
text: “A sharper-than-expected rise in consumer inflation in the coming months but viewed to be temporal in nature may not trigger a rate hike but it will likely preclude any rate cut to support growth.

title: Call for prudence as interest rates stay pat
text: “Should world oil prices stabilise and revert to US$70 to US$80 a barrel, there may be room for monetary easing to support growth should domestic growth conditions and global demand weaken substantially,” he told StarBiz.

title: Call for prudence as interest rates stay pat
text: Bank Negara, on its part, projected that investment activity will be driven by the progress of multi-year projects in both the private and public sectors, implementation of new smaller-scale public projects, continued high realisation of approved investments, as well as the ongoing implementation of national master plans.

title: Call for prudence as interest rates stay pat
text: It said the external sector will benefit from continued strength in electrical and electronics (E&E) exports, while tourist spending will be sustained albeit at a more moderate pace.

title: Call for prudence as interest rates stay pat
text: The monetary authority revealed that headline and core inflation averaged 1.6% and 2.1% in the first quarter of 2026, respectively, but noted higher global commodity prices arising from the Middle East conflict are expected to raise domestic cost pressures, causing inflation to “edge higher” although it should be contained.

title: Call for prudence as interest rates stay pat
text: HSBC’s Liu picked up Bank Negara’s subtle shifts in tone, alluding to the fact that the central bank now views the monetary policy stance to be appropriate and “consistent” with the outlook, as opposed to “supportive” in earlier statements.

title: Call for prudence as interest rates stay pat
text: “The last time Bank Negara changed the language from ‘supportive’ to ‘consistent’ was in May 2025, the meeting before its pre-emptive 25 basis points rate cut in July 2025.

title: Call for prudence as interest rates stay pat
text: That said... we maintain our long-held view that Bank Negara will keep its policy rate steady at 2.75% throughout 2026,” she added.

title: Extended OPR pause signals confidence in Malaysia’s economic resilience
text: Extended OPR pause signals confidence in Malaysia’s economic resilience

title: Extended OPR pause signals confidence in Malaysia’s economic resilience
text: KUALA LUMPUR: Bank Negara Malaysia’s (BNM) decision to maintain the Overnight Policy Rate (OPR) at 2.75 per cent for a sixth consecutive Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) meeting reflects growing confidence in the resilience of Malaysia’s domestic economy despite mounting global uncertainties, economists said.

title: Extended OPR pause signals confidence in Malaysia’s economic resilience
text: The benchmark OPR has remained unchanged since July 2025, making it one of BNM’s longest policy pauses in recent years.

title: Extended OPR pause signals confidence in Malaysia’s economic resilience
text: They said the prolonged pause indicates that current monetary settings remain supportive of growth while givng policymakers flexibility to respond to evolving external risks, including geopolitical tensions and global trade uncertainties.

title: Extended OPR pause signals confidence in Malaysia’s economic resilience
text: Bank Muamalat Malaysia Bhd chief economist Dr Mohd Afzanizam Abdul Rashid said BNM’s earlier pre-emptive measures in July 2025 have provided sufficient policy stability under the current economic environment.

title: Extended OPR pause signals confidence in Malaysia’s economic resilience
text: "Under the current environment where growth remains good, the need to lower the OPR is less urgent,” he told Bernama.

title: Extended OPR pause signals confidence in Malaysia’s economic resilience
text: The central bank decided to maintain the OPR at 2.75 per cent at today’s third of six MPC meetings scheduled for 2026.

title: Extended OPR pause signals confidence in Malaysia’s economic resilience
text: He said Malaysia’s growth momentum remains intact for now despite concerns over rising global oil prices and the prolonged conflict in West Asia.

title: Extended OPR pause signals confidence in Malaysia’s economic resilience
text: However, Mohd Afzanizam noted that BNM would continue monitoring growth prospects closely, particularly in the second half of 2026, as external risks remain fluid.

title: Extended OPR pause signals confidence in Malaysia’s economic resilience
text: "If growth is expected to slow further, then BNM may reduce the OPR again,” he added.

title: Extended OPR pause signals confidence in Malaysia’s economic resilience
text: Meanwhile, CGS International Securities Malaysia head of economics Nazmi Idrus said the latest OPR decision was largely expected by the market and reflects BNM’s balanced approach between sustaining growth and managing inflationary pressures.

title: Extended OPR pause signals confidence in Malaysia’s economic resilience
text: "I think today’s move is largely expected.

title: Extended OPR pause signals confidence in Malaysia’s economic resilience
text: Given the difficulty in foresight, not just in Malaysia but the rest of the world, investors are looking not only at OPR movements, but also at a country’s overall resilience when assessing the ringgit and their investments,” he said.

title: Extended OPR pause signals confidence in Malaysia’s economic resilience
text: Nazmi said Malaysia remains relatively well-positioned compared with several regional economies as subsidy measures and stable fuel supply conditions continue to cushion inflationary pressures.

title: Extended OPR pause signals confidence in Malaysia’s economic resilience
text: He added that the stable interest rate environment also provides greater visibility for businesses and investors in planning their spending and investment decisions amid continued global volatility.

title: Extended OPR pause signals confidence in Malaysia’s economic resilience
text: - Bernama

title: Govt closely monitors development projects, prioritises public interest, says Akmal Nasrullah
text: Govt closely monitors development projects, prioritises public interest, says Akmal Nasrullah

title: Govt closely monitors development projects, prioritises public interest, says Akmal Nasrullah
text: KULIM: The Economy Ministry will continue to closely monitor the implementation of development projects, particularly those under its purview, to ensure they remain on track and are not delayed, especially for ongoing projects of public interest.

title: Govt closely monitors development projects, prioritises public interest, says Akmal Nasrullah
text: Its Minister, Akmal Nasrullah Mohd Nasir, said the ministry was continuously assessing the need for government intervention to address the impact of the crisis through the weekly National Economic Action Council (MTEN) meeting.

title: Govt closely monitors development projects, prioritises public interest, says Akmal Nasrullah
text: He said the government needed to ensure that development could continue, as the spillover benefits from development projects would have a lasting impact on the people and the country.

title: Govt closely monitors development projects, prioritises public interest, says Akmal Nasrullah
text: "We must ensure that development continues, as the spillover effects of these development projects are not felt overnight.

title: Govt closely monitors development projects, prioritises public interest, says Akmal Nasrullah
text: "In cases where projects cannot be continued, for example, water supply-related projects, we are talking about future needs that span many years,” he told reporters after a working visit to the Kulim Hi-Tech Park (KHTP) here on Thursday (May 7).

title: Govt closely monitors development projects, prioritises public interest, says Akmal Nasrullah
text: Akmal Nasrullah said that at the same time, should the fiscal burden continue to rise, the government needed to prioritise projects that were of public interest, especially those already in progress.

title: Govt closely monitors development projects, prioritises public interest, says Akmal Nasrullah
text: "That is why our advice to all parties is to keep finding ways to maintain current performance, as well as to proceed with planned developments,” he said.

title: Govt closely monitors development projects, prioritises public interest, says Akmal Nasrullah
text: Meanwhile, Akmal Nasrullah said his visit to the Kulim Hi-Tech Park (KHTP) was to assess the latest developments in Phase Five of the industrial park, which must be implemented smoothly.

title: Govt closely monitors development projects, prioritises public interest, says Akmal Nasrullah
text: He said that among the matters given attention were water and energy supply aspects to support the needs of industrial development in the area.

title: Govt closely monitors development projects, prioritises public interest, says Akmal Nasrullah
text: "The federal government has provided RM132mil in development funds for infrastructure purposes, as well as a RM195mil loan for land acquisition works and related matters,” he said.

title: Govt closely monitors development projects, prioritises public interest, says Akmal Nasrullah
text: — Bernama

title: China vessel conducting ‘illegal’ research near gas-rich Reed bank, says Philippines
text: China vessel conducting ‘illegal’ research near gas-rich Reed bank, says Philippines

title: China vessel conducting ‘illegal’ research near gas-rich Reed bank, says Philippines
text: MANILA: The Philippine Coast Guard (PCG) has accused China of conducting illegal marine scientific research near the oil- and gas-rich Reed bank within Manila’s exclusive economic zone in the South China Sea, according to a statement on Thursday (May 7).

title: China vessel conducting ‘illegal’ research near gas-rich Reed bank, says Philippines
text: “We will continue to challenge any illegal activities that undermine our sovereignty and sovereign rights,” said the PCG commandant, Admiral Ronnie Gil Gavan.

title: China vessel conducting ‘illegal’ research near gas-rich Reed bank, says Philippines
text: PCG aircraft spotted Chinese research vessel Xiang Yang Hong 33 near Iroquois Reef during a maritime patrol on May 6.

title: China vessel conducting ‘illegal’ research near gas-rich Reed bank, says Philippines
text: The PCG said the Chinese vessel was seen deploying a service boat towards the reef, confirming the ongoing “unauthorised” research operations.

title: China vessel conducting ‘illegal’ research near gas-rich Reed bank, says Philippines
text: One Chinese Coast Guard vessel and 13 Chinese maritime militia ships were also seen around the reef, it added.

title: China vessel conducting ‘illegal’ research near gas-rich Reed bank, says Philippines
text: It said the Chinese research vessel departed China on April 15 and conducted operations near Second Thomas Shoal, Sabina Shoal, Mischief Reef, and Jackson Atoll in recent weeks.

title: China vessel conducting ‘illegal’ research near gas-rich Reed bank, says Philippines
text: The PCG also monitored 28 Chinese maritime militia ships near Thitu island during the same maritime patrol.

title: China vessel conducting ‘illegal’ research near gas-rich Reed bank, says Philippines
text: The Philippines said China had no authorisation to conduct the marine research and called it a violation of Philippine sovereign rights as well as the United Nations Convention on the Law of the Sea.

title: China vessel conducting ‘illegal’ research near gas-rich Reed bank, says Philippines
text: Beijing’s Embassy in Manila did not immediately respond to a request for comment.

title: China vessel conducting ‘illegal’ research near gas-rich Reed bank, says Philippines
text: - Reuters

title: Lee Hsien Loong's Terengganu visit strengthens strategic ties
text: Lee Hsien Loong's Terengganu visit strengthens strategic ties

title: Lee Hsien Loong's Terengganu visit strengthens strategic ties
text: KUALA TERENGGANU: Singapore Senior Minister Lee Hsien Loong's working visit to Terengganu on Thursday (May 7) further strengthened strategic cooperation between the two sides in areas such as renewable energy and the economy.

title: Lee Hsien Loong's Terengganu visit strengthens strategic ties
text: In a statement on Thursday, the Terengganu state government said Lee and his delegation were briefed on the state's aspirations and sustainable development plans under the Terengganu Sejahtera Master Plan (PITAS 2030) during a Development Briefing Session at Wisma Darul Iman here.

title: Lee Hsien Loong's Terengganu visit strengthens strategic ties
text: Among the key topics discussed was Terengganu's renewable energy agenda, with the state highlighting its potential as a green energy hub through projects such as the Hybrid Hydro Floating Solar (HHFS) project at Tasik Kenyir, Malaysia's largest hybrid floating solar plant, with a full capacity of 2.5 gigawatts.

title: Lee Hsien Loong's Terengganu visit strengthens strategic ties
text: The state delegation, led by Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Ahmad Samsuri Mokhtar, also shared updates on economic development and investment opportunities in the silica industry and logistics infrastructure, including the East Coast Rail Link (ECRL) and Kemaman Port.

title: Lee Hsien Loong's Terengganu visit strengthens strategic ties
text: During the hour-long session, both sides also discussed strategies to strengthen tourism connectivity, with Singapore being Malaysia's largest source market for foreign tourists.

title: Lee Hsien Loong's Terengganu visit strengthens strategic ties
text: The Singapore delegation included Lee's wife, Ho Ching; Minister of State, Ministry of Foreign Affairs & Ministry of Social and Family Development, Zhulkarnain Abdul Rahim; and Minister for Law and Second Minister for Home Affairs Edwin Tong.

title: Lee Hsien Loong's Terengganu visit strengthens strategic ties
text: Also accompanying the delegation were Singapore Member of Parliament Joan Pereira, Singapore High Commissioner to Malaysia Vanu Gopala Menon and officials from Singapore's Ministry of Foreign Affairs.

title: Lee Hsien Loong's Terengganu visit strengthens strategic ties
text: Also present to welcome the delegation were Terengganu State Secretary Datuk Mohd Azmi Mohamad Daham, state government leaders and department heads.

title: Lee Hsien Loong's Terengganu visit strengthens strategic ties
text: Lee is currently on a five-day working visit to Pahang and Terengganu, which began on Monday and ends tomorrow.

title: Lee Hsien Loong's Terengganu visit strengthens strategic ties
text: – Bernama

title: Bursa Malaysia extends gains for fifth day; ringgit hits seven-week high
text: Bursa Malaysia extends gains for fifth day; ringgit hits seven-week high

title: Bursa Malaysia extends gains for fifth day; ringgit hits seven-week high
text: KUALA LUMPUR: Bursa Malaysia extended its gains for a fifth straight session on Thursday after Bank Negara kept its benchmark interest rate unchanged at 2.75%, in line with market expectations.

title: Bursa Malaysia extends gains for fifth day; ringgit hits seven-week high
text: Market optimism was also boosted by positive regional sentiment amid continued enthusiasm surrounding artificial intelligence (AI)-related stocks.

title: Bursa Malaysia extends gains for fifth day; ringgit hits seven-week high
text: At 5pm, the FBM KLCI rose 1.98 points, or 0.11%, to close at 1,758.85 after hitting an intraday high of 1,768.46 and a low of 1,758.42.

title: Bursa Malaysia extends gains for fifth day; ringgit hits seven-week high
text: There were 660 gainers, 583 losers and 593 counters traded unchanged on the Bursa Malaysia.

title: Bursa Malaysia extends gains for fifth day; ringgit hits seven-week high
text: Turnover stood at 3.8 billion shares valued at RM4.25bil.

title: Bursa Malaysia extends gains for fifth day; ringgit hits seven-week high
text: Dealers said sentiment was supported by Bank Negara’s decision to maintain the OPR at 2.75%, which provided clarity on the domestic monetary policy outlook.

title: Bursa Malaysia extends gains for fifth day; ringgit hits seven-week high
text: They added that continued optimism over AI-related stocks and stronger regional markets also lifted investor appetite across Asian equities.

title: Bursa Malaysia extends gains for fifth day; ringgit hits seven-week high
text: Among the gainers on Bursa Malaysia were Nestle, which surged RM3.20 to RM104.10, F&N, which rose 92 sen to RM30.02, Allianz, which climbed 62 sen to RM21.42, and UMS Integration, which gained 54 sen to RM7.37.

title: Bursa Malaysia extends gains for fifth day; ringgit hits seven-week high
text: On the losing side, PETRONAS Chemicals fell 59 sen to RM5.28, MISC slid 31 sen to RM8.37, Gas Malaysia dropped 20 sen to RM5.30, while Malaysian Pacific Industries declined 18 sen to RM41.22.

title: Bursa Malaysia extends gains for fifth day; ringgit hits seven-week high
text: On the forex market, the ringgit rose 0.41% to 3.9093 against the US dollar, its highest level in seven weeks.

title: Bursa Malaysia extends gains for fifth day; ringgit hits seven-week high
text: Year-to-date, the local currency has strengthened 3.88% against the greenback.

title: Bursa Malaysia extends gains for fifth day; ringgit hits seven-week high
text: The ringgit also gained against the Singapore dollar, rising 0.31% to 3.0870, its highest level in about six weeks.

title: Bursa Malaysia extends gains for fifth day; ringgit hits seven-week high
text: According to Bursa Malaysia data, foreign investors and local institutions were net buyers on Wednesday, purchasing RM123mil and RM209mil worth of equities, respectively, while retail investors were net sellers at RM332mil.

title: Bursa Malaysia extends gains for fifth day; ringgit hits seven-week high
text: Around the region, MSCI’s Asia ex-Japan stock index rose 1.76%, with most regional markets ending higher.

title: Bursa Malaysia extends gains for fifth day; ringgit hits seven-week high
text: China’s blue-chip CSI300 Index and the Shanghai Composite Index both gained 0.48% to close at 4,900.51 and 4,180.09, respectively.

title: Bursa Malaysia extends gains for fifth day; ringgit hits seven-week high
text: Hong Kong’s benchmark Hang Seng Index climbed 1.57% to 26,626.28.

title: Bursa Malaysia extends gains for fifth day; ringgit hits seven-week high
text: Japan’s Nikkei 225 Index surged 5.58% — its biggest gain in more than a year — to close at a record high of 62,833.84, while South Korea’s Kospi advanced 1.43% to 7,490.05.

title: Johor’s Coronation Square set to spur RM9bil in economic spin-offs, create 60,000 jobs, says MB
text: Johor’s Coronation Square set to spur RM9bil in economic spin-offs, create 60,000 jobs, says MB

title: Johor’s Coronation Square set to spur RM9bil in economic spin-offs, create 60,000 jobs, says MB
text: JOHOR BARU: Coronation Square is expected to generate more than RM9bil in economic spin-offs and create some 60,000 jobs, says Johor Mentri Besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi.

title: Johor’s Coronation Square set to spur RM9bil in economic spin-offs, create 60,000 jobs, says MB
text: Onn Hafiz said the outcomes of the landmark integrated project are fully aligned with the state government's Maju Johor 2030 vision.

title: Johor’s Coronation Square set to spur RM9bil in economic spin-offs, create 60,000 jobs, says MB
text: “What is particularly exciting about developments of this scale are the tangible economic benefits they bring to our state.

title: Johor’s Coronation Square set to spur RM9bil in economic spin-offs, create 60,000 jobs, says MB
text: “From construction to operations, Coronation Square will create employment opportunities across multiple sectors.

title: Johor’s Coronation Square set to spur RM9bil in economic spin-offs, create 60,000 jobs, says MB
text: “As I understand it, Coronation Square will generate more than RM9bil in economic spin-offs and create up to 60,000 jobs over the long term,” he said.

title: Johor’s Coronation Square set to spur RM9bil in economic spin-offs, create 60,000 jobs, says MB
text: Also present at the event were Johor Economic and Investment advisor Datuk Seri Hasni Mohammad and Singapore's Consul-General in Johor Baru Ng Kuan Khai.

title: Johor’s Coronation Square set to spur RM9bil in economic spin-offs, create 60,000 jobs, says MB
text: He said this in his speech during the groundbreaking ceremony of the Coronation Square Mall on Thursday (May 7).

title: Johor’s Coronation Square set to spur RM9bil in economic spin-offs, create 60,000 jobs, says MB
text: He said the Coronation Square project is vital for the three key roles it plays, including shaping and revitalising the Johor Baru city centre.

title: Johor’s Coronation Square set to spur RM9bil in economic spin-offs, create 60,000 jobs, says MB
text: “It also reinforces both our cross-border and public connectivity while having a significant and inclusive economic impact,” he said.

title: Johor’s Coronation Square set to spur RM9bil in economic spin-offs, create 60,000 jobs, says MB
text: The new retail mall, expected to be completed in 2029, is a key feature of Coronation Square, a landmark integrated development rising in the heart of the city’s Ibrahim International Business District (IIBD).

title: Johor’s Coronation Square set to spur RM9bil in economic spin-offs, create 60,000 jobs, says MB
text: The 1.25 million sq ft mall will anchor Coronation Square’s eight-tower masterplan, which includes premium offices, a fully sold residential component, a hospital and a hotel.

title: Asean power grid among top priorities at 48th Asean Summit
text: Asean power grid among top priorities at 48th Asean Summit

title: Asean power grid among top priorities at 48th Asean Summit
text: HANOI: The implementation of the Asean Power Grid (APG) has been identified as one of Asean’s key economic priorities in 2026 under the Philippines’ chairmanship, aiming to strengthen regional energy security and resilience against global uncertainties.

title: Asean power grid among top priorities at 48th Asean Summit
text: Speaking ahead of the 48th Asean Summit on Wednesday (May 6), Philippine Undersecretary of Trade Allan Gepty said the initiative is among the bloc’s “priority economic deliverables” that are timely, forward-looking and strategic for the region.

title: Asean power grid among top priorities at 48th Asean Summit
text: The memorandum of understanding on the Asean Power Grid is expected to promote cross-border electricity trade, enabling member states to share energy resources and stabilise power supply across the region.

title: Asean power grid among top priorities at 48th Asean Summit
text: The move is considered particularly important as South-East Asia faces mounting pressure from rising energy costs and potential supply disruptions caused by geopolitical tensions, especially conflicts in the Middle East.

title: Asean power grid among top priorities at 48th Asean Summit
text: Philippine officials stressed that stronger regional energy connectivity is essential to reducing risks and ensuring long-term stability.

title: Asean power grid among top priorities at 48th Asean Summit
text: Alongside the power grid initiative, Asean is also working to finalise the Asean-Canada Pre-Trade Agreement, which aims to expand trade networks and create more economic opportunities for member states.

title: Asean power grid among top priorities at 48th Asean Summit
text: Other priorities include promoting digital trade, attracting investment in green and high-growth sectors, and developing sustainable agriculture systems.

title: Asean power grid among top priorities at 48th Asean Summit
text: Gepty emphasised that amid growing global uncertainties, Asean's coordinated response is crucial to maintaining trade flows, supporting businesses and sustaining economic growth momentum.

title: Asean power grid among top priorities at 48th Asean Summit
text: This year’s summit, chaired by President Ferdinand Marcos Jr, is held under the theme “Navigating Our Future, Together”, focusing on strengthening economic resilience and advancing regional cooperation.

title: Asean power grid among top priorities at 48th Asean Summit
text: Asean leaders are scheduled to officially convene on Friday.

title: Asean power grid among top priorities at 48th Asean Summit
text: — Vietnam News/ANN

title: Hajiji: Semporna key catalyst for Sabah's Blue Economy agenda
text: Hajiji: Semporna key catalyst for Sabah's Blue Economy agenda

title: Hajiji: Semporna key catalyst for Sabah's Blue Economy agenda
text: KOTA KINABALU: Sabah's east coast Semporna district is set to be a key catalyst for the state's economic transformation through the Blue Economy agenda, says Chief Minister Datuk Seri Hajiji Noor.

title: Hajiji: Semporna key catalyst for Sabah's Blue Economy agenda
text: He said the district's extraordinary potential could generate large-scale economic spillover effects if managed strategically, benefiting not only the local community but also the entire state.

title: Hajiji: Semporna key catalyst for Sabah's Blue Economy agenda
text: "In other words, Semporna's success is Sabah's success," he said at a dinner held in conjunction with his working visit to the Semporna district as part of the Eastern Sabah Security Zone (EssZone) security inspection on Wednesday (May 6).

title: Hajiji: Semporna key catalyst for Sabah's Blue Economy agenda
text: He said Semporna has already taken the lead in several important initiatives that could serve as benchmarks, particularly in the high-impact and sustainable marine tourism sector.

title: Hajiji: Semporna key catalyst for Sabah's Blue Economy agenda
text: For example, he said Sipadan Island has successfully implemented a daily visitor quota system, which has now become a model for high-value and sustainable tourism management, ensuring a balance between environmental conservation and economic activities.

title: Hajiji: Semporna key catalyst for Sabah's Blue Economy agenda
text: "At the Tun Sakaran Marine Park, we are witnessing harmonious synergy between tourism, biodiversity conservation and community participation.

title: Hajiji: Semporna key catalyst for Sabah's Blue Economy agenda
text: "This is the foundation of the community-based tourism concept that we want to expand because it ensures that Semporna's natural wealth is returned to its people," he said.

title: Hajiji: Semporna key catalyst for Sabah's Blue Economy agenda
text: Hajiji said the state government is now intensifying efforts to transform the marine aquaculture sector to create a shift from raw material production to a value-added economy.

title: Hajiji: Semporna key catalyst for Sabah's Blue Economy agenda
text: He said among the government's main focuses are the expansion of integrated commercial-scale aquaculture zones, the introduction of more efficient and environmentally friendly modern sea cage technology, and strong support for seaweed entrepreneurs to increase productivity and ensure export-grade quality products.

title: Hajiji: Semporna key catalyst for Sabah's Blue Economy agenda
text: "At the same time, we are also working to attract high-impact investments in the downstream seafood processing sector so that the economic value chain can be comprehensively controlled," he said.

title: Hajiji: Semporna key catalyst for Sabah's Blue Economy agenda
text: Hajiji said to support the growth of the sector, the government is also formulating plans to upgrade critical supporting infrastructure in Semporna.

title: Hajiji: Semporna key catalyst for Sabah's Blue Economy agenda
text: These include modernisation of jetty facilities and logistics networks, the development of a comprehensive cold chain system to ensure seafood quality, broader access to international export markets, as well as the provision of more organised, safe and world-class public tourism facilities, he added.

title: Hajiji: Semporna key catalyst for Sabah's Blue Economy agenda
text: The chief minister also stressed that the major development agenda in Semporna would not become a reality without strong grassroots leadership and a high commitment to sustainability principles.

title: Sabah-Dalian pact anchors maritime and blue economy growth
text: Sabah-Dalian pact anchors maritime and blue economy growth

title: Sabah-Dalian pact anchors maritime and blue economy growth
text: KOTA KINABALU: Sabah has opened up trade, investment, industry, and Blue Economy cooperation with China’s Dalian province following the signing of two memorandums of understanding and three letters of intent (LOI).

title: Sabah-Dalian pact anchors maritime and blue economy growth
text: The Sabah-Dalian Trade Cooperation Mission led by Deputy Chief Minister III Datuk Ewon Benedick, witnessed the signing of the MoUs and LOIs that cover education, technology, maritime, and the Blue Economy sectors in Dalian on Wednesday (May 6).

title: Sabah-Dalian pact anchors maritime and blue economy growth
text: Among the MoUs signed were a collaboration between Dalian Maritime University (DMU) and Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS), and a technology partnership on functional drinking water conversion between Jetama and Dalian Bowen Software Technology Co. Ltd.

title: Sabah-Dalian pact anchors maritime and blue economy growth
text: The three LOIs involved friendship cooperation between the Dalian Municipal Federation of Industry and Commerce and the Sabah Maju Jaya (SMJ) Secretariat, collaboration between Dalian Ocean University and SMJ, and cooperation between Beijing Open University International Curriculum Centre and Sabah’s Education, Science and Innovation (KPSTI) Ministry.

title: Sabah-Dalian pact anchors maritime and blue economy growth
text: Ewon, who is state Industrial Development, Entrepreneurship and Transport Minister, witness the document exchanges alongside representatives of the Dalian government officials during the Dalian-Sabah Trade and Investment Partnership Session: Interactive Dialogue on the Blue Economy Sector.

title: Sabah-Dalian pact anchors maritime and blue economy growth
text: Speaking at the event, Ewon said the agreements reflect the strong friendship between Malaysia and China while opening wider opportunities for economic cooperation between Sabah and Dalian in trade, investment, industry, and the Blue Economy.

title: Sabah-Dalian pact anchors maritime and blue economy growth
text: “Sabah is strengthening its position as a regional hub for maritime trade, logistics, industrial development, and Blue Economy investments.

title: Sabah-Dalian pact anchors maritime and blue economy growth
text: Dalian, meanwhile, is renowned as a maritime, logistics, and industrial hub with strengths in port development, marine technology, shipbuilding, and coastal industry development,” he said.

title: Sabah-Dalian pact anchors maritime and blue economy growth
text: Ewon said Sabah’s focus on 15 key industries under the Blue Economy framework, includes aquaculture, maritime logistics, marine biotechnology, ocean energy technology, and ocean monitoring.

title: Sabah-Dalian pact anchors maritime and blue economy growth
text: He said ongoing projects such as the Kudat Blue Economy Industrial Park, a desalination project at POIC Lahad Datu, and floating solar facilities at the Babagon Dam.

title: Sabah-Dalian pact anchors maritime and blue economy growth
text: He welcomed strategic cooperation with partners from China, particularly Dalian, in technology transfer, knowledge sharing, capacity building, and joint ventures in high-impact sectors.

title: Sabah-Dalian pact anchors maritime and blue economy growth
text: He also disclosed that Sabah recorded manufacturing sector investments totalling RM7.6bil in 2025, an increase of 208% compared to RM2.47bil the previous year.

title: Sabah-Dalian pact anchors maritime and blue economy growth
text: Of this, he said RM6.6bil was foreign direct investment (FDI), while RM1bil was domestic investment.

title: Economic growth means little if benefits do not reach the rakyat, says Perak MB
text: Economic growth means little if benefits do not reach the rakyat, says Perak MB

title: Economic growth means little if benefits do not reach the rakyat, says Perak MB
text: IPOH: Economic growth, investment and achievement figures are important, but the real test of any policy is whether its benefits are truly felt by the rakyat, says Perak Mentri Besar Datuk Seri Saarani Mohamad.

title: Economic growth means little if benefits do not reach the rakyat, says Perak MB
text: “We may record good growth on paper, but if the benefits do not reach the people comprehensively, then a gap will exist between what we are proud of as a government and what the people actually experience,” he said during his speech at the opening of the Perak policy and research conference held at a hotel here Thursday (May 7).

title: Economic growth means little if benefits do not reach the rakyat, says Perak MB
text: Saarani said this was why the Perak government adopted the theme “Hasil Dirasai, Keyakinan Dibina” for the conference, which he described as a more honest and grounded approach to governance.

title: Economic growth means little if benefits do not reach the rakyat, says Perak MB
text: He said the theme emerged from constructive discussions during the state leadership retreat earlier this year and reflected the administration’s intention to measure success based on real impact rather than implementation or spending alone.

title: Economic growth means little if benefits do not reach the rakyat, says Perak MB
text: “When a parent breathes a sigh of relief because their child has secured a job, that is a result.

title: Economic growth means little if benefits do not reach the rakyat, says Perak MB
text:
“When young people can see a future in Perak without having to migrate elsewhere, that is a result,”

“When small traders are no longer burdened by crippling costs, that is a result,” he said.

title: Economic growth means little if benefits do not reach the rakyat, says Perak MB
text: He added that easing the burden on small traders and improving the efficiency and friendliness of government services were also among the outcomes the state government aimed to achieve.

title: Economic growth means little if benefits do not reach the rakyat, says Perak MB
text: Saarani said public confidence could not be built merely through announcements, but through the people’s actual experiences with government policies and services.

title: Economic growth means little if benefits do not reach the rakyat, says Perak MB
text: He said the state government must move beyond relying solely on economic figures and place greater emphasis on the people’s wellbeing.

title: Economic growth means little if benefits do not reach the rakyat, says Perak MB
text: “Although Perak’s economy showed stronger growth, with the state’s GDP rising from 2.7%in 2023 to 4.4% in 2024 and its economic value reaching RM86.2bil, such achievements alone are insufficient.

title: Economic growth means little if benefits do not reach the rakyat, says Perak MB
text: “Prosperity from an administrative perspective could be measured through investments, revenue and growth, but wellbeing for the rakyat is about feeling safe, valued and not marginalized,” he said.

title: AirTrunk expands footprint
text: AirTrunk expands footprint

title: AirTrunk expands footprint
text:
Data centres expected to benefit local economy

ISKANDAR PUTERI: The expansion of AirTrunk in Johor will bring more economic and employment opportunities for locals, says Lee Ting Han.

title: AirTrunk expands footprint
text: The Johor investment, trade, consumer affairs and human resources committee chairman said the expansion involves two new data centre projects in the SiLC industrial park, known as JHB 3 and JHB 4, covering a combined land area of 27ha.

title: AirTrunk expands footprint
text: “AirTrunk already has two existing projects, JHB 1 and JHB 2, which have been completed and are operational.

title: AirTrunk expands footprint
text:
ALSO READ: Locals hopeful Johor investment gains will be felt by all

“The new projects will have a total electricity capacity of about 380 megawatts, and both have received approval of the state executive council on April 15.

title: AirTrunk expands footprint
text: "The next step is to begin construction,” he said in an interview yesterday.

title: AirTrunk expands footprint
text: He added that AirTrunk’s investment is one of the largest in Johor in this sector.

title: AirTrunk expands footprint
text:
ALSO READ: Setting up to benefit from future investments

Lee noted that opportunities for locals are currently concentrated in construction and supply chains, including building materials, cabling and systems infrastructure.

title: AirTrunk expands footprint
text: While some foreign companies are involved, many have established local entities, allowing Malaysians to participate in the ecosystem, he said.

title: AirTrunk expands footprint
text: “Core components such as servers are still largely imported but local companies are actively involved in supplying supporting infrastructure,” he added.

title: AirTrunk expands footprint
text: On job creation, he said that for JHB 3 and JHB 4, more than 100 direct jobs are expected to be created, with up to 1,000 jobs generated locally across related sectors.

title: AirTrunk expands footprint
text: Lee also said more than 80% to 90% of data centre jobs in Johor are filled by Malaysians.

title: AirTrunk expands footprint
text: Addressing concerns over the environmental impact of data centres, Lee said the state government is closely managing key issues, such as electricity, water and talent development.

title: AirTrunk expands footprint
text: He said the state had received applications for 65 data centre projects, of which 20 are operational and eight are under construction, and 37 are in various stages of application.

title: AirTrunk expands footprint
text: “Data centres do not use their full capacity immediately.

title: AirTrunk expands footprint
text: "It typically takes five to 10 years to ramp up, and we plan electricity supply accordingly to avoid affecting local communities and other industries,” he added.

title: AirTrunk expands footprint
text: The state also plans to rely on treated wastewater to reduce dependence on the treated water supply.

title: AirTrunk expands footprint
text: He emphasised that data centres should be viewed beyond immediate investment returns, describing them as critical infrastructure for the nation’s digital economy.

title: AirTrunk expands footprint
text: He stated that Johor intends to emulate successful global models, such as those found in Dublin, Ireland, and Virginia in the United States, where investments have successfully fostered wider technology ecosystems.

title: AirTrunk expands footprint
text: “At present, many tech firms are based in Kuala Lumpur and Singapore, but with strong infrastructure, we hope to attract them to Johor in the future,” he said.

title: AirTrunk expands footprint
text: Lee added that the state is currently finalising a data centre guideline framework covering electricity, water, talent development and local industry participation.

title: AirTrunk expands footprint
