text: He said the state’s long-term goal is to move up the value chain and not remain merely a data hosting location.

title: Keeping M’sia ahead of the game
text: Keeping M’sia ahead of the game

title: Keeping M’sia ahead of the game
text:
Sim: Backed by three factors, the country is well placed to navigate global uncertainties

PETALING JAYA: Malaysia’s com­petitiveness rests on three key factors – timing in relation to global conditions, its strategic geographical and economic position, and its diverse, skilled workforce, says Steven Sim.

title: Keeping M’sia ahead of the game
text: Drawing on principles from The Art of War, the Entrepreneur Development and Cooperatives Minister said success in uncertain times comes down to “tian shi, di li, ren he” (timing, geographical advantage and human unity).

title: Keeping M’sia ahead of the game
text: He said these strengths put Malaysia in a better position to navigate ongoing global uncertainties, including geopolitical tensions and economic volatility, while continuing to support business growth and innovation.

title: Keeping M’sia ahead of the game
text: “The crisis is not unique to Malaysia... it is a global one ­driven by geopolitics and wider uncertainties.

title: Keeping M’sia ahead of the game
text: “However, this does not mean we lack the opportunity or capability to move forward,” he said in his speech during the Bisnes Sinar Harian Awards (Bisa) 2026 yesterday.

title: Keeping M’sia ahead of the game
text: Sim said the country must continue strengthening its support for entrepreneurs, particularly small and medium enterprises (SMEs), which are increasingly demonstrating strong growth potential.

title: Keeping M’sia ahead of the game
text: He cited the example of a nasi lemak entrepreneur, Saleha, who started from a roadside stall selling a few hundred packets a day and has now scaled up to around 15,000 packets daily, with plans to reach 30,000 packets.

title: Keeping M’sia ahead of the game
text: “I’ve told them the ministry will help them achieve that target.

title: Keeping M’sia ahead of the game
text: “The question is how we can help them achieve that next stage of growth.

title: Keeping M’sia ahead of the game
text: That is where the ministry plays its role,” he said.

title: Keeping M’sia ahead of the game
text: Sim also said SMEs should not be confined to traditional or low- value industries.

title: Keeping M’sia ahead of the game
text: “It is not enough to say SMEs are only strong in products such as keropok lekor or snacks.

title: Keeping M’sia ahead of the game
text: We should also be asking why Malay­sian entrepreneurs cannot move into higher-value sectors such as semiconductors,” he added.

title: Keeping M’sia ahead of the game
text: Sim also highlighted innovation in the oil and gas-related sector, where a Malaysian company has developed palm oil-based lubricants as part of its efforts to advance green technology and industrial upgrading.

title: Keeping M’sia ahead of the game
text: “This shows that Malaysian entrepreneurs are not only scaling up but also innovating at a technological level,” he said.

title: Keeping M’sia ahead of the game
text: “At the same time, the ministry is committed to helping these SMEs grow, even during the current global crisis,” he said.

title: Keeping M’sia ahead of the game
text: Meanwhile, Karangkraf Media Group chairman Firdaus Hussa­muddin said the event is more than just a ceremony, describing it as a recognition of the resilience and determination of Malaysian entrepreneurs operating in a challenging economic environment.

title: Keeping M’sia ahead of the game
text: She said the awards honour business owners who continue to grow despite uncertainty while also providing visibility for SMEs which form the backbone of the economy.

title: Keeping M’sia ahead of the game
text: “We honour the courage of entrepreneurs who persist in difficult conditions.

title: Keeping M’sia ahead of the game
text: “Sinar Harian stands alongside them as a consistent voice, reflecting the realities and aspirations of the people, including entrepreneurs across the country,” she said.

title: Keeping M’sia ahead of the game
text: Firdaus added that success should not be measured solely by profit or scale but also by inte­grity, social impact and contri­bution to nation-building, adding that these values are essential in building a sustainable business ecosystem.

title: Moody’s names Thailand among most resilient emerging markets
text: Moody’s names Thailand among most resilient emerging markets

title: Moody’s names Thailand among most resilient emerging markets
text: BANGKOK: Moody’s Ratings has released its latest analytical article, saying several large emerging markets, including Thailand, have become better able to withstand global economic shocks over the past five years without suffering a sharp jump in risk premiums or losing market access, as seen in previous crises.

title: Moody’s names Thailand among most resilient emerging markets
text: Thailand was cited as one of five large emerging-market economies — alongside Malaysia, India, Indonesia and Mexico — that have shown greater resilience to global shocks over the past five years, despite facing the Covid-19 crisis, the global interest-rate tightening cycle, banking-sector stress and trade tensions.

title: Moody’s names Thailand among most resilient emerging markets
text:
Policy reforms help emerging markets absorb shocks

The rating agency explained that many emerging economies have continued to face volatility in bond yields and exchange rates in line with the global interest-rate cycle.

title: Moody’s names Thailand among most resilient emerging markets
text: However, credit risk spreads, or risk premiums, have not surged as severely as in past crises.

title: Moody’s names Thailand among most resilient emerging markets
text: This reflected continued market confidence in the policy frameworks and macroeconomic management capacity of these countries.

title: Moody’s names Thailand among most resilient emerging markets
text: The report divided emerging economies into three broad groups: highly resilient, conditionally resilient and vulnerable.

title: Moody’s names Thailand among most resilient emerging markets
text: India, Thailand, Malaysia, Indonesia and Mexico were placed in the best-performing group, supported by early policy reforms, particularly in inflation-targeting frameworks, exchange-rate flexibility, debt management and the development of local-currency financial markets.

title: Moody’s names Thailand among most resilient emerging markets
text: These factors have allowed the countries to absorb shocks through market mechanisms without the pressure escalating into a credit or funding crisis.

title: Moody’s names Thailand among most resilient emerging markets
text: Moody’s said the performance of the five emerging markets was reflected in four key market indicators: volatility in sovereign bond risk spreads; volatility in bond-yield differentials against the United States; exchange-rate depreciation; and volatility in local-currency bond yields.

title: Moody’s names Thailand among most resilient emerging markets
text: By contrast, Turkey, Argentina and Nigeria were placed among the most vulnerable economies, facing high market pressure due to policy constraints, inflation and external risks.

title: Moody’s names Thailand among most resilient emerging markets
text:
Thailand among top five on early reforms and strong buffers

Moody’s cited Thailand and India as examples of countries with “structural resilience”, placing them among the economies seen as best prepared to deal with future global shocks.

title: Moody’s names Thailand among most resilient emerging markets
text: A key factor was the early implementation of important policies that supported stability before later volatility emerged.

title: Moody’s names Thailand among most resilient emerging markets
text: These countries also benefited from clear and predictable monetary policy frameworks, inflation remaining within expected ranges, and exchange rates that could adjust flexibly to market conditions.

title: Moody’s names Thailand among most resilient emerging markets
text: This flexibility helped reduce the risk that currency volatility would spill over into prolonged inflationary pressure or force abrupt policy changes.

title: Moody’s names Thailand among most resilient emerging markets
text: Both countries also stood out for having strong and accessible buffers.

title: Moody’s names Thailand among most resilient emerging markets
text: India relies on domestic funding sources, supported by deep domestic markets and large reserves, while Thailand benefits from external strength, backed by a solid long-term balance-of-payments position and low external debt.

title: Moody’s names Thailand among most resilient emerging markets
text: However, Moody’s warned that India’s relatively high debt burden and weak fiscal balance limited its room to respond to continued shocks.

title: Moody’s names Thailand among most resilient emerging markets
text: For Thailand, the rising debt burden risks weakening the country’s ability to withstand crises over the longer term.

title: Moody’s names Thailand among most resilient emerging markets
text: - The Nation/ANN

title: Gold jumps over 2% as Middle East peace hopes send oil, dollar lower

text: Gold prices climbed more than 2% on Wednesday after U.S. President Donald Trump indicated a possible peace deal may be reached with Iran, sending the dollar and crude lower as inflation concerns ebbed somewhat.

text: Spot gold jumped 2.4% to $4,667.39 per ounce, as of 0632 GMT, having hit its highest since April 28.

title: Gold jumps over 2% as Middle East peace hopes send oil, dollar lower
text: U.S. gold futures for June delivery rose 2.4% to $4,678.20.

title: Gold jumps over 2% as Middle East peace hopes send oil, dollar lower

title: Gold jumps over 2% as Middle East peace hopes send oil, dollar lower

text: A weaker U.S. currency makes dollar-priced metals cheaper for holders of other currencies.

]USD/]

Elevated crude oil prices can stoke inflation, increasing the likelihood of higher interest rates.

title: Gold jumps over 2% as Middle East peace hopes send oil, dollar lower

text: MACC to propose charging two involved in RM1.1bil semiconductor deal, says Azam

text: PUTRAJAYA: The Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission has proposed bringing charges against two individuals over an investigation into a RM1.1bil semiconductor investment deal involving the Economy Ministry and a UK-based firm, says Tan Sri Azam Baki.

title: MACC to propose charging two involved in RM1.1bil semiconductor deal, says Azam
text: However, the MACC chief commissioner declined to reveal the identities of the two individuals.

title: MACC to propose charging two involved in RM1.1bil semiconductor deal, says Azam
text: He said the investigation papers are nearing completion and will be submitted to the deputy public prosecutor soon.

title: MACC to propose charging two involved in RM1.1bil semiconductor deal, says Azam
text: "We have recorded statements from 22 witnesses, including political analyst James Chai and former economy minister Datuk Seri Rafizi Ramli.

title: MACC to propose charging two involved in RM1.1bil semiconductor deal, says Azam
text: "Investigations are almost at the final stage.

title: MACC to propose charging two involved in RM1.1bil semiconductor deal, says Azam
text: At this point, there are only two more witnesses we need statements from.

title: MACC to propose charging two involved in RM1.1bil semiconductor deal, says Azam
text: One is in Taiwan and another is in the country," he said at a press conference here on Wednesday (May 6).

title: MACC to propose charging two involved in RM1.1bil semiconductor deal, says Azam
text: Azam said the probe is being conducted under Section 23 of the MACC Act 2009 for abuse of power, Section 420 of the Penal Code for cheating and Section 409 of the Penal Code for criminal breach of trust.

title: MACC to propose charging two involved in RM1.1bil semiconductor deal, says Azam
text: "We expect the investigation papers to be submitted to the deputy public prosecutor in the near future.

title: MACC to propose charging two involved in RM1.1bil semiconductor deal, says Azam
text: It will then be up to the Attorney General's Chambers to decide whether there is a case to prosecute," he said.

title: MACC to propose charging two involved in RM1.1bil semiconductor deal, says Azam
text: Azam said investigators had identified two individuals they would recommend for prosecution.

title: MACC to propose charging two involved in RM1.1bil semiconductor deal, says Azam
text: "We have identified two individuals whom we will propose to be charged in court.

title: MACC to propose charging two involved in RM1.1bil semiconductor deal, says Azam
text: "I am not saying they will definitely be charged.

title: MACC to propose charging two involved in RM1.1bil semiconductor deal, says Azam
text: Investigators can recommend charges if the evidence suffices but the final decision rests with the Attorney General,.

title: MACC to propose charging two involved in RM1.1bil semiconductor deal, says Azam
text: "I am not going to mention any names.

title: MACC to propose charging two involved in RM1.1bil semiconductor deal, says Azam
text: Whoever wants to speculate, that is up to them.

title: MACC to propose charging two involved in RM1.1bil semiconductor deal, says Azam
text: We do not," he said.

title: Masidi backs push to position Sabah as digital economy hub
text: Masidi backs push to position Sabah as digital economy hub

title: Masidi backs push to position Sabah as digital economy hub
text: PETALING JAYA: The Malaysia Digital Chamber of Commerce (MDCC) has briefed Sabah Deputy Chief Minister Datuk Seri Panglima Masidi Manjun on plans to host the 10th Malaysia Digital Economy Forum (MyDEF 10.0) in Kota Kinabalu, Sabah on Aug 11.

title: Masidi backs push to position Sabah as digital economy hub
text: The chamber said the forum is proposed as a national platform to accelerate Malaysia’s digital transformation, with Sabah positioned as a key driver for the digital economy in East Malaysia.

title: Masidi backs push to position Sabah as digital economy hub
text: The MDCC delegation was led by its emeritus chairman Dr Chris Daniel Wong and organising chairman Alvis Loo, accompanied by Sabah chapter chairman Datuk Seri David Sew and adviser Tony Hsing.

title: Masidi backs push to position Sabah as digital economy hub
text: During the courtesy visit on Tuesday (May 5), discussions centred on positioning Sabah as a digital economy hub by leveraging its strategic location and improving infrastructure to attract regional investments and technology partnerships.

title: Masidi backs push to position Sabah as digital economy hub
text: Both sides also explored alignment with the Sabah Maju Jaya (SMJ) 2.0 agenda, particularly in advancing digitalisation, innovation and inclusive growth.

title: Masidi backs push to position Sabah as digital economy hub
text: Other focus areas included developing digital talent and empowering youth through greater participation of local startups and small and medium enterprises (SMEs), as well as strengthening the investment and innovation ecosystem by connecting investors, venture capital firms and government agencies.

title: Masidi backs push to position Sabah as digital economy hub
text: The forum is expected to cover key themes such as artificial intelligence, cybersecurity, digital tourism, SME digitalisation and the blue economy.

title: Masidi backs push to position Sabah as digital economy hub
text: Masidi welcomed the initiative and expressed support for efforts to position Sabah as a competitive digital economy hub, stressing the need for strong collaboration between the public and private sectors.

title: Masidi backs push to position Sabah as digital economy hub
text: Meanwhile, Loo said MyDEF 10.0 aims to bring together policymakers, industry leaders and innovators to drive tangible outcomes for the digital economy.

title: Masidi backs push to position Sabah as digital economy hub
text: The visit marks a step towards closer collaboration between MDCC and the Sabah government ahead of the proposed forum.

title: Global finance leaders flag shifting capital flows, AI impact at Milken
text: Global finance leaders flag shifting capital flows, AI impact at Milken

title: Global finance leaders flag shifting capital flows, AI impact at Milken
text: May 4 (Reuters) - Top global investors and executives gathered at the Milken Institute Global ⁠Conference in Beverly Hills on Monday to discuss geopolitical tensions, private credit risks and opportunities, shifting capital flows and the economic impact of artificial intelligence.

title: Global finance leaders flag shifting capital flows, AI impact at Milken
text:
Here is what they had to say:

JONATHAN GRAY, ⁠PRESIDENT AND CHIEF OPERATING OFFICER, BLACKSTONE

"Now we have this war, which the UAE has done an extraordinary job navigating.

title: Global finance leaders flag shifting capital flows, AI impact at Milken
text: Through each of those crises, the people in this room, all ‌of us, would have said, 'Oh, I'm nervous.

title: Global finance leaders flag shifting capital flows, AI impact at Milken
text: What's going to happen?'

title: Global finance leaders flag shifting capital flows, AI impact at Milken
text: And yet the US economy and the global economy powered through, the markets powered through and my expectation is that will continue."

title: Global finance leaders flag shifting capital flows, AI impact at Milken
text:
JIM ZELTER, PRESIDENT, APOLLO GLOBAL MANAGEMENT

"It's going to be a variety of private credit conversations.

title: Global finance leaders flag shifting capital flows, AI impact at Milken
text: And so, for a few of us on the stage who have been asked about the private credit issues the last six to eight months, last eight weeks or whatever, it's such a small area of the BDCs (Business Development Companies).

title: Global finance leaders flag shifting capital flows, AI impact at Milken
text: "It's really missing the big ​plot.

title: Global finance leaders flag shifting capital flows, AI impact at Milken
text: The big plot is the ocean of private capital in aggregate.

title: Global finance leaders flag shifting capital flows, AI impact at Milken
text: And if we ask ourselves, there's a massive IPO pipeline.

title: Global finance leaders flag shifting capital flows, AI impact at Milken
text: "In ⁠the past, you went public because you needed access to capital.

title: Global finance leaders flag shifting capital flows, AI impact at Milken
text: Well, the last ⁠four or five years have shown us that that's not the case anymore."

title: Global finance leaders flag shifting capital flows, AI impact at Milken
text:
WALEED AL MOKARRAB AL MUHAIRI, DEPUTY GROUP CEO, MUBADALA

"Private credit is really interesting for the reason that it is filling a market need.

title: Global finance leaders flag shifting capital flows, AI impact at Milken
text: ⁠Now, ‌it may be that you have to design your portfolio a little bit differently.

title: Global finance leaders flag shifting capital flows, AI impact at Milken
text: It may be that you want to look at resiliency, but it is inexorable that this asset class is going to grow and that it is doing okay as long as you're a good picker."

title: Global finance leaders flag shifting capital flows, AI impact at Milken
text:
RON O'HANLEY, CEO, STATE STREET

"The Iran war, and what that is triggering now, I believe will be a big realignment of capital flows.

title: Global finance leaders flag shifting capital flows, AI impact at Milken
text: ⁠There's $3.2 trillion that the Gulf states and the various sovereign wealth funds have deployed now, and that has been ​an enormous export of capital to lots of people in this room, ‌really, all over the world."

title: Global finance leaders flag shifting capital flows, AI impact at Milken
text:
HARVEY SCHWARTZ, CEO, CARLYLE

"I think the real win (from AI) is when you see companies delivering better outcomes, innovating faster and actually being more productive.

title: Global finance leaders flag shifting capital flows, AI impact at Milken
text: So I'm a buyer ⁠of the productivity story.

title: Global finance leaders flag shifting capital flows, AI impact at Milken
text: "I'm not a ​buyer of the, you know, we have massive unemployment.

title: Global finance leaders flag shifting capital flows, AI impact at Milken
text: I just, I'm not an advocate of that scenario.

title: Global finance leaders flag shifting capital flows, AI impact at Milken
text: "I think there are a number of concerns that I would describe as important but not systemic that are being conflated.

title: Global finance leaders flag shifting capital flows, AI impact at Milken
text: And now, there's this narrative around systemic out there, and I do not think that is right.

title: Global finance leaders flag shifting capital flows, AI impact at Milken
text: I think that we are probably seeing a move into a different part of the credit cycle.

title: Global finance leaders flag shifting capital flows, AI impact at Milken
text: I would argue that that is healthy.

title: Global finance leaders flag shifting capital flows, AI impact at Milken
text: "If you go back to 2008, why ⁠the system suffered was that it was banks that were at the center.

title: Global finance leaders flag shifting capital flows, AI impact at Milken
text: Banks are concentrators of risk.

title: Global finance leaders flag shifting capital flows, AI impact at Milken
text: All ​that risk was concentrated and a financial crisis turned into an economic crisis.

title: Global finance leaders flag shifting capital flows, AI impact at Milken
text: Private credit is exactly the opposite, it is a distributor of risk."

title: Global finance leaders flag shifting capital flows, AI impact at Milken
text:
MARCIE FROST, CEO, CALPERS

"I think (AI is) wonderful, wonderful technology, but it will disrupt these entry-level positions.

title: Global finance leaders flag shifting capital flows, AI impact at Milken
text: And then, are there really retraining programs for the gig economy?

title: Global finance leaders flag shifting capital flows, AI impact at Milken
text: Probably not.

title: Global finance leaders flag shifting capital flows, AI impact at Milken
text: They would have to do that on their own as independent contractors.

title: Global finance leaders flag shifting capital flows, AI impact at Milken
text: "I think some of the more recent headlines around software and exposure in the ⁠debt markets were partially related to retail investors having access.

title: Global finance leaders flag shifting capital flows, AI impact at Milken
text: And were these instruments really set up for the liquidity requirements that would have been necessary, I would have the same concerns about retail investors accessing private equity in the same way."

title: Global finance leaders flag shifting capital flows, AI impact at Milken
text:
DANIEL SIMKOWITZ, CO-PRESIDENT, MORGAN STANLEY

"I would say we think the noise is overdone in private credit, but that will create an opportunity.

title: Global finance leaders flag shifting capital flows, AI impact at Milken
text: There is an M&A wave coming.

title: Global finance leaders flag shifting capital flows, AI impact at Milken
text: The financing of that M&A wave, especially given some of the noise, is going to enable some alpha to be generated by being the financier into that M&A market."

title: Global finance leaders flag shifting capital flows, AI impact at Milken
text:
OSCAR FAHLGREN, CHIEF INVESTMENT OFFICER, MUBADALA CAPITAL

"We're undoubtedly standing just in front of the worst energy ​crisis we have seen in living memory.

title: Global finance leaders flag shifting capital flows, AI impact at Milken
text: We have not felt the effects of the closure of the Strait of Hormuz on the energy markets quite ⁠yet.

title: Global finance leaders flag shifting capital flows, AI impact at Milken
text: It is hard to see what will happen to the real economy once that comes through."

title: Global finance leaders flag shifting capital flows, AI impact at Milken
text:
FREDERICK POLLOCK, CHIEF INVESTMENT OFFICER, GCM GROSVENOR

"There's no systematic overvaluation or undervaluation in the credit markets today.

title: Global finance leaders flag shifting capital flows, AI impact at Milken
text: You have pockets where the values ​are wrong, but that's always true, right?

title: Global finance leaders flag shifting capital flows, AI impact at Milken
text: "Meaning things get stale, they are actually undervalued.

title: Global finance leaders flag shifting capital flows, AI impact at Milken
text: There is an opportunity to purchase or there is ‌an AI disruption.

title: Global finance leaders flag shifting capital flows, AI impact at Milken
text: There is a tech disruption.

title: Global finance leaders flag shifting capital flows, AI impact at Milken
text: Things that people thought safe, say software lending, is ​not really safe.

title: Global finance leaders flag shifting capital flows, AI impact at Milken
text: And there needs to be an appropriate revaluation of those assets."

title: Global finance leaders flag shifting capital flows, AI impact at Milken
text:
JOHN VIBERT, HEAD OF CREDIT, PGIM

"The market continues to be priced closer to perfection and seems to be looking through a lot of the geopolitical risks that are out there.

title: Global finance leaders flag shifting capital flows, AI impact at Milken
text: A lot of the geopolitical risk is not yet priced in."

title: Global finance leaders flag shifting capital flows, AI impact at Milken
text: (Reporting by Pritam Biswas and Prakhar Srivastava in Bengaluru; Editing by Jonathan Ananda)

title: Malaysia must capitalise on West Asia crisis, says Fernandes
text: Malaysia must capitalise on West Asia crisis, says Fernandes

title: Malaysia must capitalise on West Asia crisis, says Fernandes
text: PUTRAJAYA: Malaysia should seize the ongoing West Asia crisis as an opportunity to rethink its economic model and enhance its long-term growth, says Capital A Bhd chief executive officer Tan Sri Tony Fernandes.

title: Malaysia must capitalise on West Asia crisis, says Fernandes
text: He said global supply disruptions, particularly involving key routes, such as the Strait of Hormuz, could have prolonged economic effects on the country, underscoring the need for Malaysia to remain resilient.

title: Malaysia must capitalise on West Asia crisis, says Fernandes
text: “Malaysia has done well diplomatically, however, this crisis is an opportunity to rethink our economic model, from subsidies to energy to productivity,” he said at the “Malaysia’s Strategic Position in a Turbulent Global Economy” forum yesterday.

title: Malaysia must capitalise on West Asia crisis, says Fernandes
text: Fernandes said that to achieve and maintain growth, the country must make difficult decisions, especially regarding the re-evaluation of subsidies.

title: Malaysia must capitalise on West Asia crisis, says Fernandes
text: “We need to move away from this subsidy mentality.

title: Malaysia must capitalise on West Asia crisis, says Fernandes
text: The billions spent on subsidies could be redirected into more productive industries, industries that create jobs, drive growth, and strengthen the economy,” he said.

title: Malaysia must capitalise on West Asia crisis, says Fernandes
text: Fernandes also said the current situation presents an opportunity to reassess energy security.

title: Malaysia must capitalise on West Asia crisis, says Fernandes
text: “I am a strong advocate for nuclear energy; the safety concerns are outdated, technologies like small modular reactors are much safer, and Malaysia should aim for energy independence,” he said.

title: Malaysia must capitalise on West Asia crisis, says Fernandes
text: On the aviation sector, Fernandes said the current crisis, driven by surging energy prices and supply disruptions, is more severe than the Covid-19 pandemic.

title: Malaysia must capitalise on West Asia crisis, says Fernandes
text: “During Covid, there was an understanding that you could not fly.

title: Malaysia must capitalise on West Asia crisis, says Fernandes
text: “In this instance, we are flying, and everyone wants to be paid on time, while oil prices have literally doubled overnight,” he said.

title: Malaysia must capitalise on West Asia crisis, says Fernandes
text: Fernandes added said that airlines are increasingly facing significant cost pressures after jet fuel prices rose sharply from about US$85 (RM336) per barrel to between US$180 and US$240.

title: Malaysia must capitalise on West Asia crisis, says Fernandes
text: “We went into Hari Raya having sold tickets at a jet fuel price of US$85, and then we woke up one day and jet fuel had gone up to US$180 and peaked at US$240.

title: Malaysia must capitalise on West Asia crisis, says Fernandes
text: In my time, we have never seen anything like it,” he said.

title: Malaysia must capitalise on West Asia crisis, says Fernandes
text: Fernandes added that refining margins also surged from about US$20 to US$120 within a week, compounding the impact on airlines.

title: Malaysia must capitalise on West Asia crisis, says Fernandes
text: “I have been talking to the government about extending credit so that we can catch up, because obviously we have to increase fares, but that takes time to reflect the oil costs we have already paid for,” he said.

title: Malaysia must capitalise on West Asia crisis, says Fernandes
text: — Bernama

text: KUALA LUMPUR: RHB Bank Bhd’s leading indicator suggests there is no evidence of a slowdown in Malaysia’s Gross Domestic Product (GDP) growth in the second quarter of this year (2Q 2026) despite heightened geopolitical premiums.

title: RHB indicator points to steady 2Q26 GDP growth, no signs of slowdown
text: Group chief economist and head of market research Barnabas Gan said the approach also suggested a 5.3 per cent year-on-year GDP growth in 2Q 2026, in line with 1Q 2026’s clip.

title: RHB indicator points to steady 2Q26 GDP growth, no signs of slowdown
text: "We keep our outlook for Malaysia’s annual GDP growth at 4.7 per cent in 2026, albeit a strong 2Q 2026 growth clip, if materialised, would suggest upside bias towards 5.0 per cent,” he said in a research note today.

title: RHB indicator points to steady 2Q26 GDP growth, no signs of slowdown
text: Gan added that the index has guided us in formulating RHB forward-looking views on GDP momentum for Malaysia and has accurately predicted the past crises, including the dot.com bubble bust, global financial crisis of 2008, and COVID-19, relative pickup in growth momentum in second half of 2023, and pace at which it will accelerate post the slowdown.

title: RHB indicator points to steady 2Q26 GDP growth, no signs of slowdown
text: "The construction of this index is underpinned by our in-house machine-learning capabilities, enabled by big data and statistical expertise, whereby the index statistically leads GDP growth by two quarters,” he added.

title: For foreign workers in the Mideast, risk from the Iran war collides with economic strain at home
text: For foreign workers in the Mideast, risk from the Iran war collides with economic strain at home

title: For foreign workers in the Mideast, risk from the Iran war collides with economic strain at home
text: SOUTH-EAST ASIA (AP): He had met his 6-year-old son only once.

title: For foreign workers in the Mideast, risk from the Iran war collides with economic strain at home
text: A few days together in a life otherwise spent apart.

title: For foreign workers in the Mideast, risk from the Iran war collides with economic strain at home
text: For 15 years, Mohammad Abdullah Al Mamun worked in Saudi Arabia, sending money home to his family in one of the poorest areas of Bangladesh.

title: For foreign workers in the Mideast, risk from the Iran war collides with economic strain at home
text: This year, he had planned to return, build a larger house with his savings and spend time with the child he barely knew.

title: For foreign workers in the Mideast, risk from the Iran war collides with economic strain at home
text: Then, on March 8, a missile struck his workers’ camp.

title: For foreign workers in the Mideast, risk from the Iran war collides with economic strain at home
text: He suffered severe burns and later died.

title: For foreign workers in the Mideast, risk from the Iran war collides with economic strain at home
text: He was among more than two dozen foreign workers killed across the Mideast after the United States and Israel went to war with Iran in February.

title: For foreign workers in the Mideast, risk from the Iran war collides with economic strain at home
text: Tens of millions of foreign workers have helped build the Gulf Arab states' modern, oil-fueled economies - with many not fully sharing in their prosperity.

title: Fire and Rescue optimising resources amid rising costs, global instability, says DG
text: Fire and Rescue optimising resources amid rising costs, global instability, says DG

title: Fire and Rescue optimising resources amid rising costs, global instability, says DG
text: PUTRAJAYA: The Fire and Rescue Department is strategically optimising its resources to address current challenges, including financial pressures arising from global economic instability, says its director-general Datuk Seri Nor Hisham Mohammad.

title: Fire and Rescue optimising resources amid rising costs, global instability, says DG
text: He said global economic instability, compounded by the crisis in West Asia, has led to various constraints, particularly in financial management and rising fuel prices.

title: Fire and Rescue optimising resources amid rising costs, global instability, says DG
text: "However, the department remains optimistic in continuing to carry out its responsibilities efficiently and effectively," he said in a statement in conjunction with the 2026 World Firefighters’ Day celebration on Sunday (May 4).

title: Fire and Rescue optimising resources amid rising costs, global instability, says DG
text: Nor Hisham said among the approaches adopted are prudent cost-saving measures and efforts to explore more innovative operating methods, including the public-private-people partnership model.

title: Fire and Rescue optimising resources amid rising costs, global instability, says DG
text: He said the department would continue to enhance its operational and administrative capabilities through asset improvements, continuous training, technology integration and strengthened preparedness strategies.

title: Fire and Rescue optimising resources amid rising costs, global instability, says DG
text: "By upholding the principle of 'work more with less', we prove that limitations are not an obstacle to providing the best service.

title: Fire and Rescue optimising resources amid rising costs, global instability, says DG
text: "Ask not what your country can do for you, ask what you can do for your country.

title: Fire and Rescue optimising resources amid rising costs, global instability, says DG
text: Do not question what the country gives us, but instead ask ourselves what we can give to the country," he said in his message to department personnel.

title: Fire and Rescue optimising resources amid rising costs, global instability, says DG
text: Nor Hisham said the department would also strengthen the welfare, safety and psychosocial support aspects of its members, given that their duties are constantly exposed to various risks.

title: Fire and Rescue optimising resources amid rising costs, global instability, says DG
text: "We are aware that the duties of firefighters each day inevitably expose them to a range of risks, both internal and external.

title: Fire and Rescue optimising resources amid rising costs, global instability, says DG
text: "Therefore, various important initiatives have been implemented by the department through multiple channels to ensure that the welfare and well-being of personnel are always safeguarded, in addition to strengthening safety, training and psychosocial support aspects," he said.

title: Fire and Rescue optimising resources amid rising costs, global instability, says DG
text: - Bernama

title: Fernandes urges Malaysia to capitalise on West Asia crisis, rethink economic model
text: Fernandes urges Malaysia to capitalise on West Asia crisis, rethink economic model

title: Fernandes urges Malaysia to capitalise on West Asia crisis, rethink economic model
text: PUTRAJAYA: Malaysia should seize the ongoing West Asia crisis as an opportunity to rethink its economic model and enhance long-term growth, said Capital A Bhd chief executive officer Tan Sri Tony Fernandes.

title: Fernandes urges Malaysia to capitalise on West Asia crisis, rethink economic model
text: He said global supply disruptions, particularly involving key routes such as the Strait of Hormuz, could have prolonged economic effects on the country, underscoring the need for Malaysia to remain resilient.

title: Fernandes urges Malaysia to capitalise on West Asia crisis, rethink economic model
text: "Malaysia has done well diplomatically, but this crisis is an opportunity to rethink our economic model, from subsidies to energy to productivity,” he said at the "Malaysia’s Strategic Position in a Turbulent Global Economy” forum here today.

title: Fernandes urges Malaysia to capitalise on West Asia crisis, rethink economic model
text: Fernandes said that to achieve and maintain growth, the country must make difficult decisions, especially regarding the re-evaluation of subsidies.

title: Fernandes urges Malaysia to capitalise on West Asia crisis, rethink economic model
text: "We need to move away from this subsidy mentality.

title: Fernandes urges Malaysia to capitalise on West Asia crisis, rethink economic model
text: The billions spent on subsidies could be redirected into more productive industries, industries that create jobs, drive growth, and strengthen the economy,” he said.

title: Fernandes urges Malaysia to capitalise on West Asia crisis, rethink economic model
text: Fernandes also said the current situation presents an opportunity to reassess energy security.

title: Fernandes urges Malaysia to capitalise on West Asia crisis, rethink economic model
text: "I am a strong advocate for nuclear energy; the safety concerns are outdated, technologies like small modular reactors are much safer, and Malaysia should aim for energy independence,” he said.

title: Fernandes urges Malaysia to capitalise on West Asia crisis, rethink economic model
text: About the aviation sector, Fernandes said the current crisis, driven by surging energy prices and supply disruptions, is more severe than the COVID-19 pandemic.

title: Fernandes urges Malaysia to capitalise on West Asia crisis, rethink economic model
text: "During COVID, there was an understanding that you could not fly.

title: Fernandes urges Malaysia to capitalise on West Asia crisis, rethink economic model
text: In this instance, we are flying, and everyone wants to be paid on time, while oil prices have literally doubled overnight,” he said.

title: Fernandes urges Malaysia to capitalise on West Asia crisis, rethink economic model
text: Fernandes said airlines are facing significant cost pressures after jet fuel prices rose sharply from about US$85 per barrel to between US$180 and US$240.

title: Fernandes urges Malaysia to capitalise on West Asia crisis, rethink economic model
text: "We went into Hari Raya having sold tickets at a jet fuel price of US$85, and then we woke up one day and jet fuel had gone up to US$180 and peaked at US$240.

title: Fernandes urges Malaysia to capitalise on West Asia crisis, rethink economic model
text: In my time, we have never seen anything like it,” he said.

title: Fernandes urges Malaysia to capitalise on West Asia crisis, rethink economic model
text: Fernandes added that refining margins also surged from about US$20 to US$120 within a week, compounding the impact on airlines.

title: Fernandes urges Malaysia to capitalise on West Asia crisis, rethink economic model
text: "I have been talking to the government about extending credit so that we can catch up, because obviously we have to increase fares, but that takes time to reflect the oil costs we have already paid for,” he said.

title: Fernandes urges Malaysia to capitalise on West Asia crisis, rethink economic model
text: - Bernama

title: PETRONAS Gas to develop RGT-3 in Lumut, eyes joint development with partner
text: PETRONAS Gas to develop RGT-3 in Lumut, eyes joint development with partner

title: PETRONAS Gas to develop RGT-3 in Lumut, eyes joint development with partner
text: KUALA LUMPUR: Petronas Gas Bhd (PGB) received a notification letter from the Ministry of Economy on April 30, 2026, to develop the Third Regasification Terminal in Lumut, Perak (RGT-3) based on the floating storage and regasification unit (FSRU) concept.

title: PETRONAS Gas to develop RGT-3 in Lumut, eyes joint development with partner
text: In a filing with Bursa Malaysia today, PGB said it is also exploring the joint development of the project with a potential partner.

title: PETRONAS Gas to develop RGT-3 in Lumut, eyes joint development with partner
text: The company noted that the RGT-3 would be developed based on the FSRU concept, whereby the liquefied natural gas (LNG) storage and regasification processes would be carried out on the FSRU.

title: PETRONAS Gas to develop RGT-3 in Lumut, eyes joint development with partner
text: "The regasified LNG will then be transported via a connecting pipeline from the RGT-3 onshore and berthing facilities to the Peninsular Gas Utilisation (PGU) system.

title: PETRONAS Gas to develop RGT-3 in Lumut, eyes joint development with partner
text: This project will represent the first deployment of an FSRU in Malaysia,” it said.

title: PETRONAS Gas to develop RGT-3 in Lumut, eyes joint development with partner
text: - Bernama

title: Johor eyeing 12 million visitors, RM42.48bil in tourism receipts this year
text: Johor eyeing 12 million visitors, RM42.48bil in tourism receipts this year

title: Johor eyeing 12 million visitors, RM42.48bil in tourism receipts this year
text: ISKANDAR PUTERI: Johor is on track to welcome 12 million tourists this year with an estimated economic impact of RM42.48bil, the state assembly was told.

title: Johor eyeing 12 million visitors, RM42.48bil in tourism receipts this year
text: This is backed by strong early momentum and an aggressive lineup of tourism programmes, unity, culture and heritage committee chairman K. Raven Kumar said on Monday (May 4).

title: Johor eyeing 12 million visitors, RM42.48bil in tourism receipts this year
text: He said the Visit Johor 2026 campaign will feature between 100 and 170 events throughout the year to draw visitors from around the world.

title: Johor eyeing 12 million visitors, RM42.48bil in tourism receipts this year
text: Johor recorded 10.63 million tourist arrivals last year, comprising over 7.38 million domestic visitors and 3.25 million international tourists who stayed at least one night.

title: Johor eyeing 12 million visitors, RM42.48bil in tourism receipts this year
text: "For the first three months of 2026 alone, the state recorded over 6.07 million visitor movements, compared with 5.96 million in the same period last year," he said.

title: Johor eyeing 12 million visitors, RM42.48bil in tourism receipts this year
text: He was replying to questions from Ng Yak Howe (PH-Bentayan), Hasrunizah Hassan (BN-Pulai Sebatang), Azizul Bachok (BN-Tiram) and Rashidah Ismail (BN-Pasir Raja).

title: Johor eyeing 12 million visitors, RM42.48bil in tourism receipts this year
text: Raven Kumar said the increase is a positive start to the campaign year and indicates that promotional efforts and tourism programmes are gaining traction.

title: Johor eyeing 12 million visitors, RM42.48bil in tourism receipts this year
text: He added that Singapore remains Johor’s largest source market, followed by Indonesia, China and India, all of which recorded notable increases in visitor numbers during the January-to-March period.

title: Johor eyeing 12 million visitors, RM42.48bil in tourism receipts this year
text: Raven Kumar said the campaign was officially rolled out through programmes at major entry points by Tourism Johor in collaboration with the Tourism, Arts and Culture Ministry and Tourism Malaysia.

title: Johor eyeing 12 million visitors, RM42.48bil in tourism receipts this year
text: "At least 16 entry points across land, air and sea have been involved in the initiative to create a strong first impression for visitors entering the state," he added.

title: Johor eyeing 12 million visitors, RM42.48bil in tourism receipts this year
text: Raven Kumar also said Johor’s tourism sector also received a major boost after Tourism Johor was recognised by the Asia Record for recording the highest number of international visitors entering via a land crossing between two sovereign nations.

title: Johor eyeing 12 million visitors, RM42.48bil in tourism receipts this year
text: "A total of 24.7 million international visitors were recorded in 2025, including 19.38 million from Singapore, reinforcing Johor’s position as a key tourism gateway to Malaysia and the region," he added.

title: Johor eyeing 12 million visitors, RM42.48bil in tourism receipts this year
text: On product development, he highlighted the launch of Night Zoo Johor on March 27, offering a unique after-dark wildlife experience aimed at families and educational tourism.

title: Johor eyeing 12 million visitors, RM42.48bil in tourism receipts this year
text: He added that the upcoming Kompleks Sultan Abu Bakar and Muzium Tokoh Johor, scheduled to open in June, will strengthen the state’s cultural and heritage tourism offerings.

title: Johor eyeing 12 million visitors, RM42.48bil in tourism receipts this year
text: "To improve connectivity, plans are underway to introduce a Johor Tourist Bus service linking key attractions in Johor Baru, making it easier for visitors to move between destinations.

title: Johor eyeing 12 million visitors, RM42.48bil in tourism receipts this year
text: "Existing attractions are also being upgraded, including Hutan Bandar MBJB, which now features a zipline and water park, as well as Taman Tasek Merdeka with enhanced recreational facilities," he said.

title: Johor eyeing 12 million visitors, RM42.48bil in tourism receipts this year
text: Raven Kumar also said the state is also continuing to expand its ecotourism segment, with sites such as Kampung Sungai Melayu, Taman Negara Tanjung Piai and Taman Negara Gunung Ledang attracting nature lovers and adventure seekers.

title: Johor eyeing 12 million visitors, RM42.48bil in tourism receipts this year
text: "With strong statistics, strategic planning and continuous product development, Johor is well positioned to achieve its target of 12 million tourists and RM42.48bil in tourism receipts," he said.

title: Johor eyeing 12 million visitors, RM42.48bil in tourism receipts this year
text: He added that the state’s holistic approach of balancing new attractions, upgraded facilities and improved infrastructure would ensure Johor remains a competitive and dynamic tourism destination in the region.

title: Johor still drawing investors despite global uncertainty, says Onn Hafiz
text: Johor still drawing investors despite global uncertainty, says Onn Hafiz

title: Johor still drawing investors despite global uncertainty, says Onn Hafiz
text: ISKANDAR PUTERI: Investor confidence in Johor remains strong despite global economic and geopolitical uncertainties, says Mentri Besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi.

title: Johor still drawing investors despite global uncertainty, says Onn Hafiz
text: He acknowledged concerns about external challenges, including tensions in the Middle East and their potential impact on investments and state revenue, but stressed that Johor continues to attract strong interest from foreign investors.

title: Johor still drawing investors despite global uncertainty, says Onn Hafiz
text: Onn Hafiz said a recent working visit to China — following earlier trips to Shanghai and Beijing last year — revealed sustained enthusiasm among companies there to expand into Johor, particularly under the “China Plus One” strategy.

title: Johor still drawing investors despite global uncertainty, says Onn Hafiz
text: “More than 30 companies have expressed interest in expanding their operations in the Johor-Singapore Special Economic Zone (JS-SEZ),” he said, adding that many firms are seeking stable and competitive locations in the region.

title: Johor still drawing investors despite global uncertainty, says Onn Hafiz
text: Onn Hafiz made these remarks in reply to a supplementary question from Anuar Abdul Manap (BN-Pemanis) at the state assembly meeting held at Bangunan Sultan Ismail in Kota Iskandar here on Monday (May 4), asking whether the state can still attract investment amid the ongoing conflict in the Middle East.

title: Johor still drawing investors despite global uncertainty, says Onn Hafiz
text: He noted that feedback from global logistics company DHL highlighted Johor’s growing appeal, with the firm reportedly completing expansion targets within two years, compared with an initial projection of 15 years.

title: Johor still drawing investors despite global uncertainty, says Onn Hafiz
text: According to Onn Hafiz, the JS-SEZ, a joint initiative between Malaysia and Singapore, is increasingly seen by investors as a potential new engine of growth for Southeast Asia, beyond its role as a bilateral economic catalyst.

title: Johor still drawing investors despite global uncertainty, says Onn Hafiz
text: He said the state’s success in attracting investment was the result of coordinated efforts among federal and state agencies, including the Malaysian Investment Development Authority (MIDA), the Investment, Trade and Industry Ministry, local councils and land offices.

title: Johor still drawing investors despite global uncertainty, says Onn Hafiz
text: “Investors see Johor as offering stability and consistency at a time of global uncertainty.

title: Johor still drawing investors despite global uncertainty, says Onn Hafiz
text: This gives them confidence to do business and expand here,” he said, adding that the state government would continue to strengthen its investment ecosystem while ensuring sustainable growth.

title: Ringgit opens higher against US$ ahead of OPR announcement
text: Ringgit opens higher against US$ ahead of OPR announcement

title: Ringgit opens higher against US$ ahead of OPR announcement
text: KUALA LUMPUR: The ringgit opened higher against the US dollar on the first trading day of May, with the upcoming Bank Negara Malaysia (BNM) Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) meeting later this week, developments in the United States-Iran conflict and the release of the US non-farm payroll (NFP) data expected to set the tone for the market.

title: Ringgit opens higher against US$ ahead of OPR announcement
text: At 8 am, the domestic unit gained against the greenback to 3.9590/9725 from 3.9690/9740 at last Thursday’s close.

title: Ringgit opens higher against US$ ahead of OPR announcement
text: The local market was closed on Friday in conjunction with the Labour Day public holiday.

title: Ringgit opens higher against US$ ahead of OPR announcement
text: Bank Muamalat Malaysia Bhd chief economist Dr Mohd Afzanizam Abdul Rashid said the BNM’s MPC members will decide the Overnight Policy Rate (OPR) on May 7.

title: Ringgit opens higher against US$ ahead of OPR announcement
text: "Most economists expect the OPR to be unchanged and the latest assessment on the Iran war sees no signs of ebbing while crude oil prices remain elevated,” he added.

title: Ringgit opens higher against US$ ahead of OPR announcement
text: "There is also the NFP data points in the US on Friday which is quite critical for US Federal Reserves decision on rates.

title: Ringgit opens higher against US$ ahead of OPR announcement
text: In that sense, we expect to ringgit to maintain its narrow range posture as market sentiments will remain cautious,” he told Bernama.

title: Ringgit opens higher against US$ ahead of OPR announcement
text: Mohd Afzanizam expects US dollar-ringgit to range between RM3.95 and RM3.97 today.

title: Ringgit opens higher against US$ ahead of OPR announcement
text: Meanwhile, the ringgit traded mostly lower against a basket of major currencies.

title: Ringgit opens higher against US$ ahead of OPR announcement
text: It eased against the Japanese yen to 2.5226/5315 from 2.4907/4942 at the close on Thursday, traded almost flat against the euro at 4.6415/6574 from 4.6417/6476 and slipped versus the British pound to 5.3759/3943 from 5.3593/3661 previously.

title: Ringgit opens higher against US$ ahead of OPR announcement
text: The local currency was mixed against regional peers.

title: Ringgit opens higher against US$ ahead of OPR announcement
text: It depreciated against the Singapore dollar to 3.1095/1206 from 3.1061/1103 at Thursday’s close, fell against the Thai baht to 12.1812/2306 from 12.1711/1932 yesterday, but was marginally higher against the Indonesian rupiah at 228.3/229.2 from 228.7/229.2 and the Philippine peso at 6.44/4/6.47 from 6.45/6.47 previously.

title: Ringgit opens higher against US$ ahead of OPR announcement
text: - Bernama

title: Entrepreneur Development Ministry aims to save RM20mil under cost-saving measures, says Sim
text: Entrepreneur Development Ministry aims to save RM20mil under cost-saving measures, says Sim

title: Entrepreneur Development Ministry aims to save RM20mil under cost-saving measures, says Sim
text: GEORGE TOWN: The Entrepreneur Development and Cooperatives Ministry is targeting savings of RM20mil this year to support the government’s push for prudent spending amid global economic uncertainties.

title: Entrepreneur Development Ministry aims to save RM20mil under cost-saving measures, says Sim
text: Its minister, Steven Sim Chee Keong, said the move is in line with the Finance Ministry’s (MoF) directive to realign allocations for 2026, particularly involving operating expenditure.

title: Entrepreneur Development Ministry aims to save RM20mil under cost-saving measures, says Sim
text: "The savings do not involve development expenditure or public welfare, but will focus on government operational costs.

title: Entrepreneur Development Ministry aims to save RM20mil under cost-saving measures, says Sim
text: "For example, costs to be reduced include overseas travel, including at ministerial level; large-scale events and functions which will be minimised; as well as a temporary freeze on new hiring and reduced asset purchases as part of cost-saving measures,” he said.

title: Entrepreneur Development Ministry aims to save RM20mil under cost-saving measures, says Sim
text: He was speaking to reporters after a walkabout with Penang Chief Minister Chow Kon Yeow at the Pulau Tikus Public Market here on Sunday (May 3).

title: Entrepreneur Development Ministry aims to save RM20mil under cost-saving measures, says Sim
text: Sim said the measures are necessary as the world continues to face uncertainties, including oil supply disruptions, the energy crisis and prolonged geopolitical conflicts in the Middle East.

title: Entrepreneur Development Ministry aims to save RM20mil under cost-saving measures, says Sim
text: He said the government must ensure that public welfare is not affected and that the impact on the economy and businesses is minimised, while maintaining sufficient fiscal space to continue assisting such as subsidies, business development and welfare programmes.

title: Entrepreneur Development Ministry aims to save RM20mil under cost-saving measures, says Sim
text: At the same time, he said the ministry will maintain its development expenditure at RM750mil this year without any reduction, and remains committed to disbursing up to RM15bil in financing to support businesses, particularly micro, small and medium enterprises (MSMEs) and the corporate sector.

title: Entrepreneur Development Ministry aims to save RM20mil under cost-saving measures, says Sim
text: "This cost-saving exercise involves government spending, not the rakyat.

title: Entrepreneur Development Ministry aims to save RM20mil under cost-saving measures, says Sim
text: In fact, we will continue to channel development and welfare funds to safeguard the people and the national economy,” he said.

title: Entrepreneur Development Ministry aims to save RM20mil under cost-saving measures, says Sim
text: MOF, in a statement on April 30, stressed that the operating expenditure adjustment guidelines involve only non-critical spending, while core expenditures, including basic services, security, healthcare and education, will continue as approved under Budget 2026.

title: Tourism drives revenue and growth, baseless objections against water festival must cease
text: Tourism drives revenue and growth, baseless objections against water festival must cease

title: Tourism drives revenue and growth, baseless objections against water festival must cease
text: CALLS by Umno Youth chief Datuk Dr Akmal Saleh to cancel the Rain Rave Water Music Festival 2026 are both unconvincing and counterproductive, especially at a time when Malaysia urgently needs tourism to generate growth and revenue.

title: Tourism drives revenue and growth, baseless objections against water festival must cease
text: The Tourism, Arts and Culture Ministry exists to propel our tourism sector forward.

title: Tourism drives revenue and growth, baseless objections against water festival must cease
text: Events like this are not frivolous.

title: Tourism drives revenue and growth, baseless objections against water festival must cease
text: Instead, they are strategic efforts to draw visitors, stimulate spending, and reinforce Malaysia’s standing as a vibrant international destination.

title: Tourism drives revenue and growth, baseless objections against water festival must cease
text: Taking an emotional and confrontational position against such initiatives only risks harming our economy and tarnishing our global image.

title: Tourism drives revenue and growth, baseless objections against water festival must cease
text: At a time of global uncertainty, tourism remains one of the most effective “smokeless industries” we have.

title: Tourism drives revenue and growth, baseless objections against water festival must cease
text: Supporting a wide network of sectors, including food and beverage, hospitality, retail and entertainment, tourism delivers real and immediate economic returns.

title: Tourism drives revenue and growth, baseless objections against water festival must cease
text: In 2024 alone, international tourists contributed RM106.7bil to our economy thereby accentuating clear evidence of its importance.

title: Tourism drives revenue and growth, baseless objections against water festival must cease
text: Hosting the festival in Bukit Bintang is no coincidence.

title: Tourism drives revenue and growth, baseless objections against water festival must cease
text: It is one of Malaysia’s most prominent tourism and commercial hubs, making it an ideal location to maximise visibility and impact.

title: Tourism drives revenue and growth, baseless objections against water festival must cease
text: Large-scale events like this uplift countless small and medium businesses - from hawkers and drivers to shop owners and hoteliers—who depend on such opportunities to sustain their livelihoods.

title: Tourism drives revenue and growth, baseless objections against water festival must cease
text: With this year designated as Visit Malaysia 2026, the government has set an ambitious target of attracting 47 million visitors and generating RM300bil in revenue.

title: Tourism drives revenue and growth, baseless objections against water festival must cease
text: Achieving this requires fresh, creative approaches to tourism, and the water music festival is precisely the kind of initiative we should be supporting, not opposing without sound basis.

title: Tourism drives revenue and growth, baseless objections against water festival must cease
text: Critics should approach this matter with reason and constructive thinking, rather than allowing personal sentiments or narrow viewpoints to dictate their stance.

title: Tourism drives revenue and growth, baseless objections against water festival must cease
text: Malaysia’s diversity is one of our greatest strengths.

title: Tourism drives revenue and growth, baseless objections against water festival must cease
text: As we prepare to welcome the world, we must showcase this richness with confidence.

title: Tourism drives revenue and growth, baseless objections against water festival must cease
text: Our economic recovery has been hard-earned, and it should not be jeopardised by unfounded objections that hold the nation back.

title: Tourism drives revenue and growth, baseless objections against water festival must cease
text:
DATUK LAWRENCE LOW

MCA Economic & SMEs Affairs Committee Chairman

MCA Vice President

title: Japan’s Takaichi lays out revamped Indo-Pacific vision in speech during special visit
text: Japan’s Takaichi lays out revamped Indo-Pacific vision in speech during special visit

title: Japan’s Takaichi lays out revamped Indo-Pacific vision in speech during special visit
text: ASIA PACIFIC (Bloomberg): Japanese Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi delivered a landmark speech to convey her vision for deepening her nation’s economic and security ties with the Indo-Pacific, with a view to making the region more resilient in the face of geopolitical challenges.

title: Japan’s Takaichi lays out revamped Indo-Pacific vision in speech during special visit
text: Speaking in Hanoi after a bilateral summit on Saturday with Vietnam’s General Secretary and President To Lam, Takaichi stressed the importance of strengthening supply chains, maritime security, space development and a reliable communications infrastructure for the exchange of large amounts of data.

title: Japan’s Takaichi lays out revamped Indo-Pacific vision in speech during special visit
text: "The excessive reliance on specific countries for critical goods stems from unfairly low-priced supply conditions,” Takaichi said.

title: Japan’s Takaichi lays out revamped Indo-Pacific vision in speech during special visit
text:
"To strengthen supply chains, ensuring fair competitive conditions based on factors other than price is essential.”

She also touted the importance of "safe and free navigation of sea lanes” and pledged to channel more official development assistance to help nations build ports and airports and strengthen maritime security capabilities.

title: Japan’s Takaichi lays out revamped Indo-Pacific vision in speech during special visit
text: The premier cited patrol vessels and radar systems provided by Japan to the Philippines that serve as the "eyes” and "feet” protecting the safety of sea lanes.

title: Japan’s Takaichi lays out revamped Indo-Pacific vision in speech during special visit
text: The two sides announced six deals covering space technology, information and communications, irrigation, infrastructure development for disaster and climate resilience, and low-carbon growth.

title: Japan’s Takaichi lays out revamped Indo-Pacific vision in speech during special visit
text: No values were disclosed.

title: Japan’s Takaichi lays out revamped Indo-Pacific vision in speech during special visit
text: They also agreed on measures to raise Japan’s investment in Vietnam to $5 billion per year at an early date and to bring bilateral trade to $60 billion by 2030, according to a post on the Vietnamese government website.

title: Japan’s Takaichi lays out revamped Indo-Pacific vision in speech during special visit
text: The speech, marking a decade since her mentor and former Prime Minister Shinzo Abe introduced the "Free and Open Indo-Pacific” initiative, came as Japan is gradually raising its profile overseas while also seeking alliances to secure resources including critical minerals through robust supply chains.

title: Japan’s Takaichi lays out revamped Indo-Pacific vision in speech during special visit
text: It also came as Takaichi has projected a more assertive image in the region, including removing most restrictions on weapons exports and taking part in military drills for the first time with the US and the Philippines.

title: Japan’s Takaichi lays out revamped Indo-Pacific vision in speech during special visit
text: Her comments late last year about defending Taiwan particularly incensed Beijing, which interpreted some of her actions as signs of militarism.

title: Japan’s Takaichi lays out revamped Indo-Pacific vision in speech during special visit
text: Takaichi’s decision to deliver her speech in Hanoi may have been a strategic choice, as she was likely to find a receptive audience in a country that sees China’s maritime claims as overly ambitious.

title: Japan’s Takaichi lays out revamped Indo-Pacific vision in speech during special visit
text: Abe’s FOIP strategy focused on the rule of law, freedom of navigation and free trade among like-minded countries across the Indian and Pacific oceans.

title: Japan’s Takaichi lays out revamped Indo-Pacific vision in speech during special visit
text: The US has backed the vision, which has at times been seen as a tool to counter China’s ascendancy.

title: Japan’s Takaichi lays out revamped Indo-Pacific vision in speech during special visit
text: Takaichi’s stop in Vietnam underscores Japan’s pressing concerns over supply chains, particularly of critical minerals, as Japanese firms shift their manufacturing bases away from China to Vietnam amid political turbulence.

title: Japan’s Takaichi lays out revamped Indo-Pacific vision in speech during special visit
text: "We intend to establish economic security as a new axis of cooperation” in Vietnam, Takaichi told reporters Friday as she prepared to depart for Hanoi, highlighting that the country is key to its supply chains, including for medical supplies.

title: Japan’s Takaichi lays out revamped Indo-Pacific vision in speech during special visit
text: In recent years, Vietnam has overtaken China as a destination for Japanese investment, as the deterioration of Japan-China ties seeps into business sentiment.

title: Japan’s Takaichi lays out revamped Indo-Pacific vision in speech during special visit
text: Japan has been trying to shore up supply chains for critical minerals as it remains heavily dependent on China for rare earths, particularly when Beijing tightens export controls.

title: Japan’s Takaichi lays out revamped Indo-Pacific vision in speech during special visit
text: Cooperation with Vietnam, home to world’s sixth-largest reserves, is seen as important for economic security.

title: Japan’s Takaichi lays out revamped Indo-Pacific vision in speech during special visit
text: The opportunity for economic cooperation goes both ways.

title: Japan’s Takaichi lays out revamped Indo-Pacific vision in speech during special visit
text: Vietnam has been seeking support from Japan and other countries for oil supplies.

title: Japan’s Takaichi lays out revamped Indo-Pacific vision in speech during special visit
text: Ahead of the trip, Idemitsu Corp. said it would supply 4 million barrels of crude oil to Vietnam’s Nghi Son refinery at the request of Japanese government.

title: Japan’s Takaichi lays out revamped Indo-Pacific vision in speech during special visit
text: Vietnam and Japan upgraded ties to a comprehensive strategic partnership in 2023.

title: Japan’s Takaichi lays out revamped Indo-Pacific vision in speech during special visit
text: Japan is Vietnam’s largest provider of official development assistance, third-largest investor and fourth-largest partner in tourism and trade.

title: Japan’s Takaichi lays out revamped Indo-Pacific vision in speech during special visit
text: Bilateral trade reached $51.4 billion last year, Vietnam’s government said.

title: Japan’s Takaichi lays out revamped Indo-Pacific vision in speech during special visit
text: Japan is also Vietnam’s top labor partner with over 680,000 Vietnamese living in the country, making them the second-largest foreign community.

title: Japan’s Takaichi lays out revamped Indo-Pacific vision in speech during special visit
text: Vietnam aims to train 500 semiconductor researchers at the PhD level by 2030, with Japan ultimately expected to host about half.

title: Japan’s Takaichi lays out revamped Indo-Pacific vision in speech during special visit
text: After Vietnam, Takaichi will head to Australia for further discussions.

title: Japan’s Takaichi lays out revamped Indo-Pacific vision in speech during special visit
text: While Takaichi visits Hanoi and Canberra, Japan’s defense minister Shinjiro Koizumi will stop by Indonesia and the Philippines.

title: Japan’s Takaichi lays out revamped Indo-Pacific vision in speech during special visit
text: He will oversee the Japanese Self-Defence Forces’ first full participation in joint military exercises with the Philippines and the US, in line with the drive for deeper cooperation and stronger defense ties.

title: Japan’s Takaichi lays out revamped Indo-Pacific vision in speech during special visit
text: "FOIP is not about imposing anything on anyone.

title: Japan’s Takaichi lays out revamped Indo-Pacific vision in speech during special visit
text: It has developed flexibly, accepting diverse voices and adapting to the changing times,” Takaichi said.

title: Japan’s Takaichi lays out revamped Indo-Pacific vision in speech during special visit
text: -- With assistance from James Mayger and Akemi Terukina.
