title: Ringgit strength, IMF’s GDP forecast signal global confidence in Malaysia’s economy, says Muhammad Kamil
text: Ringgit strength, IMF’s GDP forecast signal global confidence in Malaysia’s economy, says Muhammad Kamil

title: Ringgit strength, IMF’s GDP forecast signal global confidence in Malaysia’s economy, says Muhammad Kamil
text: KUALA LUMPUR: The continued strengthening of the ringgit and the improved economic projections by the International Monetary Fund (IMF) indicate the fundamental strength of the country's economy and investor confidence in the direction of the Malaysia Madani administration.

title: Ringgit strength, IMF’s GDP forecast signal global confidence in Malaysia’s economy, says Muhammad Kamil
text: The Political Secretary to the Finance Minister, Muhammad Kamil Abdul Munim, said this development also sends a clear message that Malaysia is re-emerging as a trusted economic and investment destination in the region.

title: Ringgit strength, IMF’s GDP forecast signal global confidence in Malaysia’s economy, says Muhammad Kamil
text: According to him, CNBC Indonesia reported that the ringgit recorded an appreciation of around 2.64 per cent against the United States (US) dollar as at the end of April 2026, thus placing Malaysia at the top among Asian currencies, followed by China and Singapore.

title: Ringgit strength, IMF’s GDP forecast signal global confidence in Malaysia’s economy, says Muhammad Kamil
text: "What is even more commendable is that this trend is not temporary or speculative.

title: Ringgit strength, IMF’s GDP forecast signal global confidence in Malaysia’s economy, says Muhammad Kamil
text: "On the contrary, it shows a consistent and sustained trend due to the fundamental strength of the country's economy and investor confidence in the direction of the Malaysia Madani administration," he said in a statement today.

title: Ringgit strength, IMF’s GDP forecast signal global confidence in Malaysia’s economy, says Muhammad Kamil
text: Muhammad Kamil said that the ringgit’s strength today is not coincidental, but is supported by factors such as sustained national economic growth, positive foreign investment inflows, political stability, clear economic reforms, and international investors' confidence in the government's policies and Bank Negara.

title: Ringgit strength, IMF’s GDP forecast signal global confidence in Malaysia’s economy, says Muhammad Kamil
text: "The strengthening of the ringgit is not just a number in the financial market, but a symbol of the world's confidence in the stability and future of Malaysia's economy," he said.

title: Ringgit strength, IMF’s GDP forecast signal global confidence in Malaysia’s economy, says Muhammad Kamil
text: Muhammad Kamil said that at the same time, the IMF has raised its projection for Malaysia's gross domestic product (GDP) growth in 2026 to 4.7 per cent from 4.3 per cent previously, reflecting greater confidence in Malaysia's economy despite global economic uncertainty.

title: Ringgit strength, IMF’s GDP forecast signal global confidence in Malaysia’s economy, says Muhammad Kamil
text: "The increase in this projection clearly shows that Malaysia is on a stronger economic path compared to many other countries, driven by robust domestic demand, a diverse export base, and investor confidence in the stability of the country's policies," he added.

title: Ringgit strength, IMF’s GDP forecast signal global confidence in Malaysia’s economy, says Muhammad Kamil
text: - Bernama

title: Myanmar among countries with the largest working population, says President
text: Myanmar among countries with the largest working population, says President

title: Myanmar among countries with the largest working population, says President
text: YANGON: President U Min Aung Hlaing said that Myanmar is among the countries with the largest working population, and the large working population means that it possesses a good human resource base that is important for the country's development and economic growth.

title: Myanmar among countries with the largest working population, says President
text: He said this in his message on the occasion of International Workers' Day, on Friday (May 1).

title: Myanmar among countries with the largest working population, says President
text: He noted that Myanmar had been a member state of the International Labour Organisation (ILO) since May 18, 1948, and had been respecting and abiding by the fundamental principles and provisions of the ILO and protecting and promoting the rights of workers.

title: Myanmar among countries with the largest working population, says President
text: "Myanmar has been implementing projects in line with international employer and labour standards and existing laws, and has appointed an ILO liaison officer since 2002 to assist in efforts to eliminate forced labour."

title: Myanmar among countries with the largest working population, says President
text: The President said the ILO Liaison Officer based in Myanmar was cooperating with the ILO Liaison Officer, providing the ILO Liaison Officer with the necessary information and services, resolving complaints of forced labour through the National Forced Labour Complaints Mechanism in collaboration with the ILO, and independently organising employers' and workers' organisations at grassroots, township, region, state federations, and Myanmar National Affairs Organisations in accordance with ILO Convention 87.

title: Myanmar among countries with the largest working population, says President
text: "Myanmar is among the countries with the largest working population, and the large working population means that it possesses a good human resource base that is important for the country's development and high economic growth.

title: Myanmar among countries with the largest working population, says President
text: "For human resource development, seven Ministry of Labour Skill Training Schools, 22 accredited workplace-based training schools, and 26 NSSA-accredited training schools are offering skills training.

title: Myanmar among countries with the largest working population, says President
text: "Young people who successfully complete the skills training will improve their standard of living from the level of ordinary workers to high-value skilled workers with practical leadership skills in the workplace.

title: Myanmar among countries with the largest working population, says President
text: "These skilled workers are being issued skill certificates in accordance with the standards and specifications of the National Skill Standards Authority (NSSA).

title: Myanmar among countries with the largest working population, says President
text: These certificates are certificates that help people work both domestically and abroad and earn wages in line with their skills and qualifications.," he said.

title: Myanmar among countries with the largest working population, says President
text: The President also stated that education is being encouraged and promoted to produce good human resources with knowledge and skills, and that education includes completing at least KG+9 and providing vocational education that will enable people to become experts in agriculture, livestock, and industry.

title: Myanmar among countries with the largest working population, says President
text: "The government is making effective use of the natural resources owned by the country, transforming agricultural and livestock products into value-added products by combining them with modern technologies, and encouraging and promoting local small, medium and large enterprises (MSMEs).

title: Myanmar among countries with the largest working population, says President
text: "The government is constantly working to improve the income of workers, create job opportunities, provide vocational training to acquire skilled workers, invite investment to increase labour-based manufacturing, provide foreign job opportunities, provide social security and other benefits to workers, improve workplace safety and health, and resolve labor disputes fairly."

title: Myanmar among countries with the largest working population, says President
text: He added that rural people, who constitute about 70 percent of the Myanmar population, are primarily engaged in agriculture and livestock activities.

title: Myanmar among countries with the largest working population, says President
text: Therefore, through the development of the agricultural-based industrial sector, efforts must be made to develop micro, small and medium-sized enterprises (MSMEs) to improve the socio-economic life of the people, promote the national economy, and replace foreign imports.

title: Myanmar among countries with the largest working population, says President
text: He said the state was organising skills competitions domestically with the aim of developing workforce skills, broadening knowledge of technical and vocational skills, and aligning job skill standards with international standards.

title: Myanmar among countries with the largest working population, says President
text: It also provides opportunities for young people who win outstanding prizes in the competitions to compete in the Asean Skills Competitions held every two years.

title: Myanmar among countries with the largest working population, says President
text: "The state government will open basic vocational education courses for those who have successfully completed their military service and those who are out of school at the primary, secondary, and higher education levels."

title: Myanmar among countries with the largest working population, says President
text: The President urged the relevant ministries and organisations to take action to ensure that all citizens live happily and peacefully under the auspices of the state, and that Myanmar migrant workers who go abroad are subject to the laws of the countries they work in, and that some workers are subjected to unfair and oppressive treatment.

title: Myanmar among countries with the largest working population, says President
text: "It is better to work domestically than abroad, as there are opportunities for growth and development.

title: Myanmar among countries with the largest working population, says President
text: Therefore, instead of going abroad, they should work domestically and all citizens should live happily and peacefully under the auspices of the state."

title: Myanmar among countries with the largest working population, says President
text: - Eleven Media/ANN

title: Diversify your funding sources, says central bank
text: Diversify your funding sources, says central bank

title: Diversify your funding sources, says central bank
text: CORPORATIONS involved in building large scale renewable energy projects ought to diversify their funding sources to include capital market instruments, foreign direct investments and alternative financing structures, says Bank Negara Malaysia (BNM).

title: Diversify your funding sources, says central bank
text: “It is important to note that the banking system alone cannot and should not be expected to meet all financing needs, particularly for large-scale, capital-intensive, long gestation energy transition projects,” the central bank tells StarBiz 7 in email responses on the issue of the single customer risks.

title: MEF calls for strategic recalibration of tripartism to secure Malaysia's future
text: MEF calls for strategic recalibration of tripartism to secure Malaysia's future

title: MEF calls for strategic recalibration of tripartism to secure Malaysia's future
text: KUALA LUMPUR: The Malaysian Employers Federation (MEF) has urged a shift in tripartism to a more strategic, high-trust partnership that can help Malaysia navigate economic changes, demographic shifts and growing global competition.

title: MEF calls for strategic recalibration of tripartism to secure Malaysia's future
text: In conjunction with Workers’ Day 2026, its president Datuk Dr Syed Hussain Syed Husman said cooperation between employers, employees, trade unions and the government must go beyond consultation, with stronger shared accountability and aligned policies.

title: MEF calls for strategic recalibration of tripartism to secure Malaysia's future
text: He stressed that Workers’ Day should not be merely symbolic, but a moment for stakeholders to assess progress and recalibrate approaches to secure the country’s economic future.

title: MEF calls for strategic recalibration of tripartism to secure Malaysia's future
text: Amid rising business costs and rapid digital transformation, Syed Hussain said sustainable businesses and quality jobs must go hand in hand, adding that human capital remains key to resilience.

title: MEF calls for strategic recalibration of tripartism to secure Malaysia's future
text: To tackle issues such as skills mismatches, graduate employability and the impact of artificial intelligence, MEF proposed a next-phase tripartite framework centred on evidence-based approach with stronger industry engagement at sector level.

title: MEF calls for strategic recalibration of tripartism to secure Malaysia's future
text: The federation also called for faster policy implementation and balanced regulations that protect workers without burdening businesses, while emphasising that social justice and economic competitiveness must progress together.

title: MEF calls for strategic recalibration of tripartism to secure Malaysia's future
text: Meanwhile, the National Skilled Workers Organisation (Belia Mahir), in a separate statement, fully supports the government's initiatives to empower the welfare and protection of workers in conjunction with the 2026 Workers' Day celebration.

title: MEF calls for strategic recalibration of tripartism to secure Malaysia's future
text: Its President, Mohammad Rizan Hassan, said that Belia Mahir welcomes the proposal to extend PERKESO protection to Malaysian citizens abroad, as well as the introduction of a Post-Maternity Leave Allowance to further empower women in the economy.

title: MEF calls for strategic recalibration of tripartism to secure Malaysia's future
text: Mohammad Rizan emphasised that the nation’s economic strength lies in the quality of the relationship between employers and employees.

title: MEF calls for strategic recalibration of tripartism to secure Malaysia's future
text: "Employers need to hold employees in high regard.

title: MEF calls for strategic recalibration of tripartism to secure Malaysia's future
text: Employees are not merely labour, but strategic assets that contribute to the growth of both the organisation and the nation.

title: MEF calls for strategic recalibration of tripartism to secure Malaysia's future
text: A condescending attitude, poor communication, and a failure to appreciate employees' contributions will only weaken productivity and workforce loyalty," he said.

title: MEF calls for strategic recalibration of tripartism to secure Malaysia's future
text: At the same time, he reminded workers to shed negative work habits that could adversely affect organisational performance.

title: MEF calls for strategic recalibration of tripartism to secure Malaysia's future
text: He noted that in this era of rapid technological advancement, the development of the right attitude is the most critical factor in ensuring the Malaysian workforce remains relevant.

title: MEF calls for strategic recalibration of tripartism to secure Malaysia's future
text: "Skilled youth, in particular, must emerge as a generation that is not only technically proficient but also possesses strong integrity, responsibility, dedication and high resilience,” he added.

title: MEF calls for strategic recalibration of tripartism to secure Malaysia's future
text: - Bernama

title: Workers vital to economy, national stability, says King
text: Workers vital to economy, national stability, says King

title: Workers vital to economy, national stability, says King
text: KUALA LUMPUR: The role of workers is crucial in driving economic growth and in ensuring the stability and prosperity of the country are maintained, even in the face of increasingly challenging economic conditions, says His Majesty Sultan Ibrahim, King of Malaysia.

title: Workers vital to economy, national stability, says King
text: According to a post on Sultan Ibrahim's Facebook on Friday (May 1), His Majesty said that the welfare of workers must always be given attention, including aspects of safety, work-life balance and career development opportunities, so that they remain resilient in facing economic uncertainties.

title: Workers vital to economy, national stability, says King
text: Sultan Ibrahim and Her Majesty Raja Zarith Sofiah, Queen of Malaysia, also conveyed Workers' Day wishes to all workers who are the backbone of the nation's development.

title: Workers vital to economy, national stability, says King
text: "Their Majesties expressed their appreciation for the sacrifices and dedication shown by workers in various sectors, whether in the public or private sector," according to the post.

title: Workers vital to economy, national stability, says King
text: Their Majesties also prayed that all workers in this country are always protected and granted health and well-being in carrying out their duties.

title: Workers vital to economy, national stability, says King
text: - Bernama

text: ANZ says Australia likely to avoid recession; flags prolonged impact of Middle East war

title: ANZ says Australia likely to avoid recession; flags prolonged impact of Middle East war
text: ANZ Group CEO Nuno Matos on Friday said Australia is likely to avoid recession but the economic consequences of war in the Middle East will be evident for the foreseeable future as tighter oil supply could accelerate inflation.

title: ANZ says Australia likely to avoid recession; flags prolonged impact of Middle East war
text: The comments come after Australia's fourth-largest bank by market capitalisation reported cash profit of A$3.78 billion ($2.72 billion) for the six months through March 31, versus a Visible Alpha consensus of A$3.68 billion.

title: ANZ says Australia likely to avoid recession; flags prolonged impact of Middle East war
text: The bank also said it will increase provisions by A$175 million to cover potential loan loss stemming from the U.S.-Israel war with Iran.

title: ANZ says Australia likely to avoid recession; flags prolonged impact of Middle East war
text: National Australia Bank and Westpac have similarly announced increased provisions and flagged the war's economic consequences.

title: ANZ says Australia likely to avoid recession; flags prolonged impact of Middle East war
text: They report first-half results next week.

title: ANZ says Australia likely to avoid recession; flags prolonged impact of Middle East war
text: "Much of the potential impact of this crisis remains ahead of us," said Matos, who became ANZ CEO almost a year ago.

title: ANZ says Australia likely to avoid recession; flags prolonged impact of Middle East war
text: "But the longer the flow of oil is constrained, the greater the chance the crisis shifts from being primarily an inflation challenge to much more of a supply and growth challenge."

title: ANZ says Australia likely to avoid recession; flags prolonged impact of Middle East war
text: The consumer price index jumped 1.4% in the first quarter of the year from the previous three months, the sharpest rise since late 2023, showed data released on Wednesday.

title: ANZ says Australia likely to avoid recession; flags prolonged impact of Middle East war

title: ANZ says Australia likely to avoid recession; flags prolonged impact of Middle East war
text: Matos said the conflict has had minimal impact on credit, capital or liquidity, and ANZ's exposure to the Middle East was small.

title: ANZ says Australia likely to avoid recession; flags prolonged impact of Middle East war

title: ANZ says Australia likely to avoid recession; flags prolonged impact of Middle East war
text: Matos said he expects more AI tools, similar to Mythos, will be developed which could increase threats to banks even more.

title: ANZ says Australia likely to avoid recession; flags prolonged impact of Middle East war
text: "Cyber risk for society and financial services organisations is already very high," he said.

title: ANZ says Australia likely to avoid recession; flags prolonged impact of Middle East war

title: Singapore economy to 'slow' this year on prolonged Hormuz closure: PM Wong
text: Singapore economy to 'slow' this year on prolonged Hormuz closure: PM Wong

title: Singapore economy to 'slow' this year on prolonged Hormuz closure: PM Wong
text: SINGAPORE: Singapore's prime minister warned Friday (May 1) that economic growth will slow this year and some countries may slip into recession as the Middle East keeps the Strait of Hormuz closed.

title: Singapore economy to 'slow' this year on prolonged Hormuz closure: PM Wong
text: In a May Day speech, Lawrence Wong said the war was not expected "to be over anytime soon" and supply disruptions may worsen in the months ahead.

title: Singapore economy to 'slow' this year on prolonged Hormuz closure: PM Wong
text: Shipping through the strait, which handles a fifth of global oil and gas, has been effectively stopped since the United States and Israel began bombing Iran on February 28.

title: Singapore economy to 'slow' this year on prolonged Hormuz closure: PM Wong
text: "Here in Asia, we are especially affected because of our high dependence on energy and other critical supplies from the Gulf," Wong said.

title: Singapore economy to 'slow' this year on prolonged Hormuz closure: PM Wong
text: Some regional countries are already facing fuel shortages, airlines have cut flights and factories are reporting delays, he said.

title: Singapore economy to 'slow' this year on prolonged Hormuz closure: PM Wong
text: "Globally, inflation will rise, spreading from energy to food and then other essentials.

title: Singapore economy to 'slow' this year on prolonged Hormuz closure: PM Wong
text: Some economies may well slip into recession, and Singapore will feel the impact directly," he said.

title: Singapore economy to 'slow' this year on prolonged Hormuz closure: PM Wong
text: "Our growth this year will slow, and inflation will be higher, and all this will put real pressure on businesses, workers and households."

title: Singapore economy to 'slow' this year on prolonged Hormuz closure: PM Wong
text: He did not give any figures, but the trade ministry in February said it expected the economy to expand 2.0-4.0 percent this year, a bump from its previous forecast of 1.0-3.0 percent.

title: Singapore economy to 'slow' this year on prolonged Hormuz closure: PM Wong
text: "We must brace ourselves and be prepared for a more difficult period ahead.

title: Singapore economy to 'slow' this year on prolonged Hormuz closure: PM Wong
text: And I want to be upfront with all of you so that we are mentally prepared," he said.

title: Singapore economy to 'slow' this year on prolonged Hormuz closure: PM Wong
text: Singapore, however, is dealing with the crisis from a position of strength because of policies it implemented in the past to build energy resilience, including being a major oil refining hub and energy trading centre.

title: Singapore economy to 'slow' this year on prolonged Hormuz closure: PM Wong
text: Even if the Strait of Hormuz reopens, a return to normal will likely not be immediate, Wong warned.

title: Singapore economy to 'slow' this year on prolonged Hormuz closure: PM Wong
text: "Ports and energy infrastructure have been damaged.

title: Singapore economy to 'slow' this year on prolonged Hormuz closure: PM Wong
text: Shipping lanes will need to be cleared of mines," he said.

title: Singapore economy to 'slow' this year on prolonged Hormuz closure: PM Wong
text: "Confidence must be restored that it is safe for ships to sail through, that insurance can be obtained, and people are prepared to take risks to go through the Strait."

title: Singapore economy to 'slow' this year on prolonged Hormuz closure: PM Wong
text: - AFP

title: Gold heads for weekly loss on oil-driven inflation concerns
text: Gold heads for weekly loss on oil-driven inflation concerns

title: Gold heads for weekly loss on oil-driven inflation concerns
text: Gold was steady in thin trading on Friday, but was on course for a weekly decline as higher oil prices fuelled inflation worries and expectations of higher-for-longer interest rates.

title: Gold heads for weekly loss on oil-driven inflation concerns
text: Spot gold was steady at $4,620.57 per ounce, as of 0248 GMT, but was on track for a weekly loss of 1.8%, having dropped to a one-month low on Wednesday.

title: Gold heads for weekly loss on oil-driven inflation concerns
text: U.S. gold futures for June delivery rose 0.1% to $4,632.70.

title: Gold heads for weekly loss on oil-driven inflation concerns
text: "In the Asian session, the market is going to be quite thin because of public holidays, so we're really in a bit of a crossroads, or at least waiting for the next catalyst to make more of a directional move," said Kyle Rodda, a senior financial market analyst at Capital.com.

title: Gold heads for weekly loss on oil-driven inflation concerns
text: Financial markets in the top gold consumers, China and India, are closed for the holiday.

title: Gold heads for weekly loss on oil-driven inflation concerns
text: Iran said on Thursday it would respond with "long and painful strikes" on U.S. positions if Washington renewed attacks and restated its claim to the Strait of Hormuz.

title: Gold heads for weekly loss on oil-driven inflation concerns
text: Brent oil prices held firm above $110 a barrel as efforts to resolve the Iran conflict have hit an impasse.

title: Gold heads for weekly loss on oil-driven inflation concerns
text: U.S. inflation accelerated in March as the Iran war raised gasoline prices, reinforcing expectations that the Federal Reserve could keep interest rates on hold well into next year.

title: Gold heads for weekly loss on oil-driven inflation concerns
text: Global brokerages have also gradually stepped back from earlier expectations of two U.S. rate cuts in 2026, with forecasts now split between some easing and none due to lingering inflation risks and cautious policymakers.

title: Gold heads for weekly loss on oil-driven inflation concerns
text: The European Central Bank and the Bank of England also kept interest rates unchanged on Thursday following holds earlier in the week by the Fed and the Bank of Japan, though all signalled concerns about inflation.

title: Gold heads for weekly loss on oil-driven inflation concerns
text: While gold is traditionally seen as a hedge against inflation, elevated interest rates aimed at controlling price pressures weigh on its appeal as a non-yielding asset.

title: Gold heads for weekly loss on oil-driven inflation concerns
text: In other metals, spot silver rose 0.5% to $74.13 per ounce, platinum slipped 0.3% to $1,980.15, and palladium was up 0.2% at $1,527.29.

title: Gold heads for weekly loss on oil-driven inflation concerns
text: - Reuters

title: Govt to continue taking necessary measures to address global economic uncertainty
text: Govt to continue taking necessary measures to address global economic uncertainty

title: Govt to continue taking necessary measures to address global economic uncertainty
text: KUALA LUMPUR: The government will continue to take the necessary measures in facing global economic uncertainty, said Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim.

title: Govt to continue taking necessary measures to address global economic uncertainty
text: He said the global economic situation has become more challenging this month compared to three months ago in February.

title: Govt to continue taking necessary measures to address global economic uncertainty
text: "At the end of February, crude oil prices were at US$70 per barrel, and today they have surged to US$115 per barrel.

title: Govt to continue taking necessary measures to address global economic uncertainty
text: The increase is very steep, meaning the impact of this economic (uncertainty) is felt worldwide.

title: Govt to continue taking necessary measures to address global economic uncertainty
text: "There are (shortages) of materials for those working in plantations, (for example) a short-term shortage of phosphate, and (prices) of diesel have risen.

title: Govt to continue taking necessary measures to address global economic uncertainty
text: Those working in factories, those driving lorries...everyone is affected," he said.

title: Govt to continue taking necessary measures to address global economic uncertainty
text: He said this when delivering his Workers' Day message in conjunction with the National Workers' Day 2026 celebration held here today.

title: Govt to continue taking necessary measures to address global economic uncertainty
text: Also present were Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi, Human Resources Minister Datuk Seri R. Ramanan, Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil, and Chief Secretary to the Government Tan Sri Shamsul Azri Abu Bakar.

title: Govt to continue taking necessary measures to address global economic uncertainty
text: Anwar said Malaysia is also maintaining the subsidised RON95 petrol price under the BUDI MADANI RON95 initiative at RM1.99 per litre, even though most oil-producing countries have already raised their prices.

title: Govt to continue taking necessary measures to address global economic uncertainty
text: "That is why I ask workers to understand why I insist on keeping the fuel price at RM1.99 per litre.

title: Govt to continue taking necessary measures to address global economic uncertainty
text: "(There is) pressure from economists and the corporate sector suggesting (to me) to be realistic.

title: Govt to continue taking necessary measures to address global economic uncertainty
text: So, I am being realistic, safeguarding the national economy and the people, but I am also pondering on the fate of workers, especially those in the lower-income group," said the Prime Minister.

title: Govt to continue taking necessary measures to address global economic uncertainty
text: Meanwhile, he said the government, through the Ministry of Finance, has announced an additional RM5 billion guarantee under Syarikat Jaminan Pembiayaan Perniagaan (SJPP) to assist micro, small and medium enterprises (MSMEs) affected by global supply disruptions following the United States-Iran conflict in West Asia.

title: Govt to continue taking necessary measures to address global economic uncertainty
text: In addition, Anwar said the government, through Bank Negara Malaysia (BNM), has also announced a RM5 billion SME Stabilisation Relief Facility, bringing the total to RM10 billion to help ease the burden of MSMEs affected by the crisis.

title: Govt to continue taking necessary measures to address global economic uncertainty
text: "This is because the number of workers in MSME companies is large, and they are a key force in generating the country's economic growth," said the Prime Minister.

title: Govt to continue taking necessary measures to address global economic uncertainty
text: - Bernama

title: Govt to allocate over RM710mil to help workers hit by layoffs, says PM
text: Govt to allocate over RM710mil to help workers hit by layoffs, says PM

title: Govt to allocate over RM710mil to help workers hit by layoffs, says PM
text: KUALA LUMPUR: An economic resilience package worth more than RM710mil will be implemented to ensure the country is prepared for crises that could lead to unemployment, says Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim.

title: Govt to allocate over RM710mil to help workers hit by layoffs, says PM
text: The package is called the Progressive Acceleration for Capability and Employment.

title: Govt to allocate over RM710mil to help workers hit by layoffs, says PM
text: "Although the labour market remains strong, we must not be complacent and need to remain proactive and prepared," he said during the Labour Day celebration on Thursday (May 1).

title: Govt to allocate over RM710mil to help workers hit by layoffs, says PM
text: "Especially in this era of rapidly changing technology, we must help workers stay competitive, and for those who may lose their jobs, provide retraining with new skills aligned with current industry demands and needs," he said.

title: Govt to allocate over RM710mil to help workers hit by layoffs, says PM
text: Anwar said the package is part of the economic resilience strategy, with positive implications for human capital development and labour market stability.

title: Govt to allocate over RM710mil to help workers hit by layoffs, says PM
text: He said it will be implemented through four initiatives, starting with social protection preparedness.

title: Govt to allocate over RM710mil to help workers hit by layoffs, says PM
text: "Employment Insurance System benefits will be strengthened where the Human Resources Ministry, through Perkeso, has prepared a fund exceeding RM580mil," he said.

title: Govt to allocate over RM710mil to help workers hit by layoffs, says PM
text: The second initiative will focus on training and job placement empowerment to ensure youths and vulnerable groups remain competitive and productive.

title: Govt to allocate over RM710mil to help workers hit by layoffs, says PM
text: He said the government, through HRD Corp, has allocated RM100mil under the HRD Yakin Madani programme, supported by MyFutureJobs to facilitate job placements.

title: Govt to allocate over RM710mil to help workers hit by layoffs, says PM
text: Anwar said another initiative involves empowering gig workers.

title: Govt to allocate over RM710mil to help workers hit by layoffs, says PM
text: "To diversify skills and strengthen social security protection for gig workers, the government has channelled RM20mil to the Skills Development Fund Corporation, specifically for gig worker training," he said.

title: Govt to allocate over RM710mil to help workers hit by layoffs, says PM
text: He said the fourth initiative is an industrial training matching grant to support micro, small and medium enterprises and startups that take on industrial trainees.

title: Govt to allocate over RM710mil to help workers hit by layoffs, says PM
text: "This initiative will be funded by TalentCorp with an allocation of RM10mil," he said.

title: Asean economic ministers say Middle East tensions could slow regional growth, threaten energy security
text: Asean economic ministers say Middle East tensions could slow regional growth, threaten energy security

title: Asean economic ministers say Middle East tensions could slow regional growth, threaten energy security
text: MANILA: ASEAN economic ministers said in a joint statement on Friday that escalating tensions in the Middle East are posing a growing threat to global energy security and could significantly slow regional growth.

title: Asean economic ministers say Middle East tensions could slow regional growth, threaten energy security
text: "We expressed deep concern that the current disruptions to key maritime routes, particularly the Strait of Hormuz, where around one-quarter of global seaborne oil and liquefied natural gas (LNG) exports pass through, with over 80 per cent destined for Asia, are posing severe risks to global energy security, fuelling persistent volatility in oil and LNG prices, and sharply increasing freight, insurance, and logistics costs," the ASEAN Economic Community Council said.

title: Asean economic ministers say Middle East tensions could slow regional growth, threaten energy security
text: - Reuters

title: Workers deserve a fair share of nation's economic growth, Ramanan says in Labour Day speech
text: Workers deserve a fair share of nation's economic growth, Ramanan says in Labour Day speech

title: Workers deserve a fair share of nation's economic growth, Ramanan says in Labour Day speech
text: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia’s workers are the backbone of the nation’s growth and must have a fair share of the country’s economic progress, says Human Resources Minister Datuk Seri R. Ramanan.

title: Workers deserve a fair share of nation's economic growth, Ramanan says in Labour Day speech
text: Speaking during the Labour Day celebration at Bukit Jalil on Friday (May 1), Ramanan said every achievement enjoyed by the country was built on their sacrifices and hard work.

title: Workers deserve a fair share of nation's economic growth, Ramanan says in Labour Day speech
text: "Every solid building, every functioning system and every growth figure achieved was born from the hands of workers.

title: Workers deserve a fair share of nation's economic growth, Ramanan says in Labour Day speech
text: "When we defend workers, we uplift the standards of the entire country," he said in the presence of Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim.

title: Workers deserve a fair share of nation's economic growth, Ramanan says in Labour Day speech
text: Ramanan said the Madani government’s economic reforms had strengthened the labour market and improved Malaysia’s competitiveness.

title: Workers deserve a fair share of nation's economic growth, Ramanan says in Labour Day speech
text: He said the economy grew by 5.2% last year, while Malaysia’s ranking in the World Competitiveness Ranking improved to 23rd place from 34th previously.

title: Workers deserve a fair share of nation's economic growth, Ramanan says in Labour Day speech
text: The unemployment rate also dropped to 2.9%, the lowest in 11 years, while women’s labour force participation rose to 56.5%.

title: Workers deserve a fair share of nation's economic growth, Ramanan says in Labour Day speech
text: "They are not just participating quietly, but are actively pushing the country forward," he said.

title: Workers deserve a fair share of nation's economic growth, Ramanan says in Labour Day speech
text: Ramanan added that the government’s minimum wage policy had benefitted more than 4.4 million workers, while labour law amendments had improved employee welfare.

title: Workers deserve a fair share of nation's economic growth, Ramanan says in Labour Day speech
text: Among the reforms introduced were 98 days of maternity leave, seven days of paternity leave and the reduction of maximum weekly working hours to 45 hours.

title: Workers deserve a fair share of nation's economic growth, Ramanan says in Labour Day speech
text: However, he acknowledged that many Malaysians were still struggling with the rising cost of living.

title: Workers deserve a fair share of nation's economic growth, Ramanan says in Labour Day speech
text: "We must be honest.

title: Workers deserve a fair share of nation's economic growth, Ramanan says in Labour Day speech
text: There are still many workers who wake up early and return home late at night while worrying about the bills that need to be paid.

title: Workers deserve a fair share of nation's economic growth, Ramanan says in Labour Day speech
text: "Progress that is not shared fairly is not perfect progress," he said.

title: Workers deserve a fair share of nation's economic growth, Ramanan says in Labour Day speech
text: Beginning in June, he said, the government would implement the Lindung 24 Jam (24-hour coverage) scheme, extending social protection coverage to 9.6 million employees outside working hours.

title: Workers deserve a fair share of nation's economic growth, Ramanan says in Labour Day speech
text: Ramanan also announced that the Housewives Social Security Scheme (SKSSR) age limit would be increased from 55 to 60, benefitting more than 720,000 women.

title: Workers deserve a fair share of nation's economic growth, Ramanan says in Labour Day speech
text: "Housewives care for children, parents and sick family members.

title: Workers deserve a fair share of nation's economic growth, Ramanan says in Labour Day speech
text: They contribute greatly to the country and it is time for us to repay their service with better protection," he said.

title: Building resilience for MSMEs in a changing digital economy
text: Building resilience for MSMEs in a changing digital economy

title: Building resilience for MSMEs in a changing digital economy
text: FOR Malaysia’s micro, small and medium enterprises (MSMEs), resilience is increasingly shaped by forces beyond their immediate control.

title: Building resilience for MSMEs in a changing digital economy
text: From shifting global trade dynamics to evolving consumer expectations, the operating landscape has grown more complex in recent years.

title: Building resilience for MSMEs in a changing digital economy
text: At the same time, a new generation of digital-first consumers is raising expectations around value, convenience and experience, prompting businesses to adapt more quickly than before.

title: Building resilience for MSMEs in a changing digital economy
text: For Shopee Malaysia vice president Ian Ho, these shifts point to a broader reality.

title: Building resilience for MSMEs in a changing digital economy
text: “The environment that MSMEs operate in today is very different from even a few years ago,” he said.

title: Building resilience for MSMEs in a changing digital economy
text: “Resilience is no longer just about managing day-to-day challenges.

title: Building resilience for MSMEs in a changing digital economy
text:
It’s about having the ability to adapt and grow as the ecosystem itself evolves.”

MSMEs contribute nearly 39.5% of Malaysia’s GDP and account for 48.7% of employment.

title: Building resilience for MSMEs in a changing digital economy
text: As their role in the economy continues to expand, ensuring their long-term competitiveness has become increasingly important.

title: Building resilience for MSMEs in a changing digital economy
text:
Adapting to a more complex operating environment

While short-term support measures such as subsidies can help businesses navigate immediate pressures, Ho noted that these alone are not sufficient.

title: Building resilience for MSMEs in a changing digital economy
text: “They play an important role, especially during periods of uncertainty, but they are only one part of the picture,” he said.

title: Building resilience for MSMEs in a changing digital economy
text:
“What matters just as much is continued investment in the infrastructure and tools that businesses rely on to operate and scale.”

Over time, e-commerce platforms have evolved from transactional marketplaces into broader digital ecosystems.

title: Building resilience for MSMEs in a changing digital economy
text: Today, they connect sellers not only to customers, but also to logistics networks, financial services and cross-border opportunities that were once out of reach for many smaller businesses.

title: Building resilience for MSMEs in a changing digital economy
text: This shift is reflected in initiatives such as Shopee Lindung Niaga, a RM150mil programme aimed at supporting Malaysian sellers across logistics, working capital and fulfilment.

title: Building resilience for MSMEs in a changing digital economy
text: Rather than focusing on a single intervention, the approach brings together different forms of support that address both immediate needs and longer-term capability building.

title: Building resilience for MSMEs in a changing digital economy
text:
Balancing immediate support with long-term capability building

“In a more price-conscious environment, maintaining transaction momentum becomes important,” Ho explained.

title: Building resilience for MSMEs in a changing digital economy
text: Through measures such as Free Shipping Voucher programmes, buyers are better able to manage costs, which in turn helps sustain demand for sellers.

title: Building resilience for MSMEs in a changing digital economy
text:
“It’s about ensuring that sellers continue to have opportunities to generate sales, even as conditions change.”

Shopee has also introduced reduced commissions on an additional over 100 categories, expanding on its existing 0% commission policy for daily essentials, helping keep everyday items accessible for buyers while supporting sellers operating in these segments.

title: Building resilience for MSMEs in a changing digital economy
text: At the same time, more targeted support is being extended to sellers with specialised logistics needs, particularly those dealing with bulkier or higher-value items such as furniture and appliances.

title: Building resilience for MSMEs in a changing digital economy
text: By simplifying delivery arrangements through integrated logistics networks, these sellers can reach more customers without managing the complexity themselves.

title: Building resilience for MSMEs in a changing digital economy
text: Access to working capital remains another key consideration.

title: Building resilience for MSMEs in a changing digital economy
text: “For many small businesses, it’s not just about demand.

title: Building resilience for MSMEs in a changing digital economy
text: It’s about having the resources at the right time to respond,” Ho said.

title: Building resilience for MSMEs in a changing digital economy
text: Financing solutions offered in partnership with Monee, such as SLoan for Sellers and Quick Funds, provide greater flexibility in managing cash flow, while faster access to earnings helps address shorter-term liquidity needs.

title: Building resilience for MSMEs in a changing digital economy
text: Operational capabilities are also evolving.

title: Building resilience for MSMEs in a changing digital economy
text: Fulfilled by Shopee (FBS), which provides warehousing and logistics support, allows sellers to outsource parts of their operations that would otherwise require additional manpower or storage.

title: Building resilience for MSMEs in a changing digital economy
text: “This gives sellers more flexibility in how they manage their overheads, especially during peak periods,” Ho pointed out.

title: Building resilience for MSMEs in a changing digital economy
text: For buyers, this translates into more reliable delivery timelines and a smoother experience.

title: Building resilience for MSMEs in a changing digital economy
text: For sellers, it can also support growth, with those using FBS seeing an average uplift of around 20% in store traffic.

title: Building resilience for MSMEs in a changing digital economy
text:
A shared effort for the next phase of growth

Beyond individual programmes, Ho emphasised that resilience depends on how well the broader ecosystem functions.

title: Building resilience for MSMEs in a changing digital economy
text: “No single player can do this alone,” he highlighted.

title: Building resilience for MSMEs in a changing digital economy
text:
“It requires coordination across platforms, policymakers and the wider community to create the right conditions for businesses to grow.”

Recent measures, including expanded financing support for MSMEs, reflect a growing recognition of this shared responsibility.

title: Building resilience for MSMEs in a changing digital economy
text: At the same time, digital platforms continue to invest in infrastructure and tools that enable businesses to participate more fully in the digital economy.

title: Building resilience for MSMEs in a changing digital economy
text: For Shopee, this builds on a longstanding commitment to support local entrepreneurs since its early days.

title: Building resilience for MSMEs in a changing digital economy
text: Expanding access to markets, logistics and digital tools has helped bring more sellers online, including many outside major commercial centres.

title: Building resilience for MSMEs in a changing digital economy
text: But as the landscape continues to evolve, the focus is also shifting forward.

title: Building resilience for MSMEs in a changing digital economy
text: A new generation of entrepreneurs is emerging alongside the next wave of digital consumers.

title: Building resilience for MSMEs in a changing digital economy
text: These businesses are often more agile and digitally native, but they also operate in a more competitive and fast-changing environment.

title: Building resilience for MSMEs in a changing digital economy
text: “Access is only one part of the equation,” Ho said.

title: Building resilience for MSMEs in a changing digital economy
text:
“To succeed over time, businesses also need the skills and confidence to adapt, whether that’s in how they market their products, manage operations or expand into new markets.”

Programmes such as Shopee University and the Shopee Xpert Community continue to support capability building, alongside collaborations with ecosystem partners and government agencies to extend opportunities to underserved communities.

title: Building resilience for MSMEs in a changing digital economy
text: As Malaysia’s digital economy continues to grow, the challenge will be ensuring that MSMEs are not only able to participate but to do so sustainably.

title: Building resilience for MSMEs in a changing digital economy
text: “Resilience is not something that can be built overnight,” Ho added.

title: Building resilience for MSMEs in a changing digital economy
text:
“It comes from consistent investment, from having the right support systems in place and from an ecosystem that continues to evolve alongside the businesses it serves.”

In that sense, the next phase of growth will depend less on any single intervention and more on how these collective efforts come together to support a more resilient and inclusive MSME ecosystem.

title: Building resilience for MSMEs in a changing digital economy
text: To learn more, visit http://shopee.com.my/

title: Balancing spending cuts and growth
text: Balancing spending cuts and growth

title: Balancing spending cuts and growth
text: PETALING JAYA: Balancing reduced government spending, sustaining economic growth and ensuring citizens’ wellbeing will be the main priority amid surging energy costs and an increasingly unsustainable fuel subsidy bill based on an average Brent crude price of US$65 a barrel.

title: Balancing spending cuts and growth
text: While economists see the government’s move to achieve savings of up to RM10bil through a reduction in operating expenditure (opex) across the board announced on Wednesday as a timely measure to sustain the fuel subsidy, they urged caution and asked for clarity on the deficit reduction goals.

title: Balancing spending cuts and growth
text: Bank Muamalat Malaysia Bhd chief economist Mohd Afzanizam Abdul Rashid believes the move to cut the opex could be a form of budget recalibration as the government needs to reflect its spending with the current reality.

title: Balancing spending cuts and growth
text: CGS International (CGSI) Securities economist Ahmad Nazmi Idrus said the question would be how the government balances these savings and ensures economic growth.

title: Balancing spending cuts and growth
text: “That balance would be the key.

title: Balancing spending cuts and growth
text: It’s hard to say until we see the growth outcomes,” he added.

title: Balancing spending cuts and growth
text: Bank Negara Malaysia (BNM) projected gross domestic product (GDP) growth of between 4% and 5% this year.

title: Balancing spending cuts and growth
text: Advanced first-quarter 2026 (1Q26) GDP data showed the economy grew 5.3% year-on-year, moderating from 6.3% in 4Q25.

title: Balancing spending cuts and growth
text: Fuel subsidies for RON95 petrol and diesel surged to RM7bil in April from RM700mil before the Middle East conflict began, with the government reviewing the proposed implementation of a more targeted diesel subsidy programme for Sabah and Sarawak similar to the Budi95 programme for RON95 petrol to replace the current diesel subsidy scheme.

title: Balancing spending cuts and growth
text: At press time, the Brent crude futures contract briefly traded above US$125 a barrel as the market reacted to news of a prolonged blockade of the Strait of Hormuz and the implications for energy supply, indicating prices could trend higher.

title: Balancing spending cuts and growth
text: Brent crude spot prices have also traded above US$110.

title: Balancing spending cuts and growth
text: Socio-Economic Research Centre (SERC) executive director Lee Heng Guie said the current targeted and disciplined fiscal support over broad stimulus “is deemed appropriate during a supply-driven oil shock”.

title: Balancing spending cuts and growth
text: He noted the government should resist “overspending” to shield consumers from high energy prices given the limited fiscal space.

title: Balancing spending cuts and growth
text: “Fuel subsidies act as an implicit stimulus for consumption by keeping domestic fuel prices artificially low,” Lee added.

title: Balancing spending cuts and growth
text: He warned added spending could worsen the supply crunch, drive up prices further, and risked worsening inflation without increasing output as it stimulates demand just when goods become scarce or when production gets disrupted through shortage of raw materials and higher prices.

title: Balancing spending cuts and growth
text: “This risks creating stagflation, a combination of slower economic growth and high inflation,” Lee said.

title: Balancing spending cuts and growth
text: SERC projects GDP growth of 4% to 4.5% for Malaysia this year, but forecasts 3.5% if the conflict lasts between three and six months and a contraction of 0.5% in a scenario where it lasts more than six to 12 months.

title: Balancing spending cuts and growth
text: CGSI’s Nazmi said one option for the government to cushion the impact of spending cuts, including a further reduction of the fuel subsidy, would be to convert unused Budi95 quota into higher future quotas, or convert it to cash or vouchers for essential goods.

title: Balancing spending cuts and growth
text: “This could discourage driving and at the same time help cushion inflation impact to consumers,” he said.

title: Balancing spending cuts and growth
text: Mohd Afzanizam said the government could assist with guidance on its stance in dealing with the current high oil price environment and the impact on the deficit more clearly, which would help the markets as well as businesses and households.

title: Balancing spending cuts and growth
text: “Perhaps, the fiscal deficit target of 3.5% of GDP for 2026 would need to be revised higher, possibly in the region of 4% to 4.5%,” he said.

title: Balancing spending cuts and growth
text: HSBC Bank plc senior Asean economist Yun Liu said in a report that Malaysia’s hefty subsidy bill raises questions on what comes next for the RON95 policy, as it imposes pressures on the country’s fiscal coffers and its 2026 budget deficit forecast of 3.5% of GDP.

title: Balancing spending cuts and growth
text: She remains neutral on the country’s outlook, as the hard data points to economic resilience, while the inflation trajectory would depend on fiscal adjustments to the RON95 policy.

title: Balancing spending cuts and growth
text: “On growth, BNM is likely to maintain its cautious optimistic tone and continue acknowledging external challenges.

title: Balancing spending cuts and growth
text: “Overall, we do not expect BNM to move in either direction in 2026 and 2027.

title: Balancing spending cuts and growth

title: Balancing spending cuts and growth

title: Balancing spending cuts and growth

title: Malaysia inflation edges up in March as fuel costs rise, trade growth moderates
text: Malaysia inflation edges up in March as fuel costs rise, trade growth moderates

title: Malaysia inflation edges up in March as fuel costs rise, trade growth moderates
text: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia’s inflation ticked higher in March 2026, driven largely by rising fuel prices, while wholesale and retail trade growth moderated amid weaker vehicle sales, according to the monthly highlights from Bank Negara.

title: Malaysia inflation edges up in March as fuel costs rise, trade growth moderates
text: Headline inflation rose to 1.7% year-on-year in March from 1.4% in February, while core inflation increased to 2.1% from 2.0%.

title: Malaysia inflation edges up in March as fuel costs rise, trade growth moderates
text: The uptick was mainly attributed to higher retail fuel prices in line with elevated global oil prices.

title: Malaysia inflation edges up in March as fuel costs rise, trade growth moderates
text: However, the overall impact was cushioned by targeted subsidies, with RON95 petrol maintained at RM1.99 per litre under the BUDI95 programme, and subsidised diesel prices continuing at RM2.15 per litre in Sabah and Sarawak.

title: Malaysia inflation edges up in March as fuel costs rise, trade growth moderates
text: Bank Negara noted that lower prices for fresh food items, particularly seafood and meat, partially offset the increase in fuel costs.

title: Malaysia inflation edges up in March as fuel costs rise, trade growth moderates
text: The Index of Wholesale and Retail Trade (IOWRT) expanded by 4.4% in February, easing from 5.8% in January.

title: Malaysia inflation edges up in March as fuel costs rise, trade growth moderates
text: “Growth in the wholesale and retail segment improved.

title: Malaysia inflation edges up in March as fuel costs rise, trade growth moderates
text: This was mainly driven by the wholesale of food, beverages, tobacco, and household goods; as well as retail trade in non- specialised stores and retail sale of other household equipment in specialised stores, respectively,” Bank Negara said in the highlights.

title: Malaysia inflation edges up in March as fuel costs rise, trade growth moderates
text: This was, however, more than offset by a contraction in the motor vehicles segment, reflecting lower car sales alongside weaker motorcycle sales, maintenance and repair activity.

title: Malaysia inflation edges up in March as fuel costs rise, trade growth moderates
text: Credit conditions remained stable, with total credit to the private non-financial sector expanding by 5.6% in March, unchanged from the previous month.

title: Malaysia inflation edges up in March as fuel costs rise, trade growth moderates
text: The growth was supported by stronger business loan expansion, which rose to 5.8% from 4.6% in February, particularly among non-SMEs for working capital purposes.

title: Malaysia inflation edges up in March as fuel costs rise, trade growth moderates
text: “Household loan growth was sustained at 5.4% from February’s 5.5% amid steady loan growth across most purposes,” it said.

title: Malaysia inflation edges up in March as fuel costs rise, trade growth moderates
text: Meanwhile, corporate bond growth moderated to 5.8% from 7.4%, due to lower bond issuances compared with the same period last year.

title: Malaysia inflation edges up in March as fuel costs rise, trade growth moderates
text: Malaysia’s banking system continued to demonstrate resilience, with asset quality remaining sound.

title: Malaysia inflation edges up in March as fuel costs rise, trade growth moderates
text: Gross impaired loans stood at 1.4%, while net impaired loans were stable at 1.0%.

title: Malaysia inflation edges up in March as fuel costs rise, trade growth moderates
text: Loan loss coverage remained prudent at 125% of gross impaired loans, indicating sufficient buffers against potential risks.

title: Malaysia inflation edges up in March as fuel costs rise, trade growth moderates
text: On the external front, financial markets were affected by heightened geopolitical tensions stemming from the escalation of the West Asia conflict, which dampened global investor sentiment.

title: Malaysia inflation edges up in March as fuel costs rise, trade growth moderates
text: The ringgit weakened by 3.8% against the US dollar in March, broadly in line with regional currencies, amid a stronger greenback driven by risk-off sentiment.

title: Malaysia inflation edges up in March as fuel costs rise, trade growth moderates
text: Meanwhile, the 10-year Malaysian Government Securities (MGS) yield rose by 15 basis points, reflecting higher global inflation expectations.

title: Malaysia inflation edges up in March as fuel costs rise, trade growth moderates
text: Equities also came under pressure, with the FBM KLCI declining by 1.5% during the month as investors turned cautious.

title: Malaysia inflation edges up in March as fuel costs rise, trade growth moderates
text: Despite external headwinds, Malaysia’s financial system remained well-buffered.

title: Malaysia inflation edges up in March as fuel costs rise, trade growth moderates
text: The banking sector’s Liquidity Coverage Ratio stood at 144.6% in March, comfortably above regulatory requirements, providing a strong cushion against potential liquidity shocks.

title: Repositioning Asean in today’s evolving global order
text: Repositioning Asean in today’s evolving global order

title: Repositioning Asean in today’s evolving global order
text: GEOPOLITICAL conflict, resource constraints and economic fragmentation are reshaping the global landscape at an unprecedented pace.

title: Repositioning Asean in today’s evolving global order
text: Supply chains that once flowed seamlessly across continents are now blocked by conflict-driven energy shocks, protectionist policies and volatile commodity markets.

title: Repositioning Asean in today’s evolving global order
text: It is not surprising that the natural reaction to these developments is often pessimistic, as fears of a recession and inflationary pressures continue to escalate.

title: Repositioning Asean in today’s evolving global order
text: Today, governments are grappling with fiscal pressures, rising debt burdens and growing social inequalities; businesses are delaying investments amid uncertainty, and consumers are feeling the strain of rising food and daily living costs.

title: Repositioning Asean in today’s evolving global order
text: Navigating this environment requires more than short-term crisis response; it calls for stepping back to re-examine and rethink long-standing ‘business-as-usual’ approaches across economic structures, business operations and risk management.

title: Repositioning Asean in today’s evolving global order
text: What we are seeing may not just be a period of disruption but a turning point, particularly for Asean to reposition itself in a rapidly evolving global order.

title: Repositioning Asean in today’s evolving global order
text: Against this landscape, the region has an opportunity to emerge as a hub for sustainable development and climate-aligned innovation.

title: Repositioning Asean in today’s evolving global order
text: It stands to benefit as the global economy shifts towards a more diversified, sustainable and resilient model of value creation.

title: Repositioning Asean in today’s evolving global order
text:
Accelerating the energy transition

Oil price volatility and gas supply disruptions in conflict zones have accelerated the urgency of South-East Asia’s renewable energy ambitions.

title: Repositioning Asean in today’s evolving global order
text: Rather than being constrained by fossil fuel instability, the region can leapfrog to affordable, decentralised clean energy.

title: Repositioning Asean in today’s evolving global order
text: This can be achieved by redirecting capital from fossil fuel extraction to renewable energy, energy efficiency and storage, EV ecosystems and more resilient grids.

title: Repositioning Asean in today’s evolving global order
text: The region’s young, educated workforce and abundant renewable energy potential provide a foundation for region-wide leadership in green energy industries.

title: Repositioning Asean in today’s evolving global order
text: Its solar potential and rich biodiversity are also driving investments in green hydrogen, biofuels and nature-based solutions.

title: Repositioning Asean in today’s evolving global order
text: South-East Asia is rapidly accelerating its renewable energy ambitions.

title: Repositioning Asean in today’s evolving global order
text:
— Photo by Unsplash

Diversifying the supply chain

From critical minerals to agricultural commodities, bottlenecks and price volatility are reshaping industrial strategies globally.

title: Repositioning Asean in today’s evolving global order
text: Supply chain disruptions are shifting operating models from “just in time” to "just in case”.

title: Repositioning Asean in today’s evolving global order
text: With oil price volatility increasing the cost of plastics, some companies have switched their plastic packaging to locally sourced, renewable materials.

title: Repositioning Asean in today’s evolving global order
text: The uncertainty of raw material supply makes circularity an important part of supply chains.

title: Repositioning Asean in today’s evolving global order
text: By shifting towards repairing, reusing, refurbishing and sharing resources, businesses can reduce dependence on new raw materials while lowering energy use.

title: Repositioning Asean in today’s evolving global order
text: As companies reduce their overreliance on any single market, Asean can position itself as a multi-hub production base, accelerating technology transfer, green industrialisation and job creation.

title: Repositioning Asean in today’s evolving global order
text: This shift is already evident across the region.

title: Repositioning Asean in today’s evolving global order
text: Vietnam and Malaysia are attracting high-tech manufacturing investments; Indonesia is strengthening its position as a key battery and electric-mobility player and Thailand continues to advance its bio-circular-green economy.

title: Repositioning Asean in today’s evolving global order
text:
Sustainable agriculture

Food and agricultural instability is not only a risk to be managed, but also a catalyst for Asean to strengthen its position as a future-ready, sustainable agrifood hub.

title: Repositioning Asean in today’s evolving global order
text: The region has strong potential to become a critical source of stable and sustainable food production, creating significant opportunities for investment and innovation across the agrifood value chain.

title: Repositioning Asean in today’s evolving global order
text: The Asean region has the potential to be a key hub for stable, sustainable food production.

title: Repositioning Asean in today’s evolving global order
text:
— Photo by Unsplash

Climate-smart farming practices, regenerative agriculture, precision farming and digital agriculture solutions are gaining traction as essential tools for improving yields, resilience and resource efficiency.

title: Repositioning Asean in today’s evolving global order
text: The rising demand for more transparent, traceable and low-carbon food supply chains presents opportunities for regional producers to move up the value chain and capture higher-value markets.

title: Repositioning Asean in today’s evolving global order
text: At the same time, rising fuel prices can accelerate the adoption of sustainable biofuels and renewable diesel, with countries such as Indonesia already making progress through ambitious blending mandates and policy support.

title: Repositioning Asean in today’s evolving global order
text:
Banks as stewards of resilient economies and inclusive growth

As economic pressures mount and inequality widens, vulnerable communities face disproportionate impacts from climate and market shocks.

title: Repositioning Asean in today’s evolving global order
text: This makes social resilience, anchored in inclusive development, decent work, community empowerment and access to finance, more important than ever for national stability and sustainable corporate development.

title: Repositioning Asean in today’s evolving global order
text: For financial institutions, this moment carries added responsibility.

title: Repositioning Asean in today’s evolving global order
text: In the near term, this means understanding the pain points customers face and providing relief and support, while stepping up financial literacy and economic empowerment programmes to help them navigate rising inflation and periods of uncertainty.

title: Repositioning Asean in today’s evolving global order
text: In the longer term, the role of banks becomes more significant as the allocation of capital, structuring of financing, and the assessment of risk are not neutral activities; they shape the direction of economic development across the region.

title: Repositioning Asean in today’s evolving global order
text: In navigating this period, banks need to increasingly integrate governance, disciplined oversight and long-term clarity of purpose.

title: Repositioning Asean in today’s evolving global order
text: In a fragmented world, strong stewardship of capital deployment will influence whether economies merely weather disruption or emerge stronger from it.

title: Repositioning Asean in today’s evolving global order
text:
Seizing opportunities

The current environment is a trigger for decisive action.

title: Repositioning Asean in today’s evolving global order
text: It is accelerating collaboration, redesign and investment to strengthen resilience and drive long-term structural change.

title: Repositioning Asean in today’s evolving global order
text: The countries and companies that will thrive in the next decade are those that treat sustainability not as compliance, but as a catalyst for how they compete and build trust.

title: Repositioning Asean in today’s evolving global order
text: The scale of opportunity before Asean is considerable.

title: Repositioning Asean in today’s evolving global order
text: Areas such as transition finance, nature-based solutions and blue economy investments represent some of the region’s most underdeveloped yet high-potential frontiers.

title: Repositioning Asean in today’s evolving global order
text: As global capital increasingly seeks credible climate alignment, traceability, sustainable sourcing and ecosystem stewardship, the region’s natural endowment and demographic dividend position it well to attract long-term investment.

title: Repositioning Asean in today’s evolving global order
text: This potential can be realised if governance standards, transparency and institutional capability continue to strengthen in tandem.

title: Repositioning Asean in today’s evolving global order
text: Sustainability is fast becoming Asean’s competitive advantage.

title: Repositioning Asean in today’s evolving global order
text: The question is no longer whether the region can lead, but how decisively it will seize this moment.

title: Repositioning Asean in today’s evolving global order
text: This article is published as part of CIMB Group Holdings Bhd’s “The Cooler Earth” sustainability series.

text: KUALA LUMPUR: The FBM KLCI fell a quarter percent in morning trade as traders opted for safety ahead of the Labour Day weekend as a jump in oil prices sparked fresh inflation fears.

text: Hoewever, the softer sentiment was more noticeable on the broader market as the number of declining issues outnumbered gainers 747 to 293, for a ratio of nearly three-to-one.
