title: Cost of building materials has increased by over 12%, says Economy Ministry
text: Cost of building materials has increased by over 12%, says Economy Ministry

title: Cost of building materials has increased by over 12%, says Economy Ministry
text: PETALING JAYA: The cost of building materials has gone up by over 12% due to knock-on effects of rising fuel prices, says Akmal Nasrullah Mohd Nasir.

title: Cost of building materials has increased by over 12%, says Economy Ministry
text: "The construction sector is also experiencing the effects of rising fuel and material costs.

title: Cost of building materials has increased by over 12%, says Economy Ministry
text: Since late March 2026, the industry has reported cost pressures due to increases in diesel, bitumen, premix, logistics and building materials," the Economy Minister said during his briefing on the energy crisis on Tuesday (April 28).

title: Cost of building materials has increased by over 12%, says Economy Ministry
text:
Citing data from Construction Industry Development Board (CIDB), Akmal said the average price for seven key materials including bitumen, concrete, sand, aggregates, steel and bricks, has increased by 12.59%,

"Building materials account for the largest share of construction costs at 64.2%, followed by labour at 32.8% and machinery at 3.0%," he said.

title: Cost of building materials has increased by over 12%, says Economy Ministry
text: This means that any increase in material costs has a direct impact on project costs, contractors' cash flow and project implementation.

title: Cost of building materials has increased by over 12%, says Economy Ministry
text: The government is taking this seriously, as development projects are not merely budgetary figures but involve essential infrastructure such as roads, schools and clinics," he added.

title: Cost of building materials has increased by over 12%, says Economy Ministry
text: He said mitigation measures such as getting banks to offer loan rescheduling and restructuring options under the Syarikat Jaminan Pembiayaan Perniagaan Berhad (SJPP) scheme to ease cash flow pressures, particularly for small contractors.

title: Cost of building materials has increased by over 12%, says Economy Ministry
text: As for the labour market, he said it remains stable although delayed effects are expected.

title: Cost of building materials has increased by over 12%, says Economy Ministry
text: "A total of 5,734 job losses were recorded in the first three weeks of April 2026, lower than in March and February.

title: Cost of building materials has increased by over 12%, says Economy Ministry
text: However, impacts are expected to become more pronounced in the second quarter of 2026, particularly due to the lagged effects of the global energy crisis," he said.

title: Cost of building materials has increased by over 12%, says Economy Ministry
text: He said the government would continue to strengthen labour market resilience through social protection, skills training, job placement, support for gig workers and strengthening SMEs.

title: Food supply chain shows early stress as fuel costs rise, says Akmal Nasrullah
text: Food supply chain shows early stress as fuel costs rise, says Akmal Nasrullah

title: Food supply chain shows early stress as fuel costs rise, says Akmal Nasrullah
text: PETALING JAYA: Cost pressures are beginning to emerge in the domestic food supply chain, says Economy Minister Akmal Nasrullah Mohd Nasir.

title: Food supply chain shows early stress as fuel costs rise, says Akmal Nasrullah
text: "In terms of cost of living, selected food prices remain within a controlled range, although input pressures are beginning to emerge.

title: Food supply chain shows early stress as fuel costs rise, says Akmal Nasrullah
text: Between April 20 and April 22, price movements for selected food items remained below 10%," he said during a briefing on the energy crisis on Tuesday (April 28).

title: Food supply chain shows early stress as fuel costs rise, says Akmal Nasrullah
text: He said the average price of standard chicken rose 3.3% to RM9.70 per kg, Grade C eggs increased 7.3% to RM3.93 per 10 eggs, while beef prices declined 3.8% to RM35.92 per kg.

title: Food supply chain shows early stress as fuel costs rise, says Akmal Nasrullah
text: For seafood, Indian mackerel fell 1.1% to RM16.10 per kg, while scad and white prawns rose 0.95% to RM10.36 per kg and 0.61% to RM32.03 per kg respectively.

title: Food supply chain shows early stress as fuel costs rise, says Akmal Nasrullah
text: In the vegetable category, mustard greens rose from RM6.16 to RM6.51 per kg, spinach increased nearly 5% to RM5.59 per kg, and fresh coconut milk rose 3.6% to RM16.41 per kg.

title: Food supply chain shows early stress as fuel costs rise, says Akmal Nasrullah
text: "These trends indicate that input and logistics cost pressures are beginning to signal early stress within the domestic food supply chain," he said.

title: Food supply chain shows early stress as fuel costs rise, says Akmal Nasrullah
text: Akmal Nasrullah said the government will monitor and implement necessary measures to mitigate the impact on consumers and maintain price stability.

title: Food supply chain shows early stress as fuel costs rise, says Akmal Nasrullah
text: He said monitoring must extend beyond retail prices and cover upstream factors including fertiliser, diesel, energy, raw materials, production, transportation and distribution.

title: Food supply chain shows early stress as fuel costs rise, says Akmal Nasrullah
text: He added that the government is focusing on fertiliser supply and agricultural inputs to ensure food security, including promoting bio-organic fertilisers and circular economy practices using biomass and agricultural waste.

title: Food supply chain shows early stress as fuel costs rise, says Akmal Nasrullah
text: Akmal Nasrullah said this week's National Economic Action Council meeting focused on the electricity sector, construction industry, labour market and the medium-term economic outlook.

title: Food supply chain shows early stress as fuel costs rise, says Akmal Nasrullah
text: He said national electricity supply remains secure, adding that the main risk is uncertainty in global fuel prices.

title: Food supply chain shows early stress as fuel costs rise, says Akmal Nasrullah
text: "As the prices of fuels such as coal and gas increase, the cost of electricity generation also rises, potentially affecting consumer tariffs if not carefully managed," he said.

title: Food supply chain shows early stress as fuel costs rise, says Akmal Nasrullah
text: As at April 25, the electricity generation mix was dominated by coal at 54% and gas at 40%, meaning a large portion of supply remains exposed to global market movements.

title: Food supply chain shows early stress as fuel costs rise, says Akmal Nasrullah
text: He said coal imports in the first quarter of 2026 totalled 7.94 million metric tonnes, with annual requirements projected at 35.99 million metric tonnes.

title: Food supply chain shows early stress as fuel costs rise, says Akmal Nasrullah
text: Akmal Nasrullah said the Energy Commission is monitoring coal stock levels at power plants to ensure minimum supply requirements are maintained.

title: Food supply chain shows early stress as fuel costs rise, says Akmal Nasrullah
text: For gas, he said average consumption for electricity generation stood at about 1,011 million standard cubic feet per day in the first quarter, largely supported by domestic sources from Kerteh.

title: Food supply chain shows early stress as fuel costs rise, says Akmal Nasrullah
text: However, he said the share of gas in the generation mix is expected to decline between July and August due to maintenance works that will limit supply to the power sector.

title: Food supply chain shows early stress as fuel costs rise, says Akmal Nasrullah
text: He said under such conditions, the national electricity system must remain flexible in using alternative fuels to ensure stability.

title: Food supply chain shows early stress as fuel costs rise, says Akmal Nasrullah
text: Akmal Nasrullah said electricity demand is also rising.

title: Food supply chain shows early stress as fuel costs rise, says Akmal Nasrullah
text: "Between April 20 and April 25, the average peak demand rose 1.9% to 20,640MW, compared with 20,257MW the previous week.

title: Food supply chain shows early stress as fuel costs rise, says Akmal Nasrullah
text: "A new peak demand of 21,468MW was recorded on April 23, surpassing the previous high of 21,276MW," he said.

title: Food supply chain shows early stress as fuel costs rise, says Akmal Nasrullah
text: He said the rise was largely driven by hot weather and higher air-conditioning use.

title: Food supply chain shows early stress as fuel costs rise, says Akmal Nasrullah
text: Akmal Nasrullah said generation costs for May are projected to rise due to higher coal prices, estimated at RM21.28 per million British thermal unit compared with a base price of RM19.14.

title: Food supply chain shows early stress as fuel costs rise, says Akmal Nasrullah
text: He said the government has assured it will minimise the impact of electricity cost changes on households and businesses.

title: Food supply chain shows early stress as fuel costs rise, says Akmal Nasrullah
text: About 85% of households, or 7.5 million domestic users consuming below 600kWh, will remain fully exempt from the Automatic Fuel Adjustment charge, he added.

title: Food supply chain shows early stress as fuel costs rise, says Akmal Nasrullah
text: "The government's message is clear: electricity supply remains secure, but generation costs must be managed prudently as the country remains exposed to global fuel price fluctuations," he said.

title: Food supply chain shows early stress as fuel costs rise, says Akmal Nasrullah
text: He added that simple actions such as setting air conditioning at reasonable temperatures, switching off unused appliances and managing electricity use during peak hours can help reduce demand and stabilise generation costs.

title: Explainer-The Hormuz digital chokepoint: How does the Iran war threaten subsea cables?
text: Explainer-The Hormuz digital chokepoint: How does the Iran war threaten subsea cables?

title: Explainer-The Hormuz digital chokepoint: How does the Iran war threaten subsea cables?
text: DUBAI, April 28 (Reuters) - Iran warned last week that submarine ⁠cables in the Strait of Hormuz were a vulnerable point for the region's digital economy, raising concerns about potential attacks on critical infrastructure.

title: Explainer-The Hormuz digital chokepoint: How does the Iran war threaten subsea cables?
text: The narrow waterway, already a chokepoint for ⁠global oil shipments, is equally vital for the digital world.

title: Explainer-The Hormuz digital chokepoint: How does the Iran war threaten subsea cables?
text: Several fibre-optic cables snake across the seabed of the strait, connecting countries from India and Southeast ‌Asia to Europe via the Gulf states and Egypt.

title: Explainer-The Hormuz digital chokepoint: How does the Iran war threaten subsea cables?
text: WHAT MAKES UNDERSEA CABLES IMPORTANT?

title: Explainer-The Hormuz digital chokepoint: How does the Iran war threaten subsea cables?
text: Subsea cables are fibre-optic or electrical cables laid on the sea floor to transmit data and power.

title: Explainer-The Hormuz digital chokepoint: How does the Iran war threaten subsea cables?
text: They carry around 99% of the world's internet traffic, according to the ITU, the United Nations specialized agency for digital technologies.

title: Explainer-The Hormuz digital chokepoint: How does the Iran war threaten subsea cables?
text: They also carry telecommunications and electricity between countries, and are essential for cloud services and online communications.

title: Explainer-The Hormuz digital chokepoint: How does the Iran war threaten subsea cables?
text: "Damaged cables mean the internet slowing down or outages, e-commerce disruptions, delayed financial transactions ... and ​economic fallout from all of these disruptions," said geopolitical and energy analyst Masha Kotkin.

title: Explainer-The Hormuz digital chokepoint: How does the Iran war threaten subsea cables?
text: Gulf countries, particularly the UAE and ⁠Saudi Arabia, have been investing billions of dollars in artificial intelligence and ⁠digital infrastructure to diversify their economies away from oil.

title: Explainer-The Hormuz digital chokepoint: How does the Iran war threaten subsea cables?
text: Both nations have established national AI companies serving customers across the region — all reliant on undersea cables to move data at lightning ⁠speed.

title: Explainer-The Hormuz digital chokepoint: How does the Iran war threaten subsea cables?
text: Major ‌cables through the Strait of Hormuz include the Asia-Africa-Europe 1 (AAE-1), connecting Southeast Asia to Europe via Egypt, with landing points in the UAE, Oman, Qatar, and Saudi Arabia; the FALCON network, connecting India and Sri Lanka to Gulf countries, Sudan, and Egypt; and the Gulf Bridge International Cable System, linking all Gulf countries including Iran.

title: Explainer-The Hormuz digital chokepoint: How does the Iran war threaten subsea cables?
text: Additional networks are under construction, including a ⁠system led by Qatar's Ooredoo.

title: Explainer-The Hormuz digital chokepoint: How does the Iran war threaten subsea cables?
text: WHAT ARE THE RISKS?

title: Explainer-The Hormuz digital chokepoint: How does the Iran war threaten subsea cables?
text: While the total length of submarine cables has grown considerably between ​2014 and 2025, faults have remained stable at around 150–200 ‌incidents per year, according to the International Cable Protection Committee (ICPC).

title: Explainer-The Hormuz digital chokepoint: How does the Iran war threaten subsea cables?
text: State-sponsored sabotage remains a risk, but 70–80% of faults are caused by accidental human activities — primarily fishing and ⁠ship anchors, according to the ICPC ​and experts.

title: Explainer-The Hormuz digital chokepoint: How does the Iran war threaten subsea cables?
text: Other risks include undersea currents, earthquakes, subsea volcanoes, and typhoons, said Alan Mauldin, research director at telecom research firm TeleGeography.

title: Explainer-The Hormuz digital chokepoint: How does the Iran war threaten subsea cables?
text: The industry addresses these by burying cables, armouring them, and selecting safe routes, he said.

title: Explainer-The Hormuz digital chokepoint: How does the Iran war threaten subsea cables?
text: The Iran war, nearing the two-month mark, has brought unprecedented disruption to global energy supply and regional infrastructure, including hits to Amazon Web Services data centers in Bahrain and the UAE.

title: Explainer-The Hormuz digital chokepoint: How does the Iran war threaten subsea cables?
text: Subsea cables have been spared so far.

title: Explainer-The Hormuz digital chokepoint: How does the Iran war threaten subsea cables?
text: However, an ⁠indirect risk exists from damaged vessels inadvertently hitting cables by dragging anchors.

title: Explainer-The Hormuz digital chokepoint: How does the Iran war threaten subsea cables?
text: "In a situation of active military ​operations, the risk of unintentional damage increases, and the longer this conflict lasts, the higher the likelihood of unintentional damage," Kotkin said.

title: Explainer-The Hormuz digital chokepoint: How does the Iran war threaten subsea cables?
text: A similar incident occurred in 2024, when a commercial vessel attacked by Iran-aligned Houthis drifted in the Red Sea and severed cables with its anchor.

title: Explainer-The Hormuz digital chokepoint: How does the Iran war threaten subsea cables?
text: The degree to which damage to the cables might impact connectivity in Gulf countries depends largely on how much individual network operators ⁠rely on them and what alternatives they have, according to TeleGeography.

title: Explainer-The Hormuz digital chokepoint: How does the Iran war threaten subsea cables?
text:
NO EASY FIX

Repairing damaged cables in conflict zones poses a separate challenge to securing them.

title: Explainer-The Hormuz digital chokepoint: How does the Iran war threaten subsea cables?
text: While the physical repair itself is not overly complicated, decisions by repair vessel owners and insurers may also be impacted by the risk of damage from fighting or the presence of mines, experts say.

title: Explainer-The Hormuz digital chokepoint: How does the Iran war threaten subsea cables?
text: Permits to access territorial waters add another layer of difficulty.

title: Explainer-The Hormuz digital chokepoint: How does the Iran war threaten subsea cables?
text: "Often one of the biggest problems with doing repairs is you have to get permits into the waters where the damage is.

title: Explainer-The Hormuz digital chokepoint: How does the Iran war threaten subsea cables?
text: That can take a long time sometimes and can be ​the biggest source (of problems)," Mauldin said.

title: Explainer-The Hormuz digital chokepoint: How does the Iran war threaten subsea cables?
text: Once the conflict ends, industry players will also face the challenge of re-surveying the sea floor to determine ⁠safe cable positions and avoid ships or objects that may have sunk during hostilities, he said.

title: Explainer-The Hormuz digital chokepoint: How does the Iran war threaten subsea cables?
text: WHAT ALTERNATIVES ARE THERE IF SUBSEA CABLES FALTER?

title: Explainer-The Hormuz digital chokepoint: How does the Iran war threaten subsea cables?
text: While potential damage to subsea cables would not cause a complete connectivity ​loss — due to land-based links — experts agree that satellite systems are not a feasible replacement, as they cannot handle the ‌same volume of traffic and are more expensive.

title: Explainer-The Hormuz digital chokepoint: How does the Iran war threaten subsea cables?
text: "It's not as though you could just switch ​to satellite.

title: Explainer-The Hormuz digital chokepoint: How does the Iran war threaten subsea cables?
text: That's not an alternative," Mauldin said, noting that satellites rely on connections to land-based networks and are better suited for things in motion, like airplanes and ships.

title: Explainer-The Hormuz digital chokepoint: How does the Iran war threaten subsea cables?
text: Low-Earth-orbit networks such as Starlink are "a boutique solution, which is not scalable to millions of users, at this time," Kotkin added.

title: Explainer-The Hormuz digital chokepoint: How does the Iran war threaten subsea cables?
text: (Reporting by Federico Maccioni in Dubai; Editing by Aidan Lewis)

title: Gold hits three-week low with US-Iran talks, central bank decisions in focus
text: Gold fell to a three-week low on Tuesday, as elevated oil prices kept inflation concerns high, while investors awaited key central bank decisions this week to see if the Middle East conflict has altered the interest rate outlook.

title: Gold hits three-week low with US-Iran talks, central bank decisions in focus
text: Spot gold was down 1.1% at $4,628.88 per ounce, as of 0553 GMT, its lowest level since April 7.

title: Gold hits three-week low with US-Iran talks, central bank decisions in focus
text: U.S. gold futures for June delivery fell 1.1% to $4,643.70.

title: Gold hits three-week low with US-Iran talks, central bank decisions in focus
text: U.S. President Donald Trump is unhappy with the latest Iranian proposal on resolving the two-month war, a U.S. official said, dampening hopes for a resolution to the conflict that has disrupted energy supplies, fuelled inflation, and killed thousands.

title: Gold hits three-week low with US-Iran talks, central bank decisions in focus
text: "Geopolitical headlines are still the main driver (of gold prices).

title: Gold hits three-week low with US-Iran talks, central bank decisions in focus
text: In the event of a deal (between the U.S. and Iran) or an interim deal, the dollar should weaken, and gold will likely break out to the upside," said Edward Meir, analyst at Marex.

title: Gold hits three-week low with US-Iran talks, central bank decisions in focus
text: The dollar edged higher, and oil prices hovered above $110 a barrel, as the crucial Strait of Hormuz waterway remained largely shut.

title: Gold hits three-week low with US-Iran talks, central bank decisions in focus
text: Higher crude oil prices can stoke inflation by raising transportation and production costs, increasing the likelihood of higher interest rates.

title: Gold hits three-week low with US-Iran talks, central bank decisions in focus
text: While gold is considered an inflation hedge, high interest rates make yield-bearing assets more attractive, weighing on its appeal.

title: Gold hits three-week low with US-Iran talks, central bank decisions in focus
text: The U.S. Federal Reserve is widely expected to hold interest rates steady at the end of its two-day meeting on Wednesday.

title: Gold hits three-week low with US-Iran talks, central bank decisions in focus
text: "The Fed is going to be largely irrelevant.

title: Gold hits three-week low with US-Iran talks, central bank decisions in focus
text: They're not going to be doing anything on rates for now," said Meir, adding that it might cut rates later in the fourth quarter as "we're heading into a global slowdown."

title: Gold hits three-week low with US-Iran talks, central bank decisions in focus
text: Investors will also be focusing on other central bank decisions this week, including those from the European Central Bank, the Bank of England and the Bank of Canada.

title: Gold hits three-week low with US-Iran talks, central bank decisions in focus
text: Spot silver fell 3% to $73.23 per ounce, platinum lost 1.5% to $1,953.50, and palladium was down 2.1% at $1,445.50.

title: Malaysia’s oil shock economic playbook
text: Malaysia’s oil shock economic playbook

title: Malaysia’s oil shock economic playbook
text: THE global economy is currently facing a severe surge in oil prices.

title: Malaysia’s oil shock economic playbook
text: This extreme volatility is heavily driven by intensified geopolitical military conflict between the United States and Iran, reviving memories of the 1970s-style energy supply shock that led to widespread economic disruptions and stagflation risk.

title: Malaysia’s oil shock economic playbook
text: The International Energy Agency called it the biggest energy security threat in history, as its intensity is much larger compared with the two oil shocks (1973 to 1974 and 1978 to 1979) and the 2022 Russia-Ukraine war.

title: Malaysia’s oil shock economic playbook
text: In both 1970s oil crises, the world lost five million barrels of oil per day (bpd), while the Ukraine war has resulted in a total loss of 75 billion cubic m of gas, or roughly 628 billion barrels.

title: Malaysia’s oil shock economic playbook
text: In the current Middle East conflict, the world is losing about 13 million bpd and a total of 100 billion cubic m of gas.

title: Malaysia’s oil shock economic playbook
text: The International Monetary Fund (IMF) has warned that a protracted conflict in the Middle East, elevated oil prices and supply chain disruption would have a heavy economic toll.

title: Malaysia’s oil shock economic playbook
text: Assuming a relatively short-lived war, the IMF expects global growth of 3.1% in 2026 (estimated 3.4% in 2025).

title: Malaysia’s oil shock economic playbook
text: Under the more severe scenario of a longer-lasting conflict, in which oil and natural gas prices spike 100% to 200% relative to January 2026 and stay at that level into 2027, global economic growth would come in at only 2% in 2026.

title: Malaysia’s oil shock economic playbook
text: Economic growth below 2% would amount to “a close call for a global recession”, which has happened only four times since 1980.

title: Malaysia’s oil shock economic playbook
text: Even with a quick resolution to the war, the full restoration of oil facilities and supplies is projected to take months to more than a year, elevated oil prices and supply shortages could remain for some time, with significant “economic scarring” expected to last throughout the year.

title: Malaysia’s oil shock economic playbook
text: Governments around the world are rewriting their economic playbook at speed, implementing initiatives and measures to reduce fuel demand, protect consumers and businesses, especially micro, small and medium enterprises (MSMEs).

title: Malaysia’s oil shock economic playbook
text: The measures are ranging from austerity in public spending because of tightness in government budgets, work-from-home, mandated price caps, targeted subsidies, fuel tax relief, fuel rationing or consumption restrictions as well as encouraging energy-saving measures, including accelerating green initiatives.

title: Malaysia’s oil shock economic playbook
text: Malaysia is now operating in crisis mode, with the government convening weekly meetings (sometimes every two days) of the National Economic Action Council, to discuss urgent short and medium-term measures, setting priorities within a critical level supply of essentials.

title: Malaysia’s oil shock economic playbook
text: Malaysians are reminded to prepare for a “second wave” of the crisis, shifting from initial fuel price shocks to broader supply chain disruptions affecting production and increasing prices of daily necessities.

title: Malaysia’s oil shock economic playbook
text: The pressure pain points are expected to emerge in second quarter of financial year 2026.

title: Malaysia’s oil shock economic playbook
text: The government is focused on three main strategies.

title: Malaysia’s oil shock economic playbook
text: First, ensuring an uninterrupted supply of basic needs such as food (chicken, eggs, rice and vegetables), fuel and medicine.

title: Malaysia’s oil shock economic playbook
text: Second, prolonging our supply, including subsidised fuel through prudent demand management.

title: Malaysia’s oil shock economic playbook
text: This entails strengthening control and intensified nationwide enforcement to prevent fuel subsidy leakages and smuggling.

title: Malaysia’s oil shock economic playbook
text: Third, mitigating the impact of the oil shock and supply chain disruptions (raw material shortage and increasing input prices) on businesses.

title: Malaysia’s oil shock economic playbook
text: Targeted fuel subsidies are currently shielding individuals and households from rising global oil prices.

title: Malaysia’s oil shock economic playbook
text: Notably, 80% to 85% of the individuals and businesses are sheltering under Budi Diesel and Sistem Kawalan Diesel Bersubsidi.

title: Malaysia’s oil shock economic playbook
text: It is crucial to get it right, and the time for decisive action is now.

title: Malaysia’s oil shock economic playbook
text: Malaysia’s oil shock economic playbook must reflect a broader shift in policy thinking over the medium-term, that is, away from somewhat “comprehensive and differentiated” support towards more selective, resilience-focused intervention, targeting the most impacted and vulnerable sectors and households.

title: Malaysia’s oil shock economic playbook
text: Dealing with an oil-induced supply shock requires careful policy calibration to avoid worsening the economic impact, as broad stimulus can lead to long-term economic scarring and persistent inflation.

title: Malaysia’s oil shock economic playbook
text: Balancing immediate consumers’ and businesses’ protection with the necessity of economic adjustment to high prices requires a multi-faceted approach.

title: Malaysia’s oil shock economic playbook
text: Alternative sourcing through diplomatic and strategic negotiations is actively pursued by the government to secure additional supplies of critical inputs to support manufacturing, packaging, healthcare products and agriculture.

title: Malaysia’s oil shock economic playbook
text: These include naphtha and resin, helium, ammonia, ethylene, and phosphate rock, which is crucial for fertiliser production.

title: Malaysia’s oil shock economic playbook
text: Countries involved in this form of “barter trade” negotiations are China and Australia, and the government and Petroliam Nasional Bhd or PETRONAS are also seeking outreach to energy suppliers from third countries such as Canada, South Africa, Latin America and the Balkans.

title: Malaysia’s oil shock economic playbook
text: Cash flow tightness easing measures and financing facilities were implemented to ease the financial burden of MSMEs.

title: Malaysia’s oil shock economic playbook
text: These include a RM5bil financing guarantee under Syarikat Jaminan Pembiayaan Perniagaan for targeted sectors (construction, agriculture and agro-food, logistics and transportation, and tourism), a 12-month extension for e-invoicing transition; and interim tax relief on re-imported Malaysian goods affected by global supply chain disruptions.

title: Malaysia’s oil shock economic playbook
text: Targeted repayment assistance offered by the financial institutions is strongly encouraged.

title: Malaysia’s oil shock economic playbook
text: The current targeted and disciplined fiscal support over broad stimulus is deemed appropriate during a supply-driven oil shock.

title: Malaysia’s oil shock economic playbook
text: By stimulating demand when goods are scarce or production is disrupted by the shortage of raw materials and higher prices, added spending can worsen the supply crunch, drive up prices further, and risks worsening inflation without increasing output.

title: Malaysia’s oil shock economic playbook
text: This risk creates stagflation, a combination of slower economic growth and high inflation.

title: Malaysia’s oil shock economic playbook
text: The government should resist “overspending” to shield consumers from high energy prices given the limited fiscal space.

title: Malaysia’s oil shock economic playbook
text: Fuel subsidies act as an implicit stimulus for consumption by keeping domestic fuel prices artificially low.

title: Malaysia’s oil shock economic playbook
text: Fuel subsidies, while intended to alleviate living expenses, come with economic costs.

title: Malaysia’s oil shock economic playbook
text: It not only causes fiscal unsustainability but also “over-consumption” and prolongs the dependency on the fossil fuel system that caused the shock in the first place.

title: Malaysia’s oil shock economic playbook
text: Keeping fuel prices at artificially low levels through subsidies can discourage energy efficiency and stall the adoption of renewable alternatives.

title: Malaysia’s oil shock economic playbook
text: Fuel subsidies have ballooned tenfold to RM7bil per month from RM700mil before the oil shock.

title: Malaysia’s oil shock economic playbook
text: The still-evolving volatile oil price environment makes a recalibration of the Budget 2026 challenging, contingent on the scenarios regarding the scale, intensity and duration of the war and oil shock.

title: Malaysia’s oil shock economic playbook
text: The average price of Brent crude oil has increased by 25.5% to US$86.75 bpd as of April 20, 2026 from US$69.14 in 2025 (Budget 2026’s assumption of US$60 to US$65 bpd).

title: Malaysia’s oil shock economic playbook
text: Soaring oil prices simultaneously strain fiscal balances through high subsidy costs and dampen economic growth, forcing governments to cut non-essential spending or reduce development spending to manage deficits.

title: Malaysia’s oil shock economic playbook
text: Bank Negara Malaysia should look through a temporary supply-driven oil shock to avoid choking-off growth but must be agile to anchor inflation expectations.

title: Malaysia’s oil shock economic playbook
text: Monetary policy proves ineffective to address supply constraints.

title: Malaysia’s oil shock economic playbook
text: Lowering interest rates during an oil shock to support growth and boost aggregate demand could lead to “demand-pull” inflation, which compounds existing cost-push pressures from higher energy prices, risking fuelling inflation further.

title: Malaysia’s oil shock economic playbook
text: The government must focus on balancing immediate economic pain relief with long-term fiscal sustainability, spending prudently, targeting support more precisely and building an energy system that can withstand future global instability shocks.

title: Malaysia’s oil shock economic playbook
text: We need sustainable and future-proof policies that reduce fossil fuel dependence and expand renewable-centred energy systems, making our economy more stable, our businesses and communities more resilient and strengthening cushioning against the risks of future oil shocks.

title: Malaysia’s oil shock economic playbook
text: The challenge is not simply how to absorb today’s oil shock, but how to prepare for the next one.

title: Malaysia’s oil shock economic playbook
text: Key strategies include accelerated investment in renewable energy (such as solar, hydro), encouraging renewable energy adoption, efficiency, and electrification of transport, scaling electric vehicles (EVs), investing in grid infrastructure, and shifting industrial processes toward renewables.

title: Malaysia’s oil shock economic playbook
text: Consumer behavioural changes are crucial to shift from high consumption of fuel to efficiency and alternatives, leveraging price signals, promoting eco-driving vehicles, and enhancing infrastructure for alternatives.

title: Malaysia’s oil shock economic playbook
text: Key strategies include a gradual weaning off fuel subsidies and supporting EV adoption.

title: Malaysia’s oil shock economic playbook
text: They also involve effective traffic congestion planning in urban areas, such as implementing peak-hour pricing and using artificial intelligence for traffic management, as well as optimising driving habits.

title: Malaysia’s oil shock economic playbook
text: Other measures include adopting transit-oriented development and shifting liquefied petroleum gas usage for cost efficiency and lower carbon emissions.

title: Malaysia’s oil shock economic playbook
text: Improving public transport is another priority, aimed at reducing reliance on private vehicles through better, more connected transit options.

title: Malaysia’s oil shock economic playbook
text: It is a good move for the Transport Ministry to provide significant incentives to boost public transport usage, including a 30% discount on KTMB Electric Train Service and Ekspres Rakyat Timuran tickets for Monday to Thursday travel, in addition to continued support for the My50 unlimited travel pass.

title: Malaysia’s oil shock economic playbook
text: While the government’s fuel subsidy reform, shifting from blanket to targeted fuel subsidies, albeit still comprehensive, has provided leeway to implement demand management through fuel consumption quota adjustment, the mechanism can be enhanced to achieve better targeting of fuel subsidies.

title: Malaysia’s oil shock economic playbook
text: Adopt a three-year gradual reduction of fuel subsidies by introducing regular, small increases to the subsidised price, bringing it closer to the market price, with a six-month review cycle.

title: Malaysia’s oil shock economic playbook
text: Ultimately moving to a fully floated market price, paired with direct cash assistance for vulnerable groups, ensuring a smoother transition.

title: Malaysia’s oil shock economic playbook
text: Utilise comprehensive and central databases such as Pangkalan Data Utama or Padu for targeted identification to accurately identify households based on income level and the needy ones, using means-testing to exclude the high-income bracket group.

title: Malaysia’s oil shock economic playbook
text: Embarking on a gradual, phased removal of fuel subsidies is more receptive and least disruptive when the oil price environment is low and less volatile.

title: Malaysia’s oil shock economic playbook
text: A stable and low-price period offers a strategic “window” for reform.

title: Malaysia’s oil shock economic playbook
text: When the price gap between the subsidised domestic price and the market price is smaller, gradual and small-step price increases reduce the shock to consumers.

title: Malaysia’s oil shock economic playbook
text: Lee Heng Guie is the executive director of the Socio-Economic Research Centre.

text: PHNOM PENH: Waving their national flags, dozens of Chinese nationals protested outside Cambodia's central bank on Monday (April 27), demanding the unfreezing of accounts they opened with a financial services firm linked to cyberscamming.

title: Protesters demand Cambodia unfreeze accounts with scam-linked firm

text: Protesters said their accounts with its digital payments platform H-Pay, previously Huione Pay, had been frozen since December.

title: Protesters demand Cambodia unfreeze accounts with scam-linked firm
text: Construction and renovation company owner Wang Xijun said he had about US$50,000 locked in his account and has been unable to pay his staff for around three months.

text: "We are Chinese citizens.

title: Protesters demand Cambodia unfreeze accounts with scam-linked firm
text: "Give the people's money back!"

title: Protesters demand Cambodia unfreeze accounts with scam-linked firm
text: The US government last year accused Huione, which owned several companies offering e-commerce, payment and cryptocurrency exchange services, of laundering funds for transnational criminal groups perpetrating scams from Southeast Asia.

title: Protesters demand Cambodia unfreeze accounts with scam-linked firm
text: But the protesters in Phnom Penh say they have nothing to do with these alleged crimes and now cannot access their assets deposited with Huione, calling on the National Bank of Cambodia (NBC) to intervene.

title: Protesters demand Cambodia unfreeze accounts with scam-linked firm
text: Monday's demonstration followed protests earlier this month outside the NBC and the Chinese embassy in Phnom Penh .

title: Protesters demand Cambodia unfreeze accounts with scam-linked firm
text: Li Shangfu, 54, said many Chinese people in Cambodia had used Huione because it was "trusted" and convenient for "all our transactions".

title: Protesters demand Cambodia unfreeze accounts with scam-linked firm
text: He works in the restaurant and hotel industry and said he has tens of thousands of dollars tied up in Huione's platform.

title: Protesters demand Cambodia unfreeze accounts with scam-linked firm
text: "I want the government to give us an answer.

title: Protesters demand Cambodia unfreeze accounts with scam-linked firm
text: What exactly is the situation regarding our money?"

title: Protesters demand Cambodia unfreeze accounts with scam-linked firm
text: said Li.

title: Protesters demand Cambodia unfreeze accounts with scam-linked firm

title: Protesters demand Cambodia unfreeze accounts with scam-linked firm
text:
- 'My blood and sweat' -

The NBC has said the Huione platforms' business licences have been revoked, and Huione Pay creditors should go to the courts, while H-Pay creditors can make claims with a liquidator.

title: Protesters demand Cambodia unfreeze accounts with scam-linked firm
text: The US Treasury's Financial Crimes Enforcement Network (FinCEN) designated Huione Group a "primary money-laundering concern" last year, and prohibited US financial institutions from processing transactions with it.

title: Protesters demand Cambodia unfreeze accounts with scam-linked firm
text: Beijing has called Huione's Li "a core member" of the criminal gang of Chen Zhi, another Chinese-born accused scam boss who was operating from Cambodia before being extradited to China this year.

title: Protesters demand Cambodia unfreeze accounts with scam-linked firm
text: The Southeast Asian nation has emerged as a hub for the illicit industry in recent years, with transnational crime groups initially mostly targeting Chinese speakers before widening their reach and stealing tens of billions of dollars annually from victims around the world.

title: Protesters demand Cambodia unfreeze accounts with scam-linked firm
text: Cambodian authorities say they are cracking down, detaining and deporting more than 13,000 foreign nationals involved in online scams since early last year.

title: Protesters demand Cambodia unfreeze accounts with scam-linked firm
text: From January to April, more than 240,000 people, including Chinese, Indonesians, Indians and others, accused of scam involvement "voluntarily departed" Cambodia, the government said last week.

title: Protesters demand Cambodia unfreeze accounts with scam-linked firm
text: Monitors accused senior Cambodian officials of complicity -- allegations the government has denied.

title: Protesters demand Cambodia unfreeze accounts with scam-linked firm
text: She opened her account three years ago because Chinese customers preferred it, she said.

title: Protesters demand Cambodia unfreeze accounts with scam-linked firm

title: Malaysia's non-communicable disease crisis poses major economic threat, says Dzulkefly
text: Malaysia's non-communicable disease crisis poses major economic threat, says Dzulkefly

title: Malaysia's non-communicable disease crisis poses major economic threat, says Dzulkefly
text: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia is facing a growing non-communicable disease (NCD) crisis that is fast becoming a major economic threat, Health Minister Datuk Seri Dr Dzulkefly Ahmad warned.

title: Malaysia's non-communicable disease crisis poses major economic threat, says Dzulkefly
text: He said the latest findings from the National Health and Morbidity Survey (NHMS) revealed alarming trends, with more than half a million adults in the country living with at least four major NCDs simultaneously.

title: Malaysia's non-communicable disease crisis poses major economic threat, says Dzulkefly
text: "One in six adults has diabetes, while one in three suffers from hypertension," he said in his speech at the launch of the "Mai Kurang Gula, Garam dan Kalori" recipe book here on Saturday (April 25).

title: Malaysia's non-communicable disease crisis poses major economic threat, says Dzulkefly
text: Dzulkefly said dietary habits remained a key contributor to the worsening health situation, pointing to excessive sugar and salt intake among Malaysians.

title: Malaysia's non-communicable disease crisis poses major economic threat, says Dzulkefly
text: Some 47% of adults consumed sugar beyond the recommended level of more than 7.5 teaspoons per day, largely driven by sweetened beverages, he said.

title: Malaysia's non-communicable disease crisis poses major economic threat, says Dzulkefly
text: Meanwhile, three in four adults consumed excessive salt, averaging 7.3g daily - far exceeding the World Health Organisation's recommended limit of less than 5g per day, he added.

title: Malaysia's non-communicable disease crisis poses major economic threat, says Dzulkefly
text: He stressed that the issue extended beyond personal health, warning of its far-reaching impact on the country's economy.

title: Malaysia's non-communicable disease crisis poses major economic threat, says Dzulkefly
text: "This situation is not merely a personal health issue.

title: Malaysia's non-communicable disease crisis poses major economic threat, says Dzulkefly
text: It is a ticking time bomb for the national economy," he said.

title: Malaysia's non-communicable disease crisis poses major economic threat, says Dzulkefly
text: Citing recent estimates, Dzulkefly said NCDs cost Malaysia about RM64.2bil annually.

title: Malaysia's non-communicable disease crisis poses major economic threat, says Dzulkefly
text: He said direct healthcare costs amounted to RM12.4bil, while indirect costs - including premature deaths, absenteeism and reduced productivity due to illness - accounted for RM51.8bil.

title: Malaysia's non-communicable disease crisis poses major economic threat, says Dzulkefly
text: "The true burden of NCDs is far greater and more critical than what we see in hospitals or clinics," he added.

title: Youth bankruptcy hits 4,704 cases

title: Youth bankruptcy hits 4,704 cases
text: JOHOR BARU: A growing number of young Malaysians are falling into bankruptcy, with 4,704 cases involving individuals aged 34 and below recorded over the past five years, says Economy Minister Datuk Seri Akmal Nasrullah Mohd Nasir (pic).

title: Youth bankruptcy hits 4,704 cases
text: He said the figure accounts for 15% of the 31,517 bankruptcy cases recorded between 2021 and March 2026, raising concerns that many youths are being burdened by debt before they can achieve financial stability.

title: Youth bankruptcy hits 4,704 cases
text: “Nearly half of all bankruptcy cases are caused by personal loans, underscoring the urgent need to strengthen financial literacy among Malaysians,” he said.

title: Youth bankruptcy hits 4,704 cases
text: Speaking at the launch of the Second Chance Fast Track Policy Programme 2026, organised by the Johor Insolvency Department yesterday, Akmal Nasrullah said the government is taking proactive steps to help affected individuals rebuild their lives.

title: Youth bankruptcy hits 4,704 cases
text: “The government must not only manage failure but also help people break out of cycles of financial hardship and rebuild with dignity.

title: Youth bankruptcy hits 4,704 cases
text: “The Second Chance Policy provides a structured pathway for bankrupt individuals to restart their lives, particularly through a fast-track mechanism that accelerates the discharge process for eligible cases.

title: Youth bankruptcy hits 4,704 cases
text: “Among those prioritised under the initiative are single parents, micro-entrepreneurs, scam victims and buyers affected by abandoned housing projects,” he said.

title: Youth bankruptcy hits 4,704 cases
text: Akmal Nasrullah added that the policy was expanded under Budget 2024 to include individuals aged 40 and below with debts not exceeding RM200,000, noting that nearly 14,000 bankruptcy cases involving debts below RM50,000 had been discharged as of July 2023.

title: Youth bankruptcy hits 4,704 cases
text: He said the initiative aligns with the 13th Malaysia Plan, which emphasises social mobility through reforms in education, labour markets and inclusive economic development.

title: Youth bankruptcy hits 4,704 cases
text: Akmal Nasrullah noted that Malaysia’s household debt-to-GDP ratio stood at 84.8% at the end of 2025, highlighting the importance of responsible borrowing and sound financial management.

title: Youth bankruptcy hits 4,704 cases
text: On the economic outlook, he said Malaysia’s economy grew 5.2% in 2025, with growth projected at between 4% and 5% in 2026.

title: Youth bankruptcy hits 4,704 cases
text: Early estimates also indicate a 5.3% expansion in the first quarter of this year, reflecting continued resilience despite global uncertainties.

title: Youth bankruptcy hits 4,704 cases
text: He added that Johor stands to benefit from the Johor-Singapore Special Economic Zone, which is expected to create 20,000 high-skilled jobs, offering renewed opportunities for those seeking a fresh start.

title: Youth bankruptcy hits 4,704 cases

text: That’s up from 7.2% a year ago and the highest since the National Bureau of Statistics revamped its employment data more than two years ago, when it also started to treat that age group as a separate category.

title: Unemployment spikes for key Chinese age group as AI use spreads
text: The data adds to signs of growing distress in China’s labour force of over 700 million – the world’s biggest – after overall unemployment saw a surprise uptick last month and wage incomes expanded at their slowest rate since late 2022.

title: Unemployment spikes for key Chinese age group as AI use spreads
text: Though pressure on jobs typically increases around the Lunar New Year, and the holiday’s later-than-usual timing this year may have worsened the pain in March, other factors are increasingly coming into play.

title: Unemployment spikes for key Chinese age group as AI use spreads
text: The conflict in the Middle East, now in its eighth week, has caused a massive disruption for energy exports from the Persian Gulf, scrambling trade flows and squeezing profitability for companies.

title: Unemployment spikes for key Chinese age group as AI use spreads
text: Younger job seekers are also having to contend with what Citigroup Inc has called China’s "widespread but still shallow” adoption of AI, a transformation that eventually threatens to displace an estimated 70 million workers in the country.

title: Unemployment spikes for key Chinese age group as AI use spreads
text: "The uncertainties on costs brought by the Iran war might have disturbed hiring plans and led to a worsening of labour market indicators in March, after they showed some improvements in earlier months,” said Ernan Cui, a consumer analyst at the research firm Gavekal Dragonomics.

title: Unemployment spikes for key Chinese age group as AI use spreads
text: Unemployment increased in nearly every pocket of the labour market in March, reaching close to 17% for those aged 16 to 24.

title: Unemployment spikes for key Chinese age group as AI use spreads
text: The overall jobless rate climbed to 5.4%, the highest in a year, and went up for 31 big cities.

title: Unemployment spikes for key Chinese age group as AI use spreads
text: But the 25-to-29 cohort stands out for being more vulnerable to the potential shock from AI on the labour market.

title: Unemployment spikes for key Chinese age group as AI use spreads
text: It’s a group that was traditionally appealing for employers, encompassing new job entrants transitioning from school to work.

title: Unemployment spikes for key Chinese age group as AI use spreads
text: Poorer outcomes for people at the onset of their careers could represent the culmination of pressures on younger workers that have been building in China since the pandemic in 2020 and are now made worse by AI-powered systems – especially when it comes to the prospects for entry-level positions.

title: Unemployment spikes for key Chinese age group as AI use spreads
text: Joblessness early in life risks leaving long-lasting scarring effects and might threaten future employment, studies have shown.

title: Unemployment spikes for key Chinese age group as AI use spreads
text: Without a turnaround, China could find it hard to escape jobless growth as it relies more heavily on manufacturing, an industry that’s become far more automated over the past decade.

title: Unemployment spikes for key Chinese age group as AI use spreads
text: Even in the southern economic powerhouse of Guangdong, many factory workers are struggling as labour-intensive sectors lag high-tech businesses in export growth.

title: Unemployment spikes for key Chinese age group as AI use spreads
text: The industrial sector accounts for around 30% of China’s gross domestic product but only 20% of employment, according to Julian Evans-Pritchard, head of China economics at Capital Economics.

title: Unemployment spikes for key Chinese age group as AI use spreads
text: "Industry, which outperformed in the first quarter, is less labour-intensive than other sectors of the economy,” Evans-Pritchard said in a report Friday, adding that hiring in services and construction softened.

title: Unemployment spikes for key Chinese age group as AI use spreads
text:
"This doesn’t bode well for the consumption outlook.”

A frail labour market has already been a major obstacle to reviving household spending and the housing market.

title: Unemployment spikes for key Chinese age group as AI use spreads
text: Retail sales weakened more than expected in March, leaving the economy more dependent on exports and resulting in lopsided growth that’s heightening trade tensions abroad.

title: Unemployment spikes for key Chinese age group as AI use spreads
text: And in a sign of growing consumer caution at home, household savings as a share of disposable incomes rose to 38% in the first quarter, the highest level in three years for the period, according to Bloomberg calculations based on official data.

title: Unemployment spikes for key Chinese age group as AI use spreads
text: Gauging the health of the labour market has long been a challenge for economists, many of whom have criticised China’s unemployment statistics as underestimating the real picture due to flaws in their methodology.

title: Unemployment spikes for key Chinese age group as AI use spreads
text: Many migrant workers who lose their jobs and return to their hometown in the countryside likely won’t register in state statistics, and the official figures have tended to be relatively stable despite drastic growth swings in the economy at times.

title: Unemployment spikes for key Chinese age group as AI use spreads
text: The rise of gig work – which accounts for nearly half of urban jobs – also became a significant buffer against unemployment in recent years.

title: Unemployment spikes for key Chinese age group as AI use spreads
text: The NBS revamped its method of surveying joblessness for different groups in early 2024, after youth unemployment hit a record high.

title: Unemployment spikes for key Chinese age group as AI use spreads
text: Until the overhaul, it didn’t differentiate the 25-29 cohort from the age group of 25 to 59 years.

title: Unemployment spikes for key Chinese age group as AI use spreads
text: In a statement released at the time, the statistics service said rising education levels meant most young people graduate around age 24 and may face initial job instability, while employment generally stabilises by 29.

title: Unemployment spikes for key Chinese age group as AI use spreads
text: While it’s not yet clear if the growing unemployment rate is a seasonal blip or marks the start of a sustained trend upward, Citigroup has warned China may be near "a tipping point” in its adoption of AI, based on the bank’s survey of 1,800 people in March.

title: Unemployment spikes for key Chinese age group as AI use spreads
text: "The impact on the labour market has so far been largely in sentiment,” Citi economists said in a report last week.

title: Unemployment spikes for key Chinese age group as AI use spreads
text: "Little of the potential impact has materialised at this early stage.” – Bloomberg

title: Investors confident in Sabah's holistic approach to economic growth, says Musa
text: Investors confident in Sabah's holistic approach to economic growth, says Musa

title: Investors confident in Sabah's holistic approach to economic growth, says Musa
text: KOTA KINABALU: Sabah's growth will be steered holistically to encompass economic and human capital development, social well-being and sustainability among other key aspects, says Tun Musa Aman.

title: Investors confident in Sabah's holistic approach to economic growth, says Musa
text: The Yang di-Pertua Negeri said the state recorded RM7.6bil in investments last year, a 208% increase from 2024.

title: Investors confident in Sabah's holistic approach to economic growth, says Musa
text: "This shows investor confidence and that Sabah continues to move forward on a positive note," he said when opening the state assembly sitting here on Thursday (April 23).

title: Investors confident in Sabah's holistic approach to economic growth, says Musa
text: He said the state aims to strengthen development using more sustainable and inclusive approaches to continue boosting economic growth, balancing it with the people's wellbeing, environmental conservation and infrastructure development – main thrusts under the Sabah Maju Jaya (SMJ) 2.0 development plan.

title: Investors confident in Sabah's holistic approach to economic growth, says Musa
text: Musa said SMJ 2.0 would be boosted with a strategic focus on the blue economy, digitalisation and artificial intelligence (AI).

title: Investors confident in Sabah's holistic approach to economic growth, says Musa
text: The 13th Malaysia Plan (13MP) has also identified 10 main focuses aligned with the state's needs and potential.

title: Investors confident in Sabah's holistic approach to economic growth, says Musa
text: A total of 1,173 projects have been planned under 13MP, he added, with RM12.024bil allocated.

title: Investors confident in Sabah's holistic approach to economic growth, says Musa
text: "These plans will not be realised without the support and cooperation from all parties.

title: Investors confident in Sabah's holistic approach to economic growth, says Musa
text: "It is time for us to unite regardless of race and religion or political affiliations, to build a better Sabah with responsibility for the good of all," he said.

title: Investors confident in Sabah's holistic approach to economic growth, says Musa
text: The state assembly will sit from Monday to Thursday (April 27 to 30) next week.

title: South Korea economic growth roared past estimates in Q1, thanks to chips
text: South Korea economic growth roared past estimates in Q1, thanks to chips

title: South Korea economic growth roared past estimates in Q1, thanks to chips
text: SEOUL: South Korea's economy beat expectations in the first quarter of 2026 to recordthe fastest growth since mid-2020, as booming semiconductor demand offset the drag of weak public spending, central bank data showed on Thursday.

title: South Korea economic growth roared past estimates in Q1, thanks to chips
text: Gross domestic product expanded 1.7% in the January-March quarter from the prior three months, the Bank of Korea said, blowing through the median Reuters poll estimate of 1.0% by a wide margin.

title: South Korea economic growth roared past estimates in Q1, thanks to chips
text: The gain marks the strongest quarterly jump since the third quarter of 2020 for Asia's fourth-largest economy, when it was on a post-pandemic rebound.

title: South Korea economic growth roared past estimates in Q1, thanks to chips
text: Growth was driven by a 5.1% jump in exports, led by shipments of "IT components including semiconductors" used in artificial intelligence infrastructure, the BOK said.

title: South Korea economic growth roared past estimates in Q1, thanks to chips
text: Private consumption rose 0.5% after showing tentative signs of recovery in January and February as household confidence improved, while government expenditure grew just 0.1%.

title: South Korea economic growth roared past estimates in Q1, thanks to chips
text: Facility investment gained 4.8% after shrinking 1.7% in the last quarter of 2025.

title: South Korea economic growth roared past estimates in Q1, thanks to chips
text: From a year earlier, South Korea's economy grew 3.6%, compared with a 1.6% expansion in the fourth quarter and beating a median estimate for 2.7% growth.

title: South Korea economic growth roared past estimates in Q1, thanks to chips

title: Italy is euro zone laggard on AI usage, official data shows
text: ROME, April 22 (Reuters) - ⁠Italy has one of the ⁠lowest rates of artificial intelligence use ‌in Europe, data showed on Wednesday, highlighting a broader shortfall in digital skills in the ​euro zone's third-largest economy.

title: Italy is euro zone laggard on AI usage, official data shows
text: Just ⁠19.9% of Italians ⁠aged 16 to 74 used artificial intelligence tools ⁠last ‌year, compared with a European average of 32.7%, national ⁠statistics agency ISTAT said in its ​first such ‌survey.

title: Italy is euro zone laggard on AI usage, official data shows
text: Only Romania had a lower score, ⁠with 17.8%, ​it added.

title: Italy is euro zone laggard on AI usage, official data shows
text: Italy has the oldest population in the European Union, with a ⁠median age of 48.7 in ​2024 compared with an EU average of 44.7, according to EU statistics agency ⁠Eurostat, and it lags many peers on key digital skills indicators.

title: Italy is euro zone laggard on AI usage, official data shows
text: Nearly 10% of Italy's population had not used internet ​in the previous three ⁠months, according to a 2025 Eurostat ​survey.

title: Italy is euro zone laggard on AI usage, official data shows
