title: Carbon tax halt backed by analysts
text: Carbon tax halt backed by analysts

title: Carbon tax halt backed by analysts
text: PETALING JAYA: The decision to delay the rollout of the planned carbon tax for industries has been described as a sensible move amid prevailing economic and geopolitical uncertainties in the Middle East, though analysts say clear communication will be crucial to avoid perceptions of policy inconsistency.

title: Carbon tax halt backed by analysts
text: Centre for Market Education chief economist Alvin Desfiandi said given the current circumstances, delaying the plan appears to be a sensible step.

title: Carbon tax halt backed by analysts
text: “The pause could instead be used constructively to provide greater clarity on the scope, rate structure and collection mechanism of the carbon tax.

title: Carbon tax halt backed by analysts
text: “This will ensure the market is ready by the time the policy is implemented,” he said when contacted yesterday.

title: Carbon tax halt backed by analysts
text: Earlier, the government anno­un­­ced that it would delay the carbon tax plan for industries due to the situation in the Middle East to avoid adding pressure on the people.

title: Carbon tax halt backed by analysts
text: Desfiandi said sectors likely to be most exposed include logistics, tourism, broad-based manufacturing and agriculture.

title: Carbon tax halt backed by analysts
text: “This impact is largely due to their sensitivity to higher energy inputs, such as diesel and jet fuel, as well as potential supply chain disruptions involving key inputs, like plastics and fertilisers,” he said.

title: Carbon tax halt backed by analysts
text: Desfiandi said such uncertainty could dampen long-term green capital formation in affected indus­tries, adding that the ultimate impact would depend on the longer-term outlook and viability of green investments.

title: Carbon tax halt backed by analysts
text: Economist professor emeritus Dr Barjoyai Bardai described the decision to delay the implementation of a carbon tax as a sensible short-term “shock absorber”, though the move must clearly be time-bound to avoid undermining policy credibility.

title: Carbon tax halt backed by analysts
text: “If firms face sudden increases in fuel, raw materials and finan­cing costs, an additional tax can be inflationary rather than corrective, especially in an import- dependent economy.

title: Carbon tax halt backed by analysts
text: “That said, it’s only defensible if the delay is clearly time-bound and well-signalled,” he said.

title: Carbon tax halt backed by analysts
text: Economist Geoffrey Williams said the proposed carbon tax may appear inconsistent in a system where fossil fuels and electricity remain subsidised, given that these subsidies also contribute to carbon emissions.

title: Carbon tax halt backed by analysts
text: “It makes no sense to tax carbon when you subsidise gas, petrol and diesel as well as electricity, which create carbon.

title: Carbon tax halt backed by analysts
text: “It’s better to just remove the subsidy for high-carbon-footprint companies instead.

title: Carbon tax halt backed by analysts
text: “The carbon tax is not a good tax, and the current situation has allowed a quick get-out option which hopefully the government can use to scrap the idea altoge­ther,” he said.

title: Carbon tax halt backed by analysts
text: The Small and Medium Enter­prises Association of Malaysia (Samenta) president Datuk William Ng said while introducing the carbon tax to counter demand for such a tax in destination markets such as Europe, it is a beneficial move to delay the tax until the situation in the Middle East stabilises.

title: Carbon tax halt backed by analysts
text: “Businesses, and especially exporters, are struggling to contain costs and obtain supplies; so their time is best spent on defen­ding their business instead of adding on compliance requirements at this time,” he said.

title: Carbon tax halt backed by analysts
text: Last year, during the Budget 2026 announcement, Prime Minis­ter Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim stated that Malaysia would introduce a carbon tax this year, starting with the iron, steel and energy sectors, marking a major step towards a low-carbon economy.

title: Carbon tax halt backed by analysts
text: The Bill, expected to be tabled in June this year, will enhance transparency and accountability through carbon emission mea­surement and reporting mechanisms.

title: A wartime economy set to be different this time
text: A wartime economy set to be different this time

title: A wartime economy set to be different this time
text: I RECENTLY heard a terrifying prediction: Advances in defence technology will change the way war is waged today as much as industrialisation did in World War I.

title: A wartime economy set to be different this time
text: If true – and I don’t know one way or another, my area of expertise is economics – then we could be facing casualties on an unimaginable scale, just as the mechanisation of weaponry produced in the early 20th century.

title: A wartime economy set to be different this time
text: As I said, however, I am an economist, and this prediction got me to thinking: What will this transformation mean for the United States and the global economy?

title: A wartime economy set to be different this time
text: In the past, increases in military spending have acted as a kind of stimulus.

title: A wartime economy set to be different this time
text: But there are reasons to doubt that will happen this time, at least in the same way.

title: A wartime economy set to be different this time
text: As in the early 20th century, global politics and technology are resulting in more spending on defence.

title: A wartime economy set to be different this time
text: A more fragmented world brings more risk and less cooperation, so countries are spending more on their own military capabilities just as technology is producing new tools of warfare.

title: A wartime economy set to be different this time
text: Over the second half of the 20th century, military spending as a percentage of gross domestic product (GDP) fell.

title: A wartime economy set to be different this time
text: Despite the Cold War, stronger alliances enabled many countries to spend less on defence.

title: A wartime economy set to be different this time
text: After the fall of the Soviet Union and the rise of technology in the 1990s, many in the United States military actually argued that the country could spend less.

title: A wartime economy set to be different this time
text: Military spending rose some in the 2000s, with wars in Iraq and Afghanistan, but stayed relatively low.

title: A wartime economy set to be different this time
text: The world is now entering a new era of more military spending, as highlighted this week in both the International Monetary Fund’s (IMF) “World Economic Outlook” and the “Economic Report of the President”.

title: A wartime economy set to be different this time
text: North Atlantic Treaty Organisation countries have already started to increase their spending in response to President Donald Trump’s actions.

title: A wartime economy set to be different this time
text: Meanwhile, the United States – facing the prospects of more conflicts and a rising military in China – is not reducing its own spending.

title: A wartime economy set to be different this time
text: Congress will have the final say, but the administration has just proposed a US$1.5 trillion defence budget for 2027, a more than 40% increase from 2026, much of it dedicated to implementing and defending against new technology.

title: A wartime economy set to be different this time
text: The IMF draws on data from the postwar era to speculate on what this might mean for the world economy.

title: A wartime economy set to be different this time
text: In the past, military spending was similar to other forms of fiscal stimulus: It created jobs not only in the military but also for people who make things like submarines and fighter jets, as well as those who live in those communities.

title: A wartime economy set to be different this time
text: But the IMF cautions it can take away from spending on social services.

title: A wartime economy set to be different this time
text: It estimates that military spending has a multiplier of one, meaning governments get out what they put in, at least in GDP terms.

title: A wartime economy set to be different this time
text: But while higher spending levels may resemble those of the past, the impact on the economy may be very different because the money will be spent differently.

title: A wartime economy set to be different this time
text: Current and future wars will involve more artificial intelligence (AI)-powered autonomous technology and perhaps fewer people fighting, but they may also go on for longer and have more ambiguous outcomes.

title: A wartime economy set to be different this time
text: There will probably be smaller technology firms involved, which will create jobs, wealth and entrepreneurial activity, but the benefits won’t be as widely felt as they were with major defence contractors.

title: A wartime economy set to be different this time
text: In the past defence spending was an important creator of middle-class, blue-collar jobs.

title: A wartime economy set to be different this time
text: But modern warfare requires a smaller military and more precision weapons, which tend to be created by more educated workers.

title: A wartime economy set to be different this time
text: The good news is that defence spending will probably continue to act as an important subsidy for innovations that have broader benefits.

title: A wartime economy set to be different this time
text: The Defence Advanced Research Projects Agency, founded in 1958, produced the technology that made the internet possible.

title: A wartime economy set to be different this time
text: The AI-powered future of warfare could also produce innovations that transform the economy in ways we can’t yet imagine.

title: A wartime economy set to be different this time
text: There is another difference with the past that could prove pivotal: what all this spending does to interest rates.

title: A wartime economy set to be different this time
text: Historian Niall Ferguson has argued that once a country spends more on interest payments than on its military, its greatest days are behind it.

title: A wartime economy set to be different this time
text: This was true for Spain, France and Great Britain, which became so overwhelmed by debt that they could not adequately fund their military and maintain their global hegemony.

title: A wartime economy set to be different this time
text: One of Trump’s economic advisers, acting Council of Economic Advisers chairman Pierre Yared, has made a related point.

title: A wartime economy set to be different this time
text: Being the world’s only superpower confers what is known as the “exorbitant privilege” on a nation – that is, it can borrow money at lower interest rates.

title: A wartime economy set to be different this time
text: Losing a military conflict, in contrast, tends to mean higher interest rates and inflation.

title: A wartime economy set to be different this time
text: A high-spending hegemon such as the United States has to strike a fine balance: If it spends too much, debt-service payments can crowd out military spending.

title: A wartime economy set to be different this time
text: If it doesn’t spend enough, it becomes weaker, which could increase rates even further.

title: A wartime economy set to be different this time
text: The question of how long America’s dominance will last, then, may be less about military might than about national debt.

title: A wartime economy set to be different this time
text: On that score, a reckoning could come sooner rather than later, especially if Washington decides (as it almost certainly will) to increase defence spending without reducing social spending.

title: A wartime economy set to be different this time
text: Alternatively, if the United States prioritises military spending, it will be the first time this century that it has chosen to spend on the young instead of the old.

title: A wartime economy set to be different this time
text: Small victory.

title: A wartime economy set to be different this time
text:
— Bloomberg

Allison Schrager is a Bloomberg Opinion columnist covering economics.

title: A wartime economy set to be different this time
text: The views expressed here are the writer’s own.

title: Govt preparing long-term measures to mitigate Strait of Hormuz crisis impact, says Economy Ministry
text: Govt preparing long-term measures to mitigate Strait of Hormuz crisis impact, says Economy Ministry

title: Govt preparing long-term measures to mitigate Strait of Hormuz crisis impact, says Economy Ministry
text: PETALING JAYA: With the situation in the Strait of Hormuz showing no end in sight, the Economy Ministry said the government has begun preparing measures to deal with the long-term effects of the war on the local economy.

title: Govt preparing long-term measures to mitigate Strait of Hormuz crisis impact, says Economy Ministry
text: The ministry said the current situation has moved beyond a short-term fuel shortage shock into a global supply crisis that will affect the daily lives of Malaysians.

title: Govt preparing long-term measures to mitigate Strait of Hormuz crisis impact, says Economy Ministry
text: "The issue is not only whether physical supply is disrupted, but also involves risk premiums, insurance costs, logistics, delivery delays, and disruptions to global supply chains.

title: Govt preparing long-term measures to mitigate Strait of Hormuz crisis impact, says Economy Ministry
text: "This means it is not merely an issue of oil prices anymore-it has evolved into a global supply crisis affecting energy costs, logistics, raw materials, food, services, and ultimately, the daily lives of the people.

title: Govt preparing long-term measures to mitigate Strait of Hormuz crisis impact, says Economy Ministry
text: "As such, we are now in a phase of managing prolonged pressure, no longer just the initial shock phase," the ministry said in a statement on Tuesday (April 21).

title: Govt preparing long-term measures to mitigate Strait of Hormuz crisis impact, says Economy Ministry
text: The ministry warned that while some economic fundamentals remain resilient, the full impact of the war is expected to hurt the local economy later.

title: Govt preparing long-term measures to mitigate Strait of Hormuz crisis impact, says Economy Ministry
text: "The full impact of this crisis typically reaches the economy with a lag through logistics costs, import prices, investment decisions, and consumer sentiment so while our fundamentals remain strong, there is no room for complacency," it added.

title: Govt preparing long-term measures to mitigate Strait of Hormuz crisis impact, says Economy Ministry
text: The Statistics Department Consumer Price Index report for March 2026 showed inflation rising to 1.7% from 1.4% in February.

title: Govt preparing long-term measures to mitigate Strait of Hormuz crisis impact, says Economy Ministry
text: The department also recorded an increase in average diesel prices in Peninsular Malaysia to RM4.09 per litre from RM2.98 in February, while RON97 rose to RM4.01 from RM3.11.

title: Govt preparing long-term measures to mitigate Strait of Hormuz crisis impact, says Economy Ministry
text: "This shows that the effects of the crisis are no longer confined to global markets-they are now filtering into transportation costs, logistics, goods prices, and daily household spending," the ministry added.

title: Govt preparing long-term measures to mitigate Strait of Hormuz crisis impact, says Economy Ministry
text: The second quarter of 2026 will be used as a key period to assess whether rising costs translate into operational downsizing or job losses.

title: Govt preparing long-term measures to mitigate Strait of Hormuz crisis impact, says Economy Ministry
text: The government is engaging with the manufacturing and tourism industries to identify critical inputs and effective interventions.

title: Govt preparing long-term measures to mitigate Strait of Hormuz crisis impact, says Economy Ministry
text: During the National Economic Action Council meeting on Tuesday, several strategic mitigation measures were agreed upon to safeguard supply chains.

title: Govt preparing long-term measures to mitigate Strait of Hormuz crisis impact, says Economy Ministry
text: For manufacturing, the government will facilitate logistics and strengthen bilateral cooperation with countries such as Australia and China to secure essential inputs.

title: Govt preparing long-term measures to mitigate Strait of Hormuz crisis impact, says Economy Ministry
text: The government also plans to use the tourism industry as a buffer to generate foreign exchange.

title: Govt preparing long-term measures to mitigate Strait of Hormuz crisis impact, says Economy Ministry
text: "The government has agreed on measures including refocusing on stable markets such as ASEAN, East Asia, Australia, and India.

title: Govt preparing long-term measures to mitigate Strait of Hormuz crisis impact, says Economy Ministry
text: There are also plans to extend the Visit Malaysia 2026 campaign to 2027, targeting 47 million visitors and RM329bil in tourism revenue," the ministry said.

title: Economy Minister: Over 70% of blending depots ready for B15 biodiesel mandate
text: Economy Minister: Over 70% of blending depots ready for B15 biodiesel mandate

title: Economy Minister: Over 70% of blending depots ready for B15 biodiesel mandate
text: PUTRAJAYA: More than 70% of blending depots nationwide are equipped and ready to implement the B15 biodiesel mandate, says Akmal Nasrullah Mohd Nasir.

title: Economy Minister: Over 70% of blending depots ready for B15 biodiesel mandate
text: The Economy Minister said the transition to a higher biodiesel blend was no longer just an alternative energy policy, but a critical national economic defence strategy in the face of the prolonged conflict in the Middle East.

title: Economy Minister: Over 70% of blending depots ready for B15 biodiesel mandate
text: Akmal Nasrullah said after the government's announcement to phase in the B15 mandate, inspections by the Plantation and Commodities Ministry confirmed that the majority of depots could execute the blending using existing facilities.

title: Economy Minister: Over 70% of blending depots ready for B15 biodiesel mandate
text: "Depot operators are currently framing operational adjustments to ensure efficiency and the security of petroleum product supplies to consumers," Akmal Nasrullah said in a press conference on Tuesday (April 21).

title: Economy Minister: Over 70% of blending depots ready for B15 biodiesel mandate
text: He also said recently conducted a joint working visit with the Plantation and Commodities Minister to the Klang Valley Distribution Terminal (KVDT) and a biodiesel processing plant in Pulau Indah, Klang, to assess the ecosystem's readiness.

title: Economy Minister: Over 70% of blending depots ready for B15 biodiesel mandate
text: "This sends an important message that energy issues can no longer be viewed solely from the perspective of current supply.

title: Economy Minister: Over 70% of blending depots ready for B15 biodiesel mandate
text: "It must be addressed within a national framework that connects energy security, the strength of the commodity sector, logistics efficiency, supply chain resilience, and the nation's economic endurance," he said.

title: Economy Minister: Over 70% of blending depots ready for B15 biodiesel mandate
text: While Brent crude prices dipped to US$117.85 per barrel in mid-April from a peak of US$132.46, Akmal Nasrullah warned that the supply crisis was far from over, with geopolitical disruptions potentially impacting global energy costs for up to 18 months.

title: Economy Minister: Over 70% of blending depots ready for B15 biodiesel mandate
text: “If this uncertainty continues, the impact on energy costs and global supply chains can take up to 18 months to stabilise, depending on geopolitical developments and the restoration of trade routes.

title: Economy Minister: Over 70% of blending depots ready for B15 biodiesel mandate
text: “This means that Malaysia is not exempt from global pressures coming from various angles,” he said.

title: Economy Minister: Over 70% of blending depots ready for B15 biodiesel mandate
text: Biodiesel blending mandates are policies requiring a specific percentage of renewable fuel - typically palm oil-based methyl ester (PME)—to be mixed into fossil diesel.

title: Economy Minister: Over 70% of blending depots ready for B15 biodiesel mandate
text: The numbers in the mandates represent the percentage of palm oil in the fuel mix.

title: Economy Minister: Over 70% of blending depots ready for B15 biodiesel mandate
text: Rising diesel prices in Malaysia, driven by the Middle East conflict, are accelerating the push for higher biodiesel mandates to cut costs.

title: Economy Minister: Over 70% of blending depots ready for B15 biodiesel mandate
text: Diesel prices in Peninsular Malaysia surged from RM2.99 per litre in February to a high of RM5.97per litre as of April 16.

title: Over 4,700 retrenched in April 2026, says Economy Minister
text: Over 4,700 retrenched in April 2026, says Economy Minister

title: Over 4,700 retrenched in April 2026, says Economy Minister
text: PUTRAJAYA: Over 4,700 workers have lost their jobs in the first 16 days of April, says Akmal Nasrullah Mohd Nasir.

title: Over 4,700 retrenched in April 2026, says Economy Minister
text: The Economy Minister said the second quarter of 2026 (Q2) would be a critical period for the local job market.

title: Over 4,700 retrenched in April 2026, says Economy Minister
text: Citing data from the Social Security Organisation's (PERKESO) Employment Insurance System (EIS), he noted that 4,708 workers had been retrenched in the first 16 days of April.

title: Over 4,700 retrenched in April 2026, says Economy Minister

title: Anwar: Banking institutions must act as strategic partners to sustain domestic economy
text: Anwar: Banking institutions must act as strategic partners to sustain domestic economy

title: Anwar: Banking institutions must act as strategic partners to sustain domestic economy
text: KUALA LUMPUR: Banking institutions must shoulder their responsibility as strategic partners to sustain the domestic economy, said Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim.

title: Anwar: Banking institutions must act as strategic partners to sustain domestic economy
text: He said that although the country’s economic fundamentals remain resilient, the government will not compromise on any delays in addressing the pressures facing critical sectors, especially micro, small and medium enterprises (MSMEs), which are the pillars of the country’s economy.

title: Anwar: Banking institutions must act as strategic partners to sustain domestic economy
text: Earlier, Anwar, who is also Finance Minister, announced the RM5 billion special facility to be channelled through the banking sector to affected MSMEs.

title: Anwar: Banking institutions must act as strategic partners to sustain domestic economy
text: This complements the RM5 billion guarantee under Syarikat Jaminan Pembiayaan Perniagaan Bhd (SJPP) announced previously, bringing the total financial support to MSMEs to RM10 billion.

title: Anwar: Banking institutions must act as strategic partners to sustain domestic economy
text: "I also emphasise that access to these facilities must be expanded fairly and comprehensively, including to new MSMEs who are truly in need.

title: Anwar: Banking institutions must act as strategic partners to sustain domestic economy
text: Banks must not take a narrow approach; they must act as strategic partners to sustain the domestic economy.

title: Anwar: Banking institutions must act as strategic partners to sustain domestic economy
text: "The MADANI government will continue to act with firmness, discipline and clear priorities to safeguard local businesses, protect people’s jobs and ensure the country’s economy remains stable and resilient,” he said in a post on the X platform today.

title: Anwar: Banking institutions must act as strategic partners to sustain domestic economy
text: Anwar today held an engagement session with the chief executive officers of banking institutions to coordinate more decisive, structured actions to assist MSMEs currently facing significant strains following the global energy crisis and the West Asia conflict, which continue to affect the global supply chain.

title: China to ease people's burden by cutting retail prices of petrol and diesel from Wednesday (April 22)
text:
BEIJING, April 21 (Bernama-Xinhua) -- China will reduce retail prices of petrol and diesel from Wednesday to reflect recent changes in international oil prices, the country's top economic planner said on Tuesday

Petrol prices will be cut by 555 yuan (about US$80.91) per tonne, and diesel prices will drop by 530 yuan per tonne, according to the National Development and Reform Commission (NDRC).

title: China to ease people's burden by cutting retail prices of petrol and diesel from Wednesday (April 22)
text: Since the previous domestic pricing adjustment on April 7, international crude oil prices have fluctuated sharply.

title: China to ease people's burden by cutting retail prices of petrol and diesel from Wednesday (April 22)
text: Although oil prices rebounded notably on April 20 after falling steeply in previous days, the average price during the 10 working days considered for this round of adjustment remained lower than that recorded in the previous pricing cycle, the NDRC said.

title: China to ease people's burden by cutting retail prices of petrol and diesel from Wednesday (April 22)
text: China's three largest oil companies, China National Petroleum Corporation, China Petrochemical Corporation and China National Offshore Oil Corporation, along with other oil refineries, have been instructed to organise the production and transportation of refined oil products to ensure a stable supply.

title: China to ease people's burden by cutting retail prices of petrol and diesel from Wednesday (April 22)
text: Under China's current pricing mechanism, refined oil product prices are adjusted based on fluctuations in international crude oil prices.

title: China to ease people's burden by cutting retail prices of petrol and diesel from Wednesday (April 22)
text: Relevant government departments across regions should strengthen market supervision and inspections.

title: China to ease people's burden by cutting retail prices of petrol and diesel from Wednesday (April 22)
text: Meanwhile, they must take stringent measures to crack down on activities violating national pricing policies and safeguard market order, the NDRC noted.

title: China to ease people's burden by cutting retail prices of petrol and diesel from Wednesday (April 22)
text: -- Xinhua

title: Akmal: Govt bracing for downstream inflationary pressures
text: Akmal: Govt bracing for downstream inflationary pressures

title: Akmal: Govt bracing for downstream inflationary pressures
text: KUALA LUMPUR: Despite a robust 5.3% economic growth in the first quarter, the government is bracing for downstream inflationary pressures as global geopolitical tensions begin to disrupt supply chains.

title: Akmal: Govt bracing for downstream inflationary pressures
text: Economy Minister Akmal Nasrullah Mohd Nasir said while Malaysia’s economy remains resilient, the country cannot afford to be complacent.

title: Akmal: Govt bracing for downstream inflationary pressures
text: “Rising input costs will have a knock-on effect on the prices of everyday goods.

title: Akmal: Govt bracing for downstream inflationary pressures
text: “Inflationary pressures extend far beyond fuel prices.

title: Akmal: Govt bracing for downstream inflationary pressures
text: For exam­ple, if there are issues with the cost of fertilisers, it will impact the overall production costs,” he said.

title: Akmal: Govt bracing for downstream inflationary pressures
text: To cushion the blow and delay the impact on consumers, Akmal Nasrullah said the government has put strategic buffers in place.

title: Akmal: Govt bracing for downstream inflationary pressures
text: “We provide incentives for planters to ensure that agricultural activities can continue seamlessly.

title: Akmal: Govt bracing for downstream inflationary pressures
text: “At present, we have strong inventories and supplies.

title: Akmal: Govt bracing for downstream inflationary pressures
text: How­ever, when there is an increase in input costs like energy or raw materials, there will be downstream implications,” he said.

title: Akmal: Govt bracing for downstream inflationary pressures
text: The minister was asked to comment on the broader economic outlook following the recent uptick in inflation, which rose slightly from 1.4% in February to 1.7% in March.

title: Akmal: Govt bracing for downstream inflationary pressures
text: He said that the rate remains manageable compared to global standards due to policies implemented by the government.

title: Akmal: Govt bracing for downstream inflationary pressures
text: “Inflation must be evaluated based on the basket of goods.

title: Akmal: Govt bracing for downstream inflationary pressures
text: Global geopolitical tensions are beginning to impact energy supplies.

title: Akmal: Govt bracing for downstream inflationary pressures
text: “So we are seeing early signs of disruptions spilling over into other supply chains,” he said, adding that the government would monitor and evaluate the situation closely so that early preparations can be made.

title: Akmal: Govt bracing for downstream inflationary pressures
text: On the country’s overall economic performance, Akmal Nas­rullah said the government remains positive on the outlook.

title: Akmal: Govt bracing for downstream inflationary pressures
text: “Malaysia has better resilience compared to others.

title: Akmal: Govt bracing for downstream inflationary pressures
text:
The process of improvement must be continuous.”

Citing the energy sector, he said the government is taking a highly practical approach to upgrade the biodiesel mandate from B10 to B15 by focusing on operational capabilities rather than just relying on long-term facility development plans.

title: Akmal: Govt bracing for downstream inflationary pressures
text: Last week, the Department of Statistics Malaysia released its advanced estimates, projecting the economy to grow by 5.3% in the first quarter of this year.

title: Akmal: Govt bracing for downstream inflationary pressures
text: Chief statistician Datuk Seri Mohd Uzir Mahidin said the manu­­facturing sector maintained positive momentum in the first two months of the year with output expanding by 7.3% and 4.2% respectively, largely driven by export-oriented industries.

title: GDP likely to grow in first quarter of this year
text: GDP likely to grow in first quarter of this year

title: GDP likely to grow in first quarter of this year
text: PETALING JAYA: The gross domestic product (GDP) is expected to show resilient expansion in the first quarter of financial year 2026 (1Q26), supported by firm external trade momentum and steady domestic demand.

title: GDP likely to grow in first quarter of this year
text: This is despite growth likely having moderated from the previous quarter’s elevated pace.

title: GDP likely to grow in first quarter of this year
text: According to analysts, the advance 1Q26 GDP growth at 5.3% year-on-year (y-o-y) pointed to a still-robust performance, with underlying conditions suggesting continued stability heading into the rest of the year.

title: GDP likely to grow in first quarter of this year
text: BIMB Research noted there was a clear upside bias to growth in 1Q26, largely concentrated in March, driven by a rebound in mining and agricultural output as well as stronger exports amid firmer global commodity prices and improved production conditions.

title: GDP likely to grow in first quarter of this year
text: In its report, the research house said: “We project exports to contribute positively to GDP growth in 1Q26, supported by a notable strengthening in external trade momentum.

title: GDP likely to grow in first quarter of this year
text:
“In the first two months of 2026, Malaysia recorded a trade surplus of RM38.7bil, more than double the RM16.3bil surplus in January to February 2025 (2M25), representing a 137.6% y-o-y increase.”

“This widening surplus was primarily driven by robust export growth of 15.2% y-o-y over 2M26, while import growth remained more moderate at an average of 6.4% y-o-y,” it added.

title: GDP likely to grow in first quarter of this year
text: BIMB Research projected export growth of 13.5% y-o-y in 1Q26, citing manufacturing exports and re-exports as key contributors.

title: GDP likely to grow in first quarter of this year
text: On the broader outlook, it projected GDP growth to moderate from 5.2% in 2025 to 4.7% in 2026, placing its forecast at the upper end of Bank Negara Malaysia’s projected range of 4% to 5% for 2026.

title: GDP likely to grow in first quarter of this year
text: It emphasised that domestic demand would remain the main pillar, while commodity price strength and tourism recovery posed upside risks.

title: GDP likely to grow in first quarter of this year
text: Apex Securities Research turned more constructive on near-term growth, revising its 1Q26 GDP estimate to plus 5.3% from plus 4.9% previously.

title: GDP likely to grow in first quarter of this year
text: “Despite rising geopolitical risks from the Middle East conflict, Malaysia’s underlying fundamentals remain supportive of growth momentum for the rest of the year,” the research house said.

title: GDP likely to grow in first quarter of this year
text: It highlighted private consumption as the key anchor, supported by labour market resilience and targeted subsidies.

title: GDP likely to grow in first quarter of this year
text: Apex Securities also underscored investment continuity, noting that record-high approved investments of RM426.7bil in 2025 would likely translate into realised investments in 2026.

title: GDP likely to grow in first quarter of this year
text: Further, it said early government engagement suggested no immediate cancellations despite geopolitical tensions.

title: GDP likely to grow in first quarter of this year
text: “We maintain our 2026 GDP forecast at plus 4.7%, which remains achievable under current conditions,” Apex Securities said.

title: GDP likely to grow in first quarter of this year
text: However, it cautioned that risks were tilted to the downside, particularly if Middle East tensions persisted into 3Q26, potentially dragging growth towards the lower end of the official range.

title: GDP likely to grow in first quarter of this year
text: TA Research adopted a more neutral stance, making no changes to its 1Q26 GDP assumption, noting that the advance estimate was broadly in line with its expectations.

title: GDP likely to grow in first quarter of this year
text: “For 2026, we maintain our GDP growth forecast in the range of 4.3% to 4.7% under our base case scenario,” it said, adding that growth would be supported by domestic demand and gradual external recovery.

title: GDP likely to grow in first quarter of this year
text: Hong Leong Investment Bank Research maintained its 2026 GDP growth forecast at 4.5%, underpinned by domestic demand and continued growth in electrical and electronics exports.

title: GDP likely to grow in first quarter of this year
text: It noted steady private consumption, supported by subsidies and policy measures, but warned of downside risks from higher costs and potential disruptions in the second half of the year.

title: GDP likely to grow in first quarter of this year
text: Official data showed Malaysia’s advance GDP estimate indicated that growth moderated to 5.3% in 1Q26, easing from 6.3% in 4Q25 but remaining above the central bank’s 4% to 5% annual target.

title: GDP likely to grow in first quarter of this year
text: The moderation in 1Q26 growth reflected normalisation after strong year-end expansion, particularly in construction and services, while underlying momentum remained intact.

title: GDP likely to grow in first quarter of this year
text: Actual GDP data for 1Q26 will be released on May 15, 2026.

title: GDP likely to grow in first quarter of this year
text: Meanwhile, one analyst said the underlying GDP momentum still appeared intact, as steady consumption and improving trade conditions helped cushion the impact of a more challenging global environment.

title: GDP likely to grow in first quarter of this year
text: “Growth in 1Q26 likely remained on a firm footing, with external demand and domestic spending providing sufficient support to growth,” he said.

title: Thailand considers an emergency decree for 500 billion baht borrowing due to current global uncertainties
text: Thailand considers an emergency decree for 500 billion baht borrowing due to current global uncertainties

title: Thailand considers an emergency decree for 500 billion baht borrowing due to current global uncertainties
text: BANGKOK (Bernama): The Thai government is considering issuing an emergency decree to borrow up to 500 billion baht (RM61.61 billion) to prepare for potential economic and security crises, Deputy Prime Minister Pakorn Nilprapunt said on Monday.

title: Thailand considers an emergency decree for 500 billion baht borrowing due to current global uncertainties
text: Pakorn said the move is being assessed under the government’s constitutional authority, which permits the issuance of an emergency decree in urgent situations affecting national security or the economy.

title: Thailand considers an emergency decree for 500 billion baht borrowing due to current global uncertainties
text: He noted that under Section 172 of the Constitution, such a decree may be enacted when there is an immediate necessity but must subsequently be submitted to the House of Representatives at the earliest opportunity.

title: Thailand considers an emergency decree for 500 billion baht borrowing due to current global uncertainties
text: "The current situation requires urgent action as the country faces multiple risks, while fiscal resources remain limited and tight,” he said in a statement.

title: Thailand considers an emergency decree for 500 billion baht borrowing due to current global uncertainties
text: Pakorn highlighted growing external uncertainties, including geopolitical conflicts and environmental challenges such as the Super El Niño phenomenon, which could impact agricultural output and the wider economy.

title: Thailand considers an emergency decree for 500 billion baht borrowing due to current global uncertainties
text: He said the government needs to maintain sufficient financial reserves to respond swiftly to emergencies, although actual borrowing may not reach the full 500 billion baht ceiling.

title: Thailand considers an emergency decree for 500 billion baht borrowing due to current global uncertainties
text: "Under public debt management principles, the government must first expand its borrowing capacity to the full amount stipulated in the law,” he added.

title: Thailand considers an emergency decree for 500 billion baht borrowing due to current global uncertainties
text: Pakorn said the government may also pursue a Budget Transfer Act in parallel to strengthen financial readiness in dealing with potential crises.

title: Thailand considers an emergency decree for 500 billion baht borrowing due to current global uncertainties
text: He added that the borrowing plan would require raising the public debt ceiling from the current 70 per cent of gross domestic product (GDP), with Thailand’s public debt currently at about 66 per cent.

title: Thailand considers an emergency decree for 500 billion baht borrowing due to current global uncertainties
text: The Ministry of Finance will determine a new ceiling as fiscal space narrows.

title: Thailand considers an emergency decree for 500 billion baht borrowing due to current global uncertainties
text: Separately, Pakorn said a proposal to allow the Finance Ministry to guarantee loans of up to 150 billion baht for the Oil Fuel Fund remains under review, noting that prolonged subsidies have pushed the fund’s debt above 100 billion baht, beyond its original role as a short-term stabilisation tool.

title: Thailand considers an emergency decree for 500 billion baht borrowing due to current global uncertainties
text: -- BERNAMA

title: Govt cautious on inflation, supply chain risks despite strong Q1 growth
text: Govt cautious on inflation, supply chain risks despite strong Q1 growth

title: Govt cautious on inflation, supply chain risks despite strong Q1 growth
text: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia's economy is riding on strong momentum from the previous year, but the government remains cautious about geopolitical risks, supply chain disruptions and inflation, says the Economy Minister.

title: Govt cautious on inflation, supply chain risks despite strong Q1 growth
text: Commenting on the Statistics Department's (DOSM) advanced gross domestic product (GDP) estimates of 5.3% for the first quarter, Akmal Nasrullah Mohd Nasir said the figures reflect a continuous expansion of domestic economic activities.

title: Govt cautious on inflation, supply chain risks despite strong Q1 growth
text: "We started from a very good year in 2025, and in that sense, the momentum was carried forward to the first quarter of this year.

title: Govt cautious on inflation, supply chain risks despite strong Q1 growth
text: This growth indicates that our economic activities are continuing to expand," he said on Monday (April 20).

title: Govt cautious on inflation, supply chain risks despite strong Q1 growth
text: However, Akmal warned that global geopolitical tensions are beginning to impact energy supplies to Malaysia, with early signs of disruptions spilling over into other supply chains.

title: Govt cautious on inflation, supply chain risks despite strong Q1 growth
text:
ALSO READ: Malaysia's 1Q GDP to grow 5.3%, reflecting resilience amid elevated oil prices, global uncertainty

"Although it is still in the early stages, we are continuously monitoring and evaluating the situation so that early preparations can be made by the relevant parties," he added.

title: Govt cautious on inflation, supply chain risks despite strong Q1 growth
text: On the country's broader economic outlook, the minister said the government aims to remain positive for this year while exercising caution.

title: Govt cautious on inflation, supply chain risks despite strong Q1 growth
text: "Malaysia has better resilience compared to some other places.

title: Govt cautious on inflation, supply chain risks despite strong Q1 growth
text: But... if we are too complacent with what we have, it may affect the standards we have already (set).

title: Govt cautious on inflation, supply chain risks despite strong Q1 growth
text: "The process of improvement must be continuous, and we must increase our capabilities whenever there is an opportunity," he said.

title: Govt cautious on inflation, supply chain risks despite strong Q1 growth
text: Citing the energy sector as an example, Akmal Nasrullah noted that the government is taking a highly practical approach to upgrade the biodiesel mandate from B10 to B15 by focusing on operational capabilities rather than just relying on long-term facility development plans.

title: Govt cautious on inflation, supply chain risks despite strong Q1 growth
text: Addressing concerns over a recent uptick in inflation, which rose slightly from 1.4% in February to 1.7% in March, the minister acknowledged the spike but assured that it remains manageable when compared globally.

title: Govt cautious on inflation, supply chain risks despite strong Q1 growth
text: "We definitely see a slight increase, but inflation must be evaluated based on the basket of goods.

title: Govt cautious on inflation, supply chain risks despite strong Q1 growth
text: The policies implemented by the government show that our inflation for March remains manageable compared to other countries," he said.

title: Govt cautious on inflation, supply chain risks despite strong Q1 growth
text: Nevertheless, Akmal Nasrullah stressed that the government is not resting on its laurels, noting that inflationary pressures extend beyond just fuel prices.

title: Govt cautious on inflation, supply chain risks despite strong Q1 growth
text: "This is not something to boast about.

title: Govt cautious on inflation, supply chain risks despite strong Q1 growth
text: We know the impact is not just on fuel supply.

title: Govt cautious on inflation, supply chain risks despite strong Q1 growth
text: When input materials are affected – for example, if there are issues with the cost of fertilisers – it will definitely have a knock-on effect on overall production costs," he explained.

title: Govt cautious on inflation, supply chain risks despite strong Q1 growth
text: The government has put strategic buffers in place to mitigate these risks, he added.

title: Govt cautious on inflation, supply chain risks despite strong Q1 growth
text: "For instance, we provide incentives for planters to ensure that agricultural activities can continue seamlessly.

title: Govt cautious on inflation, supply chain risks despite strong Q1 growth
text: The immediate impact of this inflation might be slightly lagging because we currently have strong inventories and supplies.

title: Govt cautious on inflation, supply chain risks despite strong Q1 growth
text: "However, realistically, we expect that whenever there is an increase in input costs, whether it is energy or other raw materials, there will eventually be downstream implications," he said.

title: Govt cautious on inflation, supply chain risks despite strong Q1 growth
text: On Friday (April 17), DOSM said the Malaysian economy is expected to grow by 5.3% in the first quarter of the year.

title: Govt cautious on inflation, supply chain risks despite strong Q1 growth
text: Chief statistician Datuk Seri Mohd Uzir Mahidin said the manufacturing sector maintained positive momentum in the first two months of this year, with output expanding by 7.3% and 4.2%, respectively, largely driven by export-oriented industries.

title: Nation's economy growing well with US$2bil in bonds bought recently, says Anwar
text: KUALA LUMPUR:The country's economy has shown great growth with US$2bil (RM7.9bil) in Malaysian bonds bought recently, says Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim.

title: Nation's economy growing well with US$2bil in bonds bought recently, says Anwar
text: He said Malaysia’s economy was on the uptick, with record levels of investments.

title: Nation's economy growing well with US$2bil in bonds bought recently, says Anwar
text: "A war in the Middle East is sending tremors through global markets, driving energy prices up and pushing capital out of some markets.

title: Nation's economy growing well with US$2bil in bonds bought recently, says Anwar
text: "Yet in recent weeks, global funds bought over two billion dollars in Malaysian bonds, the highest inflow in 10 months," he said in his speech before launching the Defence Services Asia (DSA) and National Security (Natsec) Asia 2026 exhibitions on Monday (April 20).

title: Nation's economy growing well with US$2bil in bonds bought recently, says Anwar
text: Anwar said the ringgit has been Asia’s best-performing currency for two consecutive years.

title: Nation's economy growing well with US$2bil in bonds bought recently, says Anwar
text: "Our economy grew 5.2% last year while we also attracted record levels of investment, driven by manufacturing, semiconductors and aerospace," he added.

title: Nation's economy growing well with US$2bil in bonds bought recently, says Anwar
text: The Prime Minister said that this was because of the commitment to policy reforms and sustained engagements with companies and fund managers.

text: "They have seen a government that follows through on what it commits to.

title: Nation's economy growing well with US$2bil in bonds bought recently, says Anwar
text: They have seen an economy that held its ground while conditions around it deteriorated.

title: Nation's economy growing well with US$2bil in bonds bought recently, says Anwar
text: "And they have seen something that Malaysians ourselves do not say often enough; that this country makes things the world genuinely depends on," he said.

title: Nation's economy growing well with US$2bil in bonds bought recently, says Anwar
text: He took note of companies participating in the exhibtions involved in the defence sectors, namely those in the aerospace industry, and semiconductor plants testing and packing chips for defence and security systems.

title: Nation's economy growing well with US$2bil in bonds bought recently, says Anwar
text: "One Malaysian-owned company holds Tier 1 supplier status with a leading global aero-engine manufacturer, producing here in Malaysia components that only a small number of countries are trusted to make.

title: Nation's economy growing well with US$2bil in bonds bought recently, says Anwar
text: "The culture of precision and accountability that satisfies the most exacting commercial buyers does not change at the boundary between civil and defence aerospace.

title: Claims of “only 15% affected” out of touch with reality: entire nation is paying for diesel price hikes says MCA VP
text: Claims of “only 15% affected” out of touch with reality: entire nation is paying for diesel price hikes says MCA VP

title: Claims of “only 15% affected” out of touch with reality: entire nation is paying for diesel price hikes says MCA VP
text: KUALA LUMPUR: The claim that only 15% of Malaysians are affected by the recent diesel price increase does not reflect the broader economic reality, according to MCA Vice President and Economic & SMEs Affairs Committee Chairman Datuk Lawrence Low.

title: Claims of “only 15% affected” out of touch with reality: entire nation is paying for diesel price hikes says MCA VP
text: In a statement on Sunday (April 19), Low said the impact of higher diesel prices extends far beyond direct diesel users, as diesel is a major cost component across multiple sectors of the economy.

title: Claims of “only 15% affected” out of touch with reality: entire nation is paying for diesel price hikes says MCA VP
text: “Rising diesel costs would eventually be passed through the supply chain and reflected in the prices of goods and services paid by consumers,” he said.

title: Claims of “only 15% affected” out of touch with reality: entire nation is paying for diesel price hikes says MCA VP
text: Low argued that the key issue is not how many people purchase diesel directly, but how higher fuel costs affect transportation, construction materials and food prices.

title: Claims of “only 15% affected” out of touch with reality: entire nation is paying for diesel price hikes says MCA VP
text: He said these increases would make it difficult for any segment of the population to remain unaffected.

title: Claims of “only 15% affected” out of touch with reality: entire nation is paying for diesel price hikes says MCA VP
text:
ALSO READ: 85% of Malaysians still enjoy subsidised diesel, says Anwar

“Sectors such as logistics, construction and tourism are among the first to feel the impact of higher diesel prices.

title: Claims of “only 15% affected” out of touch with reality: entire nation is paying for diesel price hikes says MCA VP
text: “Once operating costs rise in these industries, businesses typically transfer those costs to consumers, contributing to broader inflationary pressure,” he said.

title: Claims of “only 15% affected” out of touch with reality: entire nation is paying for diesel price hikes says MCA VP
text: Low also said similar assurances had previously been made regarding electricity tariff hikes, but market prices eventually rose as businesses passed on higher costs.

title: Claims of “only 15% affected” out of touch with reality: entire nation is paying for diesel price hikes says MCA VP
text: He added that companies already facing increased expenses for fuel, gas, wages and rentals have limited ability to absorb further increases.

title: Claims of “only 15% affected” out of touch with reality: entire nation is paying for diesel price hikes says MCA VP
text: He urged the government to recognise the wider economic effects of diesel price adjustments and to introduce targeted measures to reduce the burden on businesses and consumers.

title: Claims of “only 15% affected” out of touch with reality: entire nation is paying for diesel price hikes says MCA VP
text: Among the proposals put forward were short-term relief measures for SMEs and the food and beverage sector, a review of the diesel pricing mechanism to minimise supply chain disruption, greater transparency in fuel pricing, faster transmission of global oil price declines into domestic fuel prices, and temporary loan repayment deferments to ease business cash flow.

title: Claims of “only 15% affected” out of touch with reality: entire nation is paying for diesel price hikes says MCA VP
text: Prompt government action would be necessary to address the systemic impact of rising diesel prices on the economy and cost of living, said Low.

title: Unity key to Malaysia's revival as 'Asian Tiger', says Zahid
text: Unity key to Malaysia's revival as 'Asian Tiger', says Zahid

title: Unity key to Malaysia's revival as 'Asian Tiger', says Zahid
text: SHAH ALAM: Malaysia has the potential to regain its status as an 'Asian Tiger' if its people unite and set aside their differences to face current economic challenges, says Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi.

title: Unity key to Malaysia's revival as 'Asian Tiger', says Zahid
text: The Deputy Prime Minister said the country's strength does not depend solely on economic policies but also on the unity of its people in addressing issues such as rising global oil prices, which have a direct impact on the cost of living.

title: Unity key to Malaysia's revival as 'Asian Tiger', says Zahid
text: "We are facing these challenges together.

title: Unity key to Malaysia's revival as 'Asian Tiger', says Zahid
text: We must not only pray but also strive to overcome whatever problems arise from the increase in oil prices, which leads to higher prices of goods and ultimately increases the cost of living.

title: Unity key to Malaysia's revival as 'Asian Tiger', says Zahid
text: "I am confident that Malaysia will once again become an 'economic tiger of Asia' when we unite in facing these difficulties.

title: Unity key to Malaysia's revival as 'Asian Tiger', says Zahid
text: Let us set aside our religious, political and other differences… let us all support the government so that these issues can be resolved as quickly as possible," he said.

title: Unity key to Malaysia's revival as 'Asian Tiger', says Zahid
text: He said this at the Vaisakhi celebration at Gurdwara Sahib Guru Nanak here on Saturday (April 18), which was also attended by the Gurdwara's president Datuk Bhupindar Singh and the Punjabi Party of Malaysia president Datuk Gurjeet Singh Rhande.

title: Unity key to Malaysia's revival as 'Asian Tiger', says Zahid
text: Zahid, who is also Rural and Regional Development Minister, said religious differences are not a barrier for people to help one another, as demonstrated by gurdwara institutions that stepped forward to assist flood victims regardless of race and religion during the major floods that hit Selangor in December 2021.

title: Unity key to Malaysia's revival as 'Asian Tiger', says Zahid
text: "We saw firsthand how gurdwaras stepped in to assist flood victims when help was most needed.

title: Unity key to Malaysia's revival as 'Asian Tiger', says Zahid
text: This support was not limited to the Sikh community but extended to Malays, Chinese, Indians and people of all backgrounds, with aid provided by gurdwaras in Shah Alam.

title: Unity key to Malaysia's revival as 'Asian Tiger', says Zahid
text: "This is a commendable gesture and a positive example that we should emulate – not only by doing good deeds but by demonstrating them through sincere actions, not merely words or writing, as shown by the Sikh community in Shah Alam, Selangor and across Malaysia," he said.

title: Unity key to Malaysia's revival as 'Asian Tiger', says Zahid
text: On Vaisakhi, Ahmad Zahid said the celebration is important not only to the Sikh community but also as a reflection of Malaysia's diverse religious and cultural festivities.

title: Unity key to Malaysia's revival as 'Asian Tiger', says Zahid
text: He said that as a multiracial nation, Malaysians must continue to foster mutual respect to ensure that harmony and stability are always preserved.

title: Unity key to Malaysia's revival as 'Asian Tiger', says Zahid
text: "Malaysia is a multiracial country and we must respect all religions.

title: Unity key to Malaysia's revival as 'Asian Tiger', says Zahid
text: For our own faith to be respected, we must extend the same respect to others," he said.

title: Unity key to Malaysia's revival as 'Asian Tiger', says Zahid
text: Vaisakhi is celebrated by the Sikh community to mark the beginning of the new year in the Sikh calendar.

title: Unity key to Malaysia's revival as 'Asian Tiger', says Zahid
text: – Bernama

title: Mudra: RM200mil allocation driven by positive response to ministry financing scheme, says Steven Sim
text: Mudra: RM200mil allocation driven by positive response to ministry financing scheme, says Steven Sim

title: Mudra: RM200mil allocation driven by positive response to ministry financing scheme, says Steven Sim
text: PUTRAJAYA: Allocation exceeding RM220mil under the Indian Entrepreneur Capital and Economic Stimulus Package (Mudra) this year marks an increase of RM60mil, driven by strong response to financing schemes by the Entrepreneur Development and Cooperatives Ministry, says Steven Sim.

title: Mudra: RM200mil allocation driven by positive response to ministry financing scheme, says Steven Sim
text: The minister said the package was aimed at strengthening the ecosystem of Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises (MSMEs) as well as cooperatives owned by the Indian community in the country.

title: Mudra: RM200mil allocation driven by positive response to ministry financing scheme, says Steven Sim
text: "The allocation exceeding RM220mil this year represents an increase of RM60mil compared with approximately RM160mil channelled in 2025,” he said in a statement on Saturday (April 18).

title: Mudra: RM200mil allocation driven by positive response to ministry financing scheme, says Steven Sim
text: He said Mudra would be implemented through five agencies under his ministry, namely Tekun Nasional, Bank Rakyat, SME Bank, Amanah Ikhtiar Malaysia (AIM) and the Malaysian Cooperative Commission (SKM).

title: Mudra: RM200mil allocation driven by positive response to ministry financing scheme, says Steven Sim
text: He said Tekun Nasional would provide RM120mil through the Indian Community Entrepreneur Development Scheme (SPUMI), followed by SME Bank with RM50mil via the Vanigham Financing Scheme, while Bank Rakyat has allocated RM15mil for the Bank Rakyat Indian Entrepreneur Financing-i (BRIEF-i).

title: Mudra: RM200mil allocation driven by positive response to ministry financing scheme, says Steven Sim
text: "AIM is providing RM27mil through the Indian Women Entrepreneur Empowerment Programme Fund (PENN), while SKM is allocating RM11mil, comprising RM1mil under Bakti Madani and RM10mil for the SKM Revolving Capital Fund (TMPSKM),” he said.

title: Mudra: RM200mil allocation driven by positive response to ministry financing scheme, says Steven Sim
text: He said Mudra was not merely short-term financial assistance, but a strategic measure to enhance the competitiveness of Indian community MSMEs in ensuring business sustainability amid global energy uncertainties.

title: Mudra: RM200mil allocation driven by positive response to ministry financing scheme, says Steven Sim
text: He said the ministry remained committed to building an inclusive entrepreneurial ecosystem, in line with the aspirations of Malaysia Madani.

title: Mudra: RM200mil allocation driven by positive response to ministry financing scheme, says Steven Sim
text: He said as of March 31, the implementation of the initiative had shown very encouraging performance, with RM33.48mil approved, directly benefiting 1,805 Indian community entrepreneurs nationwide.- Bernama

title: South Korean President pledges ‘tangible contribution’ to Hormuz passage at summit
text: South Korean President pledges ‘tangible contribution’ to Hormuz passage at summit

title: South Korean President pledges ‘tangible contribution’ to Hormuz passage at summit
text: SEOUL: President Lee Jae Myung said South Korea was prepared to make a “tangible contribution” to securing passage through the Strait of Hormuz, voicing concern over the closure’s far-reaching impact on the global economy and supply chains.

title: South Korean President pledges ‘tangible contribution’ to Hormuz passage at summit
text: Lee spoke during a videoconference on Friday (April 17) hosted by the leaders of France and the United Kingdom on restoring freedom of navigation through the strategic chokepoint, a major artery for global oil flows disrupted by the US-Israeli strikes on Iran.

title: South Korean President pledges ‘tangible contribution’ to Hormuz passage at summit
text: Lee was the first among those joining by video to speak, apart from the leaders of France, the United Kingdom, Germany and Italy, who attended the meeting in person in Paris, which drew representatives from around 50 countries, according to presidential spokesperson Jeon Eun-su.

title: South Korean President pledges ‘tangible contribution’ to Hormuz passage at summit
text: “President Lee emphasised that the Republic of Korea is a key stakeholder, importing around 70 per cent of its crude oil through the Strait of Hormuz, and expressed a strong willingness to make a tangible contribution to ensuring freedom of navigation in the waterway,” Jeon said in a press statement summarising Lee’s remarks.

title: South Korean President pledges ‘tangible contribution’ to Hormuz passage at summit
text: The Republic of Korea is South Korea's official name.

title: South Korean President pledges ‘tangible contribution’ to Hormuz passage at summit
text: The Strait of Hormuz is vital to South Korea, which is highly dependent on oil imports, for both energy and petrochemical industry.

title: South Korean President pledges ‘tangible contribution’ to Hormuz passage at summit
text: “President Lee proposed that the international community jointly seek ways to swiftly break the current deadlock and establish a management mechanism to ensure stability in the strait,” Jeon added.

title: South Korean President pledges ‘tangible contribution’ to Hormuz passage at summit
text: Lee also “expressed concern over a situation in which the blockade of the Strait of Hormuz — a public asset and a vital pillar sustaining global supply chains — is shaking global energy, financial, industrial and food security as a whole,” according to Jeon.

title: South Korean President pledges ‘tangible contribution’ to Hormuz passage at summit
text: During the meeting, Lee “pointed out that conditions remain such that the safety and health of sailors stranded in the strait, including South Korean nationals, cannot be sufficiently guaranteed,” Jeon added.

title: South Korean President pledges ‘tangible contribution’ to Hormuz passage at summit
text: Participating countries shared their assessments of the situation surrounding the Strait of Hormuz during the meeting.

title: South Korean President pledges ‘tangible contribution’ to Hormuz passage at summit
text: They also “agreed to promote diplomatic and military cooperation to secure freedom of navigation and safety in the waterway, while building trust after the war,” Jeon explained.

title: South Korean President pledges ‘tangible contribution’ to Hormuz passage at summit
text:
Following the summit, Lee underscored on X that “stability in the waters concerned and the guarantee of freedom of navigation are grave matters directly linked to our economy and the daily lives of our people.”

“As a globally responsible nation, we will play a responsible role in ensuring freedom of navigation in the strait based on international law,” Lee said.

title: South Korean President pledges ‘tangible contribution’ to Hormuz passage at summit
text:
“We also plan to actively seek ways to enhance diplomatic and military cooperation in preparation for any future changes in the situation.”

Lee also pledged to “take a leading role in international efforts to uphold the principle of free international navigation and the stability of global supply chains.”

Leaders and representatives from 49 countries and two international organisations took part, including those from South Korea, Canada, Australia, the Netherlands, Sweden, New Zealand, Iraq and Singapore.

title: South Korean President pledges ‘tangible contribution’ to Hormuz passage at summit
text: China and Japan were represented by officials below the head-of-state level.

title: South Korean President pledges ‘tangible contribution’ to Hormuz passage at summit
text: The summit followed a series of earlier efforts, including a virtual meeting of military chiefs from 35 countries hosted by France on March 26 and a foreign ministers’ meeting involving more than 40 countries led by Britain on April 2.

title: South Korean President pledges ‘tangible contribution’ to Hormuz passage at summit
text: South Korea attended both meetings.

title: South Korean President pledges ‘tangible contribution’ to Hormuz passage at summit
text: After the conflict began on Feb. 28, Iran effectively shut the narrow strait, and the US has since Monday been carrying out a military blockade against all vessels of all nations entering or leaving Iranian coastal waters or ports.

title: South Korean President pledges ‘tangible contribution’ to Hormuz passage at summit
text: French President Emmanuel Macron and British Prime Minister Keir Starmer co-chaired the meeting in Paris.

title: South Korean President pledges ‘tangible contribution’ to Hormuz passage at summit
text: German Chancellor Friedrich Merz and Italian Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni attended in person.

title: South Korean President pledges ‘tangible contribution’ to Hormuz passage at summit
text: The US is not part of the planning for what has been branded the new Strait of Hormuz Maritime Freedom of Navigation Initiative.

title: South Korean President pledges ‘tangible contribution’ to Hormuz passage at summit

title: South Korean President pledges ‘tangible contribution’ to Hormuz passage at summit

title: The ‘annoyance economy’ is more than just annoying
text: The ‘annoyance economy’ is more than just annoying

title: The ‘annoyance economy’ is more than just annoying
text: Everyone knows the feeling of being stuck on hold, or spending time clicking through an endless series of pages to cancel a subscription, or screening spam calls, or trying to change a flight.

title: The ‘annoyance economy’ is more than just annoying
text: These inconveniences make life less pleasant, but they are easy enough to write off as small concerns, as the mere stuff of living a contemporary existence.

title: The ‘annoyance economy’ is more than just annoying
text: It turns out that such time-sucking, tedious tasks have a meaningful economic impact.

title: The ‘annoyance economy’ is more than just annoying
text: The accumulated cost of what some refer to as the “annoyance economy” adds up to US$165bil (RM652bil) a year in lost time and wasted money for American families, according to a new report from Neale Mahoney, a Stanford University economist, and Chad Maisel, a policy fellow at Groundwork Collaborative, a progressive research organisation.

title: The ‘annoyance economy’ is more than just annoying
text: The annoyance economy includes “the everyday interactions that should be simple but often turn into fraught ordeals,” Mahoney said in an interview, including dealing with hidden fees, spam, insurance claims or byzantine cancellation processes.

title: The ‘annoyance economy’ is more than just annoying
text: The co-authors worked together in the Biden administration, including on efforts to crack down on junk fees.

title: The ‘annoyance economy’ is more than just annoying
text:
Once they left government service, Mahoney said, they started thinking about how to tackle what they saw as a “broader set of annoyances and tricks and traps and frustrations that turn simple things into things which are costly and complex.”

At first, the authors did not know what to call this group of consumer problems.

title: The ‘annoyance economy’ is more than just annoying
text: But as they kept working on the project, they landed on the term “annoyance economy.” Once they did, Mahoney said, “it became obvious that it connected these different interactions people have.” (The term had appeared elsewhere, including in a 2023 Atlantic article about consumer frustrations.)

title: The ‘annoyance economy’ is more than just annoying
text: Some of the inconveniences plaguing American consumers are a result of clunky processes and outdated regulation.

title: The ‘annoyance economy’ is more than just annoying
text: But some, Mahoney said, are “intentionally designed” to take advantage of people.

title: The ‘annoyance economy’ is more than just annoying
text: And the rise of artificial intelligence chatbots that pose as real people is advancing the capacity for companies to mislead and target consumers.

title: The ‘annoyance economy’ is more than just annoying
text: “Scammers will be more adept at using AI to scam us than we will be at using it to detect scams,” he predicted.

title: The ‘annoyance economy’ is more than just annoying
text: Intentional or not, trapping consumers can pay off for corporations: Mahoney and his colleagues have found that companies that make it harder to cancel subscriptions see revenues tick up 14% to 200%.

title: The ‘annoyance economy’ is more than just annoying
text: The annoyance economy is almost universally disliked.

title: The ‘annoyance economy’ is more than just annoying
text: People, it turns out, hate being ripped off and resent having their time wasted.

title: The ‘annoyance economy’ is more than just annoying
text: In a 2024 YouGov poll, 87% of respondents strongly or somewhat supported restrictions on telemarketers’ use of robocalling.

title: The ‘annoyance economy’ is more than just annoying
text: In a 2025 poll from Groundwork and Data for Progress, two-thirds of likely voters said they wanted Congress to make addressing such issues a priority.

title: The ‘annoyance economy’ is more than just annoying
text: Public distaste could help spur lawmakers to mandate more consumer-friendly policies and companies to dial back fees and the like.

title: The ‘annoyance economy’ is more than just annoying
text: This will take some work, Mahoney suggested, but it is possible.

title: The ‘annoyance economy’ is more than just annoying
text: “We put people on the moon 50 years ago,” he said.

title: Malaysia’s economy remains robust, well supported by E&E sector, domestic demand
text: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia’s economy is expected to remain robust, well supported by the continued strength in the electrical and electronics sector and robust domestic demand amid global headwinds.

text: Following the Department of Statistics Malaysia’s (DOSM) announcement today that the economy is projected to grow by 5.3 per cent in the first quarter of 2026 (1Q 2026) based on advance estimates, HSBC Global Investment Research said Malaysia started 2026 on a resilient footing.

title: Malaysia’s economy remains robust, well supported by E&E sector, domestic demand
text: It maintained Malaysia’s gross domestic product (GDP) growth forecast at 4.5 per cent for 2026, placing it at the mid-point of Bank Negara Malaysia’s revised growth range of 4.0 to 5.0 per cent.

title: Malaysia’s economy remains robust, well supported by E&E sector, domestic demand
text: "While the figure came in slightly below the market expectation of 5.5 per cent year-on-year (y-o-y), it exceeded our forecast of 5.1 per cent,” it said.

title: Malaysia’s economy remains robust, well supported by E&E sector, domestic demand
text: HSBC Global said two key surprises were the resilience of the manufacturing sector, which grew nearly six per cent y-o-y, driven by the ongoing boom in the electronics segment, where Malaysia is a key beneficiary in the supply chain.

title: Malaysia’s economy remains robust, well supported by E&E sector, domestic demand
text: The second was the moderation in construction growth to single digits after eight consecutive quarters of double-digit expansion.

title: Malaysia’s economy remains robust, well supported by E&E sector, domestic demand
text: "This may be an early sign that the sector’s growth is moderating,” it said.

title: Malaysia’s economy remains robust, well supported by E&E sector, domestic demand
text: Meanwhile, HSBC Global said Bank Negara Malaysia (BNM) is the only central bank in Asia that has upgraded its growth forecast despite the West Asia conflict.

title: Malaysia’s economy remains robust, well supported by E&E sector, domestic demand
text: "We do not expect BNM to move in either direction, expecting the central bank to keep its policy rate unchanged at 2.75 per cent,” it said.

title: Malaysia’s economy remains robust, well supported by E&E sector, domestic demand
text: Meanwhile, RHB Investment Bank Bhd (RHB IB) maintained a constructive outlook on Malaysia’s economy, projecting GDP growth at around 4.7 per cent in 2026, supported by domestic consumption, private investment and export activity.

title: Malaysia’s economy remains robust, well supported by E&E sector, domestic demand
text: The investment bank opined that the retail and wholesale sectors should continue to benefit from a firm labour market, steady wage growth, and rising household incomes.

title: Malaysia’s economy remains robust, well supported by E&E sector, domestic demand
text: It said manufacturing remains on a broadly positive trajectory, supported by sustained global E&E demand and a robust domestic economy, though it is sensitive to external developments and fluctuations in input costs.

title: Malaysia’s economy remains robust, well supported by E&E sector, domestic demand
text: "Private investment is projected to stay robust, driven by infrastructure rollouts, data centre developments, and continued expansion in the manufacturing sector,” it added.

title: Malaysia’s economy remains robust, well supported by E&E sector, domestic demand
text: It said Malaysia is expected to be the least negatively affected economy among ASEAN member states under the current conflict scenario.

title: Malaysia’s economy remains robust, well supported by E&E sector, domestic demand

title: Malaysia’s economy remains robust, well supported by E&E sector, domestic demand
text: RHB IB added that in a worst-case scenario, a sustained increase in oil prices to US$140 per barrel, from a pre-conflict average of US$70, could reduce GDP growth by about 0.5 percentage points to around 4.2 per cent.

title: Malaysia’s economy remains robust, well supported by E&E sector, domestic demand
text: "Should these risks materialise, Malaysia’s GDP growth in 2026 could ease to around 4.0 per cent, with external headwinds partly offset by strong domestic fundamentals, proactive policy support and its position as a net energy exporter, which may provide some cushioning,” it said.

title: Ringgit strengthens against US dollar on strong 1Q GDP estimate
text: KUALA LUMPUR: The ringgit closed higher against the US dollar on Friday, supported by positive sentiment following the release of Malaysia’s advance gross domestic product (GDP) estimates for the first quarter of 2026 (1Q 2026), said an analyst.

title: Ringgit strengthens against US dollar on strong 1Q GDP estimate
text: At 6 pm today, the local currency rose to 3.9505/9545 versus the greenback from 3.9520/9560 at yesterday’s close.

title: Ringgit strengthens against US dollar on strong 1Q GDP estimate
text: Earlier, the Department of Statistics Malaysia (DOSM) stated that Malaysia’s economy is projected to grow by 5.3 per cent in 1Q 2026 based on advance estimates despite rising global uncertainties, particularly elevated oil prices driven by geopolitical tensions.

title: Ringgit strengthens against US dollar on strong 1Q GDP estimate
text: IPPFA Sdn Bhd investment strategy director and country economist Mohd Sedek Jantan said the GDP outlook is firmer than expected, compared with Bloomberg’s consensus of 5.1 per cent, reinforcing Malaysia’s relative growth resilience within the region despite intensifying geopolitical headwinds.

title: Ringgit strengthens against US dollar on strong 1Q GDP estimate
text: "While a stronger dollar amid West Asia tensions continues to cap upside, the currency’s stabilisation reflects improving confidence in domestic demand durability and structural growth drivers, keeping the ringgit anchored within a constructive near-term range rather than a directional depreciation trend,” he told Bernama.

text: Meanwhile, Bank Muamalat Malaysia Bhd chief economist Mohd Afzanizam Abdul Rashid said full employment, sustained domestic demand, and encouraging external sector performance during January and February have helped the economy maintain a decent GDP growth.

text: At the close, the ringgit traded mostly higher against a basket of major currencies.
