text: Low inflation helps Malaysia weather energy supply shock, says BNM governor

title: Low inflation helps Malaysia weather energy supply shock, says BNM governor
text: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia’s relatively low inflation environment is expected to help the country weather the current energy supply shock, even as global oil prices are projected to remain higher for longer amid the ongoing war in West Asia, said Bank Negara Malaysia (BNM) Governor Datuk Seri Abdul Rasheed Ghaffour.

title: Low inflation helps Malaysia weather energy supply shock, says BNM governor
text: He said the central bank is projecting headline inflation to average between 1.5 per cent and 2.5 per cent in 2026, noting that the present situation reflects a supply-driven shock, with developments in global energy markets beginning to feed into domestic price dynamics.

title: Low inflation helps Malaysia weather energy supply shock, says BNM governor
text: "Certainly, what we are facing right now is really a supply shock, and it is good to see how this fits into our domestic prices,” he told CNBC in an interview held in Washington.

title: Low inflation helps Malaysia weather energy supply shock, says BNM governor
text: Abdul Rasheed is part of the Malaysian delegation led by Finance Minister II Datuk Seri Amir Hamzah Azizan at the 2026 Spring Meetings, which will take place from April 13 to April 18 at the International Monetary Fund and World Bank Group headquarters in Washington.

title: Low inflation helps Malaysia weather energy supply shock, says BNM governor
text: Abdul Rasheed also said oil prices are expected to remain elevated for an extended period, even if the war ends within the next few weeks.

title: Low inflation helps Malaysia weather energy supply shock, says BNM governor
text: "Although oil prices have gone up, we still have fuel subsidies for a group of the population under BUDI95, so that will, in a way, dampen the transmission of global cost conditions into domestic prices,” he added.

title: Low inflation helps Malaysia weather energy supply shock, says BNM governor
text: He highlighted that Malaysia’s role as a net energy exporter also helps cushion the current supply shock.

title: Low inflation helps Malaysia weather energy supply shock, says BNM governor
text: "The strengthening of the ringgit will also have some impact in terms of dampening the transmission of prices, but we are watching this very closely,” he added.

title: Low inflation helps Malaysia weather energy supply shock, says BNM governor
text: Previously, BNM released a baseline projection of crude oil prices between US$70 and US$90 per barrel, with an optimistic scenario ranging from US$90 to US$100 and a pessimistic outlook of US$110 and above.

title: Low inflation helps Malaysia weather energy supply shock, says BNM governor
text: As for the economy, BNM noted that Malaysia’s economic growth projection of 4.0 to 5.0 per cent for the year incorporates the baseline scenario and part of the downside risks.

title: Low inflation helps Malaysia weather energy supply shock, says BNM governor
text: On the domestic front, Abdul Rasheed noted that the tourism sector has recorded a strong recovery, surpassing pre-pandemic levels in both arrivals and spending, thus helping to lift the economy.

title: Low inflation helps Malaysia weather energy supply shock, says BNM governor
text: "In terms of tourist arrivals last year, it was quite good.

title: Low inflation helps Malaysia weather energy supply shock, says BNM governor
text: In fact, it surpassed the levels we saw pre-pandemic, both in terms of tourist arrivals and tourist spending itself,” he said.

title: Low inflation helps Malaysia weather energy supply shock, says BNM governor
text: Meanwhile, he assured that Malaysia’s economy is supported by stronger-than-expected growth and resilient fundamentals amid global uncertainties.

title: Low inflation helps Malaysia weather energy supply shock, says BNM governor
text: "The country’s growth performance in the third and fourth quarters of last year exceeded projections, with the economy expanding by 5.2 per cent.

title: Low inflation helps Malaysia weather energy supply shock, says BNM governor
text: "Therefore, we are entering this from a position of strength.

title: Low inflation helps Malaysia weather energy supply shock, says BNM governor
text: Entering into this from a position of strength does help us, but more importantly, we do have strong fundamentals that will help us weather this situation,” he added.

title: Low inflation helps Malaysia weather energy supply shock, says BNM governor
text: He noted that key growth drivers remain intact, with private consumption and investment continuing to show robustness.

title: Low inflation helps Malaysia weather energy supply shock, says BNM governor
text: "In terms of the drivers of growth, what we saw last year in terms of private consumption and investment was still robust, and despite all the uncertainty that we discussed last year, exports performed rather well,” he said.

title: Low inflation helps Malaysia weather energy supply shock, says BNM governor
text: On export performance, Abdul Rasheed said it was particularly supported by sustained demand for electrical and electronics, driven by rapid developments in artificial intelligence.

title: Low inflation helps Malaysia weather energy supply shock, says BNM governor

text: Malaysia's 1Q GDP to grow 5.3%, reflecting resilience amid elevated oil prices, global uncertainty

title: Malaysia's 1Q GDP to grow 5.3%, reflecting resilience amid elevated oil prices, global uncertainty
text: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia’s economy is projected to grow by 5.3 per cent in the first quarter of 2026 (1Q 2026) based on advance estimates, reflecting an economy that remains fundamentally resilient, despite rising global uncertainties, particularly elevated oil prices driven by geopolitical tensions, said the Department of Statistics Malaysia (DOSM).

title: Malaysia's 1Q GDP to grow 5.3%, reflecting resilience amid elevated oil prices, global uncertainty
text: Its chief statistician, Datuk Seri Mohd Uzir Mahidin, said the manufacturing sector maintained positive momentum in the first two months of 2026, with output expanding by 7.3 per cent and 4.2 per cent, respectively, largely driven by export-oriented industries.

title: Malaysia's 1Q GDP to grow 5.3%, reflecting resilience amid elevated oil prices, global uncertainty
text: He said seasonal festive spending during the Chinese New Year, alongside preparations for Ramadan and Aidilfitri, continued to support overall economic activities.

title: Malaysia's 1Q GDP to grow 5.3%, reflecting resilience amid elevated oil prices, global uncertainty
text: "This, coupled with the disbursement of Sumbangan Asas Rahmah (SARA) and the second phase of civil servant salary revisions, contributed to a steady growth in distributive trade sales, which increased by 7.3 per cent in January and 5.3 per cent in February 2026,” he said in a statement today.

title: Malaysia's 1Q GDP to grow 5.3%, reflecting resilience amid elevated oil prices, global uncertainty
text: On the external front, he said trade activity remained supported by continued expansion in both domestic exports and re-export.

title: Malaysia's 1Q GDP to grow 5.3%, reflecting resilience amid elevated oil prices, global uncertainty
text: A review of the sectoral performance indicates a moderation in growth momentum across the five main sectors in 1Q 2026.

title: Malaysia's 1Q GDP to grow 5.3%, reflecting resilience amid elevated oil prices, global uncertainty
text: Mohd Uzir said the services sector continued to anchor overall GDP expansion, registering a 5.4 per cent growth (4Q 2025: 6.3 per cent).

title: Malaysia's 1Q GDP to grow 5.3%, reflecting resilience amid elevated oil prices, global uncertainty
text: "This sector’s performance was attributed mainly to the growth in the wholesale and retail trade sub-sector, reflecting sustained consumer spending, supported by a stable labour market, higher household income, and ongoing people-centric initiatives to stimulate household-related activities," he said.

title: Malaysia's 1Q GDP to grow 5.3%, reflecting resilience amid elevated oil prices, global uncertainty
text: Meanwhile, the information and communication sub-sector also contributed to the performance in the quarter, supported by rising demand for data centre and generative artificial intelligence (AI)-related activities.

title: Malaysia's 1Q GDP to grow 5.3%, reflecting resilience amid elevated oil prices, global uncertainty
text: The manufacturing sector recorded a growth of 5.8 per cent (4Q 2025: 6.1 per cent), largely supported by strong output in the electrical, electronics and optical products, as well as vegetable and animal oils and fats and food processing.

title: Malaysia's 1Q GDP to grow 5.3%, reflecting resilience amid elevated oil prices, global uncertainty
text: Mohd Uzir added that the construction sector’s growth declined to 7.8 per cent in 1Q 2026 (4Q 2025: 11.0 per cent), supported primarily by specialised construction activities and non-residential buildings.

title: Malaysia's 1Q GDP to grow 5.3%, reflecting resilience amid elevated oil prices, global uncertainty
text: The agriculture sector grew 2.8 per cent (4Q 2025: 5.4 per cent), attributed to the growth in oil palm and livestock sub-sectors.

title: Malaysia's 1Q GDP to grow 5.3%, reflecting resilience amid elevated oil prices, global uncertainty
text: Meanwhile, the mining and quarrying sector slipped to negative 1.1 per cent from 2.0 per cent growth in the preceding quarter, influenced by lower production, particularly in crude oil and condensate and natural gas.

title: Malaysia's 1Q GDP to grow 5.3%, reflecting resilience amid elevated oil prices, global uncertainty
text: Following these advance estimates, the preliminary GDP data, which provides a detailed and comprehensive analysis of economic performance for 1Q 2026, will be released on May 15, 2026.

text: Malaysia's inflation rises 1.7% in March

title: Malaysia's inflation rises 1.7% in March
text: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia's inflation increased modestly to 1.7 per cent in March 2026, with the consumer price index (CPI) climbing to 136.4 from 134.1 a year earlier, the Department of Statistics Malaysia (DOSM) said.

title: Malaysia's inflation rises 1.7% in March
text: In a statement today, chief statistician Datuk Seri Dr Mohd Uzir Mahidin said Malaysia's inflation in March 2026 was mainly driven by an increase in the transport group to 1.6 per cent from -0.7 per cent in February 2026.

title: Malaysia's inflation rises 1.7% in March
text: Inflation was also driven by increases in the personal care, social protection and miscellaneous goods and services group, which rose by seven per cent (February 2026: 6.9 per cent).

title: Malaysia's inflation rises 1.7% in March
text: According to DOSM, the insurance and financial services group recorded 4.9 per cent (February 2026: 4.7 per cent); alcoholic beverages and tobacco, 2.7 per cent (February 2026: 2.6 per cent); restaurant and accommodation services, 2.6 per cent (February 2026: 2.5 per cent); health, 1.4 per cent (February 2026: 1.2 per cent); information and communication, 1.4 per cent (February 2026: 0.5 per cent); housing, water, electricity, gas and other fuels, 1.2 per cent (February 2026: 1.1 per cent); and recreation, sport and culture, 1.0 per cent, (February 2026: 0.8 per cent).

title: Malaysia's inflation rises 1.7% in March
text: "About 62.5 per cent of items (358 out of 573) recorded price increases.

title: Malaysia's inflation rises 1.7% in March
text: Nonetheless, of this total, 348 items (97.2 per cent) registered increases of 10 per cent or less, while only 10 items recorded increases of more than 10 per cent in March 2026.

title: Malaysia's inflation rises 1.7% in March
text: "The remaining 171 items (29.8 per cent) showed a decline, while 44 items remained unchanged," it added.

title: Malaysia's inflation rises 1.7% in March
text: The food and beverages group, which contributes 29.8 per cent of the total CPI weight, registered inflation of 1.1 per cent in March 2026 (February 2026: 1.3 per cent).

title: Malaysia's inflation rises 1.7% in March
text: This was driven by subgroups of food away from home, 2.3 per cent (February 2026: 2.4 per cent), and food at home, 0.1 per cent (February 2026: 0.3 per cent).

title: Malaysia's inflation rises 1.7% in March
text: Inflation for the housing, water, electricity, gas and other fuels group increased to 1.2 per cent in March 2026 compared with 1.1 per cent in February 2026.

title: Malaysia's inflation rises 1.7% in March
text: This was driven by inflation in the subgroup of maintenance, repair and security of dwelling, 3.3 per cent (February 2026: 3.2 per cent).

title: Malaysia's inflation rises 1.7% in March
text: DOSM said the average price of unleaded petrol RON97 in March 2026 was RM4.03 per litre compared with RM3.11 per litre in February 2026.

title: Malaysia's inflation rises 1.7% in March
text: Meanwhile, the average price for Diesel in Peninsular Malaysia increased to RM4.12 per litre compared with RM2.98 per litre in February 2026.

title: Malaysia's inflation rises 1.7% in March
text: However, the average price of Diesel for Sabah, Sarawak and Labuan remained at RM2.15 per litre.

title: Malaysia's inflation rises 1.7% in March
text: The average market price of unleaded petrol RON95 increased to RM3.16 per litre (February 2026: RM2.55 per litre), compared with the subsidised price of RM1.99 per litre.

title: Malaysia's inflation rises 1.7% in March
text: Mohd Uzir said eight states recorded inflation above the national rate of 1.7 per cent, namely Pahang (2.5 per cent), Labuan (2.4 per cent), Negeri Sembilan (2.2 per cent), Kuala Lumpur (2.2 per cent), Sabah (2.1 per cent), Johor (2.0 per cent), Kedah (1.9 per cent) and Pulau Pinang (1.8 per cent).

title: Malaysia's inflation rises 1.7% in March
text: However, the remaining eight states recorded inflation below or equal to the national rate, with Kelantan recording the lowest inflation at 0.7 per cent in March 2026.

title: Malaysia's inflation rises 1.7% in March
text: Month-on-month, headline inflation in March 2026 increased to 0.3 per cent compared with 0.2 per cent in February 2026.

title: Malaysia's inflation rises 1.7% in March
text: This was driven by the transport group (2.1 per cent, February 2026: 0.2 per cent); information and communication, (0.8 per cent, February 2026: -0.2 per cent); insurance and financial services (0.3 per cent, February 2026: 0.0 per cent); health (0.2 per cent, February 2026: 0.0 per cent); recreation, sport and culture (0.2 per cent, February 2026: 0.0 per cent) and education (0.2 per cent, February 2026: 0.1 per cent).

title: Malaysia's inflation rises 1.7% in March
text: Regionally, Malaysia's inflation of 1.7 per cent in March 2026 was lower than Vietnam (4.7 per cent), the Philippines (4.1 per cent), Indonesia (3.5 per cent), and South Korea (2.2 per cent).

title: Economy Ministry's agri scheme generates RM22mil in revenue
text: Economy Ministry's agri scheme generates RM22mil in revenue

title: Economy Ministry's agri scheme generates RM22mil in revenue
text: PUTRAJAYA: The People’s Income Initiative-Agricultural Entrepreneurship (IPR-Intan) programme under the Economy Ministry has generated cumulative revenue of about RM22mil since its introduction in 2023, says Akmal Nasrullah Mohd Nasir (pic).

title: Economy Ministry's agri scheme generates RM22mil in revenue
text: "One of the (IPR programme) segments is Intan, which focuses on agriculture, and so far it has produced about 2,300 participants," the Economy Minister told reporters after visiting the IPR-Intan project site on Friday (April 17).

title: Economy Ministry's agri scheme generates RM22mil in revenue
text: The programme includes the cultivation of crops such as chillies and cucumbers and not only enables the target group to earn additional income, but also supports the government’s efforts to ensure the country’s basic food supply remains stable.

title: Economy Ministry's agri scheme generates RM22mil in revenue
text: Akmal Nasrullah said the chilli cultivation project using fertigation (the direct application of water-soluble fertilisers to plants' root zones) on 4.04ha has benefited 20 participants, each earning an average monthly income of about RM3,000.

title: Economy Ministry's agri scheme generates RM22mil in revenue
text: "The project, now in its fifth round, has produced over 200 tonnes of chillies worth approximately RM1.5mil, allowing working capital to be sourced from the proceeds.

title: Economy Ministry's agri scheme generates RM22mil in revenue
text: "The programme demonstrates that assisting the target group is also included in our agenda to raise the people’s income, as well as to eradicate poverty," he said.

title: Economy Ministry's agri scheme generates RM22mil in revenue
text: Akmal Nasrullah said that this year, the ministry has agreed to implement 20 new IPR-Intan projects, benefiting 600 participants from the B40 group, as well as the poor and hardcore poor.

title: Economy Ministry's agri scheme generates RM22mil in revenue
text: He said this includes the expansion of the programme's Putrajaya project, which will see an additional 20 participants, bringing the total to 40.

title: Economy Ministry's agri scheme generates RM22mil in revenue
text: IPR-Intan Putrajaya is carried out on land belonging to the Putrajaya Corporation, and managed by the Farmers’ Organisation Authority in collaboration with the Kuala Langat Area Farmers’ Organisation.

title: Economy Ministry's agri scheme generates RM22mil in revenue

title: Malaysia to roll out B15 biodiesel in stages to protect fuel supply chain
text: Malaysia to roll out B15 biodiesel in stages to protect fuel supply chain

title: Malaysia to roll out B15 biodiesel in stages to protect fuel supply chain
text: PUTRAJAYA: The implementation of the B15 biodiesel blend rate will be carried out in stages to avoid disruption to the existing operations of the industry, said Economy Minister Akmal Nasrullah Mohd Nasir (pic).

title: Malaysia to roll out B15 biodiesel in stages to protect fuel supply chain
text: He said the progressive approach is important as operational aspects, particularly at blending centres, need to be organised meticulously without disrupting the current supply chain.

title: Malaysia to roll out B15 biodiesel in stages to protect fuel supply chain
text: "If it involves the arrangement of operations at blending centres without any infrastructure upgrades, the blending rate can be increased from B10 to B15.

title: Malaysia to roll out B15 biodiesel in stages to protect fuel supply chain
text: However, this must be done progressively and in stages to ensure that existing operations are not disrupted," he said.

title: Malaysia to roll out B15 biodiesel in stages to protect fuel supply chain
text: He said this to reporters after visiting the 'Inisiatif Pendapatan Rakyat-Usahawan Tani' (IPR-INTAN) project here on Friday (April 17).

title: Malaysia to roll out B15 biodiesel in stages to protect fuel supply chain
text: Basically, B15 refers to a blend of 15% palm oil-based biodiesel with 85% petroleum diesel, compared with B10 currently.

title: Malaysia to roll out B15 biodiesel in stages to protect fuel supply chain
text: B10 refers to 10%t biodiesel and 90% regular diesel.

title: Malaysia to roll out B15 biodiesel in stages to protect fuel supply chain
text: He said that to achieve a higher blending level, such as B20, further improvements and infrastructure investments are still needed.

title: Malaysia to roll out B15 biodiesel in stages to protect fuel supply chain
text: "If we want to reach a higher level of B20 and B30, investment in infrastructure needs to be made, specifically upgrading the infrastructure.

title: Malaysia to roll out B15 biodiesel in stages to protect fuel supply chain
text: So for the medium-term targets, there is indeed a need for us to upgrade the infrastructure," he said.

title: Malaysia to roll out B15 biodiesel in stages to protect fuel supply chain
text: The government previously agreed to increase the biodiesel blend rate from B10 to B15, starting with B12, at no additional cost, following developments in the Middle East crisis.

title: Malaysia to roll out B15 biodiesel in stages to protect fuel supply chain
text: Akmal Nasrullah was previously reported as saying that the measure aims to ensure the availability of the country's diesel supply for a longer period and to reduce dependence on fossil fuel imports and global market fluctuations.

title: Malaysia to roll out B15 biodiesel in stages to protect fuel supply chain
text: He also emphasised that the restructuring of the economy through the transition to renewable energy (RE) needs to be accelerated.

title: Malaysia to roll out B15 biodiesel in stages to protect fuel supply chain
text: Meanwhile, he said he and the Plantation and Commodities Minister, Datuk Seri Dr Noraini Ahmad, will also visit a facility in Port Klang on Saturday (April 18) to assess its readiness for biodiesel production.

title: Malaysia to roll out B15 biodiesel in stages to protect fuel supply chain
text: It is understood that the premises in question are KL-Kepong Oleomas Sdn Bhd (KLKOM), a Malaysian-based oleochemical manufacturing company.

title: Malaysia to roll out B15 biodiesel in stages to protect fuel supply chain
text: KLKOM is part of the KLK OLEO division of Kuala Lumpur Kepong Bhd, one of the world's largest plantation companies.

title: Malaysia to roll out B15 biodiesel in stages to protect fuel supply chain
text: – Bernama

title: Cautious optimism on growth prospects
text: Cautious optimism on growth prospects

title: Cautious optimism on growth prospects
text: PETALING JAYA: Local economists are keeping a cautious outlook on Malaysia’s growth prospects for this year and 2027, despite the International Monetary Fund’s (IMF) generous upgrade of the country’s 2026 gross domestic product (GDP) projection to 4.7%.

title: Cautious optimism on growth prospects
text: In its latest World Economic Outlook report released on Tuesday, the fund notably improved its GDP expansion forecast for Malaysia by 0.4 percentage points from its last prediction in January, although it had in the same note turned bearish on the global economy by downgrading its growth expectations.

title: Cautious optimism on growth prospects
text: The IMF’s report pointed out that the Middle East conflict has already halted late-2025 economic momentum, leaving the world economy drifting toward an “adverse scenario” even before any escalation.

title: Cautious optimism on growth prospects
text: Senior economist at United Overseas Bank Julia Goh observed that the upgrade for Malaysia’s growth still falls within Bank Negara Malaysia’s (BNM) wider forecast range of 4% to 5%.

title: Cautious optimism on growth prospects
text:
Citing the IMF that artificial intelligence (AI)-related growth could contribute 0.1 percentage points to global GDP expansion, she told StarBiz: “Given Malaysia’s exposure to AI through its established position in the global value chain for semiconductors, as well as growing investments in digital services and data centres, we would connect Malaysia’s upgrade to that part of the story.”

Another important factor, she said, is that Malaysia is relatively more cushioned from the effects of the Middle East conflict as a net energy exporter, while targeted subsidies as well as fiscal assistance is in place to support the domestic economy.

title: Cautious optimism on growth prospects
text: Crucially however, Goh believes that despite there being hopes of an extended ceasefire and a climb down of risks at this stage of the conflict, the damage to oil and gas flows and energy sufficiency in Asia is significant, coupled with the spike in energy costs and global supply chain disruptions.

title: Cautious optimism on growth prospects
text: Hence, while the outlook for Malaysia still looks neutral to positive, the growth risks are tilted more to the downside,” she cautioned.

title: Cautious optimism on growth prospects
text: Economics professor at Sunway University Dr Yeah Kim Leng opined that Malaysia’s strong role in global semiconductor and related exports, which are relatively less affected by energy shocks, could be a contributor to its IMF growth forecast upgrade.

title: Cautious optimism on growth prospects
text: In addition, he said a stable labour market, along with low unemployment and a healthy financial system also provide an additional layer of support for the economy to cushion the fall in external demand, particularly if the Chinese and European economies were to weaken further amid the global slowdown.

title: Cautious optimism on growth prospects
text:
That said, dark clouds continue to loom over the horizon, particularly since there has not been a solid end to the war in Iran, with Yeah telling StarBiz: “If a prolonged conflict pushes oil prices sustainably above US$100 to US$110 per barrel, BNM would be confronted with a classic yet difficult dilemma on how to support the domestic economy slowing under cost shocks without letting imported inflation becoming entrenched.”

In such a supply-driven scenario, he explained that aggressive interest rate hikes would risk deepening the output contraction, while doing little to address the root cause which is energy shortages and higher import bills.

title: Cautious optimism on growth prospects
text: Yeah warned that this raises the spectre of stagflation, where growth stagnates even as prices rise, and a key challenge is to distinguish temporary and persistent shocks.

title: Cautious optimism on growth prospects
text: “If the oil price spike is expected to be short-lived, fiscal tools such as targeted subsidies, temporary import duty reductions, soft loans, and credit guarantees for affected businesses would be more effective than monetary tightening.

title: Cautious optimism on growth prospects
text: “However, if second-round effects emerge such as broad-based wage or price increases that could undo inflation expectations, then a modest and pre-emptive rate hike, even at some cost to near-term growth, may be necessary to signal credibility and prevent a wage-price spiral,” he elaborated.

title: Cautious optimism on growth prospects
text: He added that a sharper global slowdown would hit Malaysia hardest through the trade channel, given that exports make up nearly 70% of the country’s GDP.

title: Cautious optimism on growth prospects
text: Yeah pointed out that electrical and electronics (E&E) products, particularly semiconductors, are most exposed to falling demand from key partners like China and the United States, before also pointing out that energy prices are another major channel.

title: Cautious optimism on growth prospects
text: “While high oil prices benefit Malaysia, it could lead to a global downturn that in turn will drive crude prices lower, squeezing government revenue.

title: Cautious optimism on growth prospects
text: “Financial conditions, through portfolio outflows and ringgit weakness, could add further downward pressure on growth.

title: Cautious optimism on growth prospects
text: Tourism could play a positive role during the current Visit Malaysia Year 2026 but only when flight disruptions and high ticket prices caused by the ongoing Middle East crisis ease,” he said.

title: Cautious optimism on growth prospects
text: Yeah said the most vulnerable sectors are export-oriented manufacturing, including electronics, petrochemicals, and rubber gloves.

title: Cautious optimism on growth prospects
text: Wholesale, transport, and logistics would suffer indirectly from lower activity.

title: Cautious optimism on growth prospects
text: In contrast, he is confident that domestically focused services such as telecommunications, utilities, healthcare, and education are best positioned to withstand shocks from the Middle East conflict, as they rely less on foreign demand.

title: Cautious optimism on growth prospects
text: In line with the prudent mood, economist and investment strategist at IPP Global Wealth Mohd Sedek Jantan remarked that Malaysia’s growth outlook remains sustainable “only conditionally”, and within a narrower margin of outperformance, given the economy’s continued exposure to global trade.

title: Cautious optimism on growth prospects
text: He said this is particularly relevant as China and the United States together account for roughly 40% of global GDP, meaning any synchronised slowdown across these two economies would inevitably transmit through global trade and manufacturing cycles.

title: Cautious optimism on growth prospects
text: Having said that, he noted that the key shift is that Malaysia is no longer operating as a pure trade proxy, but is increasingly characterised by a twin-engine growth structure, where external demand is complemented by a more potent and better-transmitted domestic cycle.

title: Cautious optimism on growth prospects
text: “This reflects a re-weighting rather than a decoupling of Malaysia’s growth function.

title: Cautious optimism on growth prospects
text: In our analysis, where GDP was revised to 4.6% (from 4.5% previously), the upgrade was driven primarily by stronger domestic transmission efficiency, not by any improvement in global trade conditions,” he explained.

title: Cautious optimism on growth prospects
text: Consumption, public spending and investment are now moving more synchronously, while the investment cycle is generating a pipeline effect that sustains growth independently of contemporaneous export demand.

title: Cautious optimism on growth prospects
text: However, this does not eliminate external constraints, as Mohd Sedek said Malaysia’s growth remains sensitive to global conditions, and a sharper slowdown would still pull expansion towards the 4% range.

title: Cautious optimism on growth prospects
text: If global growth weakens sharply, he believes Malaysia will feel it first and most clearly through trade, followed by financial conditions, with energy prices and tourism playing secondary roles.

title: Cautious optimism on growth prospects
text: Echoing Yeah’s view, he said Malaysia’s trade channel remains the dominant transmission mechanism, particularly through E&E and intermediate goods linked to China and advanced economies.

title: Cautious optimism on growth prospects
text: A slowdown there feeds directly into export volumes and industrial production.

title: Cautious optimism on growth prospects
text: “But what often matters just as much is the financial channel, where tighter global liquidity, currency pressure and capital flow volatility amplify the shock by raising the cost of capital and weighing on investment sentiment.

title: Cautious optimism on growth prospects
text: “Trade hits first, but financial conditions determine how deep the slowdown becomes,” said Mohd Sedek.

title: RM130mil to cushion impact of Mid-East conflict
text: RM130mil to cushion impact of Mid-East conflict

title: RM130mil to cushion impact of Mid-East conflict
text:
Selangor allocation buffers vulnerable groups against inflation

SELANGOR has pledged RM130.42mil in financial aid to buffer vulnerable groups against the economic fallout from the Middle East conflict.

title: RM130mil to cushion impact of Mid-East conflict
text: Mentri Besar Datuk Seri Amirudin Shari said the Selangor Resilience Strengthening Package Phase 1 initiative would be implemented as early as May or June.

title: RM130mil to cushion impact of Mid-East conflict
text: The funds were allocated following the state budget review and several savings measures, including the cancellation of Hari Raya Aidilfitri open houses.

title: RM130mil to cushion impact of Mid-East conflict
text: “RM5mil will be allocated for ‘Jelajah Ehsan Rahmah’ (JER) through Selangor Agricultural Development Corporation (PKPS), which will take place once a month in each state constituency,” said Amirudin during a press conference at Wisma Dewan Negeri Selangor, Shah Alam.

title: RM130mil to cushion impact of Mid-East conflict
text: “This will ease the cost of living for low-income groups, and is expected to benefit up to 168,000 households.

title: RM130mil to cushion impact of Mid-East conflict
text:
“This effort is in addition to the existing allocation of RM5mil, which brings the total for JER 2026 to RM10mil.”

JER is a state initiative by PKPS that sells basic goods such as chicken, eggs, rice and cooking oil at prices 30% lower than the market rates.

title: RM130mil to cushion impact of Mid-East conflict
text: To increase food supply stocks, Amirudin said the state would allocate RM50mil for the Selangor Food Warehouse (GMS).

title: RM130mil to cushion impact of Mid-East conflict
text: Amirudin: Selangor Resilience Strengthening Package Phase 1 initiative will be implemented as early as May or June.

title: RM130mil to cushion impact of Mid-East conflict
text: “In the event of any shortage of food imports, Selangor can activate the GMS, which has stocks such as rice and protein, so that the price of raw goods is not increased too drastically,” he said.

title: RM130mil to cushion impact of Mid-East conflict
text: “We will also introduce ‘Kita Selangor’ voucher worth RM100 per month for a six-month period to 50,000 Selangor households.

title: RM130mil to cushion impact of Mid-East conflict
text:
“This is to manage the effects of inflation and to ensure no one lives in a state of extreme poverty if they lose their jobs or sources of income this year.”

Amirudin said RM30mil was allocated for the voucher initiative.

title: RM130mil to cushion impact of Mid-East conflict
text: “The state, through all 12 local authorities (PBTs), will also help school students by providing free breakfast in all 873 schools during this period.

title: RM130mil to cushion impact of Mid-East conflict
text: “Every day, one school in each PBT will receive this assistance starting in August,” he said.

title: RM130mil to cushion impact of Mid-East conflict
text: He added that each PBT would be given RM1mil and be tasked with ensuring nutritious meals of high quality.

title: RM130mil to cushion impact of Mid-East conflict
text: To support farmers, fishermen and similar agricultural smallholders affected by the high price of diesel, Amirudin said RM25mil would be allocated to cover the cost of fuel or agricultural inputs.

title: RM130mil to cushion impact of Mid-East conflict
text: “For example, 18,000 rice farmers will be given RM500 for each hectare of land cultivated for one crop season; 2,000 fishermen will be given RM300 assistance per month for a six-month period.

title: RM130mil to cushion impact of Mid-East conflict
text: “Similarly, for farmers, breeders and aquaculture entrepreneurs, RM450 will be distributed every month for a six-month period,” he said.

title: RM130mil to cushion impact of Mid-East conflict
text: To encourage the use of public transport, Amirudin said a monthly allocation of RM30 would be given to 50,000 Selangor residents for a six-month period to reduce the cost of riding a demand-responsive transit (DRT) van, bus or train.

title: RM130mil to cushion impact of Mid-East conflict
text: “RM5.4mil will be allocated for this, marking an increase from the RM5mil allocated under the state budget.

title: RM130mil to cushion impact of Mid-East conflict
text: “This brings the total allocation for public transport to RM10.4mil,” said the Sungai Tua assemblyman.

title: RM130mil to cushion impact of Mid-East conflict
text: For small-and-medium enterprises, Amirudin said RM6mil would be allocated, through the state cooperative Kohijrah, to cover the costs of operations and goods for cooperatives in the retail and logistics sectors.

title: RM130mil to cushion impact of Mid-East conflict
text: “Platform Selangor will also be expanded starting next May so that more local traders can benefit from the ecosystem.”

title: MIER: US-Iran conflict to impact business performance over the next two to three months
text: MIER: US-Iran conflict to impact business performance over the next two to three months

title: MIER: US-Iran conflict to impact business performance over the next two to three months
text: PETALING JAYA: The Malaysian Institute of Economic Research (MIER) expects the impact from the US-Iran conflict to affect business performance over the next two to three months, particularly among small and medium enterprises (SMEs).

title: MIER: US-Iran conflict to impact business performance over the next two to three months
text: In a statement, MIER said a key expectation is that logistics costs and input prices will “inevitably rise”, which will in turn be “quickly reflected in the cost of living”.

title: MIER: US-Iran conflict to impact business performance over the next two to three months
text: MIER stated sectors such as agriculture, printing, food services and small-scale enterprises are likely to be particularly affected, with some businesses already beginning to feel the impact.

title: MIER: US-Iran conflict to impact business performance over the next two to three months
text: “Implications were also highlighted in relation to food security, including concerns over fertiliser shortages, rising diesel prices for agricultural machinery, and recent drought conditions in Kedah.

title: MIER: US-Iran conflict to impact business performance over the next two to three months
text: These factors may contribute to disruptions in the food supply chain and affect national food self-sufficiency,” it said.

title: MIER: US-Iran conflict to impact business performance over the next two to three months
text: The conflict, MIER said, has disrupted a key trade route for Russian fertiliser exports, while China has initiated a fertiliser export ban, raising concerns over the outlook for fertiliser costs.

title: MIER: US-Iran conflict to impact business performance over the next two to three months
text: Notably, Russia and China are the two largest exporters of fertiliser to Malaysia, together accounting for more than 45% of imports in 2024.

title: MIER: US-Iran conflict to impact business performance over the next two to three months
text: “In this context, there is a need to strengthen domestic food production, particularly in light of declining yields, prolonged dry weather conditions, and constrained fertiliser supply,” MIER said.

title: MIER: US-Iran conflict to impact business performance over the next two to three months
text: In its consultations with industry and businesses, MIER said participants generally agreed that prolonged conflict could increase the likelihood of insolvencies, bankruptcies, and business failures across vulnerable sectors.

title: MIER: US-Iran conflict to impact business performance over the next two to three months
text: It was also suggested that the government adopt a more pre-emptive approach to managing these risks should economic conditions deteriorate further.

title: MIER: US-Iran conflict to impact business performance over the next two to three months
text: With regard to financing, it was noted that microfinancing schemes and assistance for SMEs are available.

title: MIER: US-Iran conflict to impact business performance over the next two to three months
text: MIER noted the banking system has also introduced targeted repayment assistance programmes to support viable businesses facing temporary financial difficulties.

title: MIER: US-Iran conflict to impact business performance over the next two to three months
text: “Furthermore, enabling agencies and the financial sector indicated that they are actively monitoring signs of portfolio distress, drawing on experience from past crises, including the Covid-19 pandemic,” it said.

title: MIER: US-Iran conflict to impact business performance over the next two to three months
text: Nonetheless, MIER said access to information and support programmes remains “fragmented and limited”.

title: MIER: US-Iran conflict to impact business performance over the next two to three months
text: Looking at past crisis-related funding interventions, MIER said significant allocations have not always effectively reached certain categories of SMEs.

title: MIER: US-Iran conflict to impact business performance over the next two to three months
text: “It was therefore suggested that relevant information could be better disseminated through business bodies and associations to improve accessibility for affected enterprises,” MIER said.

title: Thai Songkran named April’s No 1 global destination for 2026
text: Thai Songkran named April’s No 1 global destination for 2026

title: Thai Songkran named April’s No 1 global destination for 2026
text: BANGKOK: Thailand’s Songkran festival has been named the world’s No 1 destination for April 2026, in another boost for the country’s tourism and cultural appeal as officials expect at least 6.5 million people to take part in celebrations nationwide and generate no less than 30 billion baht (US$940 million) in economic activity.

title: Thai Songkran named April’s No 1 global destination for 2026
text: The ranking was cited by Culture Minister Sabida Thaised on April 15, who said Thailand had been placed first for April 2026 by UK-based online travel outlet Big 7 Travel.

title: Thai Songkran named April’s No 1 global destination for 2026
text: Sabida said the strong international response reflected the enduring power of Thai Songkran as a distinctive cultural treasure, one capable of offering visitors from around the world an experience built on joy, warmth and friendship.

title: Thai Songkran named April’s No 1 global destination for 2026
text: She said Songkran is not simply a festival of fun, but a space where human connections are renewed, a time for family and relatives to come together, and for even strangers to share smiles, happiness and memorable moments.

title: Thai Songkran named April’s No 1 global destination for 2026
text: That, she said, is the charm of Thai culture that people across the world can genuinely feel.

title: Thai Songkran named April’s No 1 global destination for 2026
text: The minister added that this year’s success was not the work of any one agency alone, but the result of people across Thailand helping to welcome international visitors as good hosts.

title: Thai Songkran named April’s No 1 global destination for 2026
text: She thanked the public for showing kindness, generosity and the warmth associated with the Thai smile, as well as for taking part in the “Proudly Wearing Thai Across the Land” campaign, which she said helped reflect the nation’s identity and pride in its culture.

title: Thai Songkran named April’s No 1 global destination for 2026
text: Although the main Songkran holiday period has already passed, celebrations are continuing in several provinces, including Chon Buri, Samut Prakan, Uthai Thani and Nan, where both traditional and creative activities are still being held for visitors.

title: Thai Songkran named April’s No 1 global destination for 2026
text: Sabida invited both Thais and international tourists to continue travelling and experience the many different local expressions of Songkran still taking place around the country.

title: Thai Songkran named April’s No 1 global destination for 2026
text: She said the Ministry of Culture would continue working to promote and elevate Thailand’s cultural capital as a key force in driving the economy, society and the country’s image on the global stage in a sustainable way.

title: Where experience meets academic excellence
text: Where experience meets academic excellence

title: Where experience meets academic excellence
text: AS the global economy increasingly demands upskilling and mental agility, the definition of a “classroom” has evolved.

title: Where experience meets academic excellence
text: Institutions like Open University Malaysia (OUM) reflect this shift, offering flexible learning models that allow working adults to pursue higher education without stepping away from their careers.

title: Where experience meets academic excellence
text: For more than two decades, the university has worked to dismantle the barriers between professional responsibilities and academic aspirations.

title: Where experience meets academic excellence
text: Next month, this journey reaches a significant milestone: more than 7,000 graduates will gather at the World Trade Centre Kuala Lumpur (WTCKL) for OUM’s 30th Convocation.

title: Where experience meets academic excellence
text: Spanning four days from May 9 to 12, the event is more than a ceremony; it is proof of the university’s role as a flexible learning hub and a launchpad for diverse Malaysian ambitions.

title: Where experience meets academic excellence
text:
Bridging industry and insight

Among the thousands of graduates this year is Kubrina Dass, a seasoned professional in the high-stakes world of sports marketing.

title: Where experience meets academic excellence
text: Since 2009, Dass has worked behind the scenes in the management of some of Malaysia’s most iconic sporting figures, including Datuk Seri Lee Chong Wei and Datuk Nicol David.

title: Where experience meets academic excellence
text: Her role involves negotiating complex sponsorship deals and aligning brand identities with the grit and grace of national athletes.

title: Where experience meets academic excellence
text: Despite her success, Dass, 39, sought to deepen her understanding of human behaviour to better navigate her career.

title: Where experience meets academic excellence
text: Dass chose OUM after realising traditional full-time programmes couldn’t accommodate her demanding schedule.

title: Where experience meets academic excellence
text: She chose to pursue a Master of Psychology at OUM, a decision driven by the need for academic rigour that could coexist with a demanding work schedule.

title: Where experience meets academic excellence
text: “I negotiate deals with brands and convince them why a national athlete will bring meaning and value to their campaigns,” Dass explains.

title: Where experience meets academic excellence
text:
“My studies taught me how to navigate through many characters, how to keep my head up, and the importance of remaining humble.”

The turning point for Dass was OUM’s accessibility.

title: Where experience meets academic excellence
text: Having been accepted into a conventional full-time master’s programme elsewhere, she realised that physical attendance requirements were incompatible with her career.

title: Where experience meets academic excellence
text: “I needed a university that was recognised, approved by the Higher Education Ministry, and built for working adults,” she recalls.

title: Where experience meets academic excellence
text: “With OUM, I could take my classes anywhere.

title: Where experience meets academic excellence
text: Even in the middle of a Kuala Lumpur traffic jam, I could log in through my phone.

title: Where experience meets academic excellence
text:
As all the lectures are recorded, I could study anywhere anytime.”

The ageless pursuit of knowledge

While Dass represents the mid-career professional looking for an edge, Dr Abdul Malik Yusoff, 70, proves that the thirst for knowledge has no expiration date.

title: Where experience meets academic excellence
text: A veteran in the medical field, Dr Malik recently completed his Master of Occupational Safety and Health Risk Management.

title: Where experience meets academic excellence
text: With a long career in occupational health and clinical practice, Dr Malik has been a clinic director since 1994 and since last year, also serves as a board member for a marketing firm.

title: Where experience meets academic excellence
text: Dr Malik proves that education has no age limit, completing his master’s decades into an already established career.

title: Where experience meets academic excellence
text: For Dr Malik, OUM’s Open, Distance and Digital Education (ODDE) model was the only logical choice.

title: Where experience meets academic excellence
text: “I chose OUM because it champions lifelong learning,” he says.

title: Where experience meets academic excellence
text:
“The model allowed me to study without putting my ongoing professional contributions on hold.”

He notes that the OUM environment is uniquely tailored to mature students.

title: Where experience meets academic excellence
text: “There was a genuine sense of camaraderie in our forums.

title: Where experience meets academic excellence
text: We weren't just students; we were professionals sharing lived experiences.

title: Where experience meets academic excellence
text: “At OUM, past experience isn’t just a memory, it becomes a powerful academic asset,” he adds.

title: Where experience meets academic excellence
text:
A legacy of quality and accessibility

Dass and Dr Malik’s experiences reflect a broader body of approximately 140,000 graduates whom OUM has nurtured since its inception in 2000.

title: Where experience meets academic excellence
text: Established by a consortium of Malaysia’s first 11 public universities, OUM has stayed true to its motto of "University for All."

title: Where experience meets academic excellence
text:
Today, the university operates five distinct faculties:

Education

Business and Management

Technology and Applied Sciences

Computing and Analytics

Social Sciences and Humanities

With over 60 programmes accredited by the Malaysian Qualifications Agency (MQA) and recognised by various professional bodies, the university ensures that flexible learning is supported by recognised academic standards.

title: Where experience meets academic excellence
text: While the learning delivery – assignments, lectures, and examinations – is fully online, OUM maintains a human touch through 35 Learning Centres nationwide.

title: Where experience meets academic excellence
text: These centres act as focal points for students who require face-to-face support or a dedicated space for academic consultation.

title: Where experience meets academic excellence
text:
The future of learning

As the 7,000 graduates prepare to walk across the stage at WTCKL this May, they represent the enduring spirit of a Malaysia that is constantly learning.

title: Where experience meets academic excellence
text: Whether they are young parents, corporate leaders, or retirees, these graduates prove that the traditional constraints of time and geography are no longer obstacles to self-improvement.

title: Where experience meets academic excellence
text: For those inspired by the success of the Class of 2026, the door remains open.

title: Where experience meets academic excellence
text: Whether you are looking to advance your career like Dass or formalise decades of experience like Dr Malik, OUM provides the framework to turn your ambition into reality.

title: Where experience meets academic excellence
text: To find out more about OUM programmes and how to kick-start your own learning journey, call 03-7801 2000, email admission@oum.edu.my, or visit www.oum.edu.my

title: ‘High claims payout may be due to medical inflation’
text: ‘High claims payout may be due to medical inflation’

title: ‘High claims payout may be due to medical inflation’
text: PETALING JAYA: Medical inflation could have contributed to the high medical claims payout in 2025, says Dr Saravanan Thambirajah.

title: ‘High claims payout may be due to medical inflation’
text: The Consultative Council on Private Healthcare Costs member said medical inflation is a key driver, given the rise in the cost of hospital stays, specialist consultations and diagnostic tests, adding that the increase in rates is significantly higher than general inflation.

title: ‘High claims payout may be due to medical inflation’
text: “The rising insurance payouts, particularly medical claims, which now form the largest portion, indicate a sustained increase in healthcare costs and utilisation.

title: ‘High claims payout may be due to medical inflation’
text: This is not just a normal trend; it reflects systemic pressures within the healthcare system, including rising hospital charges, more frequent treatments and possibly more complex or chronic conditions among the population,” said Saravanan, who is also the chief executive officer of the Federation of Malaysian Consumers Associations.

title: ‘High claims payout may be due to medical inflation’
text: He said the trend signals deeper structural issues.

title: ‘High claims payout may be due to medical inflation’
text: “The sharp rise in claims, especially in the private healthcare sector, points to weak cost controls and limited transparency in pricing.

title: ‘High claims payout may be due to medical inflation’
text: It raises serious concerns about the effectiveness of regulatory oversight on private healthcare charges and utilisation,” he said.

title: ‘High claims payout may be due to medical inflation’
text: He said there is an urgent need for the government to strengthen regulation, improve price transparency and ensure accountability among private healthcare providers.

title: ‘High claims payout may be due to medical inflation’
text: “Without intervention, the current trajectory is unsustainable and will continue to erode consumers’ ability to access affordable healthcare,” he said.

title: ‘High claims payout may be due to medical inflation’
text: Sunway University economics professor Dr Yeah Kim Leng said medical inflation, estimated at 11–12% annually, is a key factor.

title: ‘High claims payout may be due to medical inflation’
text: “Other probable causes, such as rising insurance penetration where more people are covered, increasing health issues related to critical illness and non-communicable diseases, as well as an ageing population, could have also contributed to the record high claims.

title: ‘High claims payout may be due to medical inflation’
text: “Post-Covid-related health problems could also be a factor for the surge in payout claims,” he said.

title: ‘High claims payout may be due to medical inflation’
text: The sharp increase, he said, could be due to a high claims ratio that causes insurance companies to make losses, thereby rendering the risk-pooling insurance programmes unsustainable.

title: ‘High claims payout may be due to medical inflation’
text: “Higher insurance premiums render such schemes unaffordable, especially to the low, middle and upper middle income households.

title: ‘High claims payout may be due to medical inflation’
text: “In turn, the healthcare burden is shifted to government hospitals and clinics, thereby necessitating higher budget allocation for the health sector,” he said.

title: ‘High claims payout may be due to medical inflation’
text: Guided by the Parliament-approved Health White Paper, critical reforms are being pursued by the Health Ministry to address the challenges ranging from financing, manpower training to resource allocation while optimising the provision of public and private healthcare services,” he added.

title: ‘High claims payout may be due to medical inflation’
text: Life Insurance Association of Malaysia’s 2025 annual report revealed that medical claims increased by 5.3% from RM8.9bil in 2024 to RM9.4bil in 2025.

title: ‘High claims payout may be due to medical inflation’
text: The payout accounted for the largest share of the claims in 2025 at 53.9%.

title: ‘High claims payout may be due to medical inflation’
text: The report revealed that the amount of claims payout for death, disability, medical, bonuses and others increased by 3.4% from RM16.8bil in 2024 to RM17.4bil in 2025.

title: ‘High claims payout may be due to medical inflation’
text: Disability benefits, which accounted for 0.9% of the total payout, recorded the largest increase of 77.4% from RM89mil in 2024 to RM157.9mil in 2025.

title: ‘High claims payout may be due to medical inflation’
text: The total payout for claims amounted to RM17.4bil last year, up by 3.4% from RM16.82bil in 2024.

title: Turning AI adoption into profitability
text: Turning AI adoption into profitability

title: Turning AI adoption into profitability
text: PETALING JAYA: Malaysia’s challenge in artificial intelligence (AI) is no longer about adoption, but turning that uptake into real economic and corporate value, says McKinsey & Co at a media roundtable held in conjunction with the release of “AI in South-East Asia: An Era of Opportunity” report.

title: Turning AI adoption into profitability
text: McKinsey & Company Malaysia managing partner Vidhya Ganesan said an estimated two million small and medium enterprises (SMEs) have adopted AI, a recent case study showed.

title: Turning AI adoption into profitability
text: However, only a small proportion of these SMEs have seen meaningful financial returns.

title: Turning AI adoption into profitability
text: She said only a tenth of these businesses report value of any kind flowing through to the bottomline.

title: Turning AI adoption into profitability
text: Vidhya said at the national level, it also does not lie in investment but how these investments translate into economic outcomes.

title: Turning AI adoption into profitability
text: “In terms of raw investments into data centres, cloud, from the hyperscalers, there’s no shortage at all,” Ganesan said.

title: Turning AI adoption into profitability
text: “But if you really look into the translation of that to how it is flowing through into value capture for gross domestic product, how many extra good jobs have we created?

title: Turning AI adoption into profitability
text:
That is really where the tension is.”

She noted that Malaysia already has one of the highest concentrations of data centres globally, particularly in Johor, underscoring the scale of infrastructure investment taking place.

title: Turning AI adoption into profitability
text: However, she stressed that the country must move beyond simply hosting such infrastructure and instead focus on capturing higher-value activities.

title: Turning AI adoption into profitability
text: “It requires us to move up the value chain beyond being rent seekers just for our land, which is effectively going beyond innovation to starting to own intellectual property,” she said.

title: Turning AI adoption into profitability
text:
“It’s effectively ensuring we don’t repeat what we did with not being able to move up the semiconductor value chain.”

On the question of what Malaysia gains from hosting large-scale data centres, Vidhya Ganesan said while there were real benefits, there were also trade-offs.

title: Turning AI adoption into profitability
text: “The disadvantages are very clear.

title: Turning AI adoption into profitability
text: The drag it creates on land, on water, energy is very clear and acknowledged,” she said.

title: Turning AI adoption into profitability
text: However, she noted that the presence of hyperscalers also brings strategic spillover benefits beyond infrastructure investment.

title: Turning AI adoption into profitability
text: “The fact that these hyperscalers have this quantum of investment in the country means the regional leadership, global leadership of some of these hyperscalers show up here every quarter,” Ganesan said.

title: Turning AI adoption into profitability
text: She said these visits create direct engagement opportunities with local enterprises and government-linked companies, often leading to deeper collaboration.

title: Turning AI adoption into profitability
text: Ganesan added that such interactions can create a “virtuous cycle” of investment, innovation and partnership, that would otherwise not materialise.

title: Turning AI adoption into profitability
text: She also pointed to a potential “talent halo effect” from hyperscaler presence, provided Malaysia can fully capitalise on it.

title: Turning AI adoption into profitability
text: She added that this would require supportive policies, particularly around attracting and retaining foreign talent, alongside ongoing domestic initiatives.

title: Turning AI adoption into profitability
text: “Can we accelerate and do better?

title: Turning AI adoption into profitability
text: Absolutely yes,” she said.

title: Turning AI adoption into profitability
text: The findings mirror broader regional trends highlighted in the McKinsey report, which found that while companies have invested in AI, financial impact remains limited.

title: Turning AI adoption into profitability
text: “More than 70% of the companies are saying we are adopting AI, but we are not really seeing the transformative impact,” said McKinsey partner Vivek Lath, a principal author of the report.

title: Turning AI adoption into profitability
text: The report surveyed 330 companies using AI across six South East Asian economies – Indonesia, Malaysia, the Philippines, Singapore, Thailand, and Vietnam – panning multiple industries and company sizes.

title: Turning AI adoption into profitability
text: Across the region, the report found that most organisations allocated between 11% and 40% of their technology budgets to AI, yet returns remain modest.

title: Turning AI adoption into profitability
text: Roughly six in 10 companies reported less than a 5% improvement in earnings before interest and taxes, while nearly one in five saw no impact at all.

title: Turning AI adoption into profitability
text: This pattern mirrors global trends, where many companies have seen some efficiency gains from individual AI use cases but struggle to scale that impact and translate it into bottom-line value.

title: Turning AI adoption into profitability
text: This has created what Lath described as a paradox, where companies invests but struggle to extract value.

title: Turning AI adoption into profitability
text: A key issue remains how companies that deploy AI do not fundamentally rethink how their businesses operate.

title: Turning AI adoption into profitability
text: “If you are using AI without really re-imagining the processes, you are digitising the inefficiencies in the processes,” he said, adding that businesses need to have a “rewired” approach in which AI embeds into business-led transformation supported by changes in operating models, talent, and data capabilities.

title: Turning AI adoption into profitability
text: Talent remains a key constraint in Malaysia, particularly the shortage of professionals who can bridge business and technology.

title: Turning AI adoption into profitability
text: “The issue is much more one of what we call translators,” said Ganesan.

title: Turning AI adoption into profitability
text: She added that value creation is disproportionately driven by mid-tier companies, suggesting policy and corporate focus should prioritise scaling these firms.

title: Turning AI adoption into profitability
text: At the ecosystem level, Lath said governments have a key role in enabling broader participation through shared platforms and infrastructure.

title: Turning AI adoption into profitability
text: “Governments should create platforms where some of the small scale players who could not have done things on their own are able to capture the benefit as well,” he said.

title: ECB to warn bankers about new Anthropic model risks, source says
text: ECB to warn bankers about new Anthropic model risks, source says

title: ECB to warn bankers about new Anthropic model risks, source says
text: FRANKFURT, April 15 (Reuters) - European Central Bank ⁠supervisors are set to quiz bankers about the risks that Anthropic's new artificial intelligence model might supercharge cyberattacks, one source ⁠familiar with the situation told Reuters on Wednesday.

title: ECB to warn bankers about new Anthropic model risks, source says
text:
Anthropic's Mythos is seen by cybersecurity experts as posing significant challenges to ‌the banking industry and its legacy technology systems, raising alarm bells among regulators in Britain and the U.S.

ECB supervisors are gathering information about the model, with a view to asking banks on their watch about their preparedness for this new possible source of risk, said the source who spoke on condition of anonymity because they ​are not authorised to comment publicly on the matter.

title: ECB to warn bankers about new Anthropic model risks, source says
text: Unlike in the U.S., this effort ⁠will be carried out via the ECB's regular dialogue ⁠with bank staff and no ad-hoc meeting with top management has been scheduled yet.

title: ECB to warn bankers about new Anthropic model risks, source says
text: An ECB spokesperson declined to comment.

title: ECB to warn bankers about new Anthropic model risks, source says
text: Mythos' capabilities to code ⁠at ‌a high level have given it a potentially unprecedented ability to identify cybersecurity vulnerabilities and devise ways to exploit them, experts told Reuters.

title: ECB to warn bankers about new Anthropic model risks, source says
text: This aspect is why Anthropic has said the current iteration, Claude Mythos Preview, will not be made generally available.

title: ECB to warn bankers about new Anthropic model risks, source says
text: Instead, the ⁠company announced Project Glasswing, in which it invited major tech companies, cybersecurity vendors and ​JPMorgan Chase, along with several dozen other ‌organizations, to privately evaluate the model and prepare defences accordingly.

title: ECB to warn bankers about new Anthropic model risks, source says
text:
TRUMP BACKS AI SAFEGUARDS IN BANKING SYSTEM

U.S. Treasury Secretary Scott ⁠Bessent and Federal Reserve Chair ​Jerome Powell convened an urgent meeting with bank chief executives last week to warn them about the risks, which President Donald Trump acknowledged on Wednesday.

title: ECB to warn bankers about new Anthropic model risks, source says
text: Trump also backed government safeguards.

title: ECB to warn bankers about new Anthropic model risks, source says
text: St. Louis Fed President Alberto Musalem told Reuters the development emphasized the need for the U.S. central bank to "revisit how we're thinking ⁠about cybersecurity" and to check in with banks about their own "resilience and ​robustness to cyber risk in this new world."

title: ECB to warn bankers about new Anthropic model risks, source says
text: "Our cyber teams are engaged with the substance of it, not the news of it, but the actual substance of it.

title: ECB to warn bankers about new Anthropic model risks, source says
text: And we're evaluating," Musalem told Reuters in an interview on Wednesday.

title: ECB to warn bankers about new Anthropic model risks, source says
text: Britain's Technology Secretary Liz Kendall and Security Minister Dan Jarvis ⁠sounded a similar warning to businesses on Wednesday, saying Mythos was "substantially more capable at cyber offence" than any model previously tested by the government's AI Security Institute.

title: ECB to warn bankers about new Anthropic model risks, source says
text: A new generation of AI models is "becoming capable of doing work that previously required rare expertise: finding weaknesses in software, writing the code to exploit them, and doing so at a speed and scale that would have been impossible even a year ago," ​they said in an open letter to businesses.

title: ECB to warn bankers about new Anthropic model risks, source says
text: Bank of England Governor Andrew Bailey said this week ⁠that central banks and financial regulators must quickly understand the implications of the new model.

title: ECB to warn bankers about new Anthropic model risks, source says
text: In Canada, Mythos was discussed at a meeting last ​Friday about cybersecurity attended by representatives of the Finance Ministry and the Bank of ‌Canada as well as bank executives, a ministry spokesperson said.

title: ECB to warn bankers about new Anthropic model risks, source says
text: The ECB already ​had listed tech risk as one of its top priorities for the 2026-2028 period.

title: ECB to warn bankers about new Anthropic model risks, source says
text: (Reporting by Francesco Canepa; Additional reporting by Paul Sandle in London, Howard Schneider in Washington and Promit Mukherjee in Ottawa; Editing by Emelia Sithole-Matarise and Paul Simao)

title: Hatyai revives: Songkran breathes new life into city after floods
text: Hatyai revives: Songkran breathes new life into city after floods

title: Hatyai revives: Songkran breathes new life into city after floods
text: SONGKHLA, Thailand: Several months after devastating floods submerged business premises and disrupted livelihoods, Songkhla is showing signs of recovery as an influx of tourists during the Songkran festival breathes new life into its economy.

title: Hatyai revives: Songkran breathes new life into city after floods
text: Celebrated annually from April 13 to 15, Songkran marks the traditional Thai New Year.

title: Hatyai revives: Songkran breathes new life into city after floods
text: Rich in religious and cultural significance, the festival has also evolved into a major tourism event, drawing visitors from across the globe.

title: Hatyai revives: Songkran breathes new life into city after floods
text: In Hatyai’s city centre, what was once a subdued atmosphere has transformed into a lively scene, with thousands of local and international tourists crowding the main streets to take part in the annual water festivities.

title: Hatyai revives: Songkran breathes new life into city after floods
text: Splashes of water, music and laughter now fill the city, reflecting a stark contrast to the post-flood conditions that had previously paralysed daily activities.

title: Hatyai revives: Songkran breathes new life into city after floods
text: For small traders, the celebration presents an opportunity to rebuild their lives after enduring a challenging period marked by property damage and business disruptions.

title: Hatyai revives: Songkran breathes new life into city after floods
text: A street food vendor, Somchai Wongsawat, 45, said the earlier floods caused significant losses as equipment and supplies were damaged, with some traders taking weeks to resume operations.

title: Hatyai revives: Songkran breathes new life into city after floods
text: "During the floods, this area was deserted.

title: Hatyai revives: Songkran breathes new life into city after floods
text: Water rose into our shops, and we could not operate at all,” he said.

title: Hatyai revives: Songkran breathes new life into city after floods
text: However, he noted that the situation has improved markedly, with the influx of tourists,s particularly from Malaysia, boosting daily sales.

title: Hatyai revives: Songkran breathes new life into city after floods
text: "Business is very good now.

title: Hatyai revives: Songkran breathes new life into city after floods
text: Many tourists are coming, including from Malaysia.

title: Hatyai revives: Songkran breathes new life into city after floods
text: This is helping us recover gradually,” he added.

title: Hatyai revives: Songkran breathes new life into city after floods
text: Another trader, Naree Chaiyaporn, 38, said this year’s Songkran celebration has brought renewed hope of recouping previous losses.

title: Hatyai revives: Songkran breathes new life into city after floods
text: Although recovery remains incomplete, she said the increase in customers has strengthened confidence that the local economy will stabilise in the near future.

title: Hatyai revives: Songkran breathes new life into city after floods
text: Meanwhile, local youths view Songkran as a symbol of the city’s resurgence following the disaster.

title: Hatyai revives: Songkran breathes new life into city after floods
text: A university student, Anan Srisawat, 21, said this year’s celebration is particularly meaningful as it signals a return to normalcy after a difficult period.

title: Hatyai revives: Songkran breathes new life into city after floods
text: "After the floods, many people felt sad and stressed.

title: Hatyai revives: Songkran breathes new life into city after floods
text: But when Songkran arrives, everyone comes out, celebrates and forgets their hardships for a while,” he said.

title: Hatyai revives: Songkran breathes new life into city after floods
text: Another youth, Pimchanok Rattanawong, 19, said the festival is not only about enjoyment but also about restoring community spirit.

title: Hatyai revives: Songkran breathes new life into city after floods
text: "Songkran makes us feel that the city is alive again.

title: Hatyai revives: Songkran breathes new life into city after floods
text: With so many tourists, the atmosphere has returned to how it used to be,” he said.

title: Hatyai revives: Songkran breathes new life into city after floods
text: In addition to small traders, hotel and accommodation operators have also recorded a surge in bookings compared with the post-flood period.

title: Hatyai revives: Songkran breathes new life into city after floods
text: A local hotel operator, Chalida Kittisak, 48, said occupancy rates have risen significantly since early this week, with most rooms booked by foreign tourists and Malaysian visitors.

title: Hatyai revives: Songkran breathes new life into city after floods
text: "We are fully booked for several days throughout Songkran.

title: Hatyai revives: Songkran breathes new life into city after floods
text: This is what we have been hoping for after such a difficult period,” she said.

title: Hatyai revives: Songkran breathes new life into city after floods
text: Tuk-tuk drivers and transport service providers are benefiting from increased demand in line with the rise in tourist arrivals.

title: Hatyai revives: Songkran breathes new life into city after floods
text: At the same time, local authorities have stepped up safety measures and traffic control to ensure the celebrations proceed smoothly and safely.

title: Hatyai revives: Songkran breathes new life into city after floods
text: These measures include deploying security personnel at key locations and maintaining continuous monitoring to prevent any untoward incidents.

title: Hatyai revives: Songkran breathes new life into city after floods
text: Overall, this year’s Songkran celebration not only reflects the vibrancy of Thai culture but also symbolises Hatyai’s recovery as a tourism destination following the disaster.

title: Hatyai revives: Songkran breathes new life into city after floods
text: Earlier reports stated that prolonged heavy rainfall caused severe flooding across eight provinces in southern Thailand, affecting nearly 360,000 people in 48 districts, according to the Department of Disaster Prevention and Mitigation.

title: Hatyai revives: Songkran breathes new life into city after floods
text: The affected provinces, including Phatthalung, Satun and Songkhla, are among the key destinations frequently visited by Malaysian tourists, particularly during the year-end holiday season.

title: Hatyai revives: Songkran breathes new life into city after floods
text: Hatyai is located about 26 kilometres south-west of Songkhla City, which is the administrative and cultural capital.

title: Hatyai revives: Songkran breathes new life into city after floods
text: - Bernama

title: IMF raises Malaysia's real GDP growth forecast to 4.7% for 2026, 4.3% in 2027
text: IMF raises Malaysia's real GDP growth forecast to 4.7% for 2026, 4.3% in 2027

title: IMF raises Malaysia's real GDP growth forecast to 4.7% for 2026, 4.3% in 2027
text: KUALA LUMPUR: The International Monetary Fund (IMF) has raised its projection for Malaysia's real gross domestic product (GDP) growth to 4.7 per cent for 2026, representing an upward revision of 0.4 percentage points.

title: IMF raises Malaysia's real GDP growth forecast to 4.7% for 2026, 4.3% in 2027
text: According to its April 2026 World Economic Outlook (WEO) released today, the IMF also estimated Malaysia’s real GDP growth at 4.3 per cent in 2027.

title: IMF raises Malaysia's real GDP growth forecast to 4.7% for 2026, 4.3% in 2027
text: In 2025, Malaysia recorded a strong growth of 5.2 per cent, supported by resilient domestic demand.

title: IMF raises Malaysia's real GDP growth forecast to 4.7% for 2026, 4.3% in 2027
text: In its January 2026 WEO "Global Economy: Steady amid Divergent Forces”, the IMF previously set Malaysia's 2026 GDP at 4.3 per cent.

title: IMF raises Malaysia's real GDP growth forecast to 4.7% for 2026, 4.3% in 2027
text: Bank Negara Malaysia (BNM) estimates Malaysia’s economy to grow between four per cent and five per cent this year, with the country’s domestic resilience and diversified export structure continuing to provide buffer to navigate the current external headwinds mainly from the conflict in West Asia.

title: IMF raises Malaysia's real GDP growth forecast to 4.7% for 2026, 4.3% in 2027
text: The IMF projects global growth to be 3.1 per cent in 2026 and 3.2 per cent in 2027, slower than its recent pace of about 3.4 per cent in 2024-2025, and to settle at about that rate in the medium term, slower than its historical (2000-2019) average of 3.7 per cent.

title: IMF raises Malaysia's real GDP growth forecast to 4.7% for 2026, 4.3% in 2027
text: The forecast for 2026 is revised downward by 0.2 percentage point and that for 2027 is unchanged.

title: IMF raises Malaysia's real GDP growth forecast to 4.7% for 2026, 4.3% in 2027
text: It said the relatively modest downward revision to global growth in the reference forecast relative to the January 2026 WEO Update owing to continued tailwinds partially offsetting the negative shocks from the conflict, including lower tariffs, pre-existing policy support, and carryover from stronger-than-expected outturns at the end of 2025 and the first quarter of 2026 in some cases.

title: IMF raises Malaysia's real GDP growth forecast to 4.7% for 2026, 4.3% in 2027
text: "This masks significant variation across countries, with lower-income commodity-importing economies being hit particularly hard through higher energy and food prices as well as foreign exchange depreciation,” it said.

title: IMF raises Malaysia's real GDP growth forecast to 4.7% for 2026, 4.3% in 2027
text: IMF said growth in advanced economies is projected to be 1.8 per cent in 2026 and 1.7 per cent in 2027.
