title: Wall St rallies on US-Iran talk hopes, earnings lift
text: Wall St rallies on US-Iran talk hopes, earnings lift

title: Wall St rallies on US-Iran talk hopes, earnings lift
text: NEW YORK: The Nasdaq climbed 2% while the S&P 500 finished up 1% and near its record closing high on increasing optimism about the prospects for a Middle East resolution while investors also assessed the latest batch of bank earnings and US inflation readings.

title: Wall St rallies on US-Iran talk hopes, earnings lift
text: Talks to end the Iran war could resume in Pakistan over the next two days, US President Donald Trump told the New York Post on Tuesday, after the collapse of weekend negotiations prompted Washington to impose a blockade on Iranian ports.

title: Wall St rallies on US-Iran talk hopes, earnings lift
text: Meanwhile, the US State Department said Israel and Lebanon had agreed to launch direct negotiations at a mutually agreed-upon time and place after a US-hosted meeting in Washington on Tuesday, although it was not immediately clear if they agreed to a framework for peace.

title: Wall St rallies on US-Iran talk hopes, earnings lift
text: With volatile oil prices dramatically impacting inflation expectations, the market has been highly sensitive to developments in the Middle East, with any headlines about setbacks sending stocks lower, while even tentative signs of an off-ramp have been sufficient to encourage investors eager for positive news.

title: Wall St rallies on US-Iran talk hopes, earnings lift
text: "We don't have a resolution yet but investors don't want to miss the rebound," said Burns McKinney, portfolio manager at NFJ Investment Group, Dallas.

title: Wall St rallies on US-Iran talk hopes, earnings lift
text: Meanwhile, Tuesday's inflation data provided some encouragement as US producer prices increased less than expected in March as the cost of services was unchanged.

title: Wall St rallies on US-Iran talk hopes, earnings lift
text: Ameriprise chief market strategist Anthony Saglimbene also cited a solid start to the US earnings season as a boost for stocks.

title: Wall St rallies on US-Iran talk hopes, earnings lift
text: "The market is kind of moving past this concept of peak uncertainty.

title: Wall St rallies on US-Iran talk hopes, earnings lift
text: There's been a lot of uncertainty in the market, whether that's coming from the Iran conflict, AI disruption fears, inflation concerns or Federal Reserve policy concerns," Saglimbene said.

title: Wall St rallies on US-Iran talk hopes, earnings lift
text: "Markets are starting to kind of walk away from some of the worst-case scenarios for these events and because valuations have improved over the last couple of weeks and months, investors are buying the dip right now."

title: Wall St rallies on US-Iran talk hopes, earnings lift
text: The S&P 500 gained 81.14 points, or 1.18%, to finish at 6,967.38 compared with its record close of 6978.60 on January 27.

title: Wall St rallies on US-Iran talk hopes, earnings lift
text: On Monday, the benchmark index had closed above its finish on February 27 – the last trading day before the US and Israel started their war against Iran.

title: Wall St rallies on US-Iran talk hopes, earnings lift

title: Wall St rallies on US-Iran talk hopes, earnings lift
text: The Dow Jones Industrial Average rose 317.74 points, or 0.66%, to 48,535.99, marking its highest close since early March.

title: Wall St rallies on US-Iran talk hopes, earnings lift

title: Wall St rallies on US-Iran talk hopes, earnings lift
text: On the earnings front, BlackRock shares rallied 3% after the asset manager reported a rise in first-quarter profit, helped by strong inflows into its exchange-traded funds and a sharp increase in performance fees.

text: Citigroup shares closed up 2.6% after hitting their highest level since late 2008 following earnings that beat first-quarter profit estimates.

title: Wall St rallies on US-Iran talk hopes, earnings lift
text: However, JPMorgan had a less enthusiastic reception to its first-quarter results, while Wells Fargo shares fell after interest income fell short of market expectations.

title: Wall St rallies on US-Iran talk hopes, earnings lift

title: Wall St rallies on US-Iran talk hopes, earnings lift

title: Wall Street’s earnings fantasies face harsh reality check
text: Wall Street’s earnings fantasies face harsh reality check

title: Wall Street’s earnings fantasies face harsh reality check
text: SOMETHING doesn’t quite add up.

title: Wall Street’s earnings fantasies face harsh reality check
text: US oil prices have risen over 70% in the last year, inflation remains above 3%, geopolitical risk is off the charts.

title: Wall Street’s earnings fantasies face harsh reality check
text: But Wall Street’s earnings outlook could not be rosier.

title: Wall Street’s earnings fantasies face harsh reality check
text: As the first-quarter (1Q) reporting season kicks into gear this week, the Nasdaq is higher than it was before the Iran war started on Feb 28, and the broader S&P 500 isn’t far behind.

title: Wall Street’s earnings fantasies face harsh reality check
text: It’s almost as if the last six weeks didn’t happen.

title: Wall Street’s earnings fantasies face harsh reality check
text: But even if the shaky ceasefire in the Middle East turns into a permanent cessation in hostilities – a big “if” – the economic damage and price impact will still last for months, possibly years.

title: Wall Street’s earnings fantasies face harsh reality check
text: The magnitude of these shifts, their duration, and ultimate impact on the US economy are all up for debate.

title: Wall Street’s earnings fantasies face harsh reality check
text: But their existence isn’t.

title: Wall Street’s earnings fantasies face harsh reality check
text:
Bank of America economists summed it up neatly: “Even if the ceasefire persists, we are unlikely to move back to the pre-war scenario.”

Yet that’s exactly where Wall Street seems to think we’re heading.

title: Wall Street’s earnings fantasies face harsh reality check
text:
High hopes

In some ways, the stock market’s nonchalance shouldn’t come as a surprise, as Wall Street has proven surprisingly bulletproof amid the ructions of US President Donald Trump’s second term.

title: Wall Street’s earnings fantasies face harsh reality check
text: The recovery from his “Liberation Day” tariff turmoil a year ago was stunning.

title: Wall Street’s earnings fantasies face harsh reality check
text: It underscored how resilient Wall Street has become to shocks and how confident investors are in the money-spinning powers of artificial intelligence (AI).

title: Wall Street’s earnings fantasies face harsh reality check
text: In that light, the LSEG consensus forecast for 13.9% year-on-year growth in 1Q earnings seems reasonable.

title: Wall Street’s earnings fantasies face harsh reality check
text: That would be in line with the previous four quarters and may help justify the Nasdaq and S&P 500’s return to pre-war levels.

title: Wall Street’s earnings fantasies face harsh reality check
text: The outlook for the rest of this year, however, is more puzzling.

title: Wall Street’s earnings fantasies face harsh reality check
text: Consensus forecasts for earnings growth in the remaining three quarters are 20%, 22%, and 19.9%, respectively.

title: Wall Street’s earnings fantasies face harsh reality check
text: That would represent the strongest growth in US corporate earnings since 2018, excluding the pandemic-distorted year of 2021, according to Tajinder Dhillon, head of earnings research at LSEG.

title: Wall Street’s earnings fantasies face harsh reality check
text: But if corporate America is to rack up profits at such a breakneck speed, it will have to overcome a growing number of obstacles.

title: Wall Street’s earnings fantasies face harsh reality check
text:
Economic clouds darken

The whiff of stagflation is in the air.

title: Wall Street’s earnings fantasies face harsh reality check
text: The consumer price index report last Friday showed that petrol prices in March spiked 21% on the month, while other motor fuels, including diesel, soared more than 30%.

title: Wall Street’s earnings fantasies face harsh reality check
text: Both were record rises.

title: Wall Street’s earnings fantasies face harsh reality check
text: Meanwhile, consumers have never been more glum.

title: Wall Street’s earnings fantasies face harsh reality check
text: The University of Michigan’s survey also released last Friday showed that consumer sentiment had slumped to its lowest on record.

title: Wall Street’s earnings fantasies face harsh reality check
text: It’s easy to see why.

title: Wall Street’s earnings fantasies face harsh reality check
text: Growth this year will likely be weaker than it would have been absent the war.

title: Wall Street’s earnings fantasies face harsh reality check
text: Bank of America economists lowered their 2026 US gross domestic product growth forecast by 50 basis points (bps) to 2.3% – a significant revision.

title: Wall Street’s earnings fantasies face harsh reality check
text: They also raised their core personal consumption expenditures inflation outlook by 70 bps to 3.1%.

title: Wall Street’s earnings fantasies face harsh reality check
text: And that may be optimistic: with oil 70% more expensive than it was a year ago, headline inflation may creep up toward 4%, moving even farther from the US Federal Reserve’s (Fed) 2% target.

title: Wall Street’s earnings fantasies face harsh reality check
text: This has altered the expected path of US interest rates in the coming months.

title: Wall Street’s earnings fantasies face harsh reality check
text: At the start of the year, futures markets implied 60 bps of easing by December.

title: Wall Street’s earnings fantasies face harsh reality check
text: Those expectations have now evaporated, and the Fed’s next move appears as likely to be a hike as a cut – something unthinkable only six weeks ago.

title: Wall Street’s earnings fantasies face harsh reality check
text: Of course, none of this is set in stone.

title: Wall Street’s earnings fantasies face harsh reality check
text: The economy is much less oil-intensive than it used to be, consumer sentiment increasingly bears little relation to actual spending patterns, and companies could prove resilient if these shocks are short-lived.

title: Wall Street’s earnings fantasies face harsh reality check
text: Still, it would be remarkable if none of this dented profits.

title: Wall Street’s earnings fantasies face harsh reality check
text: For starters, much of this earnings boom is expected to be powered by the tech industry.

title: Wall Street’s earnings fantasies face harsh reality check
text: The all-powerful sector’s earnings are expected to rise 46% in the 1Q, meaning tech would account for nearly 80% of the expected US$74bil total profit growth.

title: Wall Street’s earnings fantasies face harsh reality check
text: But “Big Tech”, with its US$800bil capital expenditure plans on energy-intensive data centres and other AI infrastructure this year – much of it debt-financed – is now particularly sensitive to both soaring energy prices and the cost of money.

title: Wall Street’s earnings fantasies face harsh reality check
text: Equity investors are optimistic by nature, but the positivity permeating Wall Street is striking.

title: Wall Street’s earnings fantasies face harsh reality check
text: The question now is whether corporate guidance in the coming weeks deflates those high hopes.

title: Wall Street’s earnings fantasies face harsh reality check
text:
— Reuters

Jamie McGeever is a columnist for Reuters.

title: Wall Street’s earnings fantasies face harsh reality check
text: The views expressed here are the writer’s own.

title: IMF cuts China 2026 economic growth forecast to 4.4%
text: BEIJING (AFP): The IMF on Tuesday lowered its forecast for Chinese economic growth this year to 4.4 per cent, despite reduced US tariffs and stimulus measures aimed at mitigating the impact of the Middle East conflict.

title: IMF cuts China 2026 economic growth forecast to 4.4%
text: The new projection, published in the International Monetary Fund's latest World Economic Outlook report, is slightly lower than its January forecast of 4.5 per cent and well off last year's official five per cent expansion.

title: IMF cuts China 2026 economic growth forecast to 4.4%
text: The Washington-based organisation said "lower US effective tariff rates on Chinese goods, and stimulus measures offset the negative impact of the shock induced by the Middle East conflict" on the world's second-largest economy.

title: IMF cuts China 2026 economic growth forecast to 4.4%
text: Exports, the engine of China's growth, remained robust, it said.

title: IMF cuts China 2026 economic growth forecast to 4.4%
text: But the country's overall economic outlook remains bleak, with growth expected to slow to 4.0 per cent in 2027 owing to "structural headwinds", the Fund said.

title: IMF cuts China 2026 economic growth forecast to 4.4%
text: It cited "a grinding slowdown in the housing sector, a declining labour force, decreasing returns on investment, and slower productivity growth".

title: IMF cuts China 2026 economic growth forecast to 4.4%
text: The IMF also cut its 2026 growth forecast for emerging and developing Asia to 4.9 per cent from 5.0 per cent -- a significant slowdown from the region's 5.5 per cent expansion recorded last year.

title: IMF cuts China 2026 economic growth forecast to 4.4%
text: "In several South and Southeast Asian economies, disruptions in the Middle East are expected to reduce tourism and remittance inflows, thereby weakening domestic demand," the Fund said.

title: IMF cuts China 2026 economic growth forecast to 4.4%
text: Among those being hit was the Philippines, with the IMF cutting its outlook for growth by 1.5 percentage points as war shocks compound the negative effects of a weaker-than-expected 2025 result.

title: IMF cuts China 2026 economic growth forecast to 4.4%
text: But India's economy is now tipped to grow 6.5 per cent this year, a rise of 0.1 percentage point from the previous forecast, with the Fund saying the reduction in US tariffs -- from 50 per cent to 10 per cent -- will soften the impact of the Middle East conflict.

title: IMF cuts China 2026 economic growth forecast to 4.4%
text: Reflecting the worldwide disruption of energy supplies caused by the war in the Middle East, global economic growth for 2026 was revised downwards by 0.2 points to 3.1 per cent.

title: Singapore leads Asia in tightening policy on oil-price shock
text: Singapore leads Asia in tightening policy on oil-price shock

title: Singapore leads Asia in tightening policy on oil-price shock
text: SINGAPORE (Bloomberg): Singapore tightened its monetary policy settings in a widely expected move, becoming the first in Asia to respond to rising inflation risks stemming from a surge in energy prices linked to the Middle East conflict.

title: Singapore leads Asia in tightening policy on oil-price shock
text: The Monetary Authority of Singapore, which uses the exchange rate as its main policy tool rather than interest rates, said Tuesday it would increase the slope of its policy band, as predicted by 15 of 18 economists in a Bloomberg survey.

title: Singapore leads Asia in tightening policy on oil-price shock
text: It left the width and the level at which the band is centered unchanged.

title: Singapore leads Asia in tightening policy on oil-price shock
text: "Singapore’s imported energy costs have already risen,” the central bank said in a statement, noting that oil prices will likely remain elevated for some time even if supplies from the Middle East are restored.

title: Singapore leads Asia in tightening policy on oil-price shock
text:
"As higher energy costs pass through supply chains worldwide, a broader range of Singapore’s import costs will increase.”

The local dollar was steady at 1.2734 versus the greenback after paring slight gains following the announcement.

title: Singapore leads Asia in tightening policy on oil-price shock
text: The Singapore dollar is the top-performing currency in Southeast Asia versus the US dollar since the outbreak of the Iran War.

title: Singapore leads Asia in tightening policy on oil-price shock
text: With the latest move, Singapore has emerged as the first in Asia to tighten monetary policy in response to the Iran war, while regional peers have largely stayed on hold as they assess the economic fallout from the conflict.

title: Singapore leads Asia in tightening policy on oil-price shock
text: Last week, the Reserve Bank of India and the Bank of Korea kept interest rates unchanged as they monitor the impact of surging oil prices on the economy.

title: Singapore leads Asia in tightening policy on oil-price shock
text: Indonesia, the Philippines and Thailand - all due to decide on monetary policy in the coming weeks - have in the past few weeks signaled they would hold rates.

title: Singapore leads Asia in tightening policy on oil-price shock
text: Singapore’s central bank on Tuesday also raised its outlook for core inflation to 1.5%-2.5%, from 1%-2% previously, adding that price hikes could erode real incomes and crimp demand over the next few quarters.

title: Singapore leads Asia in tightening policy on oil-price shock
text: A separate release from the Ministry of Trade and Industry showed the city state’s gross domestic product contracted in the first three months of the year from the fourth quarter, but on year-on-year terms it still expanded a handsome 4.6%.

title: Singapore leads Asia in tightening policy on oil-price shock
text: "The MAS appears to be leaving the door open for another move in the July meeting, depending on how inflation and growth evolves,” according to Chua Hak Bin, regional co-head of macro research at Maybank.

title: Singapore leads Asia in tightening policy on oil-price shock
text:
"We think the Iran war will have a disproportionately larger impact on inflation than growth, and cannot rule out another tightening move at the July meeting.”

In its statement, MAS also said that it stood "ready to curb excessive volatility in the S$NEER.” The last time it used a similar phrase was in March 2020, according to Australia & New Zealand Banking Group’s head of Asia research Khoon Goh who expects another round of tightening in July.

title: Singapore leads Asia in tightening policy on oil-price shock
text:
"Given that they will have to intervene anyway when the S$NEER is at the upper bound, that comment suggests that they will not want to see the S$NEER weaken too much suggesting they may provide a floor should the Singapore dollar weaken due to geopolitical factors.”

According to estimates from MUFG Bank Ltd, the S$NEER - the central bank’s trade-weighted measure of the currency against a basket of its trading partners - is now sitting about 0.5%-0.6% below the top of its policy band.

title: Singapore leads Asia in tightening policy on oil-price shock
text: The MAS, which holds four policy reviews per year, had loosened its settings twice in 2025 - January and April - to help support growth, then stood pat through the rest of last year as the trade-reliant nation largely shrugged off the impact of President Donald Trump’s tariff onslaught.

title: Singapore leads Asia in tightening policy on oil-price shock
text: The Middle East conflict will likely send global inflation up to 2.7% this year, jumping from 1.8% last year, while global growth could be much slower than expected, according to the monetary authority’s macroeconomic review published Tuesday.

title: Singapore leads Asia in tightening policy on oil-price shock
text: A more severe energy shock would dim the outlook further, the report said.

title: Oil demand to plunge as Mideast uncertainty persists, IEA warns
text: SINGAPORE (AFP): Demand for crude oil will likely decline this year for the first time since the Covid pandemic slammed the global economy six years ago, weighed down by Mideast war disruptions, the IEA warned Tuesday.

title: Oil demand to plunge as Mideast uncertainty persists, IEA warns
text: Surging prices caused by the Strait of Hormuz's closure and damage to production facilities will force countries and industries to curtail oil use, and "demand destruction will spread as scarcity and higher prices persist", the International Energy Agency said in its monthly report.

title: Oil demand to plunge as Mideast uncertainty persists, IEA warns
text: It noted that its forecasts assume a "base case" of oil shipments resuming in May through Hormuz, which Tehran has effectively closed since the US and Israel began bombing Iran on February 28.

title: Oil demand to plunge as Mideast uncertainty persists, IEA warns
text: This would lead to a decline in demand of 1.5 million barrels per day (bpd) in the second quarter, "the sharpest since Covid-19 slashed fuel consumption", the agency said.

title: Oil demand to plunge as Mideast uncertainty persists, IEA warns
text: Overall demand is forecast to have contracted by 800,000 bpd in March and is seen dropping by 2.3 million bpd in April.

title: Oil demand to plunge as Mideast uncertainty persists, IEA warns
text: But a "protracted case" if the Strait of Hormuz remains closed would lead to persistently high prices that crimp demand by an even higher average of five million bpd through the rest of this year.

title: Oil demand to plunge as Mideast uncertainty persists, IEA warns
text: "In this case, energy markets and economies around the world need to brace for significant disruptions in the months to come," the agency warned.

title: Oil demand to plunge as Mideast uncertainty persists, IEA warns

title: Oil demand to plunge as Mideast uncertainty persists, IEA warns
text: Oil prices have nearly doubled since the Mideast war began and remain near $100 a barrel, with prices of refined products like petrol and jet fuel rising even higher.

title: Oil demand to plunge as Mideast uncertainty persists, IEA warns

title: Oil demand to plunge as Mideast uncertainty persists, IEA warns
text: Countries are also tapping into crude stock reserves to soften the blow from lost Gulf exports, and inventories fell by 85 million barrels overall in March.

title: Oil demand to plunge as Mideast uncertainty persists, IEA warns
text: IEA executive director Fatih Birol has repeatedly said the agency stands ready to approve the release of more reserves if needed.

title: Oil demand to plunge as Mideast uncertainty persists, IEA warns
text: But some analysts say energy traders are increasingly betting that neither Iran nor the United States want the war to continue, and are banking on talks producing a ceasefire.

title: M'sia to increase biodiesel blend mandate from B10 to B15, says Economy Minister
text: PUTRAJAYA: Malaysia is set to increase its biodiesel blend mandate from B10 to B15, starting with an initial rollout of B12, to strengthen the nation’s energy security.

title: M'sia to increase biodiesel blend mandate from B10 to B15, says Economy Minister

title: M'sia to increase biodiesel blend mandate from B10 to B15, says Economy Minister
text: Biodiesel blending mandates are policies requiring a specific percentage of renewable fuel - typically palm oil-based methyl ester (PME) — to be mixed into fossil diesel.

title: M'sia to increase biodiesel blend mandate from B10 to B15, says Economy Minister
text: The numbers in the mandates represent the percentage of palm oil in the fuel mix.

title: M'sia to increase biodiesel blend mandate from B10 to B15, says Economy Minister
text: “The ongoing B10 implementation proves that the foundation for this already exists.

title: M'sia to increase biodiesel blend mandate from B10 to B15, says Economy Minister
text: Therefore, the government has agreed to increase the biodiesel blend rate to B15, beginning with B12,” he said in a press briefing on Tuesday (April 14).

title: M'sia to increase biodiesel blend mandate from B10 to B15, says Economy Minister
text: Akmal Nasrullah’s remarks came following the National Economic Action Council (MTEN) meeting on Tuesday (April 14).

title: M'sia to increase biodiesel blend mandate from B10 to B15, says Economy Minister
text: He said the increased use of biodiesel is a strategic move to prolong Malaysia’s domestic disel supply.

text: Meanwhile, Akmal Nasrullah said the government has laid out a biofuel mitigation plan that includes the phased upgrading of depots to accommodate B20 and B30 blends under the upcoming 13th Malaysia Plan (13MP).

title: M'sia to increase biodiesel blend mandate from B10 to B15, says Economy Minister
text: To ensure a smooth transition, Akmal Nasrullah said Putrajaya will hold engagement sessions with the Oil Industry Technical Committee (OITC).

title: M'sia to increase biodiesel blend mandate from B10 to B15, says Economy Minister
text: “The government will continue to monitor global developments, ensure our energy supply remains sufficient, and accelerate reforms towards a more resilient energy system as a national strategic necessity,” he added.

title: M'sia to increase biodiesel blend mandate from B10 to B15, says Economy Minister
text: Akmal Nasrullah also said Malaysia has massive untapped potential in this sector, pointing out that last year, the country produced 975,207 metric tonnes of biodiesel, despite having a total production capacity of 2.36 million metric tonnes.

title: M'sia to increase biodiesel blend mandate from B10 to B15, says Economy Minister

title: Rise above politics, Malaysians must prepare for prolonged global crisis, urges Sultan Nazrin

title: Rise above politics, Malaysians must prepare for prolonged global crisis, urges Sultan Nazrin
